title: Abóbora de Covilhete (Candied Pumpkin)
body:
## Ingredients

- pumpkin
- sugar equal in weight to the cooked pumpkin
- musk

## Directions

1. Once the pumpkin is split, weigh it raw on a scale, and take as many arratels of sugar as there are of pumpkin
2. Place the raw pumpkin in a pot to cook
3. Once cooked, grind in a stone mortar and pass through a sieve so the water drains, then place in cloth and wring well so no water remains
4. Place the arratels of sugar in a pot over fire, and once the sugar is clean, add the pumpkin, cooking it in the sugar
5. When it no longer falls from the spoon when it is turned with it, it is done
6. Add the musk desired, place in covilhetes, and dry in the sun
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Ade de Marquim (Marquim's Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck, half-roasted
- salt and pepper
- slices of prosciutto
- loin or bacon, also salted and peppered
- butter
- thin dough leaves

## Directions

1. After the duck is half-roasted and well salted and peppered, insert slices of prosciutto, loin, or bacon (also salted and peppered) inside
2. Coat the duck very well with butter
3. Cover the top with thin dough leaves
4. Wrap in paper greased with butter and return to the fire
5. Finish roasting, then remove the paper and place on a plate
6. Serve at table
7. Optionally, you may also make a crust of beaten eggs and sugar or breadcrumbs on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Ades Ordinarias (Ordinary Roasted Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- one roasted duck, either wild or domestic
- white wine
- two ounces of butter
- one quince in separate quarters
- all the adubos (seasonings)
- toasted bread slices
- lemon

## Directions

1. As the duck roasts, collect the drippings until it is half-roasted
2. Add a splash of white wine to the drippings
3. Place the entire duck in a cooking vessel with two ounces of butter, one quince in separate quarters, and all the seasonings
4. When cooked, place on a plate with toasted bread slices, the sauce, quince quarters, and lemon on top
5. Serve at table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Aguas para Beber e para Sorvetes (Drinking Waters and for Sorbets)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- ripe fruit (such as cherries, strawberries, blackberries, currants, or other)
- sugar

## Directions

1. In each three quarts of water, pour one pound of ripe fruit such as cherries, strawberries, blackberries, currants, or any other.
2. Crush with a spoon and mix very well in the water.
3. Strain through a cloth and sweeten with sugar.
4. Strain again through a cloth filter.
5. Put in a cool place and serve this water when occasion arises.
6. If it is for sorbets, add more sugar, put it in an ice cream maker of Flanders sheets, surround it with snow and salt, and as it begins to freeze, scrape off what sticks to the sides with a spoon.
7. Cover the ice cream maker with its lid and stir constantly with it until it is equally frozen, then fill cups with it at the moment you wish to drink it, and serve it without delay.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Alcachofras (Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- butter
- egg yolks
- broth
- cinnamon
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Clean the artichokes, remove the stem from inside, and after cooking them very well
2. Stuff them inside and between leaf and leaf in such a manner that they look like a pastry with the filling you wish
3. Place in a frying pan lined with butter with egg yolks below and on top, and a drop of broth
4. Cook slowly on gentle heat below and above, bind with egg yolks
5. Serve at table over bread slices with cinnamon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Alcachofras Cozidas (Boiled Artichokes)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- water
- salt
- butter
- ham essence
- butter
- coarse pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Trim evenly the tips of the artichoke leaves
2. Leave them to simmer in water and salt with a piece of butter
3. When cooked, place them to drain, remove the hay with a spoon, arrange them on the plate, and serve with sauce of essence bound with butter, coarse pepper, and a dash of vinegar
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Alcachofras Fritas (Fried Artichokes)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- cold water
- vinegar
- eggs
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- pork lard or beef butter or olive oil

## Directions

1. Trim the artichokes, cut into quarters, remove the hay and first leaves
2. Soak in cold water and a little vinegar, then drain
3. At serving time, put them in a bowl with eggs, flour, salt, pepper, and a dash of vinegar
4. Mix everything very well and fry little by little in pork lard that is not too hot
5. When fried, serve garnished with parsley for an entre-mets course
6. For meatless days, fry in beef butter or olive oil
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Alcachofras Recheadas (Stuffed Artichokes)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- ham or bacon slices
- ground meat, fish, or herbs for filling
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- fine herbs

## Directions

1. Trim and clean the artichokes, remove the hay from the middle, and stuff them with any ground meat, fish, or herbs
2. Place them to cook between slices of bacon, with half a spoon of broth, salt, pepper, two cloves, and a bundle of fine herbs
3. When cooked, place them to drain on a sieve and serve afterwards with ham essence
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Alcachofras à Provençal (Artichokes Provençal)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salt
- pepper
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Trim the tips of the artichokes, season with salt, pepper, and olive oil
2. Place them in a baking dish, put them to cook in the oven, and leave them to simmer little by little
3. When softened, remove them, place on the plate, add a drop of olive oil, and serve as an entre-mets
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Alcatra de Vacca à Bréza (Braised Beef Brisket)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef weighing fifteen or twenty pounds
- thick bacon for larding
- twine
- bacon slices for the bottom and sides of the pot
- beef slices
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- onions
- carrots
- two heads of garlic
- a bundle of fine herbs
- half a spoon of broth
- half a quartilho of white wine

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef weighing fifteen or twenty pounds and lard it with thick bacon
2. Tie it with twine and place it in a pot with a bottom lined with bacon slices and beef slices, seasoned with salt, pepper, cloves, onions, carrots, two heads of garlic, and a bundle of fine herbs
3. Cover with beef and bacon slices as below
4. Cook slowly between coals
5. Pour half a spoon of broth and half a quartilho of white wine and let it simmer slowly
6. When cooked, remove the twine, drain of its fat, place on a plate with the ragú of vegetables or other sauce as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Alcatra de Vacca à Cardeal (Beef Brisket Cardinal Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef
- thick bacon for larding
- two ounces of pounded saltpeter
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- fine herbs
- a little juniper seed
- a glazed earthenware pot with lid
- bacon slices
- cloth and twine
- one canada of red wine
- two of water
- cloves studded onions
- one head of garlic
- carrots
- parsnips
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- thyme
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Remove the eyes from the underside of a piece of beef and lard it with thick bacon
2. Season without piercing the surface part, then rub with two ounces of pounded saltpeter
3. Season with salt, pepper, spices, fine herbs, and a little juniper seed
4. Place in a glazed earthenware pot with lid where it shall remain for four or five days
5. Cover with some bacon slices and wrap in cloth, tie with twine
6. Place to cook in a pot with one canada of red wine and two of water, with some cloves studded onions, one head of garlic, carrots, parsnips, bay leaf, marjoram, thyme, and nutmeg
7. When cooked, let it cool in the same broth
8. When cold, unwrap, trim, and serve for fiambre on a plate with its napkin
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Alerões de Peru com Essência de Presunto (Turkey Wings with Ham Essence)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey wings
- bacon finely minced

## Directions

1. After blanching and larding with finely minced bacon a dozen turkey wings (recipe continues beyond the page)
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Alerões de Peru com Queijo Parmezam (Turkey Wings with Parmesan Cheese)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal slices
- ham slices
- parsley sauce
- fine herbs
- bacon rendered
- turkey wings
- white wine
- cullis
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of a casserole with slices of veal and some slices of ham, a bed of parsley sauce, fine herbs, and rendered bacon
2. Place on top a dozen well-trimmed turkey wings
3. Put them to cook on gentle heat moistened with a little white wine
4. When cooked, remove them and add to what remains in the bottom of the casserole a spoonful of cullis and put to boil
5. Once reduced, pass this sauce through a sieve, remove the fat, and put a little in the bottom of the serving plate with a little grated cheese
6. Arrange the wings on top, pour the rest of the sauce over them, cover with grated cheese
7. Put it to color in the oven or over a stove, color it on top with a hot spatula, and serve immediately for an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Alerões de Peru em Crépinia (Turkey Wings in Crépinette)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey wings
- chicken breast forcemeat à crème
- caul
- eggs
- fine breadcrumbs
- lard
- parsley sauce fried

## Directions

1. After trimming, soaking, and blanching a dozen turkey wings, put them to cook in a good white broth
2. When cooked and cold, cover all around carefully with a forcemeat of chicken breasts à crème well bound
3. Wrap them in a piece of caul moistened in eggs so it adheres
4. Dust them with fine grated bread and fry them in lard
5. When fried and of good color, serve garnished with fried parsley sauce
6. They can also be colored in the oven or on the grill and served with clear essence sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Alerões de Perú Recheados para Huma Entrada (Stuffed Turkey Wings for an Entrée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey, chicken, or chicken breast skins
- forcemeat à crème
- chicken or capon fillets roasted
- béchamel sauce
- eggs
- bacon slice
- bread of good taste

## Directions

1. For this you need copper molds imitating turkey wings, which will be lined inside with the skins of turkeys, chickens, or chickens from which the breasts have been removed
2. On these place a forcemeat à crème, finishing filling the molds with fillets of roasted capons or chickens placed in a béchamel sauce bound and of good taste
3. On top of this a layer of the forcemeat that was placed below, and cover with the rest of the skins, soldered with eggs, and on top of everything a slice of bacon
4. Once done, put them to cook in the oven or gentle steam for about an hour
5. Remove them, drain the fat, turn the molds carefully onto their plate, and serve with sauce of good taste as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Allumélas de Brixovas (Fried Anchovies)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty anchovies
- olive oil
- salt
- flour
- white wine

## Directions

1. Open the anchovies in two parts and remove the spines
2. Soak in a quart of olive oil
3. Remove the salt, place on cloth, and dry
4. Pass through a batter made with flour, white wine, and a little olive oil
5. Fry
6. Fried and of good color, serve hot as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Almojavenas de Requeição (Curd Cheese Almojavenas)
body:
## Ingredients

- Curd cheese
- Six egg yolks
- Half arratel of grated bread of ló
- Half arratel of sugar
- One quarter of cow butter
- Flower water

## Directions

1. Put curd cheese in a pot with six egg yolks, half arratel of grated bread of ló, half arratel of sugar, one quarter of cow butter, and flower water.
2. Beat this mixture.
3. Pour it into a baking pan and bake it in the pan.
4. Shape into almojavenas, pass them through sugar with cinnamon on top, and send to the table.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Almondegas (Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of lamb from the leg
- a quarter of bacon
- aromatics
- three eggs
- a crumb of grated bread
- seasonings
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean two arratels of lamb from the leg, removing nerves and skin
2. Finely chop with a quarter of bacon and aromatics
3. Add three eggs, grated bread crumb, seasonings, vinegar, and salt
4. Finely chop everything again very well
5. Heat a bowl over the fire
6. Cook the meatballs in it over low heat, making them the size desired
7. Season again with all seasonings
8. Bind with three egg yolks and lemon juice
9. Place on a plate greased with one ounce of cow butter with slices of bread
10. Place the meatballs on top with thick broth
11. Top with cinnamon and lemon, and take to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Almondegas de Perum (Turkey Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- one turkey
- half an arratel of bacon
- aromatics
- all seasonings
- grated bread crumb
- vinegar
- broth from the pan
- three egg yolks
- cinnamon
- lemon

## Directions

1. Mince one turkey with half an arratel of bacon and aromatics
2. Add all seasonings, grated bread crumb, and vinegar
3. Let simmer on low heat
4. Heat a bowl with broth from the pan
5. Add the meatballs to the broth as they are formed
6. At the end, thicken with three egg yolks
7. Place on slices with cinnamon and lemon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Alãos de Cabrito (Kid Goat Hands)
body:
## Ingredients

- kid goat hands, skinned
- eggs
- flour
- citron slices
- bread slices
- chicken or mutton broth
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. After skinning the kid goat hands, cook them first, then place them in conserve for four hours
2. Pass them through a batter of eggs and flour and place on a silver plate or frying pan over slices of bread also passed through the same batter, with thin citron slices between hand and hand
3. Make a sauce of rich chicken broth or sour and sweet mutton broth and pour it over the hands
4. Send to the table after browning with cinnamon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Amores de Oitella (Veal or Mutton Sweetbreads)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal or mutton sweetbreads
- onion slices
- sage sprigs
- marjoram
- bay leaf
- salt
- pepper
- lemon slices
- water
- vinegar
- flour

## Directions

1. Take the quantity of sweetbreads needed
2. Cut into pieces five or six inches long
3. Marinate with onion slices, sage sprigs, marjoram, bay leaf, salt, pepper, lemon slices, half a cup of water, and a little vinegar
4. After marinating two or three hours, place on cloth
5. Dry, coat in flour, fry in hot frying oil
6. Fried and of good color, serve as an entrée
7. Can also be passed through a batter if desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Andulhas de Porco (Pork Chitterlings)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- pork intestines
- water and vinegar
- bay leaves
- thyme
- marjoram
- pork lard
- pork meat
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- fine herbs
- a little fennel
- broth
- white wine

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the intestines
2. Soak them in water and vinegar with bay leaves, thyme, and marjoram
3. Cut part of them into fillets and similarly cut pork lard and pork meat
4. Season everything with salt, pepper, spices, fine herbs, and a little fennel
5. Season and ensure it has good taste
6. Fill the larger intestines but leave a third part empty so they do not burst
7. Tie them at both ends
8. Make them all of the same length
9. Cook them in a sauce with broth, white wine, fine herbs, and lard
10. Once cooked, let them cool in the same sauce
11. Remove them and drain on a cloth
12. Once dried, fry them on both sides
13. Serve as an appetizer
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Andulhas de Vitela (Veal Chitterlings)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal small intestines
- pork fat
- veal udder
- salt
- pepper
- fine spices
- fine herbs
- two shallots, chopped
- four or five egg yolks
- handful of grated bread
- one quartilho of milk

## Directions

1. Fill the small veal intestines with a piece of blanched pork fat and a veal udder, both cut into thin strips or slices
2. Place in a casserole seasoned with salt, pepper, fine spices, fine herbs, and two chopped shallots
3. Add four or five egg yolks, a handful of grated bread, and one quartilho of milk
4. Heat on the fire while stirring constantly
5. Once thickened and having good taste, fill the intestines
6. Tie them as instructed
7. Cook them in the same way
8. Once cooked, cooled, and dried, cover them with fine grated bread
9. Fry them on a gentle fire on both sides
10. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Andulhas à Crema (Cream Chitterlings)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four onions
- butter
- veal sweetbread
- pork belly
- pork lard
- one ear
- two breasts of roasted chicken
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- fine herbs
- a little fennel or anise
- one quartilho of milk or egg yolks
- a little grated bread
- intestines for filling

## Directions

1. Cut three or four onions into thin fillets
2. Sauté them in a casserole in melted butter
3. Once sautéed, remove from the heat
4. Add veal sweetbread, pork belly, lard, and one ear, all blanched and chopped finely
5. Add two breasts of roasted chicken, also in fillets
6. Place back on the fire seasoned with salt, pepper, spices, fine herbs, and a little fennel or anise
7. Sauté everything
8. Add one quartilho of milk or egg yolks and a little grated bread
9. Once thickened and having good taste, fill the intestines
10. Tie them as instructed
11. Cook as the preceding ones
12. Once cooled, cover them with fine grated bread
13. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Argolinhas de Amendoas (Almond Threads Candy)
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of sugar
- one arratel of almonds, finely ground
- some flour

## Directions

1. Put one arratel of sugar in a pan and bring to thread point
2. When at thread point, remove from heat and add one arratel of finely ground almonds and some flour that is sufficient to cook well until they clean the pan well
3. See if they can be drawn into threads, and when drawn, let cool
4. Send to the oven; when they return, cover with one arratel of sugar put at low thread point
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Arraia Assada nas Grelhas (Grilled Ray)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two halves of ray
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- breadcrumbs
- remoulade sauce or anchovy coulis
- ray liver

## Directions

1. After the ray is cleaned, skinned, and trimmed, cut it into two halves
2. Melt a piece of butter in a casserole
3. Place the ray inside, season with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
4. Remove and cover with fine breadcrumbs
5. Place on grills to roast, basting from time to time
6. When roasted on both sides and of good color, serve with remoulade sauce or, if not available, with anchovy coulis and the ray liver pounded, making sure it is hot and spicy
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Arraia Assada nas Grelhas (Grilled Skate)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- skate halves
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. After parboiling, skinning, and cleaning two skate halves
2. In a pan, melt a piece of butter
3. Place the skate inside, season with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
4. Remove, cover with fine breadcrumbs
5. Place on grill to roast, basting occasionally
6. When roasted on both sides and golden in color, serve
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Arraia Cozida (Boiled Skate)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- skate
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- butter
- capers
- anchovies
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the skate in two halves
2. Remove the liver and entrails
3. Wash very well
4. Simmer five or six minutes in water, salt, and a little vinegar
5. Remove the skin from both sides with the back of a knife
6. Clean up, place on plate
7. Serve with bound sauce made with butter, capers, anchovies, pepper, and a drop of vinegar
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Arraia de Molho Pardo (Ray with Brown Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- finely chopped onion
- two anchovies
- one cup of white or red wine
- half spoon of meat broth
- half spoon of substance
- salt
- pepper
- small pinch of saffron
- bay leaf
- sprig of marjoram
- two halves of fried ray with whole capers
- vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Brown the flour in butter with finely chopped onion
2. Add two anchovies
3. Pour in one cup of white or red wine, half a spoon of meat broth, and half a spoon of substance
4. Season with salt, pepper, a small pinch of saffron, a bay leaf, and a sprig of marjoram
5. Let it boil for a moment
6. Add the two halves of fried ray with whole capers
7. Serve hot with a splash of vinegar or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Arraia Frita (Fried Ray)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- ray
- water
- vinegar
- butter
- fine herbs
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- onion in slices
- saffron
- cloves
- lemon in slices
- clarified butter
- flour
- fried saffron

## Directions

1. Cut the ray into equal pieces
2. Blanch, skin, and marinate for two or three hours in warm water, vinegar, butter, fine herbs, salt, pepper, garlic, onion slices, saffron, cloves, and lemon slices
3. After marinating, drain in a sieve
4. Dry in a cloth
5. Flour the pieces
6. Fry in clarified butter
7. Serve with fried saffron slices arranged around
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Arroz Doce (Sweet Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- One arratel of rice
- One pint of milk
- One arratel of sugar
- Flower water
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Soak one arratel of rice in water.
2. Cook it with one pint of milk and one arratel of sugar and flower water.
3. When it is cooked, send to the table with cinnamon on top.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Assada de Lebre (Roasted Hare)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- lard
- blood of the hare
- liver
- penned
- blood
- picante sauce or sweet sauce

## Directions

1. Skin the hare, open it by the belly, remove the tripe and bile, keep the fresadura and blood
2. Break the bones of the thighs with the flat of the knife, and putting the legs one into the other, place it to stiffen over some coals and rub it with its own blood
3. When cold, lard it with fine lard and put it to roast on the spit
4. After roasted, serve it with picante sauce or with sweet sauce
5. Also after pounding the liver and serving in peverada sauce, pass it through a sieve, put it on the fire, bind it with the rest of the blood, and serve it separately
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Atida (Egg Yolk Dish)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- dozen egg yolks
- half dozen egg whites
- melted butter
- chopped sorrel
- a bit of garlic
- grated nutmeg
- Parmesan cheese, grated (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks and whites together
2. Put them in melted butter with chopped sorrel and a bit of garlic
3. Add grated nutmeg and optionally grated Parmesan cheese
4. Stir constantly until cooked
5. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bacalhau Assado nas Grelhas (Grilled Cod)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- olive oil or melted butter
- bread crumbs
- orange or lemon juice

## Directions

1. If the cod pieces are large, cut them in half; otherwise cut into various pieces
2. Cook them, and when cooked, transfer to a plate
3. Drain them and pass through olive oil or melted butter
4. Cover with grated bread
5. Grill on the grates
6. Serve with olive oil and orange or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bacalhau à Bexamela (Cod with Béchamel Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cod
- béchamel sauce
- egg yolks
- egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook the cod and shred it very finely
2. Mix it into a well-made béchamel sauce of good flavor
3. If making a single dish, add half a dozen egg yolks and a quarter of a loaf before serving
4. Mix in the beaten egg whites
5. Grease the bottom of a dish with butter
6. Place the cod mixture inside
7. Bake in the oven or under a baking dish lid
8. When it rises and looks good, serve hot in the same dish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bacalhau à Provençal (Cod Provençal)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- water
- olive oil
- garlic
- fresh butter
- water or milk

## Directions

1. Choose the clearest cod possible and soak it for one or two days, changing the water two or three times
2. Drain and place it on the fire in sufficient water until it comes to a boil
3. Remove from heat and let it rest half an hour
4. Break it into pieces, removing the bones
5. Cook it on the fire with olive oil and chopped garlic
6. Add a good piece of fresh butter and a drop of water or milk
7. Stir constantly over gentle heat until it binds
8. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Beef Brisket Grated or Browned in the Oven
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brisket
- six egg yolks
- broth
- half a spoon of culinary salt
- ground pepper
- breadcrumbs
- slices of bread toasted with eggs
- fried

## Directions

1. After cooking in a pot in the ordinary manner, remove to a platter
2. After trimming, take six egg yolks mixed in a little broth, half a spoon of salt, and ground pepper
3. Thickening all this over the fire, spread it over the piece of beef, which is covered with breadcrumbs
4. After browning, serve it garnished with slices of bread toasted with eggs and fried
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Beef Brisket with Cabbage and Sausages
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- eight equal pieces of beef brisket, well trimmed
- broth
- shallot
- two cloves of garlic
- thyme
- one bay leaf
- six or eight quarters of cabbage
- seven or eight whole onions
- salt
- whole pepper
- one dozen whole sausages

## Directions

1. Soak the pieces of beef brisket and then blanch them in boiling water
2. Cook them in broth with a shallot, two cloves of garlic, thyme, and a bay leaf
3. When they are half-cooked, add six or eight quarters of cabbage that have been blanched and pressed, well tied, along with seven or eight whole onions, seasoned with a little salt and whole pepper
4. When everything is almost cooked, add one dozen whole sausages
5. When everything is cooked, remove the meat and vegetables, drain them well on a sieve, and once they are well dried of their fat, place the pieces of beef in the middle of the plate and garnish completely with the cabbage, onions, and the sausages on top
6. Pass the broth through a sieve, remove the fat from it, pour it over, and serve everything well hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Beef Loaf (Pão de Vacca)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lean meat from the cow's thigh
- half of it combined with fat meat, well cleaned of skins
- bacon cut into small cubes
- some small cubes of ham
- salt
- pepper
- shallots
- parsley
- chives
- half a pint of brandy
- four egg yolks
- bread

## Directions

1. Choose the necessary amount of lean meat from the cow's thigh and make a mince with half of what you have of meat with fat, well cleaned of skins
2. Once everything is minced, place it in a bowl or casserole with enough bacon cut into small cubes and some small cubes of ham
3. Season with salt, pepper, shallots, parsley, chives, half a pint of brandy, and four egg yolks
4. After everything is well mixed with a spoon of bread, line a casserole with strips of bacon, arrange the mince well in it, cover it with its lid well sealed around with flour mixed in vinegar
5. Cook in its own steam for three or four hours
6. Once well cooked, let it cool, and served cold it is used as a middle course dish. It can also be served hot with whatever sauce seems appropriate
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Beef Loin Braised (Lombo de Vacca à Brèza)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin, tender and tenderized
- bacon
- ham
- veal strips
- brains
- salt
- pepper
- culinary salt
- Champagne wine
- capers
- truffles
- mushrooms
- cardoons
- chicory
- celery
- green beans
- cucumbers

## Directions

1. Take a tender and tenderized beef loin, remove the skins, and lard it well with thick bacon, ham, veal strips, and brains, everything well seasoned as is customary
2. Cook it in a good well-nourished braize
3. When well cooked, serve it with a sauce made with culinary salt and Champagne wine, capers, truffles, mushrooms, and a ragú of vegetables such as cardoons, chicory, celery, green beans, and cucumbers
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Beef Loin Fricandeau (Lombo de Vacca de fricandó)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two beef loins
- bacon
- good broth
- a strip of bacon
- a piece of ham
- a bundle of herbs
- one onion with two cloves

## Directions

1. Take one or two beef loins, remove the skins, and beat them between two cloths with the back of a knife
2. Lard them with fine bacon, soak them, and then pass them quickly through boiling water
3. Remove them from the water and plunge them in cold water
4. Cook them in good broth with a strip of bacon, a piece of ham, a bundle of herbs, and an onion with two cloves
5. Once cooked, pass the broth through a sieve, remove the fat, and reduce it until thick
6. Then add the fricandeau with the bacon side facing down, and when it has a good color, remove it carefully
7. Loosen what remains stuck to the bottom of the casserole with a little broth
8. Add a little culinary salt, and once it tastes good, pass it through the sieve again and serve well hot with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Beef Loin in Manguito (Lombo de Vacca em manguito)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- bacon, minced
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- garlic
- shallots
- salt
- pepper
- bacon strips

## Directions

1. Take a beef loin and after preparing it, bore a hole through it from end to end with a stick
2. With a knife, fill the hole with minced bacon, parsley, chives, mushrooms, garlic, shallots, salt, and pepper
3. Tie the two ends with string and roast it covered with strips of bacon
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Beef Loin Marinated and Roasted on a Spit (Lombo de Vacca marinado, e assado no espeto)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin, tender
- fine bacon
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- onion slices
- garlic cloves
- shallot sauce

## Directions

1. Take a tender beef loin and after trimming, lard it with fine bacon in the form of fricandeau
2. Marinate it in a little vinegar, salt, pepper, bay leaf, onion slices, and some garlic cloves
3. After it has been in the marinade for two or three hours, remove it, place it on a spit covered with sheets of paper, and roast it
4. Once well roasted but not too dry, serve it with a piquant shallot sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Beef Loin with Lettuce Stems (Lombo de Vacca com tallos de alface)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- lettuce stems
- ham essence
- lemon juice
- sorrel ragú or spinach ragú

## Directions

1. Take a beef loin, cut it into two or three pieces, and lard each one separately
2. Prepare and finish as fricandeau
3. Take the lettuce stems and cook them in a well-nourished white broth
4. Once well cooked and well dried, place them in a ham essence, adding what remained stuck to the bottom of the casserole
5. Once well flavored, place the stems around the meat and serve with lemon juice. It can also be served with a sorrel or spinach ragú
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Biscoitos de Avos (Egg Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen eggs with whites
- one arratel of sugar
- one arratel of flour
- a small amount of fennel, chosen
- rose water

## Directions

1. Beat a dozen eggs with whites and one arratel of sugar
2. Add one arratel of flour, a small amount of fennel, and rose water
3. After beating this mixture very well, place with a spoon in basins on baking sheets at the oven door with flour underneath the sheets
4. When half-cooked, remove, cut with a knife to desired size, and turning them, finish cooking in the oven
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Biscoitos de la Reina (Queen's Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- fifteen eggs with five whites removed
- one arratel of powdered stone sugar
- one arratel of flour
- powdered sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Beat fifteen eggs (removing the whites from five) with one arratel of stone sugar in powder for the space of three hours
2. When beaten, add one arratel of flour, stirring until it incorporates with the eggs
3. Cut onto paper to the length of a palm and height of a finger
4. Put in the oven with powdered sugar on top
5. When half-cooked, remove and detach from the paper with a knife
6. Put back in the oven to bake through
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Biscoitos de Massa (Butter Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of flour
- half an arratel of butter without salt
- half an arratel of sugar
- half a dozen egg yolks
- lukewarm water
- a stone of salt

## Directions

1. Knead a quarter of flour with half an arratel of butter without salt and half an arratel of sugar
2. Add half a dozen egg yolks and lukewarm water with a stone of salt dissolved in it
3. When well kneaded, form small biscuits the size of a finger or make little rings
4. Bake in a basin
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Biscuits de Amêndoas (Almond Biscuits)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half an arratél of almonds
- Fine sugar for grinding
- Six egg yolks
- Two ounces of flour
- Half a pound of fine sugar
- Eight well-beaten and raised egg whites
- Fine sugar mixed with flour for dusting

## Directions

1. Peel, wash and dry half an arratél of almonds, grind them in a stone mortar until they become very fine powder, adding from time to time a little fine sugar
2. When ground, pass them to a bowl, add six egg yolks, two ounces of flour, half a pound of fine sugar and beat for a quarter hour
3. After beating, add eight well-beaten and raised egg whites, mix everything very well
4. Pour into square paper forms, two fingers wide, and greased with butter inside
5. Pulverize with fine sugar mixed with flour and put them in a very mild oven
6. When cooked, remove the paper while hot and serve as necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Biscuits de colher (Spoon Biscuits)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Composition same as the preceding biscuits
- One additional egg white according to the quantity made

## Directions

1. Prepare the composition the same as the preceding biscuits, adding one additional egg white according to the quantity being made
2. Fill a small silver spoon with the mixture and place gracefully and lengthwise the small pieces on sheets of white paper
3. Sprinkle a little fine sugar on top and put them to bake in a very mild oven
4. When cooked, remove them, detach them quickly from the paper with a knife and serve them as fresh as possible
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Biscuits de Farinha de arroz (Rice Flour Biscuits)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Four ounces of rice flour, sifted through fine silk sieve
- One arratél of fine sugar
- Eight egg yolks
- A little finely chopped lime peel
- Fifteen or sixteen egg whites, beaten and well raised

## Directions

1. Sift through a very fine silk sieve four ounces of rice flour, put it in a bowl with one arratél of fine sugar, eight egg yolks, a little finely chopped lime peel
2. Beat everything with two spoons for the time of a quarter hour
3. Carefully mix fifteen or sixteen well-beaten and raised egg whites
4. Form afterwards some paper boxes greased with butter inside, put the composition in them
5. Put them to bake in a very mild oven
6. When cooked, remove them from the boxes, cover them with white glaze made with one egg white, fine sugar and some drops of lemon juice
7. Put them in the oven again and when the glaze is dry, remove them and serve them when it is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Biscuits de vários doces (Biscuits of Various Sweets)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three ounces of citron or lemon peel
- A little orange flower in powder, covered
- Two small spoonfuls of marmalade, of apricots or any other
- Four ounces of fine sugar
- Four or five egg yolks
- Egg whites, well beaten

## Directions

1. Grind in a stone mortar two or three ounces of citron or lemon peel, add a little orange flower powder covered, two small spoonfuls of marmalade of apricots or any other, four ounces of fine sugar, four or five egg yolks
2. After everything is ground and well mixed, pass through a sieve with force until everything dries
3. Add afterwards the well-beaten egg whites, mix everything well
4. Form the biscuits with a spoon on sheets of paper, like the preceding ones
5. Pulverize with fine sugar on top, bake in a mild oven and serve them like the preceding ones
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Biscuits ordinários (Ordinary Biscuits)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks, quantity necessary for the biscuits to be made
- Fine sugar, equal weight to the yolks
- Flour, weight equal to half the eggs removed from the balance
- A little lime peel, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Take the necessary quantity of eggs for the biscuits to be made and weigh them on a balance
2. Weigh with the same amount of fine sugar
3. Remove half of the eggs from the balance and weigh the same amount of flour with the remaining eggs on the other side
4. Put the yolks in a bowl or basin with the sugar and a little finely chopped lime peel
5. Beat everything very well for the time of half an hour
6. Add the well-beaten and raised egg whites afterwards
7. Mix everything carefully, adding the flour little by little and gently mixing it with the composition after it is very well prepared
8. Form some paper boxes, grease them with butter inside, fill them to the middle with this mixture
9. Sprinkle a little fine sugar on top and put them to bake in an oven that does not have too much heat for the time of half an hour
10. When cooked and with a golden color, remove from the oven and before they cool remove them from the boxes to serve them when it is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bolinhos de amêndoa (Almond Balls or Marzipans)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One arratél of almonds
- Up to three egg whites
- A little marmalade or apricot preserve or any other
- A little orange flower covered and reduced to powder
- Fine sugar for drying
- More fine sugar for preventing sticking
- Egg whites, half beaten
- Fine sugar for coating

## Directions

1. Blanched, cleaned and dried one arratél of almonds, grind them in a stone mortar, adding from time to time up to three egg whites
2. When ground, add a little marmalade or apricot preserve or any other, a little orange flower covered and reduced to powder
3. Mix everything and dry it with fine sugar on the fire
4. Once dried, pour it on a table with another little sugar until the paste stops sticking to the hands
5. Cut afterwards into pieces, make them into little rolls, form the marzipans in the shape desired
6. Pass them through half-beaten egg whites, place them on fine sugar and pulverize them on all sides with the same sugar
7. When well covered with it, place them on sheets of white paper and these on sheets of copper
8. Put them to bake in a mild oven and serve them when necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bollo de Caracol (Snail Roll)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- cream or franchipane cream
- eggs
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Stretch out some puff pastry the length of the table, cut into two strips six fingers wide
2. Put cream or well-bound franchipane cream in the middle, brush the pastry edges with beaten eggs, and roll like a sausage
3. Roll into a snail shape on a greased paper sheet, brush with egg, and bake in the oven
4. When cooked and golden, serve dusted with fine sugar as a between-course dish
5. It can also be filled with marmalade or any fruit preserve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolo de Amendoas (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half an eighth of flour
- four eggs with their egg whites
- a little bit of butter
- one quarter of fine sugar
- a few grains of salt
- six ounces of ground almonds

## Directions

1. Take half an eighth of flour, four eggs with their egg whites, a little bit of butter, one quarter of fine sugar, a few grains of salt, six ounces of ground almonds
2. Knead everything together
3. When bound and of good consistency, form the cake
4. Place it on buttered paper
5. Put it to cook in the oven with moderate heat
6. After baked, browned, and glazed, serve it when desired
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolo de Amêndoas (Almond Cake)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half an ounce of flour
- four eggs with their whites
- a little bit of butter
- a quarter of fine sugar
- some grains of salt
- six ounces of ground almonds

## Directions

1. Mix everything together, and when it is bound and of good consistency, form the cake
2. Place on buttered paper
3. Put in the oven to bake with moderate heat
4. After baking, when browned and glazed, serve when desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bolo de Bacia (Basin Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quarter of flour
- cold water
- salt
- two egg yolks
- little butter
- rose water
- one arratel of almonds pounded
- one arratel of sugar in point
- a dozen strands of white blancmange
- six egg yolks
- a quarter of sugar
- half a dozen cooked egg yolks
- a little lemon
- half a dozen royal eggs
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Knead half a quarter of flour with cold water seasoned with salt
2. Add two egg yolks, little butter, and rose water
3. When very well kneaded, cut it into strands
4. Spread into thin leaves the size of the basin
5. In a basin well greased with butter, put in a leaf
6. Over it put one arratel of almonds pounded, made into marzipan in one arratel of sugar in point
7. Cover with another leaf
8. Over it put a dozen strands of white blancmange beaten with six egg yolks and a quarter of sugar
9. Cover with another leaf greased with butter
10. Over it put some soft eggs with cinnamon on top
11. Cover with another greased leaf
12. Put over it half a dozen cooked egg yolks, a little lemon around, half a dozen royal eggs in the middle
13. Send to the oven
14. When cooked, if desired, cut into slices of desired size
15. Pass through sugar in point and serve at the table sprinkled with cinnamon
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolo de Queijo (Cheese Cake)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of fresh Alentejo cheese, well softened
- an equal amount of fresh butter
- one ounce of flour
- five or six eggs

## Directions

1. Pound in a stone mortar one pound of fresh Alentejo cheese, well softened, with an equal amount of fresh butter
2. After pounding, place on the table and add to it one ounce of flour and five or six eggs
3. Mix everything together
4. When the dough is bound, wrap it in a damp cloth for an hour
5. Remove it, form the cake, pour it out, bake in the oven, and serve plain
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bolo de Queijo (Cheese Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of fresh, soft cheese from Alem-Tejo
- another pound of fresh butter
- one eighth of flour
- five or six eggs

## Directions

1. Grind in a stone mortar one pound of fresh, soft cheese from Alem-Tejo with another pound of fresh butter
2. After being ground, place it on the table
3. Add to it one eighth of flour and five or six eggs
4. Knead everything together
5. When the dough is bound, wrap it in a damp cloth for one hour
6. Then remove it, form the cake, pour it into a casserole, bake it in the oven
7. Serve it plain
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolo Flamengo (Flemish Cake)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of flour
- two fresh eggs
- half a pound of butter
- some grains of salt
- milk as needed
- a little bit of yeast

## Directions

1. Take one ounce of flour, two fresh eggs, half a pound of butter, some grains of salt, and mix everything with as much milk as necessary, adding a little bit of yeast
2. When the dough is bound and kneaded, place it on a sheet of paper, cover with a warm towel, and let it ferment by the fire for five quarters of an hour
3. Then form the cake as usual, place on buttered paper
4. Put in the oven to bake
5. When baked, eat it when desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bolo Flamengo (Flemish Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one eighth of flour
- two fresh eggs
- half a pound of butter
- a few grains of salt
- milk
- a bit of yeast

## Directions

1. Take one eighth of flour, two fresh eggs, half a pound of butter, a few grains of salt
2. Knead everything with as much milk as necessary
3. Add to it a bit of yeast
4. When the dough is bound and worked, place it on a sheet of paper
5. Cover it with a warm towel
6. Let it ferment by the fire for five quarters of an hour
7. Then form the cake as usual
8. Place it on buttered paper
9. Put it to cook in the oven
10. When baked, eat it when desired
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolo à Bernardille (Bernardille Cake)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half an ounce of flour
- four eggs with their whites
- a bit of lime peel, chopped
- a drop of flower water
- simple water

## Directions

1. Mix all together with simple water, and after being well kneaded, let it rest for an hour
2. Then stretch it out, cover with butter, fold it or roll it, stretch it again, finish it like puff pastry, and place in a round shape the thickness of a thumb
3. Put it in the oven to bake
4. Serve glazed with sugar and a hot spatula
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bolo à Magdalena (Magdalena Cake)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- eight whole eggs
- three quarters of fine sugar
- one pound of butter
- lime peel
- candied orange peel, chopped
- water

## Directions

1. After everything is mixed and kneaded, stretch it so it is the thickness of a thumb
2. Place on buttered paper
3. Bake in the oven
4. After baking, serve glazed with sugar and a hot spatula
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bolo à Poloneza (Polish Cake)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of flour
- a little milk
- half a dozen eggs with their whites
- half a pound of melted beef fat
- an equal amount of chopped Alicante raisins
- a drop of brandy
- salt
- a little ground coriander seed

## Directions

1. Break the flour in a little milk, and after being well dissolved, add to it the eggs with their whites, melted beef fat, chopped raisins, brandy, salt, and ground coriander seed
2. After everything is well mixed, pour into a buttered casserole dish
3. Put in the oven to bake for an hour and a half
4. When baked and well coloured, serve hot, dusted with fine sugar
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bolo á Bernouille (Bernouille Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half an eighth of flour
- four eggs with two egg whites
- a bit of lemon zest, chopped
- a drop of plain water

## Directions

1. After being well kneaded, let it rest for one hour
2. Then extend it, cover it with butter, fold or roll it
3. Extend it again, finish it like puff pastry
4. Place it in round form the thickness of one inch
5. Put it to cook in the oven
6. Serve it glazed with sugar and pass a hot spatula over it
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolo á Bernouille (Bernouille Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half an ounce of flour
- four eggs with two extra whites
- a piece of chopped lemon rind
- a drop of water

## Directions

1. Take half an ounce of flour, four eggs with two extra whites, a piece of chopped lemon rind, a drop of water
2. After being well kneaded, let rest for an hour
3. Stretch it out, cover with butter, fold or roll, stretch again, and finish like puff pastry
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolo á Magdalena (Magdalena Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- eight whole eggs
- three quarters of fine sugar
- one pound of butter
- lemon zest
- orange flower, chopped
- water

## Directions

1. Knead one pound of flour, eight whole eggs, three quarters of fine sugar, one pound of butter, lemon zest, and chopped orange flower with water
2. After everything is kneaded and worked, extend it so it becomes the thickness of one inch
3. Place it on buttered paper
4. Bake it in the oven
5. After it is baked, serve it glazed with sugar and pass a hot spatula over it
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolo á Poloneza (Polish Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of flour
- some milk
- half a dozen eggs with their egg whites
- half a pound of melted beef fat
- another half pound of chopped Alicante raisins
- a drop of spirits
- salt
- a small amount of chopped coriander seed

## Directions

1. Dissolve half a pound of flour in some milk
2. After being well dissolved, add to it half a dozen eggs with their egg whites, half a pound of melted beef fat, another half pound of chopped Alicante raisins, a drop of spirits
3. Season it with salt and a small amount of chopped coriander seed
4. After everything is well mixed, pour it into a casserole greased inside with butter
5. Put it to cook in the oven for an hour and a half
6. When cooked and well colored, serve it hot, dusted with fine sugar
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolos de Amendoa (Almond Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- four arratels of almonds, very well beaten
- four arratels of sugar in wire point
- two egg whites
- eight egg yolks
- one good spoon of butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat four arratels of almonds very well with four arratels of sugar in wire point
2. Add two egg whites and beat again
3. Put on the fire
4. When the pot shows the bottom, add eight egg yolks and one good spoon of butter
5. After giving it a couple of turns, place where desired
6. Spread with flour on the cakes, putting them in a basin away from each other
7. Send to cook
8. After cooked, pass through sugar and serve at the table with cinnamon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolos de Assucar (Sugar Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- half an alqueire of flour from Alem-Tejo
- two arratels of Madeira sugar
- one arratel of butter
- four egg yolks
- one glass of white wine
- rose water
- wheat
- salt

## Directions

1. Put into half an alqueire of flour from Alem-Tejo two arratels of Madeira sugar
2. Add one arratel of butter, four egg yolks, one glass of white wine, rose water, wheat, and salt
3. Rub this dough very well in the hands
4. Form little cakes
5. When they come from the oven, sprinkle with rose water and cover well
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolos de Campiene (Campiene Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- thick paper mold shaped like a manguito box
- clarified butter
- flour
- beer yeast
- eggs
- butter
- grated lemon rind
- sugar in thread stage
- colored nonpareils

## Directions

1. Make a paper mold in the shape of a manguito box, grease inside with butter, and fill halfway with dough made of flour, beer yeast, eggs, butter, and grated lemon rind, like brioche dough
2. Bake in the oven for two or three hours
3. When cooked, remove the paper, spread with clarified butter and sugar in thread stage, and serve with colored nonpareils on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolos de Ovos (Egg Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three dozen egg yolks, well beaten
- sugar in espada point

## Directions

1. Put two or three dozen very well beaten egg yolks in a tall pot that must be over heat with sugar in espada point
2. As they begin to curdle, stir with a spoon so they do not separate
3. When already curdled, drain the sugar
4. Let it take color in the same pot
5. Turn onto a plate and color it on the other side, putting a little sugar that was drained into the pot
6. After coloring, arrange on the plate and serve at the table with cinnamon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolos de Rodinhas (Spiral Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- water
- salt
- little butter
- one egg yolk
- pork butter
- two egg yolks beaten with butter
- soft eggs
- sugar in point
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make a dough with flour, water, salt, little butter, and one egg yolk
2. When very well kneaded, spread it
3. Put pork butter on it generously and roll it up in the breadlike dough well greased
4. Cut the cakes round and fry them
5. Open the puff pastry with a small roll
6. Put in each ball two egg yolks beaten with sufficient butter
7. Always open the puff pastry and fill with soft eggs if desired
8. After frying, pass through sugar in point with cinnamon on top and serve at the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolos Podres (Crumbly Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- half alqueire of sifted flour
- four arratels of sugar
- two canadas of oil
- one arratel of very finely ground almonds
- one vintem of clove, sweet herb, cinnamon, and salt
- yeast in small pieces

## Directions

1. Take four arratels of sugar, clean and brought to a thick paste consistency, add two canadas of oil and one arratel of very finely ground almonds, and one vintem of clove, sweet herb, cinnamon, and salt
2. Allow this to boil very well, then gradually add the half alqueire of sifted flour, stirring with a spoon until well cooked
3. Once cooked, place in a bowl and stir until it can be kneaded
4. While kneading, gradually add yeast in small pieces, continuing to knead
5. Once fully kneaded, cover until risen
6. Form the cakes, pressing well with the hands, place in basins, and send to the oven
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolos Podres (Soft Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- half an alqueire of sifted flour
- four arratels of clean sugar in pastille point
- two canadas of oil
- one arratel of very well pounded almonds
- one vintem of cloves
- fennel
- cinnamon
- salt

## Directions

1. For half an alqueire of sifted flour, take four arratels of clean sugar in pastille point
2. Add two canadas of oil and one arratel of very well pounded almonds
3. Add one vintem of cloves, fennel, cinnamon, and salt
4. When this boils very well, gradually add the half alqueire of sifted flour, stirring
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bolos à Delfina (Delphina Cakes)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a bit of royal paste
- half a dozen almond biscuits
- candied orange peel
- grated lime peel

## Directions

1. Take a bit of royal paste, dissolve it in eggs, add half a dozen almond biscuits, candied orange peel, and grated lime peel, all in powder form
2. Form the cakes to the size desired
3. Place on buttered Flanders paper
4. Put in the oven to bake with moderate heat
5. When baked, cover with white glaze, and when it is dry, serve as the others
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Bolos á Delfina (Delfina Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- a bit of royal dough
- eggs
- half a dozen almond biscuits
- orange flower
- grated lemon zest
- white icing

## Directions

1. Take a bit of royal dough
2. Dissolve it in eggs
3. Add to it half a dozen almond biscuits, orange flower and grated lemon zest, all in powder
4. Form the cakes in the size desired
5. Place them on a buttered Flanders sheet
6. Put them to cook in the oven with moderate heat
7. When baked, cover them with white icing
8. When this is dry, serve them like the others
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bribigões de Tigellada (Clams in Earthenware Pot)
body:
## Ingredients

- clams
- flour
- olive oil
- vinegar
- garlic
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash the clams very well from the sand
2. Place them in a pot to open
3. Once opened, wash them again in clean water
4. Dry them in a clean cloth
5. Flour them with some flour powder
6. Send them to the oven in an earthenware pot with olive oil, vinegar, garlic, and lemon juice
7. Once cooked, serve at table in the same pot
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bribigões de Tigellada (Cockles in a Bowl)
body:
## Ingredients

- cockles
- water
- flour
- olive oil
- vinegar
- garlic
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash the cockles very well from the sand, place them to open in a pot
2. When open, wash them again in clean water and dry them in a clean cloth
3. Coat them with flour, place them in the oven in a bowl with olive oil, vinegar, garlic, and lemon juice
4. After cooked, send them to the table in the same bowl
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bringellas de Tigellada (Eggplants in Earthenware Pot)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplants
- water
- salt
- flour
- frying pan
- melted butter
- vinegar
- honey
- clove

## Directions

1. Cook the eggplants in quarters and scald them inside
2. Stew them a little, cook them in water and salt, and after squeezing and flouring with flour
3. Place them in a frying pan (not one on top of another)
4. Pour melted butter on top to coat them all, and a sauce of vinegar tempered with honey, as much as is needed to make it sweet, and ground clove
5. Cook in the oven and serve at table with cinnamon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bringellas Recheadas (Stuffed Eggplants)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplants
- eggplant flesh
- herbs
- cow butter
- breadcrumbs
- eggs
- Alentejo cheese
- bread slices
- egg yolks
- broth
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Clean and cook the eggplants in halves
2. Remove the flesh from inside, chop it along with part of the eggplants and herbs sautéed in cow butter, breadcrumbs, eggs, and Alentejo cheese
3. Stuff the eggplants with this mixture
4. Place in a hollow plate or frying pan over already-moistened bread slices with a thick sauce made of two egg yolks dissolved in a little broth in which the herbs will be sautéed
5. Cook on gentle heat below and above, first pouring the sauce that remained in the sauté, and with cinnamon on top and grated cheese
6. Serve at table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Bringellas Recheadas de Outro Modo (Stuffed Eggplants Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplants
- onion
- butter
- olive oil
- oregano
- raw eggs
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- cheese
- sugar
- cinnamon
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Cook eggplants in halves and squeeze them
2. Remove all the flesh from inside some of them, and chop others
3. After chopping, mix them with a small amount of onion that must be already fried in butter and olive oil on the heat
4. Add oregano and raw eggs, and cook them on the same heat, stirring until they are very dry
5. Place them on a cutting board or stump, chop them with eggs, breadcrumbs, and cheese, and season with sugar, cinnamon, and all the seasonings
6. Stuff the eggplants with this mixture, bread them with eggs, fry them with cinnamon and sugar
7. Serve at table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cabeça de Porco (Pig's Head)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's head
- Bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Nutmeg
- Ginger
- Coriander seeds
- Bay leaves
- Marjoram
- Oregano
- Water
- Wine
- Onion
- Carrots
- Parsnips
- Lemon
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Rosemary

## Directions

1. Cut the pig's head at the shoulders, singe it, and clean it well
2. Open it from the bottom and remove the bones from the neck, jaws, and snout up to the eyes
3. Soak it to bleed and then dry it
4. Place it on a cloth, lard it with thick bacon, and season with enough salt, pepper, cloves, nutmeg, ginger, coriander seeds, bay leaves, marjoram, oregano, all crushed
5. Cover with sheets of bacon, wrap in a cloth, tie it very well and tightly, put it in a pan, add equal parts water and wine, rounds of onion, carrots, parsnips, rounds of lemon, parsley, bay, little thyme, and rosemary, whole salt and pepper, and let it boil slowly for five or six hours
6. When cooked, let it cool in its own broth to gain more flavor, then drain it, remove the cloth, and when well dried and trimmed, serve it on a napkin garnished with parsley as a cold dish and as an accompaniment
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Cabeça de Porco em Achar (Pickled Pork Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork head
- sage
- thyme
- marjoram
- cinnamon
- clove
- pepper
- ginger
- strong vinegar
- whole spices

## Directions

1. Cook a pork head very well with sage, thyme, marjoram, cinnamon, clove, pepper and ginger, all whole
2. After cooking, remove the bones while still hot and wrap it in thick cloth in the shape of cheese
3. Keep it in a very tight press for twenty-four hours
4. Cut into long slices and place them in very strong vinegar with whole spices
5. Keep in this pickle for as long as desired
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cabeça de Porco Montez Recheada (Stuffed Wild Boar Head)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild boar head
- Toucinho fino (fine bacon)
- Sal (salt)
- Pimenta (pepper)
- Adubos (seasonings)
- Hervas finas (fine herbs)
- Lombos de vacca (beef loins)
- Lombos de porco (pork loins)
- Línguas (tongues)
- Orelhas de porco manso (domestic pig ears)
- Pedaços de barriga (belly pieces)
- Toucinho (bacon)
- Presunto (ham)
- Salitre (saltpeter)
- Agua (water)
- Vinho branco (white wine)
- Cravos (cloves)
- Cebola (onion)
- Raizes (roots)
- Alhos (garlic)
- Tomilho (thyme)
- Louro (bay leaf)
- Manjerição (marjoram)
- Falsa (parsley)

## Directions

1. Singe the head, clean it, and cut it open from the bottom
2. Remove all bones except those of the snout tip and jaw points so that the teeth are visible after cooking
3. Soak in water to remove blood, then drain
4. Lard all the meat of the neck with thick bacon seasoned with salt, pepper, seasonings, and fine herbs
5. Cut beef loins, pork loins, tongues, domestic pig ears, belly pieces, bacon, and ham into fillets and season as the head with common salt, saltpeter, pepper, seasonings, and chopped fine herbs
6. Place the seasoned meat in brine for three or four days
7. Remove and place on cloth with ears facing down
8. Fill the interior with loins interspersed with all remaining ingredients, keeping it full and tight
9. Sew closed with needle and thread and wrap in strong cloth
10. Secure and tie well with cord
11. Cook in an adobo (broth) as described for domestic pig head for six hours in water, white wine, salt, whole pepper, cloves, onion rings, roots, garlic, thyme, bay leaf, and marjoram
12. After cooking and cooling, drain briefly, press in a press or under a great weight until well compressed
13. Trim and serve on a napkin garnished with parsley and ham slices
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Cabeça de Vitela (Veal Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal head
- string
- broth
- lard, diced
- whole pepper
- ginger
- vinegar
- ground pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the veal head at the neck joint
2. Peel it as one would peel a suckling pig
3. While it cooks, place it in cold water
4. Tie the snout tightly with string so that the tip of the tongue sticks out
5. Place it to cook in a new pot with lard diced, whole pepper, and some ginger
6. Once cooked, remove the lower jaw
7. When you wish to put it on the plate, open the upper jaw with a knife along the brains so that vinegar and ground pepper can enter inside
8. Also sprinkle vinegar and ground pepper on top of the whole head, putting vinegar first so the pepper sticks
9. Send to the table with fresh parsley on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cabeça de Vitela Recheada (Stuffed Veal Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal head
- ground meat
- pheasants or squabs with livers
- ham
- veal tongue
- string
- beef or mutton broth
- vinegar
- whole spices
- bay leaves
- butter or lard
- veal feet, larded

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the veal head, keeping it whole
2. Make a very fine ground meat mixture and spread it inside
3. Make a layer of pheasants or squabs with their livers and a piece of ham, placing it inside between the ground meat
4. Put the veal tongue in the mouth, pointing halfway out
5. Sew the corners of the mouth closed with string so it does not fall
6. Sew closed in the same manner along the neck side
7. Place the head in a pot where beef is cooking and partially cook it
8. Then place it in a pan with a bit of broth and vinegar, whole spices, and bay leaves
9. Coat the head with butter or lard on top and put it in the oven
10. Finish cooking in this sauce, turning it occasionally from top to bottom and bottom to top
11. Once the head is cooked, place it on a plate and garnish with a layer of liver or ham mixed with eggs and lemon water, surrounded by larded veal feet
12. Send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cabeça de Vitella ao Natural (Veal Head in its Natural State)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal head, skinned and blanched
- flour
- water
- salt
- whole pepper
- one bundle of herbs (fines herbes)
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- onion studded with cloves
- pieces of parsnip
- carrots
- some slices of lemon without peel
- one glass of white wine
- broth
- vinegar
- fines herbes, chopped finely

## Directions

1. After the head is skinned and well blanched, and singed over a well-lit hearth, remove the jaws and the bone of the nose up to the eyes, lifting the skin very carefully without damaging it
2. Soak it in water for a whole night
3. Blanch it in fresh water
4. After giving it a few boilings, remove it and put it in cold water
5. Scrape the inside of the jaws very well, wash them very well, tie it with twine
6. Put it to cook in a white broth made with a little flour dissolved in a pan of water, seasoned with salt, whole pepper, one bundle of herbs, bay leaf, marjoram, onion studded with cloves, pieces of parsnip, carrots, some slices of lemon without peel, and one glass of white wine
7. When cooked, remove it and let it drain
8. After removing the bones and uncovering the brains, place it in a good shape on the serving plate with its spicy sauce on top made with a little broth, vinegar, salt, pepper, and finely chopped fines herbes
9. It can also be served with different sauces, such as ravigota, peverada, or Italian style
10. Note that it can also be made without fines herbes or roots, in case they are not available
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Cabeça de Vitella Corada no Forno (Veal Head Browned in the Oven)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked veal head
- slices of bacon
- butter
- flour
- egg yolks
- broth
- vinegar
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Take the cooked veal head as described above, remove it and let it dry well
2. Place it on a torteira or platon with slices of bacon underneath and thick sauce made with a good piece of butter, flour, egg yolks, half a glass of broth, and a little vinegar
3. Put it on the fire, and when well bound, pour it over the head
4. Cover the head with breadcrumbs, put it in the oven or under the lid of a torteira with fire, and when browned, straighten it on its plate and serve with the sauces mentioned above or with whichever seems best
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Cabeça de Vitella Recheada (Stuffed Veal Head)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one well-blanched veal head, singed
- veal brains
- pieces of veal
- beef fat
- egg yolks
- veal tongue
- veal eyes
- twine
- broth
- one quart of white wine
- roots
- one bundle with fines herbes
- onion studded with cloves
- salt
- whole pepper

## Directions

1. Take a well-blanched and singed veal head, place it on a table with cloth underneath
2. Open it between the jaws, remove all the bones without damaging the skin of the upper part
3. Make a forcemeat with the brains, pieces of veal, and beef fat, well seasoned and bound with egg yolks, mixing in the tongue and eyes cut into dice
4. Fill the head with this mixture, giving it its same shape, tying it very well with twine on all sides so the forcemeat does not escape
5. Put it to cook in a pan with broth, one quart of white wine, some roots, one bundle with fines herbes, onion studded with cloves, salt, and whole pepper
6. When cooked, remove it and let it drain
7. When dry, remove the twine and serve with spicy sauce as above
8. One can also put inside and in the middle of the forcemeat a ragout of sweetbreads, truffles, ham, chicken breasts, or squab breasts, or any other that seems appropriate
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Cabrito Assado e Lardeado (Roasted and Larded Kid Goat)
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter of kid goat
- thin lard slices

## Directions

1. Begin roasting a quarter of kid goat on the spit
2. When almost roasted, lard it with thin slices of lard half a finger in length
3. Finish roasting and send to the table over the soup of choice
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cabrito de Tigelada (Kid Goat in Tiled Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- kid goat, cut into small pieces
- lard
- bread slices
- eggs

## Directions

1. Braise the kid goat in small pieces with lard
2. Once cooked and seasoned, place it in a frying pan over slices of bread
3. Pour six beaten eggs on top and brown it
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cabrito Dourado (Gilded Kid Goat)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted kid goat
- eggs
- butter
- cheese
- bread
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon

## Directions

1. Once the kid goat is roasted, cut it into pieces and pass them through beaten eggs and fry in butter
2. Place a cheese soup in a silver plate or low frying pan, consisting of a quarter of cheese in slices, bread of ten réis in slices, and a quarter of sugar
3. Butter the plate, place the bread slices as a bed, and on each bed of slices another of cheese slices with sugar and cinnamon on top
4. Finally pour over all of this the beaten egg that remained from covering the kid goat
5. Brown it and send to the table with the kid goat, cinnamon, and lemon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Calda de Ginjas ou de Cerejas (Sour Cherry or Cherry Syrup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cherries or cherries, ripe and sound
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the stems and pits from two pounds of ripe, sound cherries.
2. Put them in a quart of water.
3. Bring to a boil for two minutes.
4. Strain through a sieve.
5. When strained, pour into two pounds of sugar in the hair stage.
6. Let it all boil until the sugar takes on the necessary consistency, like the preceding one.
7. Guard it in the same manner.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Calda ou Xarope de Flor de Violas (Violet Flower Syrup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- violet flowers
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place four ounces of violet flowers in a bowl.
2. Pour a quart of boiling water over them.
3. Cover the bowl with its lid.
4. Let it infuse for two hours over gentle heat.
5. Strain through a cloth and squeeze well.
6. If it produces half a pint, pour it into two and a half pounds of sugar in the hard crack stage, which is known by dipping two fingers in cold water, then in the sugar, and dipping again in cold water—if the sugar sticks to the fingers and breaks between them.
7. Add the half pint of violet water, and incorporate it into the sugar without boiling.
8. Pour into a bowl.
9. Cover it.
10. Place it over gentle heat for three days.
11. At the end of this time, dip your fingers in it, wet them in the syrup, then open them and if they form a thread that does not break, pour it into bottles and keep for when needed.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Calda ou Xarope de Marmelos (Quince Syrup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe quinces
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and clean a dozen ripe quinces inside.
2. Chop and pound them very well.
3. Put them in a cloth and squeeze all the juice forcefully into a bowl.
4. Let it settle, then take the clearest part.
5. For each quart of juice, have one pound of sugar in the hair stage.
6. Adding this juice to the sugar, let it boil until it takes on the consistency of a syrup.
7. When cold, pour into bottles and guard as stated.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Calda para o Inverno de Toda a Qualidade de Frutas (Syrup for Winter for All Kinds of Fruits)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit (strawberries, currants, blackberries, or raspberries)
- two arratels of sugar per arratel of fruit

## Directions

1. For each arratel of fruit such as strawberries, currants, blackberries, or raspberries, reduce two arratels of sugar to the cracking point
2. When at this stage, add the fruit and give it one boil
3. Remove from heat and pour into a sieve
4. Strain the fruit through it and when the syrup is cold, pour into bottles
5. Cork and use this syrup to make drinks, chilled or otherwise
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Calda para o Inverno de Toda a Qualidade de Frutas (Winter Syrup for All Kinds of Fruit)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit (strawberries, currants, blackberries, or raspberries)
- sugar

## Directions

1. For each pound of fruit, reduce two pounds of sugar to the hard crack stage.
2. Add the fruit to the syrup.
3. Give it one boil.
4. Remove from heat.
5. Strain through a sieve.
6. Let the fruit drain and the syrup cool.
7. Pour the syrup into bottles.
8. Cork it.
9. From this syrup make cold or unfrozen drinking waters as desired.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo Amargoso (Bitter Broth for All Chest Diseases and Vomiting)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts of leaves of blessed thistle
- equal parts of centaury
- equal parts of chamomile
- equal parts of wormwood
- equal parts of veronica
- equal parts of scolopendra
- equal parts of germander
- equal parts of sour orange peel
- equal parts of gentian root
- one ounce weight
- one canada of water
- one arratel of veal
- one bruised chicken

## Directions

1. Take equal parts of leaves of blessed thistle, centaury, chamomile, wormwood, veronica, scolopendra, germander, sour orange peel, and gentian root, all dried in the shade and finely chopped
2. Mix with equality and guard in a small tin box
3. When needed, take the weight of one ounce and put it in a pot with one canada of water, one arratel of veal, and one bruised chicken
4. After it has boiled and the broth is reduced to half, strain through a napkin without pressing
5. Divide into two equal parts, of which take one in the morning on an empty stomach and another four hours after dinner
6. This can be used for fifteen or twenty days, purging before and after taking it
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo Bom para Nutrir (Good Broth for Nourishment)
body:
## Ingredients

- one plump hen
- one partridge, or two good ones
- one calf knuckle
- water
- one handful of pearl barley
- salt
- two or three egg yolks (optional)

## Directions

1. Blanch the partridges and calf knuckle in hot water first
2. Place all together in a pot nearly full of water up to the rim
3. Add one handful of pearl barley with appropriate salt stones
4. Put on the fire to simmer gently
5. When the birds are cooked, remove them with a skimmer and place on a plate
6. Strain the broth through a strainer, stirring always with a bread spoon so the substance and liquid falls into the broth
7. Pour into a bowl and serve at table
8. For greater substance, two or three egg yolks may be added
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Caldo Bom para Nutrir, e Muito Fresca (Good Broth for Nourishment, Very Fresh)
body:
## Ingredients

- one very fat chicken
- one partridge or two good ones
- one veal knuckle
- pearl barley
- salt

## Directions

1. The partridges and knuckle must first be blanched in hot water
2. Then together with the chicken put everything in a pan almost full of water to the rim
3. Add one handful of pearl barley with its appropriate stones of salt
4. Place it on the heat so that it goes boiling
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Caldo de Agriões (Sorrel Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of veal
- one canada of water
- a little salt
- three or four bundles of sorrel, well washed and chopped

## Directions

1. Put one arratel of veal to cook in a pot with one canada of water, seasoned with a little salt
2. After skimming, let it boil little by little
3. When the broth is reduced to half, add three or four bundles of well washed and chopped sorrel
4. Let them boil for a while
5. Strain through a silk sieve and serve hot in the morning and afternoon to whoever wishes to refresh themselves
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo de Cerefolio (Chervil Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of veal
- chervil, enough to extract half a quartilho of juice
- veal broth

## Directions

1. Cook a piece of veal as described in the preceding recipe and reduce the broth to half
2. Pound enough chervil to extract half a quartilho of juice
3. Mix the juice with the hot broth without letting it boil
4. Pass through a sieve and use as needed
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo de Chicória Brava (Wild Chicory Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of veal
- two stones of salt
- a handful of wild chicory, well washed

## Directions

1. Cook a piece of veal with two stones of salt in the manner described
2. Add a handful of wild chicory, well washed
3. Let it boil little by little
4. When the meat is cooked and the broth is reduced, pass through a sieve
5. Take it hot in the morning and afternoon
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo de Escorcioneira (Scorzonera Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of veal
- a dozen roots of scorzonera, well washed

## Directions

1. Make it the same way as chicory broth
2. Instead of chicory, take a dozen roots of scorzonera, well washed
3. Cook with the veal
4. When everything is cooked and the broth is reduced to half, pass through a sieve
