5. Take it hot in the morning and afternoon, using it for as long as you wish, and it is excellent for the chest
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo de Rãs e de Caracóis para Tosses Secas (Broth of Frogs and Snails for Dry Coughs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen snails
- two dozen frog thighs
- cold water
- one canada of water
- two heads of leeks only, cut very fine
- or two or three turnips cut the same way
- one handful of pearl barley
- seven or eight grains of saffron in powder

## Directions

1. Let simmer for an instant one dozen snails and two dozen frog thighs
2. After simmering, place in cold water and then grind in a stone mortar
3. Ground, place to simmer one canada of water with two heads of leeks only, cut very fine, or when not available, two or three turnips cut the same way, and one handful of pearl barley
4. After simmering and when the broth is reduced, strain through a cloth but do not press
5. Before taking it add seven or eight grains of saffron in powder
6. Take it in the morning on an empty stomach and three hours after supper, which can be continued over the course of one month or six weeks, purging oneself during this time if necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo de Vívoras para Purificar o Sangue (Broth of Vipers to Purify the Blood)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one handful each of chicory, pimpinella, chervil, and lettuce
- one chicken
- one viper, skinned alive

## Directions

1. Take one handful each of chicory, pimpinella, chervil, and lettuce, wash them and cut them fine
2. Add to them one chicken and one viper, skinned alive, to which you will remove the head, tail, and entrails, reserving the livers and the heart
3. Place everything to simmer in one canada of water
4. After simmering and when the water is reduced to half, strain through a cloth
5. Divide into two parts and take one every day on an empty stomach
6. This broth can be used for fifteen or twenty days, purging first and at the end of this time
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo em Vestidinhos com Conserva (Portable Meat Broth with Preserves)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one fresh leg of beef
- two legs of veal by weight
- eight measures of mutton
- four old turkeys
- twelve hens
- six livers and capons
- two dozen partridges
- four or five measures of clean ham without bones and fat
- two dozen cloves of the Indies
- two or three ounces of whole pepper and ginger
- seven or eight measures of shredded venison
- a bundle of carrots
- bundles of parsnips
- turnips
- leeks
- celery
- whole onions
- eggs beaten
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Clean and empty all the birds, remove feathers, soak them well along with the meat from the legs of beef and veal made in large pieces free of fat and bones
2. Wash and scald everything very well
3. Put in a large pot or kettle full of water and bring to boil slowly
4. Season with salt
5. Add two dozen cloves of the Indies, two or three ounces of whole pepper and ginger, all tied in a cloth
6. After two hours of boiling, add a decoction of seven or eight measures of shredded venison, a bundle of carrots, bundles of parsnips, turnips, leeks, celery, and whole onions, all blanched in boiling water and tied with twine
7. When all is cooked, pass the broth through a cloth with pressure
8. When almost cold and without fat, pour into a basin and place over a furnace
9. Clean it with beaten eggs and when it comes to a boil, remove from heat
10. Pass again through a cloth
11. When clear, transfer to a large pot and bring to boil slowly again, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon until the broth is reduced
12. When thick and almost at the consistency of a glaze, pour onto earthenware plates or pans, but no more than a finger's thickness of fat
13. Let cool, then cut into equal pieces each weighing about an ounce
14. Place on trays or in sieves with paper leaves underneath and set to dry in a stove or expose to north wind for several days
15. When completely dry, wrap each piece in paper and store in wide-mouthed jars very well sealed
16. To use, dissolve one or one and a half ounces in a quarter measure of boiling water depending on desired strength
17. In case of illness, give the sick one broth of these every four hours
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo Fresco e Purgativo (Fresh and Purgative Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of veal meat from the leg
- one chicken
- one canada of water
- borrage
- lettuce
- ox tongue
- wild chicory
- chervil
- pimpinella

## Directions

1. Put one arratel of veal meat from the leg and one chicken in a pan
2. Add one canada of water and place over heat
3. Skim and let simmer slowly
4. When the meat is almost cooked, add the following herbs: borrage, lettuce, ox tongue, wild chicory, chervil, and pimpinella
5. After simmering and when the meat is fully cooked, strain and press everything through a cloth
6. Remove the fat
7. Use this broth over the course of fifteen days or three weeks, or as long as seems fit, drinking it in the morning and in the afternoon
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo para Convalescentes (Broth for Convalescents)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one breast of capon
- one breast of chicken or partridge, roasted
- a piece of bread crumb soaked in broth
- one hard egg yolk
- veal broth
- chicken broth

## Directions

1. Pound in a stone mortar one breast of capon and one breast of roasted chicken or partridge
2. Add a piece of bread crumb soaked in broth and one hard egg yolk
3. After everything is pounded, put it in an earthenware pot and dissolve with warm veal and chicken broth
4. Pass through a sieve and heat, stirring always with a spoon
5. Before it boils, use it as desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo para Defluxus Catarraes (Broth for Catarrhal Defluxions)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen turnips
- one and a half canada of water
- four ounces or six ounces of sugar in thread, clarified

## Directions

1. Peel half a dozen turnips and cut into slices
2. Simmer in a pan with one and a half canada of water
3. When cooked and the broth reduced to half
4. Pour everything into a cloth and press very well
5. Then add four ounces or six ounces of sugar in thread, clarified
6. Take it hot before serving
7. Can also be taken in the morning and for as long as needed
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo para Dias de Peixe (Broth for Fish Days)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- carrots
- parsnips
- butter
- clear pea broth
- fish heads, tails and other fragments
- salt
- parsley made into a sauce
- chives
- fine herbs
- some cloves of the Indies

## Directions

1. Line the bottom and sides of a pot with sliced onions, carrots, and parsnips
2. Add a piece of butter and put on heat to sweat slowly
3. When it begins to brown, add a little clear pea broth
4. When it comes to a boil, add fish heads, tails and other fragments judged most convenient
5. Season with salt
6. Add parsley made into a sauce, chives, fine herbs, and some cloves of the Indies
7. Let boil for about one hour
8. Pass this broth through a sieve and use it to moisten various soups
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo para Doentes e para Sãos (Broth for the Sick and the Healthy)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two measures of veal meat
- one hen
- water
- salt
- one onion
- two cloves

## Directions

1. Put two measures of veal meat and one hen in an earthenware pot
2. Add one and a half measures of water
3. Skim when appropriate
4. Season with salt
5. Add one onion with two cloves
6. Let boil slowly
7. When the meat and hen are cooked and the broth is reduced to half, pass through a sieve
8. Remove the fat and use as needed
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo para Doenças de Peito (Broth for Chest Diseases)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef lung, cleaned and washed
- half a canada of water
- apples of Analfe by the ounce
- sebasts by the ounce
- tamarinds by the ounce
- dried figs by the ounce
- Alicante raisins by the ounce
- half an ounce of the five capillaries
- one dram of rock sugar in each dose

## Directions

1. Clean and wash well in much water one beef lung
2. Place to cook in half a canada of water with apples of Analfe, sebasts, tamarinds, dried figs, Alicante raisins, each thing one ounce, adding also another half ounce of the five capillaries
3. Let everything cook very well and reduce the broth to two-thirds
4. Strain and press through a cloth
5. Divide afterwards into three or four doses
6. Dissolve in each one one dram of rock sugar
7. Take it in the morning and in the afternoon for as long as necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo para Dores de Cabeça (Broth for Headaches)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a canada of veal polp cut into pieces
- leaves of betony
- leaves of lemon balm
- eyes of elder
- a small bundle of wild chicory
- the feet or tails of ten or twelve crawfish of fresh water, bruised

## Directions

1. Boil in half a canada with the veal polp cut into pieces
2. Add leaves of betony, lemon balm, and eyes of elder, a good handful of each
3. Add a small bundle of wild chicory and the feet or tails of ten or twelve crawfish of fresh water, bruised
4. After everything has boiled well and the water is reduced to half, pass through a napkin
5. Take until you are free from pain
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo para Inflamações de Peito (Broth for Chest Inflammations)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one stuffed chicken
- four cold seeds, ground

## Directions

1. Bring to simmer in one canada of water one chicken stuffed with the four cold seeds, ground
2. After skimming and when the broth is reduced to two-thirds
3. Strain through a cloth and press
4. Use it every day for as long as seems fit
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo para Obstruções do Mesentério, do Fígado e do Baço (Broth for Obstructions of the Mesentery, Liver, and Spleen)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one canada of water
- one arratel of veal meat from the thigh
- roots of wild chicories
- chervil leaves
- escalopcndra leaves
- pimpinella leaves

## Directions

1. Place to simmer in one canada of water one arratel of veal meat from the thigh, and when simmering, add roots of wild chicories, leaves of chervil, escalopcndra, and pimpinella
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo para Obstruções do Mesentério, do Fígado, e do Baço (Broth for Obstructions of the Mesentery, Liver, and Spleen)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of veal knuckle meat
- one canada of water
- roots of wild chicory
- leaves of chervil
- leaves of scolopendra
- leaves of pimpinella
- leaves of agrimony
- leaves of sorrel
- one ounce of iron filings well washed in hot water
- one dram of wormwood salt
- another dram of bruised rhubarb

## Directions

1. Bring one canada of water to boil with one arratel of veal knuckle meat
2. When boiling, add roots of wild chicory, leaves of chervil, scolopendra, pimpinella, agrimony, and sorrel, a little of each thing, all well washed and finely chopped
3. Add one ounce of iron filings well washed in hot water and tied in a cloth
4. Add one dram of wormwood salt and another dram of bruised rhubarb
5. After everything has boiled and the broth is reduced to half, remove the cloth with the filings
6. Strain through a napkin and press gently
7. Divide the broth into two doses, taking one in the morning on an empty stomach and the other four hours after dinner
8. Purge at the beginning, middle, and end of the time that you continue to take it
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo para Purificar do Sangue (Broth to Purify the Blood)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of veal meat from the loin
- three quartilhos of water
- salt
- seven or eight live freshwater crayfish

## Directions

1. After skimming and when almost cooked one arratel of veal meat from the loin in three quartilhos of water, seasoned with salt
2. Crush seven or eight live freshwater crayfish and add them to simmer for half a quarter hour with the veal
3. Strain the broth through a sieve
4. Use it as with the previous broth
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caldo para Vapores e Flatos que Sobem à Cabeça (Broth for Vapors and Flatus that Rise to the Head)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one handful each of mallow leaves, betony, mercury plant, artemisia, marsh mallow, and sorrel
- six grains of castor in powder

## Directions

1. Take leaves of mallows, betony, mercury plant, artemisia, marsh mallow, and sorrel, one handful of each, wash all very well and clean them
2. Cut them fine and place to simmer in one canada of water
3. After simmering and when the water is reduced to half, strain through a sieve
4. Divide into two parts and take one in the morning on an empty stomach and another at night
5. In the portion taken in the morning, add six grains of castor in powder
6. Use this broth for the course of fifteen days, purging at the beginning of them, in the middle, and at the end
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Camarões de salada (Shrimp Salad)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Shrimp tails
- Salt
- Pepper
- Olive oil
- Vinegar
- Chives in slices
- Parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Cook and clean the shrimp tails
2. Season with salt, pepper, olive oil, vinegar, sliced chives, and a little chopped parsley
3. Place in the plate with its sauce
4. Arrange neatly and serve garnished with parsley in the middle
5. They may also be served with remoulade sauce and a bit of mustard if desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Camarões guizados (Stewed Shrimp)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Shrimp
- Butter
- Mushrooms
- Truffles, sliced
- Parsley bouquet
- Fine herbs
- Fish stock
- Cullis of shrimp

## Directions

1. Rinse the shrimp thoroughly in two waters and clean them well
2. Pass them through the fire with butter, mushrooms, and sliced truffles
3. Add a drop of fish stock
4. Allow to simmer little by little
5. Once the sauce is reduced and of good taste, remove the butter
6. Bind with a cullis of shrimp
7. Serve hot as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Camarões guizados para dia de carne (Stewed Shrimp for Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Shrimp
- Rendered bacon
- Truffles
- Mushrooms
- Veal substance
- Cullis of shrimp
- Veal substance
- Ham

## Directions

1. Clean the shrimp as previously described
2. Pass the tails through the fire in rendered bacon with truffles and mushrooms
3. Add a little veal substance
4. Allow to simmer little by little
5. Once the sauce is reduced, remove the fat
6. Bind with a cullis of shrimp made with veal substance and ham
7. Keep over gentle heat without boiling
8. Serve well hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Camarões à Bexamêla (Shrimp with Béchamel Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Shrimp tails
- White wine
- Butter
- Fine herbs
- Béchamel sauce
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and blanch the shrimp tails
2. Cook them in an adobo made of white wine, butter, and fine herbs
3. Once cooked, transfer them to a béchamel sauce made in the customary manner and of good taste
4. Place over heat to warm
5. Bind with two or three beaten egg yolks and lemon juice
6. Serve as previously described
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Capon or Chicken Roasted (Capão, e Franga assados)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- capon or chicken
- bacon
- bacon lard
- paper with butter

## Directions

1. After the capon or chicken is plucked and aged, singe it in a well-lit furnace without smoke
2. Clean it with care, beat the breast, straighten it, and arrange it in good form
3. Lard it, rub it with bacon, clean it, singe the bristles, tie it by the thighs with twine
4. Cover the breasts with bacon strips, put it on the spit, and roast it covered with sheets of buttered paper
5. When roasted as is customary, remove the paper, and when the bacon is browned, serve it hot as a roasted dish
6. When not serving it this way, remove the bacon, cover it with breadcrumbs, brown it, and serve it hot surrounded by watercress seasoned with salt and vinegar, or if not excessively fat, lard it with fine bacon and serve it with salt, pepper, and juice of verjuice or lemon
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Capon or Chicken with Brains (Capão, ou Franga com Enxovas)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- capon or chicken
- scraped bacon
- two brains
- parsley
- chives
- salt
- pepper
- melted bacon
- reed skewers
- paper
- bacon broth
- orange juice or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the capon or chicken as above, carefully peel back the skin from the top of the breast, and stuff it with a mixture made from scraped bacon, two brains, parsley, chives, little salt, and pepper
2. Pass the skin flap through the wing tips, straighten it, tie it with twine, lard it in melted bacon, pass some reed skewers through it, tie them to the spit, cover with paper sheets, and roast it
3. When roasted, serve it with its broth, chopped brains, orange juice or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Capons or Chickens with Truffles (Çapões, ou Frangas com trufas)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- capon or chicken
- truffles
- ragout of truffles

## Directions

1. Stuff between the skin and the breast with truffles and roast as the preceding recipes
2. Serve with truffle ragout
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Caprotada de Pato (Duck Caprotada)
body:
## Ingredients

- one roasted duck, made into pieces
- two spoonfuls of fat broth from the pot
- quarter wheel of Além-Tejo cheese, grated
- parsley, chopped
- one more spoonful of broth

## Directions

1. After the duck is roasted and cut into pieces, place a plate or frying pan on bread slices
2. Pour two spoonfuls of fat broth from the pot on top
3. Cover with a quarter of grated Além-Tejo cheese mixed with chopped parsley
4. Moisten with another spoonful of broth on top
5. Place to bubble over hot coals
6. When dried, send to table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Capões e Frangas com Presunto (Capons and Chickens with Ham)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Capons or chickens
- Chicken livers
- Bacon
- Fine herbs
- String
- Pork butter or melted bacon fat
- Ham pieces
- Bacon sheets
- Paper
- Ham slices
- Spicy sauce
- Cullis
- White wine
- Demi-glace
- Fine herbs
- Garlic cloves
- Studded onion
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Stuff inside with a mixture made from their own livers, bacon, and fine herbs, straighten and tie with string
2. Sear in pork butter or melted bacon fat, and after dried and cooled, lard the entire upper part of the breast with small pieces of ham so that only the tips appear like sea urchins
3. Cover with bacon sheets and paper, and after roasted and placed on its plate, serve garnished with ham slices and spicy sauce made with a little cullis, white wine, a demi-glace, fine herbs, garlic cloves, studded onion, passed through a sieve, and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Capões e Frangas Cornichons para uma Entrada (Capons and Chickens with Gherkins for an Entry)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Capons or chickens
- Three or four fresh walnuts
- Scraped bacon
- Salt
- Scallion
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Melted bacon fat
- Bacon sheets
- Ham essence
- Two dozen blanched walnuts
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take three or four fresh walnuts, peel and well crush them
2. Mix with scraped bacon, salt, scallion, salt, pepper, and few fine herbs
3. Stuff the chickens; close and secure in melted bacon fat
4. Cover with bacon sheets
5. Serve with ham essence sauce heated at the moment of serving, or within two dozen blanched walnuts cooked in water with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Capões ou Frangas com Alcaparras (Capons or Chickens with Capers)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Capons or chickens
- Capers
- Ham essence
- Butter
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. After washing the capers, place them in a ham essence
2. Bind with butter and lemon juice
3. Serve with the prepared capons or chickens
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Capões ou Frangas com Mofserões (Capons or Chickens with Mushrooms)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Capons or chickens
- Chopped liver and bacon
- Mushrooms
- Bacon sheets
- Paper
- Mushroom or morello ragú
- Bacon lard
- Cucumber ragú
- Artichoke hearts ragú
- Whole scallions ragú

## Directions

1. Mix chopped liver, scraped bacon, and chopped seasoned mushrooms
2. Stuff the capons and chickens
3. Place to roast covered with bacon sheets and paper
4. Once roasted, remove the sheets and paper
5. Serve with mushroom ragú or morello ragú, or after larding with thick bacon and cooking in a braise, serve with cucumber ragú, artichoke heart ragú, whole scallions ragú, or any other ragú desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Capões ou Frangas com Trufas (Capons or Chickens with Truffles)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Capons or chickens
- Truffles
- Ham

## Directions

1. Stuff the truffles between the skin and breast
2. Prepare as in preceding recipes
3. Serve with truffle ragú
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Capões ou Frangas em Canelões (Capons or Chickens in Cannelloni)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Capons or chickens
- Ground mixture
- Bacon sheets
- White cloth or linen
- String
- Ham essence
- Pistachios
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the capons or chickens, cut in half, debone each piece entirely, beat between two cloths with the back of the knife
2. Cover with a well-bound ground mixture the thickness of a new crown coin, cover each one with its bacon sheet and its piece of white cloth or linen, tie very well around with string
3. Place to cook in a well-nourished braise
4. Once cooked, remove the cloth and bacon, and serve with ham essence sauce with two dozen pistachios and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Capões ou Frangas à Villeroy (Capons or Chickens Villeroy)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Capons or chickens
- Bacon sheets
- Paper
- Fine herbs
- Butter
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Open, clean, and prepare the capons or chickens, place on the spit covered with bacon sheets and paper, and roast
2. Once roasted and paper removed, placed on its plate and the breast chiseled as is customary to do to ducks, serve with hot remoulade sauce made with all kinds of fine herbs, bound with a piece of fresh butter and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Cardos com Queijo Parmezão (Cardoons with Parmesan Cheese)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Cardoons
- White broth
- Ham essence or béchamel sauce
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Cut the cardoons to desired size
2. Blanch and cook in white broth
3. When cooked, place in sauce of essence or béchamel sauce
4. Arrange on a plate with a little butter and grated cheese underneath
5. Pour the rest of the sauce on top
6. Cover with grated cheese, place to brown in the oven
7. After browning, clean the plate edges and serve for a course between meals
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Cardos Cozidos (Cooked Thistles)
body:
## Ingredients

- thistles
- onion
- butter
- milk
- almonds
- egg yolks
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the thistles
2. Cook them in water
3. Once cooked, sauté a small amount of chopped onion with herbs in a quarter of cow butter
4. When sautéed, add the cooked thistles inside
5. Season with salt, half a measure of milk made from half an arroba of almonds, and all the seasonings
6. Bind with six beaten egg yolks
7. Serve at table on slices with lemon juice on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cardos de Fernão de Sousa (Thistles of Fernão de Sousa)
body:
## Ingredients

- thistles
- herbs
- butter
- egg yolks
- eggs

## Directions

1. After cooking the thistles in water, chop them very well
2. Sauté them with herbs and butter, seasoning with all the seasonings
3. Bind them with egg yolks (like minced mutton)
4. Put them in a frying pan, and with beaten eggs on top, place them to brown
5. Serve at table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cardos à Halinna (Thistles à Halinna)
body:
## Ingredients

- thistles
- water
- salt
- onion
- butter
- dark seasonings
- orange juice

## Directions

1. Place thistles to cook in water and salt after cleaning them very well and cutting them to the size of a finger, about half a dozen
2. Once cooked, sauté a small amount of chopped onion in half an arroba of butter
3. When sautéed, add the thistles inside as they finish cooking
4. Season with dark seasonings
5. Serve at table with plenty of orange juice on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Caril para Qualquer Peixe (Curry for Any Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two onions
- Quarter measure of beef butter
- Some shrimp or clams
- Milk
- Quarter measure of almonds
- Half arratel of rice
- Water
- Salt
- Fish portions
- Seasonings

## Directions

1. Stew two well-chopped onions in a quarter measure of beef butter
2. Add some shrimp or clams with milk, a quarter measure of almonds, and cook everything until it becomes somewhat thick
3. Season with seasonings
4. Cook half arratel of rice in water and salt, place it on a plate, and on top place some portions of fish that have been cooked in water
5. Pour the curry over the top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Caril para Qualquer Peixe (Curry for Any Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- two onions, finely chopped
- one quarter of beef butter
- a few shrimp or clams
- milk
- one quarter of almonds
- seasonings
- half an arratel of rice
- water
- salt
- fish steaks

## Directions

1. Sauté the two finely chopped onions in a quarter of beef butter
2. Add a few shrimp or clams with milk and one quarter of almonds
3. Cook everything until it becomes somewhat thick
4. Season with seasonings
5. Cook half an arratel of rice in water and salt
6. Place the rice on the plate
7. Put some fish steaks cooked in water and salt on top
8. Pour the curry over the fish
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carne de Vaca ou de Carneiro Assado na Água (Roasted Beef or Mutton in Water)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton or beef
- bread
- bacon strips
- salt
- pepper
- water
- white wine or red wine
- black pepper
- cloves
- lemon juice
- bread slices or toasted bread

## Directions

1. Take a leg of mutton or the quantity desired and pound it with bread from one side and the other so it becomes very white
2. Make lardons, which are strips of bacon, adding salt and pepper to the bacon, and using a larding needle, insert the bacon into the leg of mutton
3. Take a pot with a little water that covers the leg, and let it cook until more than half the water is spent
4. Add a little chopped bacon and let the water continue to be spent until it is all gone
5. Once the water is gone, the meat will fry in the substance left, turning it from one side and the other until it is well browned
6. Then add half a canada of white wine (if you want it clear, and if thickened, red wine) and let it reduce very well over low heat with its pepper and black spices, which are pepper and cloves
7. Place on bread slices or toasted bread, and pour the sauce with the meat over it, its lemon juice, and lemon slices on top, and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro com Arroz (Mutton with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of mutton
- half arratel of bacon
- two heads of garlic
- cinnamon
- pepper
- clove
- ginger
- wine
- vinegar
- herbs
- saffron
- rice

## Directions

1. Place two arratels of mutton to braise with half arratel of bacon, two heads of garlic, cinnamon, pepper, clove, ginger, all whole, a splash of wine, a splash of vinegar, and herbs
2. When more than half cooked, season with all spices and plenty of saffron
3. Place in a frying pan, add the rice
4. Cover with a lid and place on coals
5. Cook slowly until dry
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro com Couve (Mutton with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of mutton, cut in pieces
- half arratel of bacon
- herbs
- cabbage
- two heads of garlic
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place two arratels of mutton, cut in pieces, to braise with half arratel of bacon and herbs
2. When boiling, add the cabbage and two heads of garlic with some pepper
3. When cooked, season with all spices and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro de Alfitete (Lamb with Batter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two arratels of lamb from the leg
- Flour
- Eggs
- White wine
- One quarter of melted bacon fat or lard
- One quarter of sugar

## Directions

1. When two arratels of lamb from the leg are almost roasted, make a batter of flour, eggs, white wine, one quarter of melted bacon fat (or lard) and one quarter of sugar, so that the batter becomes sweet.
2. After making this batter, spread it with a spoon over the lamb as it roasts on a brisk flame, until it sets and the batter is done.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro de Caldo Amarelo (Mutton with Yellow Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of mutton, cut in pieces
- one quart of bacon
- herbs
- vinegar
- all spices
- four egg yolks
- bread slices
- lemon juice
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Place two arratels of mutton, cut in pieces, to braise with one quart of bacon and herbs
2. When half cooked, add a splash of vinegar
3. When cooked, season with all spices
4. Thicken with four egg yolks
5. Place on plate over bread slices with lemon juice and cinnamon on top and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro de Gigote (Lamb Gigot)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of lamb
- wine
- vinegar
- pepper
- clove
- ginger
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Roast two arratels of lamb with a frying pan underneath to catch the drippings
2. Pour a splash of wine into the pan with vinegar, pepper, clove, ginger, and nutmeg
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro de Gigote (Mutton Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of mutton
- one frying pan
- wine
- vinegar
- pepper
- clove
- ginger
- nutmeg
- half arratel of bacon
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Place two arratels of mutton to roast with a frying pan underneath to catch the drippings
2. Add to the drippings a splash of wine, splash of vinegar, pepper, clove, ginger, and nutmeg
3. When the mutton is roasted, squeeze it over the drippings so all substance falls in
4. Chop the mutton with a knife
5. Melt half arratel of bacon and remove the cracklings
6. Mix with the mutton drippings
7. Put everything together to boil with the mutton and season again as necessary
8. Place on plate and send to table with lemon juice on top
9. Optional: poach some eggs to serve with it
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro Ensopado (Braised Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of lamb, halved
- half arratel of bacon
- aromatics
- seasonings
- half arratel of grated cheese
- bread slices
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Braise two arratels of lamb halved with half arratel of bacon and aromatics
2. Season with all seasonings
3. When nearly cooked, strain the broth into a hollow plate or frying pan
4. Place over slices of bread covered with half arratel of grated cheese
5. Toast it over some coals
6. Place the lamb on top with lemon juice and take to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro Estofado (Stewed Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of mutton from the shoulder near the leg
- half arratel of bacon
- two heads of garlic
- one quince, quartered
- two apples
- cinnamon
- pepper
- ginger
- clove
- nutmeg
- two bay leaves
- wine
- vinegar
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Take two arratels of mutton from the shoulder near the leg, cut in pieces with half arratel of bacon, two heads of garlic, one quince quartered, two apples, cinnamon, pepper, ginger, clove, nutmeg all whole, two bay leaves, a splash of wine, a splash of vinegar, a little water, and necessary salt
2. Put everything in a pot and seal well on the outside so the steam does not escape
3. Place on gentle heat for three hours
4. When done, serve hot at table on silver plates or frying pans with lemon juice on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro Mourisco (Moorish Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of finely chopped bacon
- aromatics
- two arratels of half-roasted lamb cut into small pieces
- seasonings
- four egg yolks
- butter
- cinnamon
- lemon

## Directions

1. Braise a quarter of finely chopped bacon with aromatics
2. When braised, add two arratels of half-roasted lamb cut into small pieces
3. Cook very well
4. When cooked, season with all seasonings
5. Bind with four egg yolks
6. Place on a plate greased with butter
7. Top with cinnamon and lemon
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro na Pimenta (Lamb in Pepper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two arratels of lamb from the leg
- a quarter of bacon
- three egg yolks
- aromatics
- vinegar
- spices
- butter from a cow

## Directions

1. Finely chop one or two arratels of cleaned lamb from the leg with a quarter of bacon, three egg yolks, and aromatics
2. Braise in a bowl
3. When nearly braised, add the necessary vinegar
4. When well cooked with little broth, season with ground spices
5. Bind with three egg yolks
6. Place on a plate greased with butter
7. Top with lemon juice and cinnamon
8. If the dish is to have egg yolks, poach them separately in a pan and place on top of the chopped meat
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro para Capela (Mutton for Capela)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of mutton from the leg, finely chopped
- half arratel of bacon, chopped with herbs
- one half quart of butter
- all spices
- two eggs
- one ten-réis bread, grated
- caldo from the pot
- four eggs for thickening
- bread slices
- cinnamon
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Finely chop two arratels of mutton from the leg
2. Separately chop half arratel of bacon with herbs and fry in one half quart of butter
3. When fried, mix with the chopped mutton and chop all together well
4. Season with all spices, add two eggs and grated bread
5. Make the capela in a frying pan with one spoonful of broth from the pot
6. Place on gentle heat underneath and on top
7. When ready to serve, thicken with four eggs, place bread slices on top, cinnamon and lemon juice on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro para Qualquer Recheado (Lamb for Any Stuffing)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of bacon with onion, finely chopped
- two arratels of lamb
- three eggs
- toasted bread
- lemon
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Finely chop a quarter of bacon with onion
2. Finely chop two arratels of lamb separately
3. Brown the bacon with half of the lamb
4. When half browned, return to the cutting board
5. Mix with the other half of lamb
6. Season with three eggs, toasted bread, lemon, and all seasonings so it becomes well soured
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro Preto (Black Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of mutton, cut very small
- half arratel of bacon
- vinegar
- all spices
- one quart of almonds
- sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut two arratels of mutton into very small pieces
2. Braise with half arratel of bacon and its vinegar
3. When cooked, season with all spices
4. Pound one quart of almonds and toast until brown
5. Add to the mutton to make it black
6. Add sugar and lemon juice to the sauce so it becomes sour and sweet
7. Place on plate over bread slices and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Carneiro Verde (Green Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of mutton, cut in pieces
- one quart of bacon
- vinegar
- herbs
- all spices
- parsley sauce with cilantro
- butter
- bread slices
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut two arratels of mutton into pieces
2. Braise with one quart of bacon, a splash of vinegar, and herbs
3. When cooked, season with all spices
4. Make a small parsley sauce with cilantro, minced
5. Spread a buttered plate with bread slices
6. Put the mutton on top with lemon juice
7. Pour the sauce on the mutton so it becomes green when sending to table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cavacas (Cavacas Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- half alqueire of sifted flour
- one arratel of butter, melted and desalted
- salt dissolved in flower water
- one dozen eggs

## Directions

1. Sift half alqueire of flour and place in a bowl
2. Pour around the flour one arratel of butter that is melted on the fire to remove the salt, and work the flour in the hands
3. When well worked, make a well where you will pour salt dissolved in flower water and one dozen eggs
4. Beat with the hand and gradually work in the flour, adding eggs little by little, and kneading
5. The dough should not be stiff but soft and dry
6. Cut the dough into pieces the size that the cavacas are to be made
7. Place on a cloth and when stretching do not knead, but stretch lightly with light fingers on basins
8. Well pricked, send to the oven
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cenouras de Pultagem (Carrots Stewed)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- cow butter
- olive oil
- onion
- salt
- vinegar or lemon
- seasonings
- honey or sugar
- flour fried and well toasted
- broth

## Directions

1. After cooking the carrots, cut them in quarters and sauté them in cow butter with olive oil and onion
2. Once sautéed, place them to cook in a pot in hot water
3. Season with salt, vinegar or lemon, all the seasonings, honey or sugar, well toasted fried flour, and a small amount of broth in which they will cook
4. Once cooked, serve at table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cenouras de Tigellada (Carrots in Earthenware Pot)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- flour powder
- butter
- parsley
- cilantro
- parsley
- onion
- oregano
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the carrots, then cut them in quarters and pass them through flour powder
2. Place them to fry, arrange them in a frying pan and pour a potage on top made by sautéing in cow butter a small amount of parsley, cilantro, parsley, onion, and oregano, seasoning with vinegar
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Chernes, Robalos, Doiradas, and Salmaes à Chambord (Various Fish Chambord Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish, cleaned and prepared
- Bacon, fine
- Minced fish filling à créma
- Truffles
- Morels
- Bacon, sliced
- Onion, sliced
- White wine
- Salt
- pepper
- Cloves
- Bay leaf
- Marjoram
- Butter
- Paper leaves
- Essence sauce
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare any of these fish and remove the skin from one side only
2. Lard with fine bacon from head to tail
3. Stuff afterwards with a minced filling à créma, put in the middle a sprinkling of truffles and morels
4. Line the bottom of a long baking dish with bacon slices and onion slices
5. Place the fish on top with white wine, season with salt, pepper, cloves, bay leaf, marjoram, and a piece of butter
6. Cover with bacon slices and paper leaves and put to cook in the oven
7. When cooked, remove the slices, and after browning, serve with lightly bound essence sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Chickens with Olives for an Entrée (Frangas com azeitonas para huma entrada)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens or capons
- bacon strips
- buttered paper
- melted bacon
- veal sweetbreads
- fat livers cut in dice
- flour
- substance broth
- white wine
- olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- parsley and fine herbs
- two or three dozen pitted olives
- water
- breadcrumbs
- orange juice or lemon juice

## Directions

1. After preparing the chickens or capons as mentioned, cover them with bacon strips and buttered paper, and roast them
2. Pass them through melted bacon in a pan with veal sweetbreads, fat livers cut in dice, dusted with a little flour, moisten with a spoonful of broth, a cup of white wine, a small spoon of olive oil, salt, pepper, and a small amount of parsley and fine herbs, and let it boil slowly
3. When cooked and of good taste, remove the fat and all the oil, add two or three dozen pitted olives boiled in a little water
4. When roasted, remove the paper and bacon, cover with fine breadcrumbs, brown it well, remove the twine, give it some strikes with the flat of the knife on the breast and at the joints of the thighs, press it between two plates, and place it for a moment with its juice in the sauce, serve it hot with orange juice or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Chicória à Portuguesa (Portuguese-Style Chicory)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Oil
- Garlic cloves, chopped
- Hot water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Chicory, chopped, cooked and squeezed
- Fried eggs

## Directions

1. Heat oil with chopped garlic cloves
2. Add a drop of hot water, a drop of vinegar, salt, and pepper
3. Bind with flour
4. Add the chopped, cooked, and squeezed chicory inside
5. Let simmer slowly
6. With good taste, serve with fried eggs for a course between meals
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Chocolate (Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- five arrátels of cocoa
- three arrátels of stone sugar
- three ounces of fine cinnamon penned
- eight vanilla pods pounded and penned

## Directions

1. Toast five arrátels of cocoa
2. After toasted clean it and remove the husk
3. Pound very well
4. Mix with three arrátels of stone sugar and three ounces of fine cinnamon penned
5. As soon as all this is very well mixed, move it on a stone like one grinds paints
6. Grind second and third time
7. When it is in paste form add eight vanilla pods pounded and penned
8. Make the cakes in the form desired
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Chocos Guisados (Braised Cuttlefish)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- cuttlefish
- water
- flour
- butter
- onion
- two anchovies
- red wine
- meat substance
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and trim the cuttlefish, cut into equal fillets
2. Blanch in boiling water
3. Brown flour in melted butter
4. Add finely chopped onion and two finely chopped anchovies, breaking them down with a spoon
5. Pour in one cup of red wine and one spoon of meat substance
6. Season with salt, pepper, and a bundle of fine herbs
7. Add the fillets and let them cook very well
8. When the cuttlefish is cooked and the sauce is reduced and of good taste, remove the herb bundle
9. Serve hot with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Chocos Pequenos Fritos (Fried Small Cuttlefish)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- small cuttlefish the size of walnuts
- salt
- flour
- olive oil or butter
- fried saffron

## Directions

1. Select small cuttlefish the size of walnuts
2. Remove only the tripe and ink sac
3. Wash very well
4. Sprinkle with finely ground salt
5. Dry well in a cloth
6. Flour them
7. Fry in very hot olive oil or butter over a lively flame
8. Serve garnished with fried saffron
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Chouriço Branco (White Sausage)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk
- Salt
- Onion rounds
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Marjoram
- Coriander seed
- Bay leaves
- Almonds, ground
- Lard, in small pieces
- Chicken or capon meat
- Egg yolks
- Egg whites, beaten
- Salt, ground
- Pepper
- Fine spices
- Pork casings

## Directions

1. Bring to a boil in a casserole a layer of milk tempered with salt, onion rounds, parsley, thyme, marjoram, coriander seed, and bay leaves
2. After all has boiled and reduced to half, remove from heat and pass through a sieve
3. Add a quarter of ground almonds, pass through a cloth with force
4. After passing, add lard cut in small pieces, three parts of chicken or capon meat, a dozen egg yolks, five or six beaten egg whites, and finish seasoning with ground salt, pepper, and fine spices
5. On heat and stirring always with the spoon, when beginning to bind and with good taste, without letting it cool, fill pork casings, which will be tied as is customary with all equality
6. After filling, let boil a quarter hour in water
7. When cooked, place to drain on a cloth, and after cooling, lay on oiled paper and serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Chouriço Preto (Black Sausage)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Onions
- Butter or melted salt pork
- Lard
- Belly and tripe of pork, cooked
- Pork blood
- Milk or broth
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine spices
- Coriander
- Fennel
- Parsley, chopped
- Pork casings
- Water
- Bay leaves
- Bread crumbs
- Orange

## Directions

1. Cut onions into small pieces, sauté in butter or melted salt pork
2. Remove from heat after sautéing
3. Add two or three pounds of lard, belly and tripe of cooked pork cut into pieces, pork blood, a quarter of milk or broth, and place on heat stirring always
4. Season with salt, pepper, fine spices, coriander, fennel, and chopped parsley
5. When beginning to bind and with good taste, without cooling, fill the casings as described
6. Serve in water with little salt and bay leaves
7. When cooked and cold, lay in oiled paper boxes and serve hot with bread crumb fillets and sour orange
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Chouriços de Freza de Vitela (Veal Sweetbread Sausages)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal intestine
- one half veal sweetbread
- three quartilhos of pork blood
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- fine herbs
- parsley
- chives
- one clove of garlic
- intestines for filling
- broth and white wine

## Directions

1. Clean and blanch one veal intestine in water
2. Cook it in one half veal sweetbread
3. Once cooked and half-cooled, chop it
4. Place it in a casserole with three quartilhos of pork blood
5. Season with salt, pepper, spices, fine herbs, parsley, chives, and one clove of garlic
6. When it has good taste, fill the intestines, passing this through a funnel
7. Tie them as instructed
8. Cook them in a sauce with broth and white wine
9. Once cooked and cooled, store them
10. Serve as the preceding ones
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Chouriços de Fígados (Liver Sausages)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen onions
- dozen and a half chicken livers, chopped
- pork lard, cut into small dice
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- fine herbs, chopped
- one quartilho of milk
- half a dozen egg yolks
- intestines for filling
- salt
- lime
- bay leaves

## Directions

1. Cook half a dozen onions in broth
2. Once cooked, drain and pound them in a stone mortar
3. Add dozen and a half chopped chicken livers
4. Once everything is chopped, place in a casserole with pork lard cut into small dice
5. Season with salt, pepper, spices, and fine chopped herbs
6. Add one quartilho of milk and half a dozen egg yolks
7. Mix everything and when it has good taste and texture, fill the intestines
8. Tie them as instructed
9. Cook them in milk with salt, lime, and bay leaves
10. Once cooked, let them cool
11. Store them as the preceding ones
12. Serve hot with orange juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Chouriços de Lagostas e Camarões (Lobster and Shrimp Sausages)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four onions, roasted in the oven or under coals
- pork lard, cut into small dice
- tails from fifty shrimp, chopped
- eight or ten egg yolks
- two spoonfuls of the meat of the same shrimp
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- fine herbs
- fine spices
- intestines for filling
- white wine

## Directions

1. Roast three or four onions in the oven or under coals
2. Chop them and pound them
3. Mix with pork lard cut into small dice
4. Add the chopped tails from fifty shrimp
5. Add eight or ten egg yolks and two spoonfuls of the meat of the same shrimp
6. Place on the heat and stir always
7. Season with salt, pepper, chopped parsley, fine herbs, and fine spices
8. Remove when it begins to thicken and before it boils, when it has good taste
9. Fill the intestines as instructed
10. Cook them in white wine, being careful to prick them with a needle so they do not burst
11. Once cooled, store them on grills with paper underneath
12. Serve hot in small plates with slices of bread crumbs interleaved and sour oranges
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Chouriços Mouros (Moorish Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh pork, lean and fat
- wine
- garlic
- cumin
- onion
- pork fat
- pork blood
- pork casings

## Directions

1. Place fresh, lean and fat pork, cut very finely, in a preserve of wine, garlic, cumin, and very finely minced onion, washed in many waters, and keep in this preserve for one or two days
2. When ready to fill the sausages, transfer the meat to another bowl without the preserve and mix it with fresh lard, minced onion, and some pork blood, enough to color the meat well
3. Fill pork casings, smoke them: after smoking, roast them and take to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Ciba em Gigote (Cuttlefish Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cuttlefish, cooked
- olive oil
- vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook a cuttlefish
2. Chop it finely
3. Put it to boil in a pan with olive oil, vinegar, and pepper
4. As soon as it finishes boiling, serve it
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cidrada (Citron Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- citrons
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the citrons of their skin so the preserve is whiter
2. Wash in two or three cold waters
3. Scald in two or three boiling waters, squeezing in a thick cloth each time
4. Once sweetened, place in three cold waters, always squeezing with the cloth
5. Grate or pound the citrons
6. Add one arratel of citron to two of sugar at hair point and proceed with the quantity desired
7. Stir while it does not dry
8. Once the bottom shows and it is done, remove from the fire and add fragrance if desired
9. Place in small paper cones
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cidrão de Conserva (Preserved Citron)
body:
## Ingredients

- citrons
- egg brine
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the citrons very well and place in a pan, or if a greater quantity, in a pot in egg brine
2. Over nine days sweeten in nine waters
3. Make the preserve with sugar and cover the same way as described for preserved pears
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cinouras Afogadas (Braised Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots
- Salt
- Vinegar or lemon
- Honey or sugar
- Roasted wheat flour
- Broth
- Fried eggs (optional)

## Directions

1. Once the carrots are braised, place them to cook in a pot with hot water.
2. Season with salt, vinegar or lemon, all the seasonings, honey or sugar, well-roasted wheat flour, and a small amount of broth in which they will cook.
3. Once cooked, send to the table.
4. Fried eggs can also be added if desired.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cinouras de Tigelada (Carrots in Tigelada)
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots
- Wheat flour
- Cow butter
- Parsley
- Cilantro
- Parsley
- Onion
- Oregano
- Vinegar
- Cinnamon
- Honey or sugar
- Six eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the carrots and cut them into quarters.
2. Pass them through wheat flour.
3. Fry them in a pan, layering them in a frying pan.
4. Make a sauce by wilting parsley, cilantro, parsley, onion, and oregano in cow butter.
5. Season the sauce with vinegar, cinnamon, all the seasonings, honey or sugar, so that it is both sour and sweet.
6. Pour this sauce over the carrots.
7. Place six eggs on top of this sauce and send to the table.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Coelho Albardado (Larded Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit, roasted
- bread slices
- lard, melted and cleaned of cracklings
- butter
- onion, cut into pieces
- herbs
- parsley
- pepper
- clove
- ginger
- saffron
- lemon

## Directions

1. Once roasted, place the rabbit on a plate over slices of bread with many cuts along the loin and legs
2. Make a sauce with a quarter of melted lard, cleaned of cracklings, a spoonful of butter, an onion cut into pieces, plenty of herbs and parsley, all finely minced
3. Season the sauce with pepper, clove, ginger, and a little saffron
4. Pour the sauce over the rabbit, add lemon on top and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Coelho Cozido em Branco (Rabbit Cooked in White)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit, skinned and washed
- lard
- ham
- herbs
- onion
- black seasonings
- salt
- partridges, pigeons, or doves (optional)

## Directions

1. After skinning and washing the rabbit well, place it to braise in a pot with a portion of lard, another of ham, herbs, an onion cut in half, black seasonings, and salt (if desired, you may also add partridges, pigeons, or doves)
2. Once cooked, send to the table very sour
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Coelho d'Oria (Oria Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- young rabbits, well larded
- onion rounds
- parsley, finely minced
- olive oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- capers

## Directions

1. After larding the young rabbits well, roast them
2. Once roasted, carve them into a plate
3. Place some onion rounds on top (which should be in water) and finely minced green parsley
4. Pour over hot olive oil and vinegar mixed with pepper and capers
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Coelho de João Pires (João Pires' Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- half arratel of lard, minced
- onion
- parsley, finely minced
- rabbits, cut into pieces
- cumin
- lemon
- olive oil
- verjuice or vinegar
- bread slices
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Sauté the minced lard with onion and finely minced parsley
2. Once sautéed, add the rabbit pieces cut into pieces
3. Once cooked, season with all the seasonings, cumin, lemon, olive oil, verjuice (if you have it) or vinegar
4. Place on slices of bread with lemon slices on top and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Coelho de João Pires (João Pires's Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of bacon, chopped
- onion
- parsley, well chopped
- rabbits, cut into pieces
- all the seasonings
- cumin
- lemon
- olive oil
- verjuice or vinegar
- bread slices
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Brown half a pound of chopped bacon with onion and well-chopped parsley
2. When browned, add the rabbits cut into pieces
3. When cooked, season with all the seasonings, cumin, lemon, olive oil, and verjuice (if available) or vinegar
4. Place on bread slices with lemon slices on top
5. Send to table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Coelho de Sellada (Rabbit in Sellada Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted rabbits
- salt
- pepper
- olive oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Roast the rabbits and cut into gigote pieces
2. Arrange on a plate of well-prepared sellada sauce with salt and pepper
3. When ready to serve, pour hot olive oil, vinegar, and pepper on top
4. Send to table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Coelho Real (Royal Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit, half-roasted
- sugar
- almonds, ground
- toasted bread crumbs
- egg yolks
- cinnamon
- clove of India
- musk

## Directions

1. After singeing and half-roasting the rabbit, mince it well
2. Place it in an arratel of sugar syrup, with half an arratel of ground almonds
3. Cook slowly for the space of an hour
4. Add five réis of toasted bread crumbs and eight egg yolks to thicken it
5. Season with cinnamon, clove of India, and musk
6. Pour into small dishes and send to the table browned
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Coelhos Agachados para huma Entrada (Crouching Rabbits for an Entry Course)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbits
- Butter
- Grated bread
- Two egg yolks
- Sausage
- Shallots
- Cucumbers
- Salt
- Pepper
- Thread
- Broth
- White wine
- A bundle of sausage, bay leaf
- Whole onion studded with cloves
- Garlic cloves
- Bacon slices

## Directions

1. Open the rabbits by the belly, clean, and eviscerate them, remove the bile from the livers
2. Make a chopped mixture with the livers, a piece of washed butter, grated bread, two egg yolks, sausage, shallots, cucumbers, salt, and pepper
3. Stuff the rabbits with this mixture, then sew the bellies shut with thread
4. Position the hind legs down under the belly and the front legs down under the snout, as if crouching
5. Secure them in this position with skewers and tied with string
6. Cook in a pan with a little broth, white wine, a bundle of sausage, bay leaf, an onion studded with cloves, some garlic cloves, salt, pepper, and bacon slices
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coelhos de Cebollada à Portuguesa (Rabbit with Onion Sauce Portuguese Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- broth
- white wine
- sage bundle
- fine herbs
- bacon strips
- onions
- olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- curry

## Directions

1. Clean the rabbits and cut into quarters
2. Cook in a braise with broth, white wine, a bundle of sage, and fine herbs, covered with bacon strips
3. Cut a dozen onions into slices and cook slowly in a pot with a little olive oil
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. When half cooked, move to a stronger flame
6. As they begin to brown, moisten with a little vinegar, broth from the braise, and a little curry
7. Reduce so the onion rings remain whole
8. When the sauce has little liquid and good flavor, remove the oil
9. Take the rabbit quarters from the braise and place on a plate with the onion ragú on top
10. Serve hot for an appetizer
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coelhos de Molho de Vilhão à Portuguesa (Rabbit in Game Sauce Portuguese Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- beef substance or broth or water
- garlic cloves
- onion
- lemon
- sage in sprigs
- marjoram
- bay leaf
- olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- rabbit livers
- rabbit

## Directions

1. Bring to a boil in a pot a quart of vinegar with another quart of beef substance or broth or water, two garlic cloves, an onion, and lemon in slices, sage in sprigs, marjoram, a bay leaf, little olive oil, salt, and pepper
2. When well boiled and the sauce reduced to half, bind it with the rabbit livers
3. Strain everything through a sieve
4. Cut the rabbits into quarters after they are roasted
5. Warm them in the sauce without boiling
6. Serve for an appetizer
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coelhos em Tartaruga (Rabbits in Turtle Shape for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- twine
- broth
- wine
- sage bundle

## Directions

1. Skin and clean the rabbits
2. Remove only the bones around the loins from underneath
3. Remove the skin in such a way that it passes through the opening along half of the front quarters of the rabbits
4. Turn it and shape it like a turtle, securing it with twine
5. Place to cook in a braise as above
6. After cooking, serve with a well-flavored sauce of your choice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coelhos Enrolados (Rolled Rabbits for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- cloth
- raw chopped forcemeat
- ham strips
- hard egg yolks
- truffles
- pistachios
- egg whites, beaten
- twine
- broth
- white wine
- sage bundle

## Directions

1. Bone the rabbits entirely except for the head and neck, which should remain attached
2. Lay them flat on a clean cloth with the loins facing down
3. Cover with a raw chopped forcemeat bound well with ham strips, hard egg yolks, truffles, and pistachios if available
4. Moisten the top with beaten egg whites
5. Roll them up carefully so the head points outward
6. Tie with twine and secure
7. Cook as the previous recipe
8. Serve with the same sauce or with anchovy and caper sauce, or Spanish-style sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coelhos Novos de Fricassé (Young Rabbits Fricassée for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- butter or rendered bacon
- salt
- pepper
- sage bundle
- fine herbs
- onion studded with cloves
- flour paste
- broth
- white wine
- truffles or cucumbers or braised artichokes
- egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the rabbits and cut into quarters
2. Brown them in a pot with butter or rendered bacon, salt, pepper, a bundle of sage, and fine herbs, and an onion studded with cloves
3. When browned, dust with flour paste
4. Moisten with broth and a little white wine
5. Skim and let simmer gently
6. When almost cooked, add truffles, cucumbers, or braised artichokes if desired
7. When everything is cooked with little broth and good flavor, remove the fat and the sage bundle
8. Bind the fricassée with egg yolks mixed with a little broth and lemon juice
9. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coelhos Recheados (Stuffed Rabbits)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- ham, chopped
- butter, washed
- grated bread
- two egg yolks
- flour
- chives
- cucumbers
- salt
- pepper
- rabbit
- thread
- bacon strips
- twine
- broth
- white wine
- bundle of sage
- bay leaf
- onion studded with cloves
- garlic cloves
- lemon juice or orange juice

## Directions

1. Chop ham with a bit of washed butter, grated bread, two egg yolks, flour, chives, cucumbers, salt, and pepper, and use this mixture to stuff the rabbits
2. After filling their bellies, tie them with thread
3. Pierce the legs downward under the belly and the front legs downward toward the snout, as if they were crouching, and secure them in this position with skewers and tied with twine
4. Place them to cook in a pot with a little broth, white wine, a bundle of sage, bay leaf, onion studded with cloves, some garlic cloves, salt, pepper, and bacon strips
5. Cook slowly and gradually
6. Pass the sauce through a sieve and remove the fat
7. Add a little curry and bring to a boil, reduce to the proper consistency
8. Serve the sauce over the rabbits with lemon juice or sour orange juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coelhos à Maruja (Rabbits à Maruja)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbits
- Butter
- Flour
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sausage
- Shallots
- Water
- A quartilho of red wine
- A bundle of sausage, thyme, bay leaf, marjoram
- Whole onion studded with cloves
- Garlic cloves
- Whole shallots, blanched in hot water
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean and skin the rabbits and cut them into pieces or quarters
2. Brown some flour in butter, then fry the rabbits in it
3. Moisten them with water and a quartilho of red wine, season with salt, pepper, and a bundle of sausage, thyme, bay leaf, marjoram, a whole onion studded with cloves, and some garlic cloves
4. Bring to a boil and remove the scum, then let simmer gently
5. When almost cooked, add a dozen whole shallots blanched in hot water
6. When finished cooking and the sauce is reduced and has good flavor, remove the fat and the herbs
7. Serve with a touch of vinegar
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Ameixas (Plum Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums
- Clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Put plums in boiling water and when soft and almost cooked, pass them to cold water.
2. Remove them, drain them, put them in clarified sugar, place on heat, and without boiling they will stay this way until they turn green.
3. Arrange them on a plate, reduce the sugar to its necessary point, pour on top, and serve like the preceding ones.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Amêndoas e Damascos verdes (Almond and Green Apricot Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Four handfuls of ash
- Almonds
- Green apricots

## Directions

1. Boil four handfuls of ash in water to make a lye, put the almonds and apricots in it, and give them one boil.
2. Remove from the lye and rub them between your hands to remove the skin, putting them in cold water.
3. Pass them to boiling water, let them cook, and when cooked, which you will know by pricking them with a needle - if it enters easily - pass them to other cold water.
4. Put them in clarified sugar, place on heat, and let them simmer in it until they turn a good green color.
5. When finished and the sugar reduced to its point, serve as desired.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Damasco (Apricot Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Whether whole or cut in half, put apricots in boiling water, give them two boils, and pass them to cold water.
2. Drain them through a sieve, place on heat in sugar at point, give them three or four boils, and skim very well.
3. Arrange on a plate, reduce the sugar, pour on top, and serve.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Framboizas (Raspberry Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberries
- Sugar at high point

## Directions

1. Cut raspberries equally at the stems, put them in sugar at high point, and place on heat. After four boils, arrange on a plate, reduce the sugar, skim it, pour on top, and serve cold.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Ginjas ou de Cereja (Sour Cherry or Cherry Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Sour cherries or cherries
- Sugar at high point

## Directions

1. Cut equally at the stems of the sour cherries or cherries, put them in sugar at high point, and place on heat.
2. After four boils, arrange on a plate, reduce the sugar, skim it, pour on top, and serve cold.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Groselhas (Currant Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants
- Sugar at high point

## Directions

1. Cut currants equally at the stems, put them in sugar at high point, and place on heat. After four boils, arrange on a plate, reduce the sugar, skim it, pour on top, and serve cold.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Marmelos (Quince Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Put quinces in boiling water and let them boil until they become soft.
2. Put them in a bowl with cold water, cut them in quarters, clean them inside, remove the skin with care, and put them in sugar.
3. Place on heat and skim very well, letting them simmer little by little.
4. When cooked and the sugar reduced, put them on a plate and serve hot with the syrup poured over.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Maçam à Pertugueza (Apple Compote in the Portuguese Manner)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen apples
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Pierce a dozen apples on top and remove the seeds and pit, arrange them whole inside a baking dish or on a silver plate with a little fine sugar underneath
2. Fill the apples with the same sugar, and put them to cook in the oven
3. When cooked, serve them hot with a little sugar on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Maçans (Apple Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen apples
- clarified sugar
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Take half a dozen apples, cut them in half, remove the pit, and peel them
2. Place them immediately in cold water
3. Remove from the water and cook them in clarified sugar, as much as needed, and the juice of half a lemon
4. When the apples are cooked, arrange them on a plate, and when the sugar is reduced to a point where it sticks to the fingers, pour it over the apples
5. Serve hot or cold, as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Morangos (Strawberry Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberries
- Sugar at high point

## Directions

1. Cut strawberries equally at the stems, put them in sugar at high point, and place on heat. After four boils, arrange on a plate, reduce the sugar, skim it, pour on top, and serve cold.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Peras (Pear Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- whole pears
- boiling water
- cold water
- clarified sugar
- slice of lemon

## Directions

1. Take the pears whole, place them in boiling water, and when they are firm and almost cooked, place them in cold water, peel them, and arrange them whole or cut in half or in quarters in another water
2. Then put them to cook in clarified sugar with a slice of lemon so they remain whiter
3. When cooked, arrange them on their plate, reduce the sugar to the necessary point, pour it over them and serve hot or cold, as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Peras (Pear Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole pears
- Clarified sugar
- A slice of lemon

## Directions

1. Take whole pears and put them in boiling water until softened and almost cooked.
2. Transfer to cold water and peel them, keeping them whole or cutting them in half or quarters.
3. Place in another water.
4. Cook in clarified sugar with a slice of lemon to keep them whiter.
5. When cooked, arrange on a plate, reduce the sugar to the necessary point, pour over the top, and serve hot or cold as preferred.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Peras assadas (Roasted Pear Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Six or eight pears for cooking, before ripening
- Clarified sugar
- A piece of cinnamon
- Three or four cloves

## Directions

1. Take six or eight pears for cooking, before they are ripe, and place them one after another in a well-heated oven, turning them from time to time so they roast evenly.
2. When placed in water and rubbed with a cloth, remove all the skin.
3. Cut them in half, clean them inside, wash them in three or four waters, and put them to cook in clarified sugar with a piece of cinnamon and three or four cloves.
4. Cover very well with a lid and let them simmer little by little until cooked.
5. Arrange them on their plate, reduce the sugar, pour the reduced sugar over the pears, and serve hot if desired.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Peras à Borguinhona (Burgundy-Style Pear Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a dozen pears with skin, or six or eight
- Clarified sugar
- A piece of cinnamon
- Two or three cloves
- Half a cup of red wine

## Directions

1. Prepare half a dozen pears with skin, or six or eight, and place them to cook in a bowl or small pan with clarified sugar, a piece of cinnamon, and two or three cloves.
2. Let them simmer little by little.
3. When half cooked, add half a cup of red wine, cover with a lid, and finish cooking slowly.
4. When cooked, place on a plate, reduce the sugar to a high point, pour over hot, and serve.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Pêssegos (Peach Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a dozen almost ripe peaches
- Clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Take half a dozen almost ripe peaches, cut them in half, and remove the pits.
2. Put them for a moment in boiling water, remove them as soon as the skin can be taken off, and place them to cook in boiling sugar that has been well skimmed.
3. Let them simmer little by little, and when cooked, reduce the sugar to the ordinary point and serve in the prescribed manner.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Pêssegos assados (Roasted Peach Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Almost ripe peaches
- Clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Follow with almost ripe peaches, place them as with pears in a well-heated oven, and turn them until the skin is charred.
2. Put them immediately in cold water, peel them very thoroughly, wash them in many waters, and place them whole to cook in clarified sugar.
3. When the peaches soften between your fingers, place them on the plate, reduce the syrup as stated, and serve like the other compotes.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compota de Pêssegos à Portuguesa (Portuguese-Style Peach Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Seven or eight almost ripe peaches
- Finely grated sugar

## Directions

1. Cut seven or eight almost ripe peaches in half and remove the pit.
2. Arrange them on a plate with finely grated sugar underneath and on top, place over gentle heat, covered with a lid with fire on top, and let them cook slowly.
3. When cooked and glazed with good color, serve hot.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Compotas de Morangos, Groselhas, e Framboesas (Compotes of Strawberries, Currants, and Raspberries)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- strawberries, currants, and raspberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make in the same manner as candied cherries
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Conferva de Café (Preserved Coffee)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of sugar at thread point
- one ounce of ground coffee

## Directions

1. Reduce one arratel of sugar to the thread point
2. Add one ounce of ground coffee and stir with a spoon until well mixed
3. Pour into a paper mold, let cool, break, and store as described
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Conferva de Chocolate (Preserved Chocolate)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of sugar at thread point
- half an ounce of chocolate

## Directions

1. Reduce one arratel of sugar to the thread point
2. Add half an ounce of chocolate only and stir until well mixed
3. Pour into a paper mold, let cool, break, and store as described
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Conferva de Flor de Violas (Preserved Violet Flowers)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of sugar
- two ounces of crushed violet flowers

## Directions

1. Reduce one arratel of sugar to the thread point
2. Add two ounces of crushed violet flowers and stir constantly with a spoon until well mixed with the sugar
3. Pour into a form made of a sheet of paper about half a finger high
4. Mark the pieces with the point of a knife
5. When half cooled, store in a dry place
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Conferva de Grojelhas (Preserved Currants)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of currants
- two cups of water
- sugar at thread point

## Directions

1. Boil one arratel of currants for an instant in two cups of water
2. Pour with a skimmer into a sieve and strain with force like the sour cherries
3. Take to the heat to dry
4. When dried and dry, add sugar at the thread point and stir for an instant over the heat
5. Pour into a paper mold, let cool, form into pieces, and store like the others
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Conserva de Chocolate (Chocolate Preserve)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- chocolate

## Directions

1. For each pound of sugar, add half an ounce of chocolate only.
2. Reduce the sugar to the hard crack stage.
3. Add the chocolate.
4. Give it one boil.
5. Remove from heat.
6. Strain through a sieve.
7. Cool the syrup.
8. Pour into bottles.
9. Cork and store.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Conserva de Peixe (Fish Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wine
- Vinegar
- Garlic
- Oregano
- Salt
- Fish portions

## Directions

1. Preserve in a mixture of wine, vinegar, garlic, oregano, and salt two portions of fish
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Conserva de Peixe (Fish Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- wine
- vinegar
- garlic
- oregano
- salt
- fish steaks
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Preserve two fish steaks in a mixture of wine, vinegar, garlic, oregano, and salt for two hours
2. Remove the fish from the preserve and roast it
3. Place the fish on a plate
4. Make a sauce from some of the preserve in which it was kept with a splash of olive oil
5. Pour the sauce over the fish and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Conserva de toda a Qualidade de Ameixas (Preserve of All Kinds of Plums)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a hundred plums
- clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Put a hundred plums in boiling water, allow them to boil until they become soft, remove them with a skimming spoon, and place them in cold water
2. Drain them afterward through a sieve, arrange them in small vessels with care so they are not bruised, pour over them hot clarified sugar, and allow them to sit until night, when the sugar will drain off
3. The drained sugar is taken to heat again, and boiling, skimmed and hot, is poured over the plums; this is repeated twice a day for four or five days
4. Advising that in the last cooking the sugar must be freshened, giving it a quarter measure of water, and placing it to boil, skimmed and reduced to its necessary point, pour it boiling over the plums
5. In the same manner are made almonds and green apricots as preserve
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Conserva para Qualquer Carne (Preserve for Any Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quartilho of wine
- half quartilho of vinegar
- one head of garlic
- one branch of oregano
- salt

## Directions

1. Put wine, half vinegar, a head of garlic, a branch of oregano, and salt to taste in a bowl
2. Keep meat in this preserve cut in pieces for three or four hours
3. Then fry the meat in butter and pass through a bit of the preserve brine, brought to a boil
4. Send hot to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Costeletas Assadas nas Grelhas com Ervas Finas (Grilled Chops with Fine Herbs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two veal rib sections
- melted bacon
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- fine breadcrumbs
- shallot sauce
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take one or two veal rib sections and cut into thin chops, slightly thinner than those described above
2. After preparation, soak in a casserole with melted bacon
3. Season with salt, pepper, minced parsley and chives
4. Pass through the fire and after passing, wrap in fine dry breadcrumbs
5. Grease and grill them on the grilling grate
6. Serve with shallot sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costeletas de Vitela de Vários Modos (Veal Chops Various Ways)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal chops
- Melted lard
- Lard
- Fine herbs
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Lard for frying
- Gravy

## Directions
