1. Marinated and prepared chops, pass them through the fire with melted lard, wrap each one in its papillote garnished with chopped lard, and seasoned with fine herbs, so that the chops remain in the middle; and placed to roast as the preceding, serve them also in the same manner
2. Sometimes a small slice of lard can be placed under each one, with a little chopped on each side
3. Also after being cooked in a braise, and when cold, they can be covered with chopped well bound, passed twice through beaten eggs, and breadcrumbs; and then fried in pork lard, served with fried gravy all around
4. They can also be made of chopped only with a pinch in the middle, applying the clean chop bone, and passing them twice through beaten eggs, and breadcrumbs, and browned in the oven with slices of lard underneath; and when they have good color, serve them with sauce of ham essence
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costeletas de Vitela em Fricandó (Veal Chops Fricandeau Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two veal rib sections
- fine bacon for larding
- water
- broth
- aspic or juice
- lemon juice
- chives ragú
- sorrel ragú
- spinach ragú
- celery ragú
- chicory ragú
- lettuce ragú
- green peas ragú

## Directions

1. Take one or two veal rib sections and cut the chops to good thickness, well trimmed of all skin
2. Cut the bones so they are not too long and lard with fine bacon
3. Soak in water for a short time and braise
4. Cook, season, and finish in the same manner as a fricandeau
5. Deglaze with a little broth on the glaze that stuck, or a little aspic
6. Remove the fat and pass through a sieve to serve under the chops with lemon juice
7. Alternatively, place under the glazed chops a ragú of well-blanched chives, and other times of sorrel, spinach, celery, chicory, lettuce, or green peas
8. Finally, serve with the sauce or ragú that seems most convenient, giving the dish the name of the garnish it has
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costeletas de Vitela em Papilheta (Veal Chops en Papillote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal rib section
- melted bacon
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- chives

## Directions

1. Take a veal rib section and cut the chops as described above and beat them slightly with the flat of the knife
2. Marinate in melted bacon with salt, pepper, fine herbs, and chives
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costeletas de Vitela em Papilhota (Veal Chops en Papillote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal chops
- Melted lard
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Chives
- Paper

## Directions

1. Take a square of veal and cut the chops as described above, and beat them somewhat with the back of a knife; then marinate them in melted lard, with salt, pepper, fine herbs, and chives
2. Take a quarter of paper, make a small hole in it so that the point of the chop bone passes through, anointing the paper on the outside, wrap them in it with their marinade; and folded neatly all around, place them to roast on the grill over another sheet of anointed paper
3. When roasted, serve them in their papillote, and without sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costeletas de Vitela para uma Entrada (Veal Chops for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two veal rib sections
- fine bacon for larding
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- fine herbs
- lemon slices
- onion slices
- melted bacon
- shallot sauce
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take one or two veal rib sections, the piece that runs from the kidney to the neck
2. Trim the top of the loin of all its skin
3. Lard with minced bacon
4. Marinate and season with salt, pepper, bay leaves, whole fine herbs, lemon slices, onion slices, and melted bacon
5. After marinating three or four hours, roast on a spit covered with buttered paper
6. Once roasted, remove the paper and serve with shallot sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costeletas de Vitela à Conty (Veal Chops Conty Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two veal rib sections
- cucumber fillets
- black truffles
- ham
- hard egg yolks
- freshwater crayfish tails
- good olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- lemon juice
- bacon slices
- paper
- good essence
- or minced raw veal
- parsley
- hard egg yolks
- cooked ham
- red omelette
- black truffles from the earth
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Take one or two veal rib sections and trim the skin from the loins very well
2. Make small incisions close together and insert fillets of cucumber, black truffles, ham, hard egg yolks, and freshwater crayfish tails into these cuts
3. Marinate with good olive oil, salt, pepper, fine herbs, and lemon juice
4. Braise in a dry braise covered with bacon slices and paper
5. Once cooked, remove the fat and serve hot with good essence and lemon
6. Alternatively, fill the incisions with minced raw veal powdered with different colors: one green with parsley cooked and minced well, another with hard egg yolks, another with cooked ham, another with red omelette, another with black truffles from the earth, and another with breadcrumbs, all well covered with bacon slices and paper with low heat on top
7. Once cooked, serve hot with the sauce that seems best
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costellas de Carneiro (Lamb Chops)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops
- butter or rendered pork fat
- salt
- pepper
- chives
- parsley, finely chopped
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the chops evenly and trim them well
2. Beat them between two cloths with the flat of a knife
3. Moisten them with butter or rendered pork fat and season with salt, pepper, chives, and finely chopped parsley
4. Dust them with bread crumbs
5. Place them on grills, basting them from time to time
6. Serve them dry
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costellas de Carneiro (Lamb Chops)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb chops
- Butter
- Pork belly
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onion
- Parsley
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the chops equally and after being well trimmed and beaten between two cloths with the flat of the knife, moisten them with butter or melted pork belly and season with salt, pepper, onion, finely chopped parsley, and sprinkle with breadcrumbs
2. Place them on grills to roast, basting them from time to time, and serve them hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costellas de Carneiro ao Gratém (Lamb Chops au Gratin)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops
- braising liquid or butter
- stock
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- veal forcemeat
- bread crumb forcemeat
- butter
- egg yolks
- chives
- parsley
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook the chops in a braise, or if not, pass them through butter
2. Moisten with stock and white wine, seasoning with salt and pepper
3. Let them cook slowly
4. When cooked, bind the sauce with a little cullis
5. Line the bottom of the serving plate with veal forcemeat
6. Cover it with forcemeat made of bread crumbs, butter, egg yolks, chives, and parsley, all finely chopped and seasoned with little salt and pepper
7. Place the prepared plate on a gentle heat so the forcemeat sets
8. Drain the fat from it
9. Arrange the chops with their sauce on top
10. Serve hot with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costellas de Carneiro em Cassarola (Lamb Chops in Casserole)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops, well aged
- salt
- pepper
- butter or rendered pork fat
- white wine
- stock
- shallot
- egg yolks
- green lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take lamb chops that are well mortified and cut them into equal smaller chops
2. Trim them and season with salt and pepper
3. Sauté them in butter or rendered pork fat, turning them from time to time until cooked
4. When browned, remove them and drain the fat from the casserole
5. Moisten what remains with a little white wine and half a spoon of stock, adding a chopped shallot
6. When the sauce is reduced, put the chops back in and before it boils, add three or four egg yolks mixed in green lemon juice
7. When bound and of good taste, serve for an entrance course, or with a spicy sauce or another as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costellas de Carneiro em Crespina (Lamb Chops in Caul)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops
- onions
- butter
- milk
- egg yolks
- caul fat
- bread crumbs
- essence
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook the chops and cover them with their glaze as described above
2. Cover them with a ragout of onions cut into slices
3. Pass the chops through butter and cook them in milk thickened with egg yolks
4. Wrap them in a piece of caul fat and pass them through beaten eggs
5. Place them in a baking dish covered with bread crumbs
6. Cook them in the oven
7. When cooked and well-colored, serve with clear essence sauce as in the preceding recipes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costellas de Carneiro em Fricandó (Lamb Chops Fricandeau)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops in two whole square pieces
- bacon
- cold water
- bacon strips
- ham slice
- parsley bundle
- onion
- stock
- sorrel sauce

## Directions

1. Take two whole square pieces of lamb chops and trim them very well
2. Remove the membranes over the fillets
3. Lard them with fine bacon and put them to soak in cold water
4. Then blanch them in fresh water
5. Transfer to a small casserole, cover with bacon strips
6. Add a slice of ham, a bundle of parsley, and an onion studded with cloves
7. Moisten with stock without salt
8. When cooked, strain the stock through a sieve, remove the fat, and reduce it
9. Apply the glaze to the chops and when sufficiently colored, serve with sorrel sauce or any other sauce that seems appropriate
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Costellas de Carneiro em Roupad (Lamb Chops in Bread Crust)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops
- stock
- salt
- parsley
- fine herbs
- raw forcemeat
- bread crumbs
- bacon strips
- lemon juice
- pork butter

## Directions

1. Trim the chops and cook them in stock with little salt, a bundle of parsley, and fine herbs
2. When cooked, strain the stock through a sieve, reduce it, and glaze the chops
3. When cold, cover them with raw forcemeat and dust with bread crumbs
4. Place them in a baking dish with bacon strips underneath
5. Cook them in the oven
6. When cooked, serve them with essence and lemon juice, or fry them in pork butter and serve garnished with fried parsley
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Couve Flor de Vários Modos (Cauliflower in Various Ways)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Water
- White broth
- Butter
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Blanch in water and cook in white broth
2. Strain over a sieve
3. Arrange on a plate and serve with butter sauce bound with pepper and vinegar
4. After cooking and arranging on the plate with butter and grated cheese underneath, cover with sauce and grated cheese, brown in the oven, and serve for a course between meals
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coxa de Vitella de Adobo (Veal Leg in Marinade)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one white veal leg
- thick bacon, seasoned
- salt
- fine spices
- fine herbs
- shallots
- one clove of garlic
- veal slices
- beef slices
- onions
- cloves
- marjoram
- twine
- whole pepper
- good white wine
- white broth or water

## Directions

1. Prepare a white veal leg so that it is round and well-larded with thick bacon seasoned and mixed with salt, fine spices, fine herbs, shallots, and one clove of garlic
2. Line a pot with bacon and veal slices, onions, cloves, and marjoram
3. Place the veal leg on top, tied with twine
4. Season with salt and whole pepper
5. Cover with other slices of veal and bacon
6. Add one bottle of good white wine and finish moistening with good white broth or water
7. Once cooked, let it cool in its own braising liquid
8. After cooling, drain it to serve on a plate over a well-folded napkin
9. It may also be served by clarifying the broth in which it was cooked and reducing it to aspic, then placing it over the leg with various decorative patterns
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coxa de Vitella em Poço (Veal Leg in Mold)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal leg
- bacon slices
- fine bacon for larding
- minced forcemeat made from the veal meat removed
- small pigeons
- truffles
- artichoke hearts in ragout
- ham essence
- Spanish sauce
- veal glaze

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from a veal leg by turning it inside out
2. Remove a good portion of meat and lard the rest with fine bacon all around from bottom to top
3. Take a round copper mold in the shape of a timbale and line it with bacon slices inside
4. Place the larded veal leg in the center on top of the slices
5. Line it with forcemeat at the bottom and all around, made from the meat removed
6. Arrange very well, then place in the middle half a dozen small pigeons, truffles, and artichoke hearts in ragout
7. Being very well arranged, cover everything with the rest of the forcemeat, well smoothed
8. Garnish with bacon slices and cook in the oven or over moderate heat below and above
9. When cooked, turn out onto a platter
10. Remove the bacon slices and, if it has good color, place gracefully on the serving dish and serve hot with ham essence or with Spanish sauce
11. If it does not have good color, it can be glazed with veal glaze
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coxa de Vitella à Bréza (Veal Leg Braised)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one round leg of veal, well-fattened and well-white
- thick bacon, well-seasoned
- veal slices
- beef slices
- roots
- parsley
- fine herbs
- one studded onion
- cloves of garlic
- bay leaf
- salt
- pepper
- white wine
- broth
- spinach ragout or sorrel ragout

## Directions

1. Take the round leg of a veal, well-fattened and well-white, and clean it of all skin and bone
2. Lard it with thick bacon well-seasoned
3. Line the bottom of a pot with slices of beef and veal
4. Place the leg on top, tied with twine, and add some roots, a bunch of parsley, fine herbs, one studded onion, cloves of garlic, a bay leaf, salt, and pepper
5. Cover with bacon slices
6. Cover well and braise on gentle heat
7. After one hour of sweating, add one quarter of white wine and two spoonfuls of broth and continue cooking
8. Once the leg is cooked, remove the twine and serve with a well-finished spinach ragout or sorrel ragout, or any other vegetable ragout or sauce as preferred
9. May also be served as a cold dish with the braising liquid clarified and made into aspic
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coxas de Coelho (Rabbit Thighs for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit thighs
- rabbit loins
- ham
- sage
- chives
- truffles
- salt
- pepper
- thread
- veal or bacon strips
- sage bundle
- fine herbs
- broth
- white wine
- curry
- lemon juice or sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Remove the thighs from two or three rabbits after skinning and cleaning
2. Remove the larger bone from underneath and enlarge them with your fingers from inside without tearing the skin
3. Fill them with a mixture made from loins of the same rabbits cut into cubes, mixed with a little ham, sage, chives, chopped truffles, seasoned with salt and pepper
4. Once filled and sewn with thread, cook on gentle heat for half an hour in a braise with veal or bacon strips, bundle of sage, and fine herbs, salt, and pepper
5. Moisten with a spoonful of broth and a quart of white wine
6. When the thighs are cooked, remove them and add half a spoonful of curry to the bottom of the braise and bring to a boil again
7. When it has good flavor, remove the fat, strain through a sieve, and serve over the rabbit thighs with lemon juice or sour orange juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coxas de Frangas or Galinhas novas ao Basílico (Young Hen Thighs with Basil)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 or 12 hen thighs
- cream
- cooked hen breast
- fat livers
- veal sweetbreads
- truffles
- béchamel
- egg yolks
- twine and needle
- egg wash
- breadcrumbs
- pork fat

## Directions

1. Remove bones from 10 or 12 hen thighs in the same manner as the previous recipe
2. Stuff them with a fine cream mixture containing a little hash of cooked hen breasts, fat livers, veal sweetbreads, and truffles, all passed in a well-bound béchamel with egg yolks
3. Sew the thighs very well around with twine and needle
4. Cook them in a well-maintained white braising liquid
5. When cooked, pass through egg wash, coat twice with fine breadcrumbs
6. Fry in hot pork fat and serve with fried parsley rings
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coxas de Galinhas com queijo Parmezão (Hen Thighs with Parmesan Cheese)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- stuffed hen thighs
- cullis
- Parmesan cheese
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- chopped meat

## Directions

1. Prepare stuffed thighs and cook as described above
2. Put a little cullis with a handful of grated Parmesan cheese at the bottom of a plate
3. Arrange the hen thighs on top
4. Pour a little cullis thickened with butter over them
5. Cover everything with grated cheese and brown in the oven
6. When browned, drain the fat and serve hot. Alternatively, a béchamel bound with egg yolks can be added, covered with half breadcrumbs and half cheese, and served the same way after browning
7. Another way: put a little chopped meat at the bottom of the plate making borders of the same chopped meat two fingers high, arrange the hen legs in the middle with some ragout garnish, cover with chopped meat and fine breadcrumbs on top, cook in the oven. When cooked, drain the fat and serve as directed
8. The legs can also be stuffed with an Italian chopped mixture made with different herbs, hen breasts, cheese, eggs, and cream, and can be diversified in various ways according to the taste of the cook
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coxas de Galinhas em folhas (Hen Thighs in Leaves)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 hens
- partridge breasts
- ham
- veal sweetbreads
- truffles
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- shallots
- fine herbs
- scraped bacon
- pork fat
- anchovy sauce
- capers

## Directions

1. Clean 3 or 4 hens, singe and scald them, remove the legs leaving plenty of skin all around
2. Remove all bones except the tip of the leg bone
3. Make a hash of partridge breasts, ham, some veal sweetbreads, and truffles, all cut into small dice and seasoned with salt, pepper, parsley, shallots, fine herbs, and mixed with scraped bacon
4. Open the thighs on a napkin and stuff them with this mixture
5. Sew them around with fine twine
6. Sear them in melted pork fat and then cook in a well-maintained braising liquid
7. When cooked, drain the fat and serve with anchovy and caper sauce, or with Italian sauce, or any other good sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coxas de Peru a Santa Menut (Turkey Thighs à Santa Menu)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey thighs
- thick bacon
- milk
- butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- allspice grains
- coriander seeds dried and crushed
- rosemary
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- parsley
- onion
- garlic

## Directions

1. Prepare the turkey thighs and lard them with thick bacon
2. Put them to cook in an adobo made with half a Canadian measure of milk, a piece of butter mixed with flour, seasoned with salt, pepper, two grains of allspice, some crushed dried coriander seeds, rosemary, bay leaf, marjoram, parsley, onion, and two cloves of garlic
3. Let them cook slowly
4. When cooked, remove them, let them drain, then pass them through the fat of the same adobo
5. Cover them with fine grated bread, sprinkle them with butter, and roast them on the grill
6. When roasted, serve with clear essence, verjuice juice, salt, pepper, or another sauce that suits best
7. The same thighs of turkey after roasting can also be served passed through the grill with Robert sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Coxas de Perus em Botinas (Turkey Legs in Botines)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three turkeys
- Partridge breasts
- Veal sweetbreads
- Cured ham
- Truffles
- Bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- String
- Pork lard
- Braise liquid
- Grated bread
- Grilling implements
- Shallot sauce
- Lemon juice
- Eggs
- Fried sauce

## Directions

1. Take two or three turkeys, singe them lightly, remove the thighs leaving enough skin around, remove the bones leaving only the small bone at the point
2. Remove also part of the inner meat
3. Stuff them with a mixture made of partridge breasts, veal sweetbreads, cured ham, and truffles all cut into small dice, well seasoned with salt, pepper, and spices, mixed with scraped bacon
4. When stuffed, wrap them with string in a circular motion and close them like a purse
5. Brown in pork lard
6. Clean them very well, then cook in a braise
7. Serve with whatever sauce seems best
8. If desired, they can also be coated with grated bread and broiled on grills, and served with shallot sauce and lemon juice
9. Also, after cooking, they can be passed through eggs and coated with grated bread, doing this twice, then fried in pork lard and served with fried sauce around them
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Creadilhas (Fried Lamb Sweetbreads)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb sweetbreads
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Skin the sweetbreads, cut into quarters
2. Sprinkle with ground salt and flour
3. At the moment of eating, fry in hot oil
4. After fried and browned, serve garnished with sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema Aveludada (Velvet Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one handful of milk
- salt
- sugar
- dry coriander seed
- cinnamon stick
- green lemon zest
- chicken marrow

## Directions

1. Season one handful of milk with salt, sugar, dry coriander seed, a stick of cinnamon, and green lemon zest
2. Put on heat to boil until reduced to two-thirds
3. When reduced and almost cold, add two or three chicken marrow fillets
4. Pass two or three times through a napkin
5. Pour into the plate
6. Place over heat with coals on top
7. As soon as it is hot and curdled, remove it, let it cool, and serve cold
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema Aveludada com Amêndoas (Velvet Cream with Almonds)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of almonds, blanched
- half a dozen bitter almonds
- milk
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- chicken marrow

## Directions

1. Grind and moisten at the same time with a drop of milk one quarter of blanched almonds and half a dozen bitter almonds
2. After well ground and made into a paste, dissolve in boiled, seasoned and reduced milk as in the preceding one
3. Add to it two or three chicken marrow fillets
4. Pass two or three times through a napkin
5. After well squeezed, pour into its plate
6. Place over moderate heat or over a saucepan full of very hot water
7. When curdled, serve it
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema Batida (Beaten Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one pan of good cow's milk
- half a pound of sifted and fine sugar
- one small spoonful of orange flower water
- a piece of gum tragacanth the size of a hazelnut, in powder

## Directions

1. Pour one pan of good cow's milk into a bowl with half a pound of sifted and fine sugar
2. Add one small spoonful of orange flower water
3. Beat on the edge with a bundle of rods, and add as much gum tragacanth powder as a hazelnut
4. Continue always beating and lifting the foam, remove it with a skimmer into a sieve with a plate underneath
5. Continue thus until all the milk is finished
6. Arrange neatly with the skimmer and serve with fruit as the final course
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema Batida com Morangos (Beaten Cream with Strawberries)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a measure of strawberries, cleaned and washed
- three or four ounces of fine sugar
- foam from beaten milk

## Directions

1. Take half a measure of cleaned and washed strawberries and put them in a pan with three or four ounces of fine sugar
2. Mash very well with a wooden spoon
3. Pass this pulp through a sieve
4. Beat it a second time with the foam of milk
5. When everything is incorporated, serve with the fruit as the preceding cream
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema d'Italiana (Italian Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one handful of milk
- salt
- coriander seed
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- one quarter of sugar
- seven egg yolks
- two or three egg whites

## Directions

1. Put to boil in a saucepan one handful of milk, seasoned with salt, coriander seed, cinnamon, lemon zest, and one quarter of sugar
2. When boiling and reduced to half, and almost cold, strain through a napkin with seven egg yolks and two or three egg whites
3. After straining, pour into a plate or porcelain cups
4. Cook in a vessel with boiling water
5. After cooked, sprinkle with fine sugar
6. Pass a hot spatula over the top
7. Serve cold if desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema de Aletria (Vermicelli Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 arratel of vermicelli
- water

## Directions

1. Boil 1 arratel of vermicelli in water
2. When half cooked, drain it
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema de Aletria (Vermicelli Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of vermicelli
- milk
- egg yolks
- clarified butter
- fine sugar
- lemon zest
- orange flower
- candied fruit
- almond biscuits
- egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. Boil one arratel of vermicelli in boiling water
2. When half cooked, strain through a sieve
3. Finish cooking in milk
4. When cooked, thickened, and dry, add egg yolks, clarified butter, fine sugar, lemon zest, orange flower and candied fruit
5. Chop the almond biscuits and mix everything well with beaten egg whites
6. Pour into the serving dish
7. Sprinkle with sugar
8. Put to cook in the oven
9. When it has a good color, serve hot as an entremet
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema de Amêndoas (Almond Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- egg yolks
- almond biscuits
- orange flower
- two ounces of chocolate

## Directions

1. Heat the milk, seasoned, and strain with egg yolks as mentioned in the preceding article
2. Add to it almond biscuits, orange flower, two ounces of chocolate, all ground, reduced to powder, and well mixed
3. Pour into deep porcelain cups
4. Cook carefully as the preceding one
5. Serve as the others
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema de Groselhas ou de Framboizes (Currant or Raspberry Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- currants or raspberries
- sugar
- milk
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil the currants in a saucepan with sugar and crush them with a spoon
2. Let them boil a little
3. Then remove them and pour into milk already boiled and seasoned as in the strawberry one, and its egg yolks
4. Strain through a napkin
5. Pour into a plate or Indian cups
6. Cook as stated and serve as the others
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema de Pasteleiro (Pastry Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 spoonfuls of finest wheat flour
- some milk
- 1.5 dozen eggs, beaten
- 1.5 to 2 Canadian measures of milk
- salt
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- 0.5 arratel of clarified butter

## Directions

1. Pour the spoonfuls of finest wheat flour into a saucepan
2. Dissolve it with a little milk
3. Add the beaten eggs and mix everything together
4. Then add 1.5 to 2 Canadian measures of milk
5. Season with salt, cinnamon, and lemon peel
6. Add half an arratel of clarified butter
7. Place over heat, stirring constantly until it is thickened and cooked, taking care that it does not stick
8. Remove from heat, let it cool, and use it for tarts, fritters, and other small dishes as needed
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema de Pistaches (Pistachio Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- pistachio nuts

## Directions

1. Heat and season half a handful of milk with salt, sugar, cinnamon, and a bit of lemon zest
2. Let it cool almost completely
3. Add to it a few well-ground pistachios
4. Pass forcefully twice through a napkin
5. Pour afterwards into the serving plate
6. Cook as above and serve garnished with pistachios around the edge
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema Ligeira e Branca (Light and White Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one pan of milk
- salt
- sugar
- three egg whites, well beaten
- orange flower water
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Season one pan of milk with salt and sugar, and put it to boil
2. When reduced to two-thirds of its volume, remove from the heat
3. Add three well-beaten egg whites to it
4. Put the pan back over the fire, beat constantly with rods
5. After it boils three or four times, pour it into its serving dish
6. Let it cool, then sprinkle with orange flower water
7. Dust with fine sugar and send to the table
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema Ligeira e Branca (Light and White Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one handful of milk
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Season one handful of milk with salt and sugar and put to boil
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema Meringada (Meringue Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- cream prepared as in Crema Queimada
- egg whites
- 2 or 3 handfuls of fine sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the cream as in the previous recipe
2. Place it on its plate
3. Beat the egg whites until stiff like snow, as for biscuits
4. Add 2 or 3 handfuls of fine sugar and mix very well
5. Spread over the cream
6. Sprinkle again with sugar
7. Place in the oven with mild heat
8. When browned, serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema Queimada (Burnt Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 spoonful of flour dissolved in milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 6 ounces of sugar
- salt
- 1 cinnamon stick
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Dissolve a spoonful of flour in milk with 8 egg yolks and 6 ounces of sugar
2. Pass through a sieve
3. Season with salt
4. Add a cinnamon stick and lemon peel
5. Place over heat, stirring constantly until it cooks little by little
6. When thickened, remove the lemon and cinnamon
7. Pour onto a plate
8. Sprinkle with fine sugar
9. Pass a hot spatula over the top
10. Serve as an entremets dish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema Virgem (Virgin Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five egg whites, well beaten with snow
- milk
- salt
- sugar
- orange flower water

## Directions

1. Beat four or five egg whites with snow
2. Mix them with milk seasoned with salt, sugar, and orange flower water
3. Pour into a dish and place it to cook over a pan full of water
4. Cover with its lid
5. Put heat on top
6. When it is set and golden with sugar and a hot spatula, serve it cold or hot if desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema à Delfina (Delfina Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- cream cooked with flour and sugar
- 7 or 8 egg whites, beaten until stiff
- thin slices of Savoy biscuits

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the cream with flour and sugar as in Crema Queimada
2. Mix in 7 or 8 stiffly beaten egg whites
3. Place in a plate to a depth of one finger's height
4. Arrange thin slices of Savoy biscuits on top
5. Cover with another layer of cream
6. Add another layer of biscuit slices on top of that
7. Add another layer of cream, and continue this way until the plate is full
8. Sprinkle sugar on top
9. Place in the oven to cook
10. When cooked and browned, serve hot as an entremets
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crema á Suissa (Swiss Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one bottle of white wine or Rhine wine
- one quarter of sugar
- a piece of green lemon peel
- whole cinnamon
- twenty-four grains of coriander seed
- six egg yolks
- a little flour

## Directions

1. Put one bottle of white wine or Rhine wine in a pan with one quarter sugar, a piece of green lemon peel, whole cinnamon, and twenty-four grains of coriander seed
2. Let it boil gently for four or five minutes
3. When reduced to two-thirds, pass it through a sieve with six egg yolks and a little flour dissolved
4. Pour into a dish and place it to cook over a pan of boiling water
5. Put a little heat on top
6. When it is set, let it cool and serve as an intermediate course
7. The same can be made with water, Madeira wine, Malaga wine, or any other wine desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Cremas de Baunilhas, de Chocolate, e de Café (Vanilla, Chocolate, and Coffee Creams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- three quartilhos of milk
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- vanilla or chocolate or coffee
- eight egg yolks
- two or three egg whites

## Directions

1. Put to boil three quartilhos of milk seasoned with salt, sugar, cinnamon, and lemon zest
2. When reduced to half a handful, grind the necessary quantity of vanilla, pour it into the milk with eight egg yolks and two or three egg whites
3. Pass everything through a sieve
4. Pour into its plate and cook as the others, and serve in the same way
5. For chocolate cream, proceed in the same manner
6. For coffee cream, brew the coffee in milk, then let it settle and clarify; mix this milk with another seasoned one and with egg yolks, pass through a sieve, pour into the plate, cook and serve as the others
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Creme de Morangos (Strawberry Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- strawberries
- sugar
- milk
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put the strawberries on the heat in a saucepan with sugar and crush them with a spoon
2. Add to milk and egg yolks
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Pour into the plate
5. Cook as the preceding one
6. Serve garnished with whole strawberries around the edge
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Crocante Vidrado (Glazed Brittle)
body:
## Ingredients

- clarified butter
- brittle dough
- fine sugar
- almond paste
- fruit jellies

## Directions

1. Grease the outside of a round casserole with butter, cover with a sheet of brittle dough, cut as desired, sprinkle fine sugar in the gaps, and bake in the oven
2. When cooked and glazed, let cool
3. When serving, place the casserole over a small flame so it releases
4. Make a base of almond paste with fruit jellies the size of the plate, place the brittle on top, and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Crocantes de Massa de Amendoas (Almond Paste Brittles)
body:
## Ingredients

- almond paste
- clarified butter
- white glaze or colored glaze
- fruit jellies

## Directions

1. Stretch out some almond paste, cut into strips, line the bottom of any round mold already greased with clarified butter
2. Add a border of the same paste around the edge so everything is joined in one piece
3. Bake in the oven at moderate heat
4. When cooked and fried, detach carefully, cover with white or colored glaze, and serve on a base of the same paste garnished with fruit jellies
5. They can also be served spread with sugar in thread stage, garnished with colored nonpareils
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Culí branco (White Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheets of veal
- Slices of ham
- Onions and roots
- Stock
- One bundle of parsley and fine herbs
- One onion studded with cloves
- Chicken breasts
- Capon breasts, or partridge breasts
- Hard egg yolks
- Bread soaked in milk

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of a casserole with sheets of veal, slices of ham, onions, and roots as for ordinary broth
2. After it has sweated and before it sticks, moisten with stock and season with one bundle of parsley and fine herbs and one onion studded with cloves
3. Let it simmer little by little
4. When the meat is cooked and of good taste, pound chicken breasts, capon or partridge breasts, hard egg yolks, and bread soaked in milk
5. Dissolve everything in the stock, pass through a sieve
6. When warm but not boiling, use for white soups, sauces, and ragus
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Culí de Camarões (Shrimp Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Onions, roots, and pieces of fish
- Stock
- One bundle of parsley and fine herbs
- Two or three cloves
- Some shrimps
- Some almonds
- Four hard egg yolks
- Two pieces of bread soaked

## Directions

1. Line a casserole with butter, onions, roots, and pieces of fish as for lean broth
2. After it has sweated and begins to stick, moisten with stock and season with one bundle of parsley and fine herbs and two or three cloves
3. Let it simmer for one hour
4. Pound some shrimps with some almonds and four hard egg yolks, and two pieces of soaked bread
5. Dissolve everything in the stock, pass through a sieve
6. Heat it and without letting it boil, use for soups, entrees, terrines, tarts, and for everything that needs shrimp broth
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Culí de Castanhas (Chestnut Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a hundred chestnuts
- Veal substance
- Ham substance

## Directions

1. Remove the first shell from half a hundred chestnuts and put them in the oven to remove the second skin
2. When peeled, boil them in substance or stock
3. When cooked, pound them in a stone mortar
4. Dissolve the pounded chestnuts with veal and ham substance
5. Pass through a strainer, put in a casserole, place over heat, skim it
6. When it has good color and good taste, use for soups, terrines, and other entrees
7. All broths of vegetables, roots, and purees should be finished in the same manner
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Culí de Perdizes para sopas (Partridge Broth for Soups)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef
- Two partridges
- Pieces of bread crusts
- Substance
- Stock

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef and two partridges, remove the breasts and pound the rest with pieces of bread crusts
2. Dissolve with good substance and stock
3. Season and give it four boilings
4. Pass through a strainer or sieve
5. Use for various soups
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Culí magro (Lean Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of butter
- Onions in slices
- Parsnips
- Carrots
- Pieces of fish
- Stock
- One spoon of substance
- Flour browned in butter
- Half a quarter of white wine
- One bundle of parsley and fine herbs
- Some cloves of garlic

## Directions

1. Put a piece of butter in a casserole, add onions in slices, parsnips, carrots, and pieces of fish
2. Let it sweat little by little
3. When it sticks, add stock and one spoon of substance, and thicken with flour browned in butter
4. Season with half a quarter of white wine, one bundle of parsley and fine herbs, and some cloves of garlic
5. When cooked and of good taste, remove the fat, pass through a sieve, and use for all sauces where necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Culí ordinário (Ordinary Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheets of veal
- Two slices of ham
- Some roots and onions
- One bundle of parsley and fine herbs
- One onion with two or three cloves
- Stock
- Flour browned in butter, or flour dissolved in substance

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of a casserole with sheets of veal and two slices of ham, some roots and onions
2. Let it sweat on gentle heat, adding a drop of stock from time to time
3. Skim and season with one bundle of parsley and fine herbs, one onion with two or three cloves
4. Thicken with browned flour or flour dissolved in substance
5. Let it simmer little by little
6. When the meat is cooked, remove the fat, pass through a sieve, and use for ragus, sauces, and whatever else is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Cuscús (Couscous)
body:
## Ingredients

- One quarter of ground grain flour
- Wheat grain
- Water

## Directions

1. Take one quarter of ground grain flour sifted through a fine sieve.
2. Put it in a mortar and sprinkle it with a limited amount of water, twisting it in your hands with great force in only one direction so that it becomes granular.
3. When all the flour is made this way, pour it into a winnowing basket and winnow it, what stays on top is the couscous and what falls below is ground again with the same fine sprinkler or with a sprig of myrtle or other clean thing that is not bitter.
4. The water used to sprinkle the flour should have a bit of wheat grain in it.
5. Once the flour is finished this way, place the couscous in a couscous maker lined very well with paper inside.
6. Place a pot of iron or clay on a trivet with water over the heat and when it boils well place the couscous maker over the mouth of the pot.
7. When the couscous is cooked, which will taste like cooked bread, remove it and return it to the mortar to break it up again with great force in only one direction and when broken up, place it in the sun to dry and harden.
8. This is how couscous is made.
9. When you want to eat it, the broth is made separately in a bowl.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Cuscús (Couscous)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- a piece of wheat
- water

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and mix with a piece of wheat.
2. Place the couscous in a couscous maker lined very well with papers inside.
3. Put an iron or clay pot with water on a trivet over the fire and bring to a boil.
4. Place the couscous maker on top of the pot opening.
5. When the couscous is cooked (it should taste like cooked bread), remove it and return it to the bowl to break it apart with great force in only one direction.
6. Once broken apart, put it in the sun to dry and harden.
7. When ready to eat, prepare the broth separately in a bowl as desired, either meat broth, sweet broth, or broth made like for vermicelli.
8. Once the broth is made and boiled, remove from heat and pour the couscous on top.
9. Let it dry and serve on plates with cinnamon on top.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Damafeos de Conserva (Preserved Apricots)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half dozen apricots
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel a dozen and a half apricots and remove the pits
2. Cut into pieces and cook in water until it becomes like marmalade
3. When dried and desiccated, add sugar and make like the preceding recipes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: De lingoados (Sole with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two sole, whole and fried
- half arratel of butter, washed
- a small amount of mustard
- onion, finely chopped
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take two sole after frying them whole, split them down the middle from top to bottom, and place them on plates
2. Take half an arratel of washed butter with a small amount of mustard and onion very finely chopped, and put it to fry with pepper, clove, and nutmeg
3. When it boils, pour this sauce over the sole
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Defina Olha Moura (Moorish Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- four arratels of beef brisket
- two beef tongues
- one dozen beef marrow bones
- two marrow bones
- one eighth of grains
- six heads of garlic
- clove
- pepper
- nutmeg
- savory
- oregano

## Directions

1. Place in a pot and cook for twenty-four hours in cork charcoal ash four arratels of beef brisket, two beef tongues, one dozen marrow bones, two marrow bones, one eighth of grains, six heads of garlic, clove, pepper, nutmeg, savory, and oregano
2. When cooked, serve on bread slices
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Descaida (Shredded Meat Preparation)
body:
## Ingredients

- Descaida meat
- pork fat (toucinho)
- aromatics (cheiros)
- egg
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Shred the descaida meat with pork fat and aromatics, as if preparing it for pastries
2. Season with all the seasonings
3. Place this shredded meat in a small dish or frying pan
4. Top with a beaten egg, or brown it over coals like tegellada
5. Serve with lemon juice on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Descaida de Outro Modo (Shredded Meat - Second Preparation)
body:
## Ingredients

- Descaida meat, shredded
- egg yolks
- chicken ovary
- broth from the pan
- cinnamon
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the shredded descaida as described above
2. Fill the chicken ovary with it, making small sausages
3. Place them in a small pot with a drop of broth from the pan
4. Season as with almonds
5. Bind with an egg yolk
6. Place on slices of bread and serve with cinnamon and lemon juice on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Descaida de Outro Modo (Shredded Meat - Third Preparation)
body:
## Ingredients

- Descaida meat, shredded
- chicken meatballs (almonds)
- yellow broth
- cinnamon
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Shred the descaida meat
2. Make small meatballs the size of buttons, seasoned with chicken meatballs
3. Cook in yellow broth as described above
4. Place on slices of bread and serve with cinnamon and lemon juice on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Desfeito de Carneiro (Shredded Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of lamb from the leg
- a quarter of bacon
- aromatics
- vinegar
- seasonings
- a quarter of grated bread
- six eggs
- butter
- bread slices
- lemon
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut two arratels of lamb from the leg into small pieces without bone
2. Braise with a quarter of bacon and aromatics
3. When well cooked, season with vinegar and seasonings
4. Give it another boil
5. After it has boiled, add a quarter of grated bread
6. Bind with six eggs
7. Place on a plate greased with butter over slices of bread
8. Top with lemon slices and cinnamon
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Diferentes Modos de Compor os Perus Velhos (Different Ways to Prepare Old Turkeys)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Old turkey
- Bacon (toucinho)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Saffron (salsa)
- Garlic (alho)
- Shallot (echalota)
- Bacon (toucinho)
- Twine (barbante)
- Broth (caldo)
- White wine (vinho branco)
- Roots (raizes)
- Onions studded with cloves (cebolas cravejadas)
- Bundle of fine herbs (capella de hervas finas)

## Directions

1. Old turkeys ordinarily serve for cold meat dishes after being cooked in an adobo sauce.
2. They serve for pies and also for different entrees after being cooked in a braze.
3. To be served cooked in adobo, after the turkey is dead and mortified three or four days, empty it skillfully, tuck the legs inside, and singe it over coals.
4. After being well cleaned, lard it with thick bacon seasoned with salt, pepper, saffron, garlic, and shallot, all finely chopped. Cover it with bacon planks, tie it with twine, and put it to cook in a pot or braising pan of its size, with broth, white wine, roots, onions studded with cloves, a bundle of fine herbs, seasoned with salt and whole pepper.
5. Let it simmer five or six hours. Once cooked, let it cool in the same adobo, and after cooling, remove it and put inside the turkey's body the broth in which it was cooked, which should be jellied, and cover the turkey with pork lard with various decorative patterns, place it on its plate with a folded napkin underneath, and serve it.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Different Ways to Prepare Beef Ribs
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef ribs
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives, minced
- a drop of oil
- breadcrumbs
- shallot sauce

## Directions

1. Once the ribs are tenderized and trimmed well and somewhat thick, season them with salt, pepper, parsley, minced chives, a drop of oil, and dust them with breadcrumbs
2. Grill them and serve with shallot sauce. They can also be cooked in a braize and served with different vegetable ragús. They can also be marinated after cooking in the braize, and once cold with oil or butter, parsley, chives, and shallots. They can also be wrapped in paper with all their marinade and grilled on top of another sheet of paper and served hot in their papillote
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Dobrada de Vacca (Beef Tripe)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tripe
- Water
- Pork belly
- Parsley
- Herbs
- Lemon
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef tripe that is tender and very white, and after scalding it well, place it to cook in plain water with a slice of pork belly, seasoned with salt, a bunch of parsley, fine herbs, and slices of peeled lemon
2. When cooked and almost cold, cut it into equal fillets and serve as white fricassee with egg yolks and lemon juice
3. It can also be served à l'Ancienne by placing the fillets in a little sauce with a piece of washed butter
4. Pies, tarts, and timbales can also be made from it
5. It is eaten well in its natural state after cooking, hot, with a sauce made of broth, vinegar, finely chopped parsley, seasoned with salt and pepper
6. It can also be eaten roasted on grills with piquant sauce after being passed through butter and sprinkled with breadcrumbs; however in whatever way it is made, it is always cut into fillets as said at the beginning
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Dobrada, Pés, e Mãos de Vitela (Tripe, Feet, and Hands of Veal)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Tripe or intestines
- Veal feet and hands
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Broth
- Vinegar
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. The tripe, or intestines, the hands, and the feet of veal, cleaned and blanched in hot water, can be cooked in a white braise, and served natural with salt, pepper, chopped parsley, little broth, and good vinegar
2. The same hands and feet after being cooked, and cut into pieces, are served as a fricassee; they are also served as a marinade and fried
3. Also the tripe after being cut into pieces, and placed in a good flavored gravy sauce, serve
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Dobrada, Pés, e Mãos de Vitella (Tripe, Feet, and Hands of Veal)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Tripe or trips
- Veal feet
- Veal hands
- Salt
- Pepper
- Chopped parsley
- Broth
- Good vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean the tripe, hands, and feet of veal
2. Blanch them in hot water
3. Cook them in a white broth and serve naturally with salt, pepper, chopped parsley, little broth, and good vinegar
4. The same hands and feet, after being cooked and cut into pieces, can be served as fricassee
5. They can also be served as marinade and fried
6. The tripe after being cut into pieces and placed in a good-tasting sauce is served with bread and grated cheese, browned in the oven
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Doces de Cidra, de Limões, Laranjas, e Bergamotas (Preserved Citrus Fruits)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- citron, lemon, orange, or bergamot fruit
- sugar, clarified

## Directions

1. Peel the fruits carefully with delicacy, clean them inside, leaving them whole or cutting into quarters
2. Place in cold water and cook in boiling water until soft and cooked through
3. Place in cold water again, remove from it, and drain
4. Put in clarified sugar and let simmer little by little over mild heat
5. When the sugar is reduced and at the proper point, remove and store
6. Serve for compotes, for covering, and for whatever else is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Doces Feitos com Mel (Sweets Made with Honey)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- honey
- egg

## Directions

1. Place honey in a pot over heat
2. Once it begins to boil, remove all the foam and allow it to cook very well
3. This will be known by removing it from the heat and placing inside it a hen's egg; if this egg goes to the bottom the cooking is imperfect; if the egg stays on top, the cooking is perfect
4. Add any type of fruit, place it over heat, allow it to boil slowly and carefully so it does not burn
5. Honey serves in place of sugar
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Doces Feitos com Mosto (Sweets Made with Must)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- must
- pears, apples, and pomegranates
- water

## Directions

1. Take an almiide of must, or as much as the quantity of sweet that is to be made, put it in a pot or kettle and place it to boil over lively heat
2. Once reduced to two-thirds and with good consistency, it serves in place of sugar for all kinds of fruits such as pears, apples, and pomegranates
3. These fruits, being first half-cooked in water, peeled and cleaned, are placed to boil in the must until they finish cooking
4. The must is reduced, which will be known by placing a drop of syrup on a plate and tilting it to one side; if it does not run, it has the right point
5. Afterward place the fruit in glazed boxes; after it cools, pour the syrup over it, cover it, and keep it in a cool place
6. The must can be made from white or black grapes equally
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Doirada, Pargos, and Other Fish à Créma (Gilt-head Bream, Sea Bream, and Other Fish in Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish, grilled
- Butter
- Bread crumb, thin slices
- Minced fish mixture with more fish
- Bread crumb, soaked in milk
- Salt
- pepper
- Nutmeg, grated
- Salt
- Scallion
- Shallots
- Egg yolks
- Egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. After grilling any of these fish on a griddle, arrange it with all its length on a plafond with butter and thin slices of bread underneath
2. Remove the meat from the upper part, leaving the meat from the lower part with the spine, head, and tail attached
3. From what was removed, make a chopped mixture with some additional piece of cod or any other fish
4. Put in a mortar about the size of a twenty-real piece of bread soaked in milk, and pounding it very well, season with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, salt, scallion, and shallots
5. Bind with seven or eight egg yolks and some beaten egg whites
6. Straighten this chopped mixture on the breast as if it were whole, cover with grated bread crumb, and put to cook in the oven
7. When cooked and with good color, pass carefully to the serving plate, where it should be served with sauce and spicy sauce underneath
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Doiradas and Robalos com Hervas Finas (Gilt-head Bream and Bass with Fine Herbs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish, cleaned
- Olive oil
- Salt
- Shallot
- Garlic, a small piece
- Salt
- pepper
- Fine herbs, pounded
- Butter
- Onion slices
- Bread, finely grated
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and open it on either side, remove the insides entirely, leaving only the head attached to the loins; if they cannot be left whole, they can be cut into fillets without separating them from the head
2. Season and marinate with olive oil, salt, shallot, a small piece of garlic, salt, pepper, all chopped, and pounded fine herbs
3. Line the bottom of a baking dish with butter and some onion slices
4. Straighten the fish on top with its fillets rolled around the head, which should remain in the middle
5. Cover with finely grated bread and put to cook in the oven
6. When cooked, pass to the serving plate carefully and serve with little sauce underneath and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Eiroz de Fricandó para Dieta de Carne (Eel Fricandeau for Meat Diet)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- large eel
- lard with fine bacon
- white wine
- onions
- bay leaves
- veal stock
- ragú of vegetables with béchamel or other sauce, or bound essence

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare a large eel
2. Cut off the head and tail tip
3. Split down the middle
4. Remove the loins
5. Lard with fine bacon
6. Boil briefly in white wine with sliced onions and bay leaves
7. Remove and place over reduced veal stock, almost at glaze point, with the larded side down
8. Cook slowly until golden like fricandeau
9. Serve with ragú of vegetables with béchamel, or any other sauce, or bound essence of good taste, as you prefer
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Eiroz de Fricassé (Eel Fricassee)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- eels or angulas
- water
- butter
- spice
- fine herbs
- studded onion
- flour
- broth
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- milk
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the eels or angulas
2. Remove the spines from the sides
3. Cut into equal pieces
4. Blanch in water
5. Stew in butter with a spice, fine herbs, and a studded onion
6. Dust with flour
7. After stewing, add a spoonful of broth and half a quartilho of white wine
8. Skim, simmer slowly
9. Season with salt and pepper
10. When cooked and sauce reduced, bind with egg yolks beaten in milk
11. Serve hot with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Eiroz de Molho Pardo (Eel in Brown Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- eels
- butter
- flour
- broth
- fish substance or stock
- salt
- pepper
- sauce
- fine herbs
- white wine
- orange juice or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the eels as mentioned
2. Remove the spines from the sides
3. Cut into pieces
4. Pass through butter and colored flour
5. Moisten with broth and fish substance or stock
6. Season with salt, pepper, sauce, and fine herbs
7. Simmer slowly, adding half a quartilho of wine
8. When cooked and sauce reduced and of good taste, serve with orange juice or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Eiroz Enrolada (Rolled Eel)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- large eel
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- sauce
- onion
- shallot
- garlic
- string
- white wine
- fine herbs
- broth
- butter
- ragú of truffles, morels, mushrooms, or other

## Directions

1. Prepare a large eel
2. Cut off the head
3. Open along the belly
4. Remove all the spine
5. Cut in two pieces of equal length
6. Season with salt, pepper, nutmeg, sauce, onion, shallot, and garlic clove, all very finely chopped
7. Make two rolls
8. Tie each with string
9. Poach in white wine with fine herbs and a little broth and a piece of butter
10. When cooked, remove the string
11. Serve with ragú of truffles, morels, mushrooms, or any other that suits
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Eiroz Enrolada Assada nas Grelhas (Rolled Eel Roasted on Grill)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- rolled cooked eels
- melted butter
- bread crumbs
- rémoulade sauce

## Directions

1. Take the rolled eels after cooking
2. Dip in melted butter
3. Cover with bread crumbs
4. Roast on grill
5. Serve with rémoulade sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Eiroz Frita à Basilica (Eel Fried à Basilica)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- eels
- creamed minced mixture
- eggs
- fine bread crumbs
- clarified butter
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. Clean and skin the eels, leaving some unpeeled
2. Cut into equal fillets
3. Place on a small amount of creamed minced mixture, well bound
4. Roll like veal patties
5. Secure with string
6. Poach for six minutes in a mixture with butter, white wine, fine herbs, and salt
7. After poaching, remove and pass twice through beaten eggs and fine bread crumbs
8. Fry in clarified butter
9. When fried and of good color, serve with fried parsley around it
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Eiroz ou Anguias Marinadas e Fritas (Eel or Angulas Marinated and Fried)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- eels
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- onions
- lemon juice
- flour
- clarified butter
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the eels as in preceding recipes
2. Cut into equal pieces
3. Marinate with vinegar, salt, pepper, bay leaf, onions, and lemon juice
4. After marinating two or three hours, drain
5. Flour and fry in clarified butter
6. Serve garnished with fried parsley around it
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Lampreia (Lamprey Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lamprey
- wine
- vinegar
- orange
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- pastry dough

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the lamprey
2. Put it in a shallow pan to drain the blood, striking it five or six times so it can curl
3. Season it with one cup of wine, half a cup of vinegar, orange, salt, pepper, clove, and nutmeg
4. Make a firm pastry box
5. Put the lamprey with sauce inside it
6. Cook it
7. When it begins to boil, remove the sauce through a hidden hole
8. When it finishes cooking, serve the pie and the sauce in a small bowl
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Lebre para Fiambre (Hare Pie for Cold Service)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Hares, boned
- Medium larding bacon, seasoned as customary
- Pastry dough
- Paper sheets buttered
- Chopped bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine spices
- Good butter
- Bacon slices
- Brandy

## Directions

1. Bone the hares and lard all the pieces with medium bacon seasoned as customary
2. Spread the dough on buttered paper sheets about half a finger thick
3. Place the hare pieces on top, arranging them closely, with chopped bacon, seasoned with salt, pepper, fine spices
4. Cover with good butter and bacon slices
5. Spread another piece of dough and place it on top, and finish with customary ornaments
6. Pour two small spoonfuls of brandy through the chimney hole
7. Put in the oven to cook for four or five hours
8. When colored golden, remove from the oven, seal the hole with dough
9. Let cool in a cool place and serve as a cold dish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Lombos de Vitella com Sangue (Veal Loin Pie with Blood)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin pieces
- Bacon
- Ham
- Pork blood or pigeon blood
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Veal, ground
- Truffle
- Garlic
- Butter, washed
- Bacon planks
- Pastry box with lid
- Eggs
- Perdiz sauce
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Lard pieces of veal loin with bacon and ham
2. Marinate them in pork or pigeon blood, seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
3. Make a ground mixture like the previous one, mixing in half a quartilho of blood, a truffle, and a clove of garlic, all chopped
4. Garnish the bottom of the pastry box with half of this mixture
5. Place the marinated veal pieces on top
6. Cover with the rest of the mixture, washed butter, and bacon planks
7. Finish the pie as the previous ones
8. Place in the oven to bake for six hours
9. When cooked, open it, remove the bacon, drain the fat
10. Serve with perdiz sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Lomlos ou Filetes de Linguados (Sole Fillet Pastry)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Minced meat
- Sole fillets, fried
- Mustard
- Shallot
- Truffles
- Cucumbers, minced
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Onion, clove-studded
- Butter
- Dough
- Eggs
- Truffle ragú
- Shrimp tails

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of a pastry box with minced meat
2. Place fried sole fillets on top with mustard, shallot, minced truffles and cucumbers, seasoned with salt and pepper, a bouquet of fine herbs, and a clove-studded onion
3. Cover everything with another little bit of minced meat, a piece of fresh butter, and close the pastry with its dough lid
4. Finish as is customary and put in the oven to cook
5. When cooked, open it, remove the bouquet and onion, drain the fat, and add a well-finished truffle ragú and shrimp tails of good flavor
6. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Láparo (Rabbit Pie)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two young rabbits or hares
- bacon, medium cut
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- fine herbs
- rabbit livers
- scraped bacon
- veal slices
- bacon slices
- broth

## Directions

1. Clean two young rabbits or hares and cut them into quarters
2. Lard them with medium-cut bacon seasoned with salt, pepper, spices, and fine herbs
3. Line the bottom of the pie with a mixture made from the rabbit livers and scraped bacon
4. Place the rabbit quarters on top, covered with the same mixture, then veal slices and bacon slices
5. Close up the pie as is customary with necessary decorations
6. Put it in the oven to cook for two or three hours
7. When cooked, remove the veal and bacon slices
8. Serve with Spanish sauce or with a sauce made from the livers of the rabbits
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Perdigotos (Partridge Pie)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Pastry dough
- Ground meat (picado)
- Young partridges
- Partridge livers
- Bacon, scraped
- Fine herbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fresh truffles, chopped
- Salsa
- Bay leaf
- Marjoram
- Bacon planks

## Directions

1. Make the pastry box of tender dough
2. Garnish the bottom with a little ground meat
3. Place the stuffed partridges on top, stuffed with a mixture made from their own livers, scraped bacon, fine herbs, salt, pepper, fresh chopped truffles, a piece of salsa, bay leaf, and marjoram
4. Cover with scraped bacon and bacon planks
5. Seal with the pastry lid
6. Ornament and gild it
7. Place in the oven to bake for two hours
8. When cooked, open it, remove the planks, onions, salsa, and all fat
9. Serve with perdiz sauce or Spanish sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Peru à Flambée (Flambéed Turkey Pastry)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey with legs folded inside
- Bacon strips
- Ham
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Lemon juice
- Dough
- Bacon, minced
- Fine herbs
- Butter
- Eggs
- Lily flowers

## Directions

1. Prepare the turkey with legs folded inside, break the thighbones
2. Singe it, then lard with thick bacon and ham
3. Season with salt, pepper, spices, and lemon juice
4. Place on dough half a finger thick, with minced bacon on top and bottom, seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
5. Cover with butter and bacon strips
6. Cover with another sheet of dough sealed with eggs and properly folded
7. Decorate with lily flowers, gild it, and cook in the oven for five or six hours
8. When cooked, remove, let cool, and serve cold with a well-folded napkin underneath
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Picado (Ground Meat Pie)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal, ground and seared in bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Culí sauce or reduced milk
- Bacon planks
- Pastry box with lid
- Sauce essence
- Lemon juice
- White sauce
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make a ground mixture of veal pieces seared in bacon, season it
2. Finish it as is customary
3. Mix in a little culí or reduced milk
4. Put everything in the pastry box
5. Cover with bacon planks
6. Place the box lid
7. Put in the oven to bake for two hours
8. When cooked, open it, remove all the fat
9. Serve with essence sauce and lemon juice, or with white sauce made with egg yolks dissolved in milk and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Pombos (Pigeon Pie)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons
- Hot water
- Truffles
- Salsa
- Chives, chopped
- Salt
- Pepper
- Veal, ground (picado)
- Onion studded with cloves
- Fine herbs
- Butter
- Bacon planks
- Pastry box with lid
- Eggs
- Ham sauce
- Veal sauce
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Scald the necessary pigeons in hot water
2. Pluck them, cook them, place the feet inside the body
3. Singe, clean, and remove the entrails
4. Sear them in melted bacon with truffles, salsa, chopped chives, and season with salt and pepper
5. Let them cool
6. Meanwhile, garnish the bottom of the pastry box with ground veal
7. Place the pigeons on top with a studded onion, a piece of salsa, and fine herbs
8. Cover with the same ground mixture, butter, and bacon planks
9. Cover with its lid
10. Finish as the previous pies
11. When cooked, remove the salsa, onion, and planks, drain the fat
12. Serve with ham and veal sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Presunto (Ham Pastry)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham from Lamego or Montange
- Beef strips
- Veal strips
- Bacon strips
- Whole seasonings
- Peppercorns
- Fine herbs
- White wine
- Dough
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare the ham, removing the skin and all bones
2. Soak as long as necessary to remove all salt
3. Cook between strips of beef, veal, and bacon, seasoned with whole seasonings, peppercorns, and fine herbs
4. Add half a bottle of white wine and let it simmer slowly over coals for five or six hours
5. Strain the substance through a sieve
6. When the ham is cold, place it in the pastry with a layer of fat below and all its substance
7. Cover with a dough lid and cook in the oven for two more hours
8. When cooked, open, drain the fat, place on its plate, and serve with Spanish sauce or any other piquant sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Presunto para Fiambre (Ham Pie for Cold Cuts)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- uma massa composta de farinha, agua, ovos, e manteiga (dough made of flour, water, eggs, and butter)
- toucinho picado e temperado (seasoned chopped bacon)
- presunto defóslado (boned ham)
- folhas de louro (bay leaves)
- pranchas de toucinho (bacon slices)

## Directions

1. Make the pie box with dough composed of flour, water, eggs, and butter
2. Line the bottom with plenty of chopped and seasoned bacon
3. Put the boned and unsalted ham inside, cover it with the remaining pounded bacon, bay leaves, and bacon slices
4. After completely covering it with dough and well sealed, put it to bake in the oven for ten or twelve hours
5. Remove it, and after it is cold, serve it on a napkin as a cold cuts dish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Presunto Quente para uma Entrada (Hot Ham Pie for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- presunto (ham)
- massa grossa (thick dough)
- toucinho picado (chopped bacon)
- pimenta (pepper)
- salsa (parsley)
- especias (spices)
- hervas finas (fine herbs)
- pranchas de toucinho (bacon slices)
- molho à Hespanhola or molho picante (Spanish sauce or spicy sauce)

## Directions

1. Take a good ham, remove all the skin and bones from the middle, soak it for two days
2. Then make a thick dough and make the pie box
3. Put plenty of chopped and pounded bacon with a little pepper, parsley, some spices, and fine herbs in the bottom
4. Place the ham on top, cover it as below, and with some slices of bacon
5. After covering it completely with the dough, put it to bake in the oven for seven or eight hours
6. When cooked, open it, drain the fat, and serve it with Spanish sauce or spicy sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Solho para uma Entrada (Flounder Pastry for a First Course)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Flounder fillets made from two pieces
- Anchovy fillets
- Eel fillets
- Good washed butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Fine herbs
- Dough
- Eggs
- Shrimp consomé

## Directions

1. Make two pieces of flounder from eel and anchovy fillets
2. Line the bottom of the pastry with good washed butter, season with salt, pepper, spices, and fine herbs all well minced
3. Arrange the flounder pieces on top
4. Place on top the same seasoning that was placed below
5. Cover with butter, close the pastry with its lid, decorate and gild
6. Cook in the oven
7. When cooked, drain the fat and serve with piquant shrimp consomé or other suitable sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Tordos (Thrush Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- six thrushes
- one half arratel of bacon chopped
- all seasonings
- lemon juice
- one eighth of flour
- one half arratel of butter
- four eggs

## Directions

1. Sauté six thrushes in one half arratel of chopped bacon; when half-cooked, season with all the seasonings, and let cool
2. Knead one eighth of flour with one half arratel of butter and four eggs, make a box in which place the thrushes with the broth from the sautéing, seasoning well with lemon
3. When baked, serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empada de Vitella de Fiambre (Cold Veal Pie)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin, choice pieces
- Thick bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Fine herbs
- Lemon juice
- White wine, small amount
- Salsa
- Pastry dough
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Lard the best meat pieces from a veal leg with thick bacon
2. Marinate for three or four hours with salt, pepper, spices, fine herbs, juice of two lemons, and a little white wine
3. Meanwhile, chop two arráteis of bacon with salsa, and after grinding and seasoning, spread half of the dough
4. Place half the bacon on top of this half of dough, and place the arranged veal pieces on top, well fitted together
5. Cover with the rest of the bacon, and on top place planks of the same
6. Place the pastry lid, ornament it in the best possible way
7. Place in the oven to bake for four or five hours
8. After cooking, let it cool
9. Serve it cold with a napkin underneath
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empada Quente de Lombos de Lebre (Hot Hare Loin Pie)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare loins
- Butter or melted bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sausage
- Shallots
- Truffles, all chopped
- Bacon, pounded with hare liver
- Fine herbs
- Whole onion studded with cloves
- Bay leaf
- Ham essence
- Bitter orange juice

## Directions

1. Pass hare loins through fire in a pan with butter or melted bacon, salt, pepper, sausage, shallots, and truffles, all chopped, and let cool
2. Line the bottom of the pie with bacon pounded with hare liver, seasoned with fine herbs
3. Place the loins on top with a whole onion studded with cloves and a bay leaf
4. Cover with the remaining chopped mixture and some bacon slices
5. Finish the pie according to custom
6. When cooked, open it to drain the fat
7. Add a sauce of ham essence with bitter orange juice
8. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Angulas para Entradas (Eel Pastries for First Courses)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Eels, cut into pieces and deboned
- Soft dough
- Minced meat
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Mustard
- Onion
- Truffles
- Cucumbers, minced
- Fine herbs
- Fresh butter
- Eggs
- Truffle ragú

## Directions

1. Cut the eels into pieces without bones and make a box of soft dough
2. Line the bottom with good minced meat
3. Place the eel pieces on top, season with salt, pepper, spices, mustard, onion, minced truffles and cucumbers, and a bouquet of fine herbs
4. Add fresh butter on top, place its soft dough lid with its decorations
5. Gild with beaten eggs as is customary and cook in the oven for two hours
6. When cooked, open it, remove the bouquet, drain all the fat, and serve with truffle ragú or any other ragú or suitable sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Atum (Tuna Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two portions of fresh tuna
- wine
- vinegar
- garlic
- a small amount of the pickling liquid
- a splash of olive oil
- black seasonings (clove, cinnamon, and pepper)
- lemon juice
- one ounce of flour

## Directions

1. After the two portions of fresh tuna have been in a preserve of wine, vinegar, and garlic for two hours, place them to roast in the oven in a frying pan with a small amount of the pickling liquid in which they were kept and a splash of olive oil
2. When half-cooked, season with all the black seasonings and lemon juice
3. Make a pastry crust of one ounce of flour, placing the tuna with its sauce inside
4. Put it to cook and send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Atum (Tuna Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- two fresh tuna steaks
- wine
- vinegar
- garlic
- olive oil
- black seasonings
- lemon juice
- flour

## Directions

1. After two fresh tuna steaks have been in a preserve of wine, vinegar, and garlic for two hours, roast them in the oven in a pan with a small amount of the broth they were in and a splash of olive oil
2. When half cooked, season with all black seasonings and lemon juice
3. Make a pastry box from one eighth of flour
4. Put the tuna with the sauce inside it
5. Cook it and serve it
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Carneiro (Mutton Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of mutton from the leg without bones
- bacon for larding
- bacon chopped
- all seasonings
- wine
- vinegar
- two dried bay leaves
- one eighth of flour
- one half arratel of butter
- four eggs

## Directions

1. Sauté in chopped bacon two arratels of mutton from the leg without bones, larded with bacon lardons
2. When half-cooked, season with all the seasonings, a touch of wine, a touch of vinegar, and two dried bay leaves, and after seasoning without finishing cooking, let cool
3. Knead one eighth of flour with one half arratel of butter and four eggs, make a box, in which place the mutton with broth
4. When the pie is made and baked, serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Galinhas (Chicken Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole chicken
- one half arratel of bacon
- all seasonings
- one eighth of flour
- one half arratel of butter
- three eggs

## Directions

1. Sauté a whole chicken in one half arratel of bacon; when sautéed, giving it a few strikes at the joints, season with all the seasonings, and let cool
2. Knead one eighth of flour with one half arratel of butter and three eggs, make a box in which the chicken fits with broth
3. When baked, serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Galinhollas (Guinea Hen Pie)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Guinea hens
- Bacon, medium thickness
- Guinea hen tripe
- Guinea hen livers
- Scraped bacon
- Truffles
- Fine herbs
- Pastry box with lid
- Fresh butter
- Bacon planks
- Eggs
- Orange juice, sour

## Directions

1. Pluck, prepare, and singe the guinea hens
2. Lard them with medium bacon
3. Make a ground mixture from the guinea hens' own tripe, livers, scraped bacon, truffles, and fine herbs
4. Stuff the guinea hens with this mixture
5. Garnish the bottom of the pastry box with chopped bacon and a little salsa
