6. Place the guinea hens inside, season them
7. Cover with fresh butter and bacon planks
8. Cover the box with its lid
9. Seal with eggs, ornament, and gild it
10. Place in the oven to bake for four hours
11. When cooked, open it, drain the fat
12. Serve with sour orange juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Javali (Wild Boar Pastry Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- One wild boar leg
- Water
- Salt
- Wine
- Sage
- Marjoram
- Fatback lard, salted and peppered
- Half alqueire of rye or wheat flour
- Pork butter
- Thyme
- Marjoram

## Directions

1. Cook one wild boar leg in water and salt
2. Then cook it in wine with sage and marjoram
3. When cooked, lard it with salted and peppered fatback lard very well
4. Knead half alqueire of rye or wheat flour with pork butter and make a large cake that fits the entire leg
5. Add thyme and marjoram, seal the pastry pie, place it in the oven to cook slowly, and send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Linguas de Carneiro (Lamb Tongue Pie)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb tongues
- Ground mixture made with blood
- Pepper
- Salsa
- Chives, chopped
- Shallots, chopped
- Butter
- Bacon planks
- Pastry box with lid
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Scald and peel the tongues
2. Place them to cook in a braise
3. When cooked, garnish the bottom of a pastry box with a ground mixture made with blood as described earlier
4. Place the tongues on top, split in half, seasoned with pepper, salsa, chopped chives, and shallots, all well chopped
5. Cover with ground mixture as below, a piece of butter, and bacon planks
6. Cover with its pastry lid sealed with eggs and gilded
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Linguas de Carneiro (Mutton Tongue Pastries)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton tongues
- Broth
- Minced meat made with blood
- Pepper
- Salt
- Shallots
- Onion
- Lard
- Butter
- Bacon strips
- Dough
- Eggs
- Caper sauce
- Anchovies

## Directions

1. Scald and peel the tongues, then cook them in broth
2. Line the bottom of a pastry box with minced meat made with blood
3. Place the tongues, split in half, on top, seasoned with pepper, salt, finely minced shallots and onion
4. Cover with minced meat as below, a piece of butter, and strips of bacon
5. Cover with a dough lid sealed with eggs and gilded
6. Cook for two hours in the oven
7. When cooked, open it, remove the bacon strips, drain the fat, and serve with caper sauce, anchovies, or other sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Lombo de Vaca (Beef Loin Pastry Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef loin, partially roasted and sliced, larded with salted and peppered fatback
- One eighth measure of flour
- Half arratel of butter
- Four eggs
- Half arratel of chopped fatback
- Pepper
- Clove
- Lemon juice
- Four dried bay leaves

## Directions

1. After the beef loin has been partially roasted, cut it into slices and lard it with salted and peppered fatback
2. Make a pastry crust from one eighth measure of flour kneaded with half arratel of butter and four eggs
3. Place the loin inside with half arratel of chopped fatback, pepper, clove, lemon juice, and four dried bay leaves
4. Cook the pastry pie and send it to the table when done
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Peixe para Dias de Carne e de Jejum (Fish Pastries for Meat Days and Fasting Days)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish, cut into pieces three fingers thick
- Tuna or needlefish if thicker
- Bacon strips
- Ham
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Fine herbs
- Butter
- Dough
- Eggs
- Clear broth

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into pieces three fingers thick, or thicker if tuna or needlefish
2. Pass them through boiling water until they are firm, then lard with seasoned thick bacon and ham
3. Line the bottom of the pastry box with a layer of minced bacon, seasoned with salt, pepper, spices, and fine herbs
4. Place the fish inside, seasoned on top as it was seasoned below
5. Cover with minced bacon, a piece of butter, and bacon strips
6. Place its dough lid, decorate it, gild it, and put in the oven to cook
7. When half-cooked, add a quarter of clear broth with the spoon and finish cooking
8. When cooked, let cool and serve cold as an appetizer
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Perdizes (Partridge Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- two partridges
- bacon lardons
- one half arratel of bacon
- pepper
- salt
- garlic cloves
- one half arratel of butter
- flour
- two bay leaves

## Directions

1. After soaking two partridges in water, lard them very well with bacon lardons, and what remains of one half arratel, after removing the lardons, very well seasoned with salt and pepper, chop with some garlic cloves
2. Make the dough with one half arratel of butter, making one large ball, place the partridges in it with chopped bacon below and above, and two bay leaves
3. Close the pie with thick dough so that when it bakes it does not burst, and when baked, serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Peru sem Osso (Boneless Turkey Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- one turkey without bones
- bacon lardons
- salt
- pepper
- one quarter of flour

## Directions

1. Make a turkey without bones in the same manner as chicken without bones by larding them very well with seasoned bacon lardons
2. Knead one quarter of flour, making the dough quite tender, place the turkey in it, seasoning it on the outside, close the pie very well
3. Place the turkey's feet in one pot and the head with beak in another, so that it appears alive, and after the pie is baked on gentle heat, serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Pés de Porco (Pork Feet Pastry Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork feet, well cooked
- Sugar (optional, for sweet preparation)
- Spices

## Directions

1. Cook the pork feet very well, remove the bones
2. If sweet is desired, pass them through sugar at the right consistency
3. Season with spices and after cooling, place each foot in its own pastry pie, divided in half, and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Salmonetes (Baked Pastry Cases with Small Red Mullet)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Small red mullet
- Livers
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sauce
- Truffles
- Tarragon
- Fine herbs
- Nutmeg, grated
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the small red mullet, removing heads and tails; set aside the livers
2. Take some of the livers and make a forcemeat with butter, salt, pepper, sauce, truffles, tarragon, fine herbs, grated nutmeg, and bind with two egg yolks
3. Make a pastry case according to custom
4. Line the bottom with fresh butter and a little forcemeat
5. Arrange the small red mullet inside, placing some forcemeat between each one
6. Season lightly with salt and pepper
7. Cover with the remaining forcemeat and washed butter
8. Close the pastry case with its pastry lid
9. Finish and gild it, then place in the oven to bake
10. When cooked, remove the lid, drain the fat, and serve with a piquant coulis made from the remaining livers and roe
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Salmonetes (Red Mullet Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- red mullets
- olive oil
- vinegar
- seasonings, ground
- pepper

## Directions

1. Soak the red mullets in a sauce of olive oil and vinegar with all the seasonings ground, for the space of two hours
2. While this happens, work the dough and make the pastries the size of the red mullets with pepper underneath and on top
3. After cooked, send them to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Solho (Solho Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fish steak of solho weighing two arratels
- one quartilho of wine
- one quartilho of vinegar
- one quartilho of water
- one head of garlic, pounded
- a bunch of oregano
- two bay leaves
- salt
- pepper
- olive oil
- lemon juice
- butter or olive oil
- pastry dough

## Directions

1. Cook one fish steak of solho weighing two arratels with one quartilho of wine, one quartilho of vinegar, one quartilho of water, one pounded head of garlic, a bunch of oregano, two bay leaves, salt, and pepper
2. When half cooked, add a splash of olive oil
3. When cooked, let it cool in a pan in which you put a small amount of the broth in which it was cooked, seasoned with all seasonings, lemon juice, and a good splash of olive oil or butter
4. Make a pastry box of one eighth of a loaf
5. Put the molho inside it with the fish
6. Cook it over low heat and serve it
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas de Vitela (Veal Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal cut into pieces
- bacon lardons
- parsley chopped
- pepper
- salt
- vinegar
- bay leaves
- lemon juice
- garlic
- flour
- butter
- eggs
- clove

## Directions

1. Cut the veal into pieces, each piece larded with small pieces of bacon, seasoned as follows: chopped parsley, pepper, salt, a touch of vinegar, and place in a conserve of wine and garlic, or vinegar of garlic and bay leaves, for one or two days
2. Take some bacon and chop it with parsley, lemon juice with all the seasonings, pepper, and clove
3. Make a dough of good flour, kneading it with eggs, cow butter, and salt; the butter should be washed first
4. In the pie box place small pieces of bacon chopped with parsley and lemon, separately at the bottom, and above them the meat, and above the meat another layer of small pieces of bacon like those below
5. Cover with the top of the pie and bake
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas Francesas (French Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of bacon chopped
- one head of garlic
- one chicken whole
- two pigeons whole
- four arratels of loin in slices
- all seasonings
- two partridges whole
- one rabbit in pieces
- one quarter of flour
- water
- salt
- one arratel of cow butter

## Directions

1. Melt two arratels of chopped bacon with one head of garlic, soak in it one whole chicken and two whole pigeons, all whole, and four arratels of loin in slices; season with all the seasonings, and place in a frying pan to cool
2. Sauté in that same bacon two whole partridges and one rabbit in pieces, and let cool
3. Knead one quarter of flour in water and salt, and knead very well, make French puff pastry with one arratel of cow butter, divide the dough into two crusts
4. On one place all the meat in layers with plenty of pepper and lemon, cover with the other, decorating the pie around as desired, bake it, and when half-baked, opening a hole on top, pour through it the broth that remained from the sautéing
5. Finish baking and serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas Inglesas (English Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of mutton chopped and seasoned
- vinegar
- all seasonings chopped
- one cooked cow tongue
- one arratel of ham
- one quarter of bacon
- lemon juice
- one quarter of flour
- one arratel of butter
- one half arratel of sugar
- six eggs
- one cup of white wine

## Directions

1. Sauté two arratels of mutton very well chopped and seasoned with vinegar and all the seasonings chopped
2. Cook one cow tongue, one arratel of ham in a pot, sautéed in one quarter of bacon, seasoned with all the seasonings and plenty of lemon, let all cool
3. Knead one quarter of flour with one arratel of butter, one half arratel of sugar, six eggs, and one cup of white wine, when very well kneaded, make two crusts
4. On one place the chopped meat, above it the pigeons, then the tongue, and above the tongue the ham, and perfect the pie by covering with another crust, making a border around it, bake it
5. When baked, brush with beaten egg, cover with powdered sugar, let brown, and serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas Romanas (Roman Pastry Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half alqueire of flour
- Water
- Salt
- Two or three dozen live birds

## Directions

1. Make a large pastry from half alqueire of flour kneaded in water and salt, covering it like a pastry pie
2. When fried, blow air into it through a small hole so that it becomes well filled with air
3. After it is very well cooked, open a round hole in the bottom and insert two or three dozen live birds into it
4. Seal the hole with the same dough and send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empadas Salchichadas (Spiced Meat Pastry Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four arratels of lamb from the leg, very well cleaned
- Half arratel of fatback
- Salt
- Pepper
- Clove
- Nutmeg
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Quarter measure of flour
- One arratel of pork butter
- Fatback lard, salted and peppered
- Wine for basting
- Bay leaves

## Directions

1. Chop four arratels of very clean lamb from the leg together with half arratel of fatback very well
2. After chopping, add salt, pepper, clove, nutmeg, a splash of wine, and another of vinegar
3. Transfer this meat to the pastry board and make a well-stretched cake from it, covering the top with salted and peppered fatback lard, and sprinkling it very well with wine
4. Fold it like a rolled pastry so that the fatback lard remains inside
5. Knead a quarter measure of flour with one arratel of pork butter, well worked, and make a large cake from it, placing the meat with bay leaves on top
6. Seal the pastry pie in the shape of a half-moon and cook it very slowly
7. This pastry pie is eaten cold and lasts all week on the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Empanadilhas de Grãos (Grain-Filled Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of sugar
- half arratel of finely ground almonds
- grains as needed
- butter or oil for frying
- pastry dough made with butter and sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take one arratel of sugar and half arratel of finely ground almonds and grains as seem necessary
2. Strain and filter the sugar, bring to spun sugar point, remove from heat
3. Add a handful of almonds, then grains, all broken up until all grains and almonds are added
4. The mixture should be thick like fartes
5. Mix all and remove the pan from heat
6. Spread on a plate to cool
7. Take pastry dough made with butter and sugar, knead and stretch, fill with the mixture like making fartes
8. Fry in butter or oil if desired and pass through sugar at point
9. Or bake in oven and pass through sugar at point and cover with cinnamon
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Entrida (Entrida Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens
- two guinea hens
- two partridges
- rabbit
- two arratels of pork rib meat
- water
- vinegar
- seasonings
- broth
- sugar
- butter
- eggs
- lemon juice
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook in a pot two chickens, two guinea hens, two partridges, a rabbit, and two arratels of pork rib meat with water as for lamb
2. Season with vinegar and all the seasonings
3. When cooked, remove the broth and dissolve three or four sugar balls and butter in it, then bring to a boil
4. When thick, finish cooking with eight eggs and juice of four lemons
5. When well reduced, place on a plate with cinnamon on top and the meat on this entrida, then serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Ervilhas à Crema (Peas with Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- tender peas
- melted butter
- flour
- hot water
- salt
- parsley bundle
- small onion
- cloves
- sugar
- egg yolks
- cream

## Directions

1. Take very tender peas and pass them through melted butter dusted with flour
2. Add then a cup of hot water, season with salt, a bundle of parsley, a small onion, and two cloves, and leave to simmer little by little
3. When cooked, add a small piece of sugar, bind with egg yolks mixed with cream, and serve for an entre-mets course
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ervilhas à Flamenga (Peas Flemish Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- water
- salt
- sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Put the peas in boiling water with salt and a piece of sugar
2. When cooked, drain them through a sieve, pass them to a casserole with butter, give them two turns on the heat, and serve for an entre-mets
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ervilhas à Jardineira (Peas Gardener Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- butter
- lettuce hearts
- parsley bundle
- small onion
- cloves
- salt
- sugar
- egg yolks
- milk

## Directions

1. Put the peas in a casserole with butter, lettuce hearts, a bundle of parsley, small onion, two cloves of India, and over gentle heat, leave to sweat and cook little by little
2. When cooked, season with salt, a piece of sugar, and bind with egg yolks mixed in milk, and serve like the others for an entre-mets
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Escabeche (Pickled Fish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- water
- olive oil
- salt
- bay leaves
- lemon juice
- lime juice
- ginger, pounded
- black spices

## Directions

1. Boil a little vinegar diluted with water in a pan
2. Add olive oil, salt, and bay leaves
3. Add lemon juice, lime juice, and pounded ginger with the other black spices
4. When boiling, adjust seasonings by tasting to ensure the escabeche is not too strong
5. Adjust the quantity according to the amount of fish it will serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Espargos com Manteiga (Asparagus with Butter)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Flour
- Hot water
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Nutmeg, grated

## Directions

1. Trim the asparagus and cook whole in water and salt
2. After cooking, strain them
3. Arrange neatly on a plate, pointing the tips toward the center
4. Make a sauce with butter dusted with flour, a drop of water, pepper, vinegar, and grated nutmeg
5. Place on heat, stirring constantly until it binds
6. Pour over the asparagus and serve for a course between meals
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Espichas de Linguas de Carneiro (Sheep Tongue Skewers)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep tongues
- water
- salt
- butter
- chives
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- broth
- egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Cook the tongues in plain water with little salt or in broth
2. Cut them into equal dice and place in a casserole
3. Sauté them in melted butter with chopped chives and salt, seasoned with salt and pepper
4. Once sautéed, sprinkle with flour and a few drops of broth
5. Once cooked and the sauce is thick and tasty, bind with some egg yolks
6. When cold, thread the pieces onto skewers, ensuring all the sauce clings to them
7. Cover with breadcrumbs and roast on the grill, basting from time to time with melted butter
8. When roasted, serve them dry or with a little broth, chopped shallot, salt, pepper, and lime juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Esquecidos (Forgotten Ones)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs (two with whites and yolks, six with yolks only)
- one arratel of powdered sugar
- one arratel of flour

## Directions

1. Put eight eggs (two complete, six yolks only) on top of one arratel of powdered sugar in a pan or small basin
2. Beat everything very well until white and thick
3. Add one arratel of flour gradually, always stirring without stopping
4. Take in small pieces rolling in the hand, wrapping in something with flour so they do not stick
5. Place in basins that are greased with butter and send to the oven
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Farinha torrada para estallecido (Toasted Flour for Sustenance)
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of sifted flour
- two and a half arratels of sugar
- ten reis of powdered sandalwood
- ten reis of flower water

## Directions

1. Mix one arratel of sifted flour, two and a half arratels of sugar, ten reis of powdered sandalwood, and ten reis of flower water
2. Put everything mixed in a new pan, sealed with dough, sent to the oven overnight
3. Then take a small amount and soak it in water as necessary for a bowl of broth, and in it place an egg yolk, it is very good, taken in the morning
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Fartes de Especies (Spiced Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight arratels of sugar at low thread point
- four arratels of finely ground almonds
- one arratel of citron in small thin pieces
- clove
- cinnamon
- a little fennel
- one quarter of grated bread by measure
- pastry dough made with butter and sugar

## Directions

1. Put eight arratels of sugar at low thread point in a pan
2. Add four arratels of finely ground almonds and one arratel of citron in small thin pieces
3. Add clove, cinnamon, and a little fennel and boil briefly
4. Remove pan from heat and add one quarter of grated bread by measure
5. The sign that this mixture is done is to drop some grated bread on top and place a finger to see if it stays dry
6. Let cool on a plate
7. Take pastry dough made with butter and sugar and shape the fartes
8. When made, send to the oven
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Fatias com Peitos de Capão (Slices with Capon Breasts)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped capon or partridge breasts, roasted
- egg yolks
- bread crumb cooked with cream
- salt
- sugar
- lemon zest, grated

## Directions

1. Take a chopped mixture of roasted capon or partridge breasts, pound it and bind it with egg yolks and bread crumb cooked with cream
2. Season with salt, sugar, and grated lemon zest
3. Place on cooked slices, glazed as the previous recipe
4. Serve the same way
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fatias com Peixe (Slices with Fish)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fish fillets such as mullet, cherne, bass, or cod
- salt
- mustard
- lemon zest
- fine sugar
- sweet and bitter almond biscuits
- butter
- egg yolks
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Leave on a table fish fillets such as mullet, cherne, bass, or cod
2. Pound in a stone mortar and season with salt, mustard, lemon zest, fine sugar, and some sweet and bitter almond biscuits, all pounded and made into a paste
3. Add a piece of butter bound with egg yolks and a piece of bread crumb soaked in milk
4. Cut bread into thin slices, place in a buttered baking dish
5. Garnish the slices with the chopped mixture
6. Bake in the oven
7. When cooked, glaze with fine sugar and hot spatula, and serve as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fatias com Presunto (Slices with Ham)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumb slices, two or three fingers wide and long
- ham slices, not too fatty
- rendered bacon fat
- broth
- vinegar
- bay leaf
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cut slices of bread crumb two or three fingers wide and long
2. Cut equal slices of ham that is not too fatty
3. Fry in a casserole with rendered bacon fat
4. When browned on both sides, place each piece on its bread slice
5. Deglaze the casserole with a little broth, add a splash of vinegar and a bay leaf
6. Pour over the slices with coarse pepper and serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fatias com Rim de Vitela (Slices with Veal Kidney)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidney, roasted with all its fat
- lemon zest, grated
- four egg yolks
- egg whites, beaten
- powdered sugar
- slices of bread crumb, two fingers wide
- butter

## Directions

1. Take a roasted veal kidney with all its fat, pound it in a mortar with a little grated lemon zest, four egg yolks and their beaten whites, and powdered sugar
2. Cut slices of bread crumb two fingers wide and place them in a buttered baking dish
3. Garnish the slices with the pounded kidney mixture
4. Bake in the oven
5. When cooked, cover with fine sugar, glaze with a hot spatula, and serve hot as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fatias com Salpicão (Slices with Hash)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted capon or partridge breast, cut in small dice
- truffles
- sweetbreads
- egg yolks
- bread slices
- eggs
- fine breadcrumb

## Directions

1. Cut into small dice a roasted capon or partridge breast, truffles, and sweetbreads, and make a ragú of good flavor and well-bound
2. Let cool
3. Add two or three egg yolks
4. Place on bread slices, spread with a knife blade moistened in eggs
5. Cover with fine breadcrumb
6. Brown in the oven and serve with clear sauce on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fatias com Toucinho (Slices with Bacon)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- long bread
- bacon interlarded with meat
- beaten eggs
- vinegar
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the two ends from a long bread
2. Lard with bacon interlarded with meat
3. Cut into slices the thickness of a finger, without crust
4. Pass through beaten eggs
5. Fry on low heat, little by little
6. When golden-brown and fried, serve with clear sauce, a splash of vinegar, and coarse pepper
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fatias com Tutano de Vaca (Slices with Beef Marrow)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumb slices
- franchipane cream with marrow
- egg whites
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Toast or roast slices of bread crumb
2. Garnish with franchipane cream with marrow
3. Cover with beaten egg whites mixed with fine sugar
4. Bake in the oven
5. When cooked, glaze with sugar and hot spatula, and serve as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fatias de Ovos (Egg Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen eggs
- twelve ounces of flour
- one arratel of very finely ground almonds
- one arratel of sifted stone sugar
- one spoon of cow butter
- flower water mixed with rose water

## Directions

1. Knead one dozen eggs with twelve ounces of flour, one arratel of very finely ground almonds, one arratel of sifted stone sugar, one spoon of cow butter, and flower water mixed with rose water
2. Once all is very well kneaded, place some flour in a basin and set wafers on top sprinkled with flower water
3. On top of these, pour the thin paste and bake in the oven
4. Once baked, cut the slices and turn them, placing to bake on the other side on other wafers
5. Once cooked, serve at table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Fatias Ofilhadas à Portuguesa (Portuguese-style Fried Slices)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumb, one or two large slices
- beaten eggs
- clarified beef butter
- powdered sugar
- cinnamon, pounded

## Directions

1. Cut into slices the bread crumb of one or two large pieces of bread
2. Pass through beaten eggs
3. Fry in clarified beef butter
4. Pass a second time through eggs and fry again
5. When well-colored, trim and drain
6. Arrange on a plate and serve with powdered sugar on top, dusted with pounded cinnamon
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fatias à Dentinha (Dentinha Slices)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- anchovy fillets
- bread crumb
- rendered bacon fat
- good oil
- coarse pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Use the bread crumb of one whole bread cut afterwards into ordinary slices
2. Fry little by little in rendered bacon fat
3. When fried and well-colored, serve with good oil, coarse pepper, and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fatias à Pempadur (Pampadura-Style Sliced Bread)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumb
- Madeira white wine or other white wine
- eggs
- Rhine wine
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the bread crumb into round or square pieces two or three fingers thick
2. Soak briefly in white wine from Madeira or any other white wine
3. After draining, pass through beaten eggs
4. Fry and repeat the same two or three times as described above
5. After frying and achieving good color, serve with Rhine wine and sugar sauce at point
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fatias à Profissiana (Professional-Style Sliced Bread)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumb
- spirits
- eggs
- clarified butter
- sugar
- white wine
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut the crumb into slices two fingers thick
2. When cut into rounds the size of an apple
3. Soak in spirits and drain
4. Pass through eggs beaten with only half the whites
5. Fry in hot clarified butter, frying little by little
6. When half fried, pass through eggs again
7. Fry and repeat the same until well covered and browned
8. Remove to a strainer, let drain, and serve with sugar and white wine sauce at point, and powdered cinnamon on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fatias à Provençal (Provençal Slices)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- oil, hot
- anchovy fillets
- olive oil
- coarse pepper
- sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Remove the crusts from bread and cut into equal slices
2. Fry in hot oil
3. When golden, drain in a sieve
4. Once drained, arrange anchovy fillets on top
5. Serve with oil, coarse pepper, and sour orange juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Favas de Fricassé (Fava Beans Fricassee)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fava beans
- butter
- parsley bundle
- savory
- small onion
- cloves
- flour
- hot water
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- milk
- parsley
- nutmeg
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Trim and clean the fava beans, stir-fry them in butter with a bundle of parsley and savory, a small onion with two cloves, and dusted with flour
2. Then add a drop of hot water, season with salt and pepper, and when cooked with little sauce, bind with egg yolks mixed in milk, chopped parsley, nutmeg, and a dash of vinegar
3. Being of good taste, serve for an entre-mets
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Favas à Portuguesa (Fava Beans Portuguese Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fava beans
- lettuce hearts
- water
- olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- small onion
- coriander

## Directions

1. Cook the fava beans with some lettuce hearts in plain water, drain them through a sieve, put them in a casserole with olive oil, season with salt, pepper, small onion, and chopped coriander, and stir-fry little by little
2. After stir-frying, add a drop of hot water and leave to simmer little by little
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Favas à Portuguesa (Portuguese-Style Broad Beans)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Broad beans
- Lettuce eyes
- Water
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Chives
- Coriander, chopped
- Hot water
- Sugar
- Eggs, fried

## Directions

1. Cook the broad beans with lettuce eyes in plain water
2. Strain through a sieve
3. Place in a casserole with oil, seasoned with salt, pepper, chopped chives, and chopped coriander
4. Sauté slowly
5. After sautéing, add a drop of hot water and let simmer slowly
6. When cooked and with little sauce, place on a plate with sugar and fried eggs on top, and serve as desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Favas à Provençal (Fava Beans Provençal)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fava beans
- olive oil
- artichokes
- salt
- pepper
- parsley bundle
- savory
- onion
- flour
- egg yolks
- vinegar
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take very tender fava beans, stir-fry them in olive oil with half a dozen also tender artichokes cut into quarters, seasoned with salt, pepper, a bundle of parsley and savory, and an onion, dusted with flour, and leave them to cook in their own substance
2. When cooked, bind them with egg yolks mixed in vinegar with grated nutmeg
3. After binding and with good taste, serve for an entre-mets
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Feijões Verdes de Fricassé (Green Beans Fricassee)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- water
- butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- egg yolks
- milk
- parsley
- garlic
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the beans in plain water, then stir-fry them with butter dusted with flour and seasoned with salt and pepper
2. Add then a spoon of broth, give them a boil, and bind with egg yolks mixed in milk with a little chopped parsley, a touch of garlic, and a dash of vinegar
3. Having good taste, serve for an entre-mets
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Feijões Verdes à Provençal (Green Beans Provençal)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- garlic
- olive oil
- green beans
- water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Make two bundles of parsley and a clove of garlic and dissolve everything in olive oil
2. Dissolve it, pour it over the beans already cooked in water
3. Season with ground salt and pepper, mix and stir everything, and serve as you see fit
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Figella Morisca (Moorish Fowl)
body:
## Ingredients

- six pigeons, halved
- six young chickens, halved
- two hens, in quarters
- bacon
- slices of ham
- a dozen egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take six pigeons and six young chickens in halves, and two hens in quarters, and after everything is half-roasted in bacon, place it in a frying pan, between half and half, place slices of ham, and fill to the top
2. When it is full, add a dozen egg yolks, and brown it in the frying pan and send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Filetes de Linguados de Fricandó, para Dia de Carne (Flounder Fillets Fricandeau Style, for Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- flounder loins
- fine bacon
- white wine
- water
- salt
- fine herbs
- reduced broth
- essence sauce
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Lard the flounder loins with fine bacon
2. Soak them in white wine, water, salt, and fine herbs
3. After being soaked, pour reduced broth into another casserole
4. Put the fillets inside with the bacon facing down
5. Let simmer slowly
6. When the broth is at the glaze point and sticking to the bacon with good color, serve with essence sauce and lime juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Filetes de Linguados de Fricandô (Sole Fillets Fricandeau Style), for Meat Day
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- sole loins
- fine bacon
- white wine
- water
- salt
- fine herbs
- reduced broth

## Directions

1. Lard the sole loins with fine bacon
2. Cook in white wine, water, salt, and fine herbs
3. After cooking, pour reduced broth into another pan
4. Place the fillets with the bacon underneath
5. Let simmer gently
6. When the broth reaches a glaze consistency and clings to the bacon with good color, serve with essence sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Filetes de Linguados em Caixa (Flounder Fillets in Box)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- flounders
- clarified butter or fine oil
- salt
- pepper
- sauce
- shallot
- garlic clove
- bay leaf leaves
- lime juice
- buttered paper
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Scale, clean, and prepare the flounders
2. Cut off the heads and tails
3. Remove the four fillets
4. Marinate them in a little clarified butter or fine oil, seasoned with salt, pepper, sauce, shallot, a clove of garlic, all chopped, bay leaf leaves, and lime juice
5. Make little boxes of paper buttered inside
6. Put the fillets inside without pressing, with the same marinade
7. Cover with breadcrumbs
8. Brush very well with butter
9. Place in the oven to cook
10. When cooked, serve with lime juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Flor de Laranja de Conserva (Preserved Orange Blossoms)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of orange blossoms, white and fresh
- juice of half a lemon
- one arratel of sugar at the point of thread

## Directions

1. Place the four ounces of orange blossoms on a table
2. Cut them into four pieces and moisten with the juice of half a lemon
3. Place in one arratel of sugar at the thread point, already on the heat
4. Stir constantly with a spoon and remove from heat immediately
5. Continue stirring until the sugar turns white around the edge of the pan
6. When this state is reached, pour into paper boxes about two or three fingers high
7. Support with a brick on each side and store in the boxes for whatever is needed
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Florada (Candied Orange Blossoms)
body:
## Ingredients

- orange blossoms or blossoms of sweet or china orange (the largest and best)
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place in a bowl with cold water the large and best orange blossoms or blossoms of sweet or china orange
2. Wash them very well so as not to break them
3. Place them in other water in another bowl
4. Bring them to boil briefly in a pot that should already be boiling
5. After boiling place them in cold water with sugar, passing them two or three more times through other waters
6. When sweet so it is not bitter, place them to drain on a sieve
7. After being dry and very well squeezed, cook in sugar at point
8. When cooked place them to cool, fanning to cool quickly
9. Add scent if desired
10. Pour into glazed pans
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Frangas com Hervas Finas para huma Entrada (Chickens with Fine Herbs for an Entrance)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two chickens
- chicken livers
- scraped pork fat
- parsley
- chives
- salt
- pepper
- half a clove of garlic
- a small piece of fresh butter
- two or three egg yolks
- pork fat for blanching

## Directions

1. Prepare one or two chickens and stuff with a filling made from their livers, scraped pork fat, parsley, chives, salt, pepper, half a clove of garlic, a small piece of fresh butter, and two or three egg yolks
2. Dry them very well so the filling does not fall out
3. Blanch them in melted pork fat and let cool
4. When cool, cover with slices of pork fat and sheets of paper
5. Roast according to custom
6. Serve with ravigote sauce or any other piquant sauce
7. The same method applies to turkeys, ducks, and young chickens
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas com Marfcos para Entrada (Chickens with Shellfish for an Entrance)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three chickens
- three or four dozen oysters
- ham essence
- butter
- flour
- shallots
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare two or three chickens in the same manner as chickens with fine herbs and roast them
2. For the ragout, blanch three or four dozen oysters in their own water, taking care not to let them boil
3. When cleaned, steep them in ham essence with a piece of butter dusted with flour and finely chopped shallots
4. When the sauce is well thickened, pour it over the chickens and serve with lemon juice
5. These same chickens or capons can also be larded with thick pork fat, cooked in a braising pan, and served with the shellfish ragout that seems best
6. They can also be served with shrimp coulis instead of ham essence
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas com Ovos (Chickens with Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickens
- Chicken livers
- Scraped bacon
- Fine herbs
- Egg yolks
- Bacon sheets
- Paper
- Hard egg yolks
- Ham essence
- Butter
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Stuff the chickens with their livers and scraped bacon, seasoned with fine herbs and bound with two egg yolks
2. Cover with bacon sheets and paper as in preceding recipes
3. Roast on the spit
4. Make a sauce with four hard-boiled egg yolks, chopped on cloth and placed in ham essence bound with butter, but do not boil
5. Serve with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas de Fricandó (Fricandeau Chickens)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two young good quality chickens
- pork fat for blanching
- fine pork fat for larding

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare one or two good young chickens and remove the breast meat
2. Season and blanch in pork fat, or if not possible, blanch in a furnace with boiling water
3. Lard with fine pork fat and cook
4. Finish in the same manner as veal fricandeau
5. Serve with a ragout of cucumbers, chicory, or any other ragout that seems appropriate
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas de Manjar Branco para Entrada (Chickens with Blancmange for an Entrance)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three chickens
- half a canada of good cow's milk
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- cooked coriander
- handful of sweet almonds, pounded
- chicken breasts, roasted
- one arratel of pork fat, finely cut
- eight egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- nutmeg
- milk for cooking

## Directions

1. After tenderizing two or three chickens, scale and clean them well
2. Debone them entirely through the neck, being careful not to pierce the skin
3. Take half a canada of good cow's milk and bring to boil with a bay leaf, marjoram, and a little pounded coriander
4. When reduced to one quartilho, add a handful of sweet pounded almonds and strain through a sieve
5. Add the breasts of two roasted chickens, one arratel of pork fat cut very fine, and eight egg yolks
6. Season with salt, pepper, spices, and nutmeg
7. Incorporate over low heat
8. When thickened and cooled, stuff the chickens and sew with needle so the filling does not escape
9. Blanch in boiling water, then cook in a casserole with hot milk, salt, and dried coriander
10. Cover with slices of pork fat and sheets of paper, letting them simmer gently
11. When cooked, remove and serve with white Queen's coulis
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas em Ballon (Chickens en Ballon)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three chickens
- Chicken breasts
- Partridge
- Turkey
- Game meat
- Ham
- Truffles
- Bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Pork fat

## Directions

1. Take two or three chickens, debone them as above, and stuff with a ground mixture made of chicken breasts, partridge, turkey, or any other tender game, ham, truffles, all cut into small dice, mixed with scraped bacon, and seasoned with salt, pepper, fine herbs
2. After filling the chickens, tie around and close them like a purse so they remain round like a buoy
3. Place to sear in pork fat
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas em Ballon (Chickens in Balloon)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three chickens
- chicken breasts
- partridge meat
- turkey meat or other tender game
- ham
- truffles
- pork fat, scraped
- salt
- pepper
- herbs

## Directions

1. Clean and bone two or three chickens as above
2. Prepare a filling made from diced chicken breasts, partridge, turkey or other tender game meat, ham, and truffles, all cut into small dice
3. Mix with scraped pork fat and season with salt, pepper, and herbs
4. Stuff the chickens and sew them around, closing them like a bag so they become round like a buoy
5. Blanch them in melted pork fat, then remove and drain them
6. Dry them very well and cover with a slice of pork fat
7. Cook in a braising pan until done
8. Serve with Italian sauce, or alternatively with Spanish sauce or clear essence and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas em Crocadas para Entradas (Chickens in Croquettes for Starters)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three fat chickens
- Bacon slices
- Ham
- Bacon strips
- Bay leaf
- Bundle of parsley
- Coriander seeds
- Cow's milk
- Butter
- Flour
- Two egg yolks
- Bread crumbs
- Pepper sauce or other sauce

## Directions

1. Take two or three fat chickens, prepare them as is customary, and after cleaning and cooking, lard them with thick bacon and ham.
2. Line the bottom of a casserole with bacon slices, place the chickens on top, season with salt, whole pepper, one bay leaf, one bundle of parsley, a little dried coriander seed.
3. Cover with other bacon slices and place to cook slowly over a low heat, then moisten with boiling cow's milk and let cook on low heat, simmering gently.
4. When cooked, let cool, then cover with a thickened sauce made with a piece of butter dusted with flour, two egg yolks, and a drop of milk.
5. Cover with fine breadcrumbs, place in the oven to brown, and serve browned with pepper sauce or any other sauce that seems better.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas em Galatina para uma Entrada (Chickens in Galantine for a Starter)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three chickens
- Minced filling, well bound
- Truffle fillets
- Bacon fillets
- Pistachio fillets
- Hard egg yolk fillets
- Bacon slices
- Linen cloth
- Broth
- White wine
- Ham essence sauce or other sauce

## Directions

1. Take two or three chickens, cut them in half and completely bone them as is done with hens in cannelloni.
2. Cover them with a fine minced filling, well bound, and over this make a cord of truffle fillets, one of bacon fillets, another of pistachios, another of hard egg yolk fillets, and continue thus to the end.
3. Cover all these fillets with a little of the minced filling and bind with beaten eggs.
4. Roll the chicken with the skin outward, cover each roll with its slice of bacon, wrap each one in its piece of linen cloth, tie by the ends and around.
5. Place to cook in a braise moistened with a little broth and white wine.
6. When cooked, remove, untie, dry, trim, and serve with ham essence sauce or any other sauce that seems better. They can also be served cold as an entrée dish.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas em Granadinas (Chickens in Granadines)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three young chickens
- Paper
- Bacon sheet
- String
- Melted bacon fat
- Fine bacon
- Forcemeat
- Fine ground mixture
- Good ragú
- Bacon sheets
- Veal
- Ham
- Demi-glace
- Studded onion
- Broth

## Directions

1. Take two or three young chickens, trim and clean, open by the back, remove all bones, and after deboning, fill with paper covered with a bacon sheet and tie with string around so they remain round
2. Sear in a pot with melted bacon fat, then let cool, dry and lard with fine bacon
3. Once larded, remove the paper and interior bacon, and fill with a raw forcemeat or a fine ground mixture and a good ragú in the middle, bound and well-flavored
4. Place to cook in a pot with bacon sheets, veal, and ham, a demi-glace, studded onion, and a spoon of broth
5. Once cooked, pass the broth through a sieve, reduce, color the chickens on the bacon side like fricandeau, and serve with a ragú or whatever sauce seems best
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas Feitas com Sangue para huma Entrada (Chickens Made with Blood for an Entrance)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three chickens
- half a canada of pork, veal, or chicken blood
- one arratel of pork fat cut into dice
- parsley
- chives
- herbs
- eight egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- pounded coriander

## Directions

1. Clean and tenderize two or three chickens
2. Debone them as with previous recipes
3. Take half a canada of pork, veal, or chicken blood and one arratel of pork fat cut into dice
4. Add parsley, chives, herbs, and eight egg yolks
5. Season with salt, pepper, and pounded coriander
6. Thicken over low heat
7. When incorporated and good tasting, cool and stuff the chickens with this mixture
8. Sew with needle and cook in a white marinade with fine herbs and dried coriander, simmering gently
9. When cooked, remove and serve with ham essence
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas or Galinhas noivas de Escalope (Young Hens Escalope-Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 young hens
- olive oil
- melted pork fat
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- shallots
- garlic clove
- ham slices
- bacon slices
- a little substance (broth)
- a drop of white wine
- a little cullis
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take 4 young hens and remove the breasts
2. Cut the breasts into thin and equal fillets
3. Marinate them in olive oil and melted pork fat with little salt, pepper, parsley, shallots, and a garlic clove, all finely chopped
4. Cover the bottom of a cassole with thin slices of ham
5. Arrange the breast fillets with their marinade on top
6. Cover with bacon slices and cook between two fires for a little while
7. Transfer the fillets to another cassole and add to the braising liquid a little substance, a drop of white wine, and a little cullis
8. Let it boil for a little time
9. When reduced, pass through a sieve and remove all the fat
10. Put the hen fillets inside, only to heat through
11. Serve with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas or Galinhas novas de marinadas (Young Hens in Marinade)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 young hen
- water
- vinegar
- a piece of butter dusted with flour
- salt
- pepper
- onion rings
- parsley
- 2 or 3 cloves
- 2 or 3 garlic cloves
- 2 bay leaves
- marjoram
- pork fat

## Directions

1. Take a young hen that is very tender, remove its skin and cut it into pieces
2. Heat water and vinegar with a piece of butter dusted with flour, salt, pepper, onion rings, parsley, cloves, garlic cloves, bay leaves, and marjoram over the stove, stirring until the butter melts
3. Once the butter is melted, add the cut hen pieces and let them take on the flavor of the marinade
4. Remove from the marinade, dry in a white cloth, dust each piece with flour
5. Fry in pork fat and serve with fried parsley rings. Alternatively, they can be passed through egg batter, fried, and served the same way
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas à Crema (Creamed Chickens)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two roasted chickens
- veal
- beef fat
- pork fat
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- parsley
- shallots
- egg yolks
- egg whites
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Take two roasted chickens and remove all the breast meat and mince it
2. Mix with a piece of veal, beef fat, and blanched pork fat
3. When all is minced, pound very well in a mortar
4. Add a bread crumb softened in milk
5. Season with salt, pepper, fine herbs, parsley, and shallots
6. Bind with egg yolks and some beaten egg whites
7. When seasoned, well blended, and pounded, fill the chicken bodies in place of the removed meat as if they were whole
8. Unite everything well with a knife blade moistened in eggs
9. Coat with breadcrumbs and put in oven to cook in a baking dish with slices of pork fat
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas à Ingleza para huma Entrada (English-Style Chickens for an Entrance)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens
- water
- salt
- flour
- pork fat
- melted butter
- broth
- parsley
- cauliflower or cabbage

## Directions

1. Clean two chickens and secure with pins
2. Blanch in water, then flour them
3. Cover with a slice of pork fat and cook in a pan with water and salt
4. When cooked, serve with melted butter thickened with a drop of broth, adding finely chopped and blanched parsley
5. Can also be garnished with cooked cauliflower, cabbage, or other vegetables
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangas à Montmorency (Chickens Montmorency)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three chickens
- Paper
- Bacon slice
- Pork lard melted
- Fine bacon for larding
- Minced filling
- Ragú
- Veal sweetbreads or truffas
- Fat livers
- Kidneys
- Mushrooms
- Bacon slices
- Veal slices
- Ham slices
- Onion studded with cloves
- Bundle of parsley
- Herbs
- Good broth
- Clear essence
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Flambe and singe two or three chickens, open them by the sides, empty them, and remove the breast bones without damaging the meat.
2. Fill them with paper covered with a bacon slice, tie as if they were whole, and place to cook in melted pork lard.
3. After cooking and cooling, lard them with fine bacon; after finishing larding, remove the bacon and paper, and fill with a minced filling and a well-bound ragú in the middle, either of veal sweetbreads or truffas, fat livers, kidneys, mushrooms, or finally whatever is most convenient.
4. After filling, sew with needle and thread, and place to cook with bacon slices, veal slices, and ham slices, onion studded with cloves, one bundle of parsley, fine herbs, and moisten with one spoonful of good broth.
5. After cooking, pass the broth through a sieve, remove the fat, and reduce until it becomes like a fricandeau, and brown the chickens in it with the bacon underneath.
6. When browned, remove the thread and serve with clear essence, strained through cheesecloth, which stuck to the bottom of the casserole, with a squeeze of lemon.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangos Assados (Roasted Chickens)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fat chickens no more than two months old
- bacon for larding
- paper spread with butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Choose fat chickens no more than two months old; after bleeding them, plucking them, and letting them rest, clean them as is customary, straighten them, and truss them over the coals
2. Lard them with bacon, put them on the spit, cover them with sheets of buttered paper, put them to roast, turning constantly with sufficient fire
3. After roasting, remove the paper, and when browned serve them hot
4. Also, instead of being larded, they can be covered with strips of bacon
5. Also after roasting, the bacon removed, cover them with fine breadcrumbs, brown them, and serve them hot as a roasted dish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangos Assados por Diversos Modos para Entradas (Roasted Chickens in Various Ways for Appetizers)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- chicken livers
- scraped bacon
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- pork butter
- bacon slices
- paper leaves
- spit

## Directions

1. Choose young chickens and after they are mortified, cleaned, and singed, leave them with their legs intact, cutting only their nails
2. Fill them with a mixture made from their own livers, scraped bacon, seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
3. After thus preparing them, tie them with twine
4. Brown them in pork butter, remove them, cover them with bacon slices and paper leaves, attach to a spit, and roast them
5. After roasting, serve them with any of the following sauces: Spanish sauce, Italian sauce, German sauce, English sauce, Ravigote sauce, Sorrel and caper sauce, Vinegar sauce, Spicy sauce, Shallot sauce, Laurel sauce, or Aspic sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangos de Fricassé com Vinho Branco (Chicken Fricassee with White Wine)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- butter or melted bacon fat
- ham
- herbs
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- hot water
- broth
- white wine
- egg yolks
- pickled parsley
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Cut and prepare in the same manner as the preceding recipes
2. The difference is that after soaking, add a glass of white wine that has been boiled, and thicken with egg yolks mixed in broth, pickled parsley, and lime juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangos de Fricassé em Agraço (Chicken Fricassee in Verjuice)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- butter or melted bacon fat
- ham
- herbs
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- hot water
- broth
- egg yolks
- verjuice
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Prepare, brown, and cook as in the preceding recipe
2. The difference is that the egg yolks should be mixed in verjuice instead, of which some whole berries blanched in water should be left
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangos de Fricassé para Huma Entrada (Chicken Fricassee for a First Course)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four chickens
- butter or melted bacon fat
- a slice of ham
- a bundle of herbs
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- hot water
- broth
- egg yolks
- pickled parsley
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Scald and pluck three or four chickens in hot water
2. Cut them into pieces and soak them for some time
3. Blanch them in hot water, pare them, and pass them through a casserole with butter or melted bacon fat, a slice of ham, a bundle of herbs, chopped onion, seasoned with salt and pepper
4. Once browned, dust with flour, add a spoonful of hot water and another of broth, and let them simmer slowly
5. When cooked, remove the fat, bundle of herbs, and onion
6. Thicken with egg yolks mixed in broth, pickled parsley, and lime juice, and serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangos de Marinada (Marinated Chickens)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- water
- vinegar
- onions
- garlic clove
- salt
- pepper
- laurel leaves
- marjoram
- chickens
- pork butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Make a marinade with a piece of butter powdered with little flour, two spoonfuls of water, vinegar, onion slices, one garlic clove, salt, pepper, laurel leaves, and marjoram
2. Put it on heat until it boils, remove it, and put the chickens cut into quarters into it
3. Leave them for two or three hours
4. After they have taken flavor, remove them, dry them, dip them in flour, and fry them in pork butter
5. When fried and browned, serve them garnished with parsley
6. Alternatively, they can be cooked in the same marinade, and after cooking, pass them through a batter made with flour, white wine, salt, pepper, and a little melted butter, then fry them and serve them as above
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangos Doces (Sweet Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large chickens
- drippings from the chickens
- 1 arratel of sugar
- half an arratel of finely ground almonds
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. After roasting two large chickens and taking their drippings, place them in one arratel of sugar at half-point consistency with half an arratel of finely ground almonds and the said drippings
2. When the chickens are cooked and the sauce is thick, place on slices of bread with sauce and cinnamon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Frangos em Brézn para Huma Entrada (Braised Chicken for a First Course)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- bacon
- ham
- veal slices
- beef slices
- paper leaves
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. After blanching and tying the chickens with legs tucked inside, lard them with bacon and ham, seasoned as is customary
2. Line a casserole with bacon and veal slices beaten thin and thin
3. Place the chickens on top with breasts facing down, season them, and cover with other slices of beef and bacon, and paper leaves
4. Close the casserole with its lid and cook between two fires, but let it simmer gently
5. When cooked, remove them, place them to drain on a sieve, and serve with the ragout that seems best
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangos em Caixa (Boxed Chickens)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted chickens
- truffles
- ham
- fat livers
- veal sweetbreads
- scraped bacon
- fine herbs
- shallots
- salt
- pepper
- good oil
- laurel
- marjoram
- onion slices
- lemon slices
- essence sauce
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Singe and stuff the chickens with a mixture made of truffles, ham, fat livers, veal sweetbreads, scraped bacon, fine herbs, shallots, salt, and pepper
2. Tie them with twine and marinate them in good oil with salt, pepper, laurel, marjoram, onion slices, and lemon slices
3. After they have taken flavor in this marinade, make paper boxes and place each chicken in its box, covered with bacon slices
4. Place them on grills to roast over gentle heat
5. When roasted, drain the fat from them and serve with essence sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangos em Forma de Peras para Huma Entrada (Chicken Shaped as Pears for a First Course)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal sweetbreads
- ham
- truffles
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- five or six chickens
- string
- white wine
- cullis

## Directions

1. Make a hash of blanched veal sweetbreads, ham, truffles, all mixed with scraped bacon, seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
2. Remove all the bones from five or six chickens, leaving only one foot attached
3. Stuff with the hash, shape them like pears, using the chicken foot as the pear stem
4. Tie with string, wrap each one in a bacon slice, and place them to cook in a braise nourished with some pieces of veal and moistened with a glass of white wine
5. When cooked, remove them, place them to drain, and when dry and properly arranged, place a spoonful of cullis in the bottom of the same braise
6. Strain through a sieve, remove the fat, and serve the chickens hot with this sauce and lime juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangos Embrulhados em Redenho (Chicken Wrapped in Caul)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- caul
- chicken breasts or veal
- cream
- bacon slices

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the chickens as in the preceding recipe
2. Spread pieces of caul on a cloth, as many as there are chickens, placing on these and around them a hash made of chicken or veal breasts in cream
3. So that the hash cannot fall out, brush the edges of the caul pieces with a brush, and wrap each one around itself with its chicken in the middle
4. Place them in the oven to cook in a baking dish with bacon slices underneath
5. When cooked, serve with sauce of ham essence or any other that seems best
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangos Feitos em Chouriços (Chicken Made as Sausages)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six chickens
- chicken breasts
- pork fat
- milk
- thyme
- bay leaf
- coriander seed
- bread crumb
- egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- bacon slices
- onion slices
- fine herbs
- cloves
- veal broth
- cullis à Rainha

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean five or six chickens thoroughly and stuff them with a hash made of roasted chicken breasts, pork fat, milk boiled with a little thyme, bay leaf, and dried coriander seed passed through a sieve, to which is added bread crumb, all bound with egg yolks and seasoned with salt and pepper
2. After the chickens are stuffed and sewn closed, blanch them in broth
3. When blanched, dry them and place them to cook in a casserole with bacon slices, onion slices, fine herbs, salt, cloves, and whole pepper, and veal broth, so that they boil and cook slowly
4. When cooked and dry, serve with cullis à Rainha sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Frangãos Alhardados (Battered Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens
- salt
- pepper
- 4 beaten eggs
- pork butter
- bread slices
- lemon

## Directions

1. After half-roasting two chickens, cut them in half and salt and pepper them
2. Pass them through four beaten eggs; once well coated, fry them in pork butter until well browned
3. Place on slices of bread, garnished with slices of lemon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Frangãos com Fideos (Chickens with Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens in pieces
- one quarter of bacon
- aromatic herbs
- vinegar
- spices
- fideos pasta
- lemon

## Directions

1. Braise two chickens in pieces with one quarter of bacon, aromatic herbs, and vinegar
2. When half-cooked, season with all the seasonings
3. Add fideos pasta (which are like toasted pine nuts) to the stew
4. Remove from the heat and place in embers while awaiting service
5. When serving, add lemon juice on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Frangãos de Caldo de Vaca (Chickens in Beef Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- beef fat
- aromatic herbs
- beef broth
- slices of paio

## Directions

1. Cook the chickens in beef fat with a head of aromatic herbs
2. Once cooked, make a soup of the same broth; place the chickens on top
3. Arrange slices of paio around the plate and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Frangãos de Fricace (Fricasseed Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread, onion, parsley, and bacon finely chopped
- butter
- water
- chickens (or hens or squabs)
- saffron
- clove
- pepper
- cinnamon
- vinegar
- bread crumbs
- egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put finely chopped bread, onion, parsley, and bacon in a pot with butter and enough water for what you wish to make, and bring to a boil
2. Meanwhile, wash the chickens well and keep them in water
3. Once the onion, parsley, and bacon mixture is well cooked, add the cut chickens
4. When cooked with the seasonings (saffron, little clove, pepper, cinnamon, and vinegar), add grated bread crumbs to thicken the sauce
5. When the bread appears to be cooked, beat egg yolks in a bowl and add them, stirring constantly so they do not curdle
6. Place on slices and serve with lemon juice on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Frangãos Estrellados (Starred Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 chickens
- salt
- pepper
- melted bacon
- toasted slices
- bacon
- lemon
- sausages
- bacon strips of ham or loin

## Directions

1. Cook four chickens in a pot; when cooked, remove whole
2. Salt and pepper well, then fry in melted bacon until browned
3. Place on toasted slices with bacon on top and lemon juice
4. Garnish this plate with sausages and bacon strips of ham or loin, and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Frangãos Fritos (Fried Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- wine
- vinegar
- garlic
- oregano
- pork butter
- slices of bread
- bacon
- spices
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Keep the chickens for the space of two hours in a preserve of wine, vinegar, garlic, and oregano
2. Fry them in pork butter with slices of bread
3. When done, melt a little chopped bacon; when melted, add a little of the preserve from the same chickens
4. Season with all the spices and bring the chickens to a boil in this sauce
5. After passing through the boil, send to the table with lemon juice and sliced lemon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Frangãos à Franceza (Chickens French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 chickens
- 2 arratels of chopped mutton
- seasoning
- bread loaf
- butter
- chicory, lettuce, or cardoon
- 6 egg yolks
- lemon
- broth

## Directions

1. After cleaning four chickens well with their beaks and feet, place them in a pot; when half-cooked, remove them
2. Stuff with two arratels of chopped mutton seasoned as for pastries and a bread loaf; open the chickens along the back
3. Place the stuffed bread in the middle of a buttered plate and arrange the chickens around it, with four portions of chicory, lettuce, or cardoon between them
4. When the plate is ready, place six egg yolks beaten with lemon and one spoonful of broth from the pot on top
5. Brown the plate and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Fricassé de Pombos feito com sangue (Pigeon Fricassee Made with Blood)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six young pigeons
- pigeon blood
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- ham
- parsley
- clove-studded onion
- substance

## Directions

1. Take five or six young pigeons and save their blood, stirring it until it cools so it does not coagulate
2. Straighten the pigeons the same way as those in compote, then brown them in a casserole with rendered bacon, salt, pepper, a slice of ham, a sprig of parsley, and a clove-studded onion
3. Moisten with substance and thicken
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fricassé de Pombos feito com Sangue (Pigeon Fricassée Made with Blood)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six young pigeons
- pigeon blood
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- a slice of ham
- a sprig of parsley
- an onion studded with cloves
- veal stock
- veal coulis

## Directions

1. Take five or six young pigeons and save their blood, stirring it as it cools so it does not coagulate
2. Prepare the pigeons the same way as those in compote
3. After passing them through the fire in a casserole with melted bacon, salt, pepper, a slice of ham, a sprig of parsley, and an onion studded with cloves, moisten them with stock and thicken with a little veal coulis
4. When cooked, skim off the fat, pass the blood through a sieve, and add it to the fricassée, taking care that it does not boil
5. When bound and of good taste, arrange on a plate and serve hot as an entrance course
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fruta de Ciringa (Choux Pastry Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a canada of milk
- a quarter of butter
- a quarter of sugar
- an eighth of flour
- a dozen eggs
- oil or melted butter for frying
- sugar in thread stage
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put half a canada of milk in a pot to boil
2. When it boils, add a quarter of butter, a quarter of sugar, and an eighth of flour
3. Cook this mixture, stirring until it becomes very hard
4. Remove the pot from the fire and add a dozen eggs, two by two, beating the mixture very well until it becomes soft
5. Fry in boiling oil or butter using a piping bag greased with butter or oil
6. When fried, pass through sugar in thread stage with cinnamon on top and serve at the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Fruta de Manjar Branco (Almond Blancmange Fruit Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- six tiguelas of almond blancmange
- twelve egg yolks
- one arratel of powdered sugar
- one eighth of flour
- half arratel of butter
- four egg yolks
- one quarter of sugar

## Directions

1. Put in a pan six tiguelas of almond blancmange with twelve egg yolks and one arratel of powdered sugar
2. Bring to a boil to thicken and let cool
3. Knead one eighth of flour with half arratel of butter, four egg yolks, and one quarter of sugar
4. When kneaded, make two cakes and stretch on a board until very thin
5. Place the beaten blancmange on one cake, spread it, and cover with the other cake
6. Cut with a wheel cutter in the shape of fartes
7. Fry in butter and pass through sugar
8. Send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Fígado de Porco (Pork Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork liver, well roasted
- pork caul fat
- onion
- salt
- vinegar
- spices
- clove
- pepper
- saffron
- garlic
- orange juice
- bread slices

## Directions

1. When you have a very well roasted pork liver, melt pork caul fat in a pot
2. Braise a chopped onion in it, and after braising, season it with salt and vinegar with spices, clove, pepper, a little saffron, and garlic
3. Add the liver and when it has simmered, remove it from the fire and add orange juice
4. Serve on bread slices and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Fígado de Vitela (Veal Liver)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal liver
- Fine lard
- Thick lard
- Peverada sauce

## Directions

1. Commonly the veal livers are eaten roasted on a spit, being first larded with fine lard, and served with peverada sauce underneath
2. Also they can be larded with thick lard, and cooked in a braise; and after being cooked serve them with the same sauce, or with any other that seems good
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fígados de Vitela em Crespílsa (Veal Livers in Caul)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal livers
- Beef fat
- Bone marrow from bread cooked in milk
- Two large onions
- Fine herbs
- Cucumbers
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg yolks
- Egg whites
- Lard slices
- Pork caul
- Egg whites beaten
- Ham essence
- Verjuice or lemon juice

## Directions

1. The veal livers, with an equal amount of beef fat, are pounded in a mortar, adding to it a bone marrow of bread cooked in milk, two large onions cut into cubes, and passed through butter, fine herbs, cucumbers, salt, pepper, egg yolks; pounded, add beaten egg whites to it; line a pot with slices of lard, and place on it a pork caul, or well washed and well dried caul, pour over it the pounded liver, cover it with the same caul well folded and moistened with beaten eggs, so that it remains stuck; cover it with paper, and place it to cook on a gentle fire above and below; when cooked, turn it over on a plate, and after drying and in fat, serve it with sauce of clear ham essence, and verjuice or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Fígados de Vitela à Italiana (Veal Livers Italian Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal liver
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Garlic clove
- Shallot
- Laurel
- Marjoram
- Good olive oil
- Butter
- Flour
- Verjuice or vinegar

## Directions

1. A veal liver cut into very thin slices, is seasoned with salt, pepper, fine herbs, a clove of garlic, and shallot, all finely chopped, laurel, marjoram, and good olive oil; once seasoned, arrange the slices very straight, and curled in a pot, and place them to cook on gentle heat for the space of an hour; when cooked, remove them with a skimmer, strain the olive oil, and pour in its place a bit of well washed butter, pulverized with flour, with verjuice or vinegar, make it bind on the heat, pour it over the liver slices, and serve them very hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Galeirões assados e recheados (Roasted and Stuffed Gannets)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Gannets
- Bread crumbs
- Fresh butter
- Flour
- Red wine
- Parsley
- Garlic, chopped
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Nutmeg, grated
- Bay leaf
- Thyme in powder
- String
- White wine
- Bread crust, grated
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Singe, clean, and remove the entrails from the gannets
2. Prepare a stuffing of bread crumbs, fresh butter, flour, two spoonfuls of red wine, parsley, chopped garlic, seasoned with salt, pepper, cloves, grated nutmeg, bay leaf, and powdered thyme
3. Mix all together and stuff inside the gannets
4. Sew the opening with thread, place on the spit and roast, basting with white wine, salt, and melted butter
5. Once roasted, serve with its own sauce, sprinkled with grated bread crust and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Galeirões assados no espejo (Gannets Roasted on the Spit)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Gannets
- Vinegar
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Gannet livers
- Nutmeg, grated

## Directions

1. Clean the gannets, remove the entrails, position the feet behind the thighs, and tie with string
2. Place on the spit and begin to roast, basting with a sauce made of vinegar, butter, salt, and pepper
3. Once roasted, make a sauce with the chopped livers of the gannets, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, and a piece of butter
4. Serve with sour orange juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Galeirões cozidos ao Cambayon (Gannets Cooked in the Cambayon Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Gannets
- Bacon or lard with pieces of eels and fish roe
- Water
- White wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeat
- Fine herbs bouquet
- Onions studded with cloves
- Butter
- Butter sauce
- Parsley
- Fish roe, chopped
- Vinegar
- Capers

## Directions

1. Pluck and singe the gannets, remove the entrails, position the feet behind the thighs, tie with string
2. Clean and wipe them
3. Lard them with pieces of eels and fish roe
4. Place them to cook in an adobo made of water, white wine, salt, pepper, nutmeat, a bouquet of fine herbs, onions studded with cloves, and butter
5. Allow to simmer little by little
6. Once cooked, remove from the adobo and serve with a sauce of butter, parsley, and chopped fish roe, vinegar, and its capers
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Galeirões de vários modos para entrada (Gannets in Various Ways for Entrée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Gannets
- Eel fillets
- Fish roe
- Gannet livers
- Butter
- Fine herbs
- Parsley
- Garlic
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg, grated
- Sour orange peel, chopped
- Egg yolks
- Fish stock
- Red wine
- Parsley
- Fine herbs
- Pennyroyal sprigs
- Onion studded with cloves
- Garlic cloves
- Turnips
- Bread crumbs, fried

## Directions

1. After cleaning the gannets, blanch and lard them with eel fillets and fish roe
2. Prepare a stuffing of gannet livers, butter, fine herbs, parsley, half a garlic clove, seasoned with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, small pieces of chopped sour orange peel, bound with two egg yolks
3. Stuff the gannets and sew the opening with thread, tie with string
4. Place in a pan with fish stock, a glass of red wine, seasoned with salt and pepper, parsley, fine herbs, sprigs of pennyroyal, an onion studded with cloves, and two garlic cloves
5. Allow to simmer little by little for four or five hours
6. Once cooked, remove from the pan, prepare a ragout of turnips cooked in the same broth, and after binding and removing the fat, add pieces of fried bread crumbs
7. Serve with sour orange or lemon juice
8. They may also be served with ragouts and different garnishes such as oysters, mussels, and others
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Galete para Dias de Carne (Roasted Poultry for Meat Days)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Poultry (galeteirões)
- Liver from the poultry
- Scraped lard (toucinho)
- Parsley (salsa)
- Small onions (cebozinhas)
- Truffles
- Salt
- Pepper
- Three or four egg yolks
- Slices of lard
- Slices of veal or beef
- One spoonful of broth
- One spoonful of white wine
- One spoonful of ham coulis (culi de presunto)
- One splash of vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove the innards from the cleaned poultry, keeping the legs inside the body
2. Stuff with a finely chopped mixture made from the poultry's livers, scraped lard, parsley, small onions, and truffles, all finely minced and seasoned with salt and pepper, bound together with three or four egg yolks
3. Close the opening with thread
4. Place in a braise with slices of lard and slices of veal or beef, seasoned and of good flavor
5. Cook between two fires
6. Add one spoonful of broth and one spoonful of white wine
7. Let simmer for four or five hours
8. When cooked, add one spoonful of ham coulis and one splash of vinegar to the bottom of the braise
9. Strain this sauce through a sieve
10. Serve hot as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Galeto ou Bolo de Boeuf (Beef or Other Meat Terrine)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- meat fillets and sinews from the loins and thighs of wild boar
- loin of beef
- leg of mutton
- ham
- parsley
- small onions
- garlic clove
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- fine herbs
- bacon cut into dice
- six egg yolks
- a quarter liter of white wine

## Directions

1. Take the meat fillets and sinews from the loins and thighs of wild boar, a loin of beef, and a leg of mutton, and add a forcemeat of ham
2. Chop everything together on a board with parsley, small onions, and a clove of garlic, seasoned with salt, pepper, some spices, and fine herbs
3. Place it in a terrine with three or four handfuls of bacon cut into dice, six egg yolks, and a quarter liter of white wine
4. Mix and blend together very well
5. Line a casserole with bacon slices and arrange the forcemeat inside so that it is well packed and united
6. Cover with other bacon slices, paper leaves, and its lid, and place in a hot oven for four or five hours
7. Allow it to cool in the same casserole; once cooked and cold, unmold it and serve on a napkin
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha
