title: Galinha Agra e Doce (Chicken Sweet and Sour)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- one arratel of lean ham
- whole seasonings
- one quince in quarters, or lacking quince, sour apples
- half arratel of sugar
- one dash of good vinegar

## Directions

1. Put one chicken in a pan with one arratel of lean ham, whole seasonings, one quince in quarters, or lacking quince sour apples, half arratel of sugar, one dash of good vinegar
2. Place to cook
3. When cooked, place it in a plate upon slices with one lemon, and send it to the table in white broth
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha Armado (Armed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken
- Two arratels of lamb
- Half arratel of bacon
- All aromatics
- Eggs from the chicken
- Vinegar
- Four eggs
- One quarter of bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Mince one chicken with two arratels of lamb, half arratel of bacon, and all aromatics, being careful to leave the eggs from the chicken whole.
2. When half of all this meat is stewed, return it to the chopping block to be minced together with the other half and season with all seasonings, vinegar, four eggs, and one quarter of bread crumbs.
3. After the meat is seasoned, assemble the chicken with the bones.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha Armado (Stuffed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- two arratels of mutton
- half arratel of pork
- all aromatics
- chicken eggs
- vinegar
- four eggs
- one quarter of grated bread
- lard of pork
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop one chicken with two arratels of mutton, half arratel of pork, and all aromatics, leaving the chicken eggs whole
2. Once half of all this meat is braised, return it to the chopping block to be chopped together with the other half
3. Season with all seasonings, vinegar, four eggs, and one quarter of grated bread
4. After the meat is seasoned, arm the chicken with the bones in a buttered frying pan
5. When it reaches the desired size, arrange strips of pork lard on top
6. Place it to cook on low heat from below and on top
7. When cooked, place a cream broth made in the form of a gilt-head bream, and send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha Assada (Roasted Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken, plucked and cleaned
- One strip of bacon, about a palm's length and four fingers' width
- Butter
- Paper
- Twine

## Directions

1. After one chicken is plucked, cleaned, and spitted, take one strip of bacon about a palm's length and four fingers' width, cut it into strips, and place it on the chicken on top and underneath.
2. Wrap the chicken in four or five sheets of paper well buttered and tie it very well with twine in many turns so that it does not come undone.
3. Put the chicken to roast, first moistening the paper on top with water so that it does not burn.
4. After the chicken is roasted, place it on the plate with the same strips of bacon, slices of bread underneath, and send it to the table.
5. In this manner one roasts capon, turkey, guinea fowl, and young chickens.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha com Cidrão (Chicken with Citron)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- one quarter of pork
- aromatics
- all seasonings
- bread in slices
- sugar
- lemon
- half arratel of citron
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cook one chicken with one quarter of pork and aromatics
2. Once cooked, season with all seasonings
3. Make bread in slices, and cut them in the manner of dice, place them in a plate where you will pour all the chicken broth so that they become white
4. Add sugar and lemon to make this broth sour and sweet, and it must be browned with heat from below and on top
5. Then place the chicken on top, cut in quarters and passed through beaten eggs with good lemon
6. Cut half arratel of citron into thin small pieces, and after passed through the same eggs, place them around the plate between the chicken, thus it goes to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha de Alfitete (Chicken with Batter)
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken
- One arratel of bacon
- Aromatics
- All seasonings
- Water
- One eighth of flour
- Half an arratel of sugar
- Six eggs
- Butter
- A splash of wine
- Bay leaves
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook the chicken with one arratel of bacon and aromatics with all seasonings and little water so that it remains well-bodied.
2. When cooked, melt the bacon and pour it into a shallow bowl in which you place the chicken in quarters to brown on low flame underneath and on top.
3. When browned, make a fine batter of one eighth flour with half an arratel of sugar, six eggs, its butter, and a splash of wine.
4. From this batter make some small balls with bay leaves, frying them and placing them in layers with sugar and cinnamon on top in a plate until full.
5. Place the chicken on top.
6. If desired, you can put egg threads on this plate.
7. In the same way one makes mutton, pigeons, young chickens, and kid goat.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha de Fernão de Sousa (Fernão de Sousa's Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- one arratel of mutton
- one arratel of pork
- three onions
- one bunch of aromatics
- vinegar
- little water
- one dozen egg yolks
- butter

## Directions

1. Put one chicken in a pan with one arratel of mutton, one arratel of pork, three onions, one bunch of aromatics, vinegar, and little water
2. Place to cook, and when more than half cooked, remove it
3. Chop the chicken in one part, the mutton in another, and the pork with the aromatics in another
4. Also chop one dozen egg yolks
5. Once this is done, place some slices in a buttered frying pan or plate
6. After moistening them with the substance left from the chicken, spread layers upon them
7. Place a layer of chicken with chopped egg yolk on top, over this meat place another of mutton with chopped egg yolk on top, and over this another of pork and aromatics with chopped egg yolks
8. Continue the layers in this order until all is finished, shaping it like the form of a hat crown
9. Place six raw egg yolks on top, and between yolk and yolk some beef marrow
10. Cook on low heat from below and on top, and send it thus in the same frying pan or plate to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha em Potagem à Francesa (Chicken in French Pottage)
body:
## Ingredients

- one roasted chicken
- its liver
- half arratel of pork
- two onions
- mustard
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- cardamom
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Roast one chicken and its liver
2. After the liver is chopped, put it in a small mustard already prepared
3. Once half arratel of pork is melted and cleaned of cracklings, fry two chopped onions in it
4. In the same pan add the mustard mixed with the liver so that everything together becomes fried again
5. Season with pepper, clove, nutmeg, cardamom, so that it becomes well spicy
6. If you wish to add aromatics, do not add mustard
7. Once this pottage is made, place the roasted chicken in pieces on the plate with this pottage and lemon juice on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha em Pé (Chicken Standing)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- half arratel of pork
- two ounces of butter
- vinegar
- aromatics
- all seasonings
- one quartilho of water
- grated bread crumbs
- twelve egg yolks

## Directions

1. Place one chicken to cook with half arratel of pork, two ounces of butter, vinegar, aromatics, all seasonings, and one quartilho of water
2. When the water is spent and the substance remains, carve the chicken
3. Without chopping the giblets, chop more meats on the block with pork and aromatics
4. Once very well chopped, put it in a bowl and season again with all ground seasonings, the crumb of ten réis of grated bread, and the chicken giblet separated into thick strips, and twelve egg yolks
5. Once everything is beaten so that it becomes well fine, put it in a frying pan with melting butter by spoonfuls, frying each spoonful on itself, and placing it on a plate upon sliced larded bread
6. After the breast is full, place lemon juice on top and send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha Ensopada (Stewed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken, cut into pieces
- One quarter of bacon
- Aromatics
- Salt
- Vinegar
- All seasonings
- Four eggs
- Parsley
- Bread slices
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut one chicken into pieces and put them to stew with one quarter of bacon, aromatics, salt, and vinegar.
2. Season with all seasonings and thicken at the end with four eggs (or if you do not wish eggs, make it a parsley potage).
3. Place on slices of bread, lemon juice on top, and send it to the table.
4. The same is made of turkey, pigeons, young chickens, and kid goats.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha Mourisca (Moorish-Style Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- One arratel of bacon, cut into dice
- One half-roasted chicken
- A splash of white wine
- Little vinegar
- Water
- Seasonings
- Two bay leaves
- Two egg yolks
- Bread slices
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Fry one arratel of bacon cut into dice and pour the clean fat from the cracklings into a bowl with the fat from a half-roasted chicken, a splash of white wine, little vinegar, and enough water.
2. Put it to stew, season with all seasonings with two bay leaves, and thicken with two egg yolks.
3. Place slices of bread underneath and lemon on top.
4. In this manner one makes turkeys, pigeons, kid goats, and loins.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha Recheada (Stuffed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half arratels of mutton
- one quarter of pork
- aromatics
- three eggs
- all seasonings
- vinegar
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Chop in one part one and a half arratels of mutton, and in another part one quarter of pork with aromatics, all very well chopped
2. Place the pork and aromatics to braise with half of the mutton, and when half braised beat it on the block mixing it with the other half of the mutton
3. Chop everything together with three eggs, all seasonings, vinegar, and grated bread
4. Once this is done, stuff the chicken, and place it to roast in a frying pan, or to cook if you wish
5. Place it upon gilded broth with lemon juice on top, and send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinha sem Osso (Boneless Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken
- Two arratels of lamb
- Half arratel of bacon
- Aromatics, finely minced
- Butter
- All seasonings
- Eight cooked eggs
- Cheese

## Directions

1. Pluck one chicken dry and while still hot, open it along the back and remove all the meat and bones, leaving only the wing tips for when it is to be assembled.
2. Mince the chicken meat with two arratels of lamb, half of bacon, finely minced aromatics fried in butter.
3. Take it to the chopping block to season with all seasonings.
4. Fill the chicken with the minced mixture and eight cooked eggs.
5. Put it to cook in the bowl on a low flame.
6. When cooked, season it once more with all seasonings.
7. Place it in a plate or skillet in which a cheese soup has already been made.
8. The same is made of young chickens, pigeons, or turkey.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Galinhas com arroz para prato de relevo (Chickens with Rice as a Presentation Dish)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 arratels of rice
- beef broth
- hens
- ham
- lean pork fat
- roots
- clove-studded onion
- egg yolks
- Parmesan cheese
- nutmeg
- butter
- good essence
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Wash and dry 1 or 2 arratels of rice
2. Cook it in broth made with beef, hens, ham, and a piece of lean pork fat with roots and a clove-studded onion
3. When the rice is cooked and half-cooled, put it in a cassole with egg yolks, grated Parmesan cheese, and a little grated nutmeg
4. Mix everything and when cooled, make a border two fingers high on the serving plate
5. Cover the bottom with a little of the same rice
6. Place whole or quartered hens cooked in a braising liquid with a spoonful of good essence on top
7. Cover with the rest of the rice and breadcrumbs and grated cheese on top
8. Sprinkle with butter and cook in the oven
9. When browned, drain the fat and serve hot. Ducks, young hens, and pigeons are served the same way
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Galinhas com Arroz à Portuguesa (Chicken with Rice Portuguese Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two fat chickens
- two pieces of ham
- four arratels of beef from the leg
- one onion studded with cloves
- one sprig of sage
- hot water or broth
- vinegar
- a sprig of mint
- coriander
- rice
- pieces of ham

## Directions

1. Pluck two fat chickens, clean them, truss them, and put them to sweat in a pot with two pieces of ham, four arratels of beef from the leg cut into three or four pieces, one onion studded with cloves, and a sprig of sage
2. After everything is browned and beginning to stick, add a drop of hot water or broth, and let it boil little by little
3. When cooked and of good taste, pass the broth through a sieve, add a little vinegar, a sprig of mint and coriander
4. After boiling, add the quantity of rice necessary so that it remains whole and dry
5. When thus cooked, arrange the chickens on the plates with the rice on top, garnished with the pieces of ham cut into slices, and serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Galinhas com Arroz à Versiana (Chicken with Rice Versiana Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two young chickens
- one quarter of rice
- a little broth
- a little beef marrow melted
- grated nutmeg
- pepper
- a little saffron dissolved in a drop of broth
- two partridge breasts
- thin slices of ham cut in cubes
- ham
- melted bacon

## Directions

1. Bone two young chickens, and after cleaning them well, remove all the bones from the neck area
2. At the same time, put a quarter of rice to cook in a little broth
3. When the rice is half open, remove it from the fire and add a little melted beef marrow, grated nutmeg, pepper, and a little saffron dissolved in a drop of broth
4. Take two partridge breasts, cut them into thin slices, cut some slices of ham into cubes, pass them quickly through the fire with melted bacon
5. Being thus browned, mix them with the rice, and when cool, stuff the chickens with this
6. After stuffing, sew them with thread or twine, truss them, and then put them to cook in broth with a piece of ham and another of bacon
7. When half cooked, add half an arratel of well-washed rice, let it boil little by little
8. When the chickens are finished cooking and the rice is dry, arrange them on the plate and serve them garnished with ham, taking care that the rice grains remain whole
9. Other times grated cheese can be added on top; and browned in the oven, serve hot as a first course
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Galinhas e Capões Feitas na Breza para Entradas (Hens and Capons Made in Braise for Starters)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Hens and capons
- Thick bacon for larding
- Salt and pepper
- Bundle of parsley
- Fine herbs
- Carrots
- Onion studded with cloves
- Bacon slices
- Veal or beef slices
- Paper leaves
- Broth
- Wine
- Vegetable ragú or other sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the hens and capons and cook them as is customary, lard them with thick bacon seasoned with salt and pepper.
2. After finishing larding, line the bottom of a casserole with bacon and veal or beef slices, and place the hens on these with the breast down, season with salt and pepper, one bundle of parsley, fine herbs, carrots, onion studded with cloves.
3. Cover with other slices of bacon and veal, and paper leaves, and place to cook between coals for a little while.
4. Moisten with half a spoonful of broth and half a quart of wine, and let simmer gently.
5. When properly cooked, remove and serve with any vegetable ragú or any other sauce that seems better.
6. These same hens and capons, if they are young, can be cooked in the same braise without browning.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Galinholla Assada (Roasted Woodcock)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock, snipe, or curlew
- Bacon lard
- Bacon slices
- Bread slices
- Sour orange

## Directions

1. After plucking and turning the bird over with its own beak, roast it over coals
2. After roasting and browning, lard it with fine bacon or cover it with bacon slices
3. Without removing the entrails, roast it on a small spit tied to a larger spit
4. Place slices of bread underneath to catch what drips from the body
5. After roasting and achieving good color, serve garnished around or on top of the same slices with sour oranges
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Galinhollas Guisadas para Prato de Entrada (Braised Woodcock for an Entrée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock
- Bacon
- Egg yolks
- Parsley
- Chopped scallion
- Salt
- Pepper
- Thread for tying
- Bacon slices
- Buttered paper

## Directions

1. After plucking and singeing the woodcock, open it along the back to remove the entrails and livers
2. Chop the livers and add scraped bacon, two egg yolks, parsley, chopped scallion, season with salt and pepper
3. Stuff the woodcock with this mixture
4. After stuffing and sewing with thread so the chopped filling does not come out
5. Turn it upside down with its own beak
6. Cover with bacon slices and buttered paper
7. Place to roast
8. After roasting, remove the paper and bacon
9. Serve with the sauce or garnish that seems appropriate, such as olives, anchovies, capers, etc.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Garupa de Veado à Alemoa (Venison Haunch, German Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- venison haunch
- twine
- bacon planks
- water
- white wine
- salt
- whole pepper
- cloves
- thyme
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- onion slices
- green lemon
- melted butter
- egg yolks
- bread
- lime peel
- sugar
- powdered cinnamon
- salt
- sweet sauce or peverada sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the haunch of a venison from the middle of the thighs to the ribs so that the hind quarters form the shape of a saddle, and when thus arranged, trimmed, and well washed, tie it with twine
2. Put it in a braising pan of its length, covered with bacon planks, moistened with water and white wine, and seasoned with salt, whole pepper, cloves, thyme, bay leaf, marjoram, some onion slices, and a piece of green lemon
3. Let it simmer for two or three hours; when cooked and almost cold, remove it and after drying, place it on a serving dish
4. Baste it two or three times with melted butter thickened with egg yolks, and cover it with bread and lime peel, both grated, sugar, powdered cinnamon, a little salt, so that all this forms a crust of the thickness of half a finger
5. Put it in the oven to brown; when it has taken on good colour, remove it and serve it with sweet sauce or bound peverada sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Gateau, ou Bolo de Flor de Laranja (Orange Blossom Cake)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of sugar clarified and reduced to hair point
- half a pound of orange blossoms
- sugar powder
- egg white

## Directions

1. Clarified and strained two pounds of sugar reduced to hair point so that these stick to one another, add to it half a pound of orange blossom and allow it to boil until the sugar returns to the same point
2. Then remove from heat, stir very well with a wooden spoon, both in the middle and on the sides, until the sugar begins to rise
3. Then add at the same instant a spoonful of fine sugar dissolved in an egg white, and stirring always with haste, pour into paper boxes the height of three fingers, sustaining them on the bottom of the basin so that the heat does not make it fall
4. Once set and cool, remove them from the molds and serve as desired
5. Cakes of violet blossoms are also made in the same manner
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Gaufes (Waffles)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four fresh eggs
- equal amount of flour
- a little orange flower water
- a little finely chopped lemon zest
- half a quartilho of good milk
- wax

## Directions

1. Break eggs into a bowl and mix with flour, orange flower water, and lemon zest
2. After mixing, add half a quartilho of good milk and stir everything with a spoon until the batter is well liquid
3. Heat the waffle iron on a well-lit brazier
4. Rub the inside with wax so they do not stick
5. When the mold is hot, pour a small spoonful of batter inside, close it immediately, and place over the flame
6. When cooked on one side, flip the iron to finish cooking the other side
7. Remove carefully and place around a stick of dough so they fold
8. Store afterwards in a dry place or in a stove for use when needed
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Gaufes (Waffles)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four fresh eggs
- Equal amount of flour to the eggs
- A little orange flower water
- A little finely chopped lime peel
- Half a quart of good milk

## Directions

1. Break three or four fresh eggs into a bowl, dissolve them with the same amount of flour, a little orange flower water and a little finely chopped lime peel
2. After dissolving, pour half a quart of good milk, mix everything with a spoon until the mixture is well liquid and at its proper point
3. When ready, heat the iron in a well-lit furnace, pass it with wax on the part
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Gelado de Leite (Frozen Milk Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one canada of cow's milk
- one arratel of sugar
- some egg yolks
- essence such as orange flower, lemon, or bergamot

## Directions

1. Pour one canada of cow's milk into a pot and let it boil for five or six minutes
2. Remove from heat and season with one arratel of sugar
3. Beat in some egg yolks
4. Pass through a sieve
5. Place on heat a second time to warm, and when it begins to thicken, remove from heat
6. Add a few drops of essence, such as orange flower, lemon, or bergamot
7. When cold, pour into a mold of tin foil
8. Place this mold inside a bucket with pounded snow, salt, or saltpetre underneath, on top, and all around
9. When frozen and ready to serve, pass the mold quickly through hot water, and detaching what is inside, turn it carefully onto the plate on which it is to be served, so it may be eaten at that instant
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Gele (Aspic)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal feet
- Veal shank
- Chicken or old hens
- Turkey feet
- All glutinous parts
- Sugar
- Lemon juice
- Cinnamon
- Half quartilho of white wine
- Ten or twelve egg whites

## Directions

1. Take veal feet, shanks, chicken or old hens, turkey feet, and all glutinous parts according to the quantity of aspic desired
2. Soak, wash, and blanch them
3. Cook for three or four hours
4. When cooked and the broth reduced to the proper consistency, strain through a sieve
5. Remove all the fat
6. Season with sugar, lemon juice, cinnamon, and half quartilho of white wine
7. Bring back to the heat with ten or twelve beaten egg whites
8. Stir everything, and when it comes to a boil two or three times, add a little cold water
9. Strain through a cloth until clear
10. Pour into molds or glasses
11. Place in a cold place, and when set, serve as is customary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Gele de Raspas de Veado (Venison Aspic)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison scrapings
- Sugar
- Lemon juice
- Cinnamon
- White wine
- Egg whites

## Directions

1. Having scraped the venison, cook for five or six hours
2. When cooked and the broth reduced to a sufficiently strong consistency, strain everything through cloth and press
3. Season with sugar, lemon juice, cinnamon, and a little white wine if it seems good
4. Bring back to the heat with beaten egg whites
5. When it comes to a boil, strain two or three times through a cloth
6. When clear and set, serve as the others
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Geléa de Maçanis (Apple Jelly)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen apples
- sugar clarified and reduced to high point

## Directions

1. Take a dozen apples, cut them into quarters, and cook them in water
2. Once cooked, place them in a cloth, squeeze them very well over an earthenware vessel
3. Once well squeezed, pour this juice into clarified sugar reduced to high point
4. Place it to boil again until, placing the skimming spoon in it and raising it in the air and tilting it to one side, the sugar falls in the form of pearls
5. When it reaches this point, remove from heat, pour into small pots, allow to cool so it keeps better, cover with paper soaked in brandy and another paper on top, and keep in a cool place
6. In the same manner are made jelly of quinces and of pears
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Gingias (Candied Cherries)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- gingias or cherries
- sugar at high point

## Directions

1. Dip the gingias or cherries in sugar at high point and place over heat
2. Once it reaches four boilings, arrange them on the plate
3. Reduce the sugar, remove the foam from it, and pour it over the top
4. Serve cold
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ginjas de Calda (Cherries in Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- water

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from the cherries and weigh out one arratel and a quarter
2. Place in a pot over fire with boiling water
3. Add the cherries to the water, giving them a good boil for a little while
4. Remove the pot from the fire
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Ginjas de Calda (Sour Cherries in Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cherries
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from the sour cherries and weigh one and a quarter arratel of them
2. Place water in a pan on the fire and bring to a boil
3. Add the cherries and give them a good boil for a short time
4. Remove the pan from the fire and with a skimming spoon or ladle remove the cherries, placing them in a bowl with cold water
5. Leave them in the water for a quarter of an hour, then drain the water and add fresh cold water
6. After another quarter of an hour, remove them and place on a plate to drain until well drained
7. Clean one arratel of sugar and place it in the pan with the cherries inside, leaving them overnight
8. The next day place on the fire to thicken, and when a drop of sugar placed on a plate no longer runs, they are done
9. Place in small pans
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Glassa di Carne (Meat Glaze)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four arratels of veal
- a piece of ham
- simple broth, as much as needed
- a pinch of salt
- fine herbs
- one onion
- two or three cloves

## Directions

1. Cut the veal into three or four pieces and place in a bowl with a piece of ham, sear it, add as much simple broth as needed.
2. Season only with a pinch of salt and fine herbs, and one onion with two or three cloves, and let it simmer little by little.
3. When the meat is cooked, pass the broth through a sieve.
4. Return to heat until it reduces, taking care that it does not burn.
5. When it begins to stick, add half a spoon of broth and stir with a wooden spoon.
6. When it is dissolved and thickened to the necessary point and with good color, serve for all game dishes, gratins, fillets, and everything desired.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Glassa Real (Royal Glaze)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half an arratel of powdered sugar, sifted and of the finest
- one or two egg whites
- some drops of orange flower water
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Place in a glazed bowl half an arratel of powdered sugar, sifted and of the finest.
2. Add to it one or two egg whites, some drops of orange flower water, and the juice of one lemon, and dissolving everything with a wooden spoon and well beaten, when it reaches the necessary point and is white as snow, place it carefully on all the dishes where it is necessary.
3. Put these in a very mild oven, and as soon as the glaze is dry, serve for side dishes.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Gorazes e Cachuchos Cozidos (Boiled Gilt-head and Red Bream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- gilt-head or red bream
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- fine herbs

## Directions

1. Scale, remove entrails and gills, wash well the gilt-head or red bream
2. Season with salt
3. Simmer gently in a broth made with water, half a quart of vinegar, salt, and some fine herbs
4. When cooked, serve with Dutch sauce
5. If eating with oil sauce, serve garnished with parsley sauce around it
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Gorazes ou Cachuchos Guizados para uma Entrada (Braised Gilt-head or Red Bream as an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- gilt-head or red bream
- flour
- oil
- butter
- anchovy
- minced scallion
- minced parsley
- wine
- fish stock
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- capers
- vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and prepare the fish
2. Make incisions on each side
3. Dry and coat with flour
4. Fry in oil
5. After frying, set aside to drain
6. In a pan, melt a piece of butter, add some flour, stir with a spoon until well browned
7. Dissolve an anchovy, minced scallion, and minced parsley in the pan
8. Add a glass of wine and half a spoon of stock
9. Place the fried fish inside, season with salt, pepper, and a bay leaf
10. Let simmer gently
11. Add capers
12. Serve with a drop of vinegar or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Gradinhas (Veal Layered Loaf)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a knob of veal
- fine bacon cut into pieces
- bacon slices
- veal slices with bacon lard
- chopped meat
- beaten eggs
- strong, well-bound forcemeat
- truffles
- artichoke hearts
- sweetbreads or young squabs, chickens, frogs, quails, or any game

## Directions

1. Cut a knob of veal into slices and beat it between two cloths with the flat of a knife
2. Lard it with fine bacon cut into pieces
3. Line a casserole with bacon slices and arrange the veal slices with the larding facing down, joined with chopped meat and beaten eggs to make them stick together
4. Cover the inside completely with a strong, well-bound forcemeat
5. Place in the middle a ragout of truffles, artichoke hearts, and sweetbreads or, if lacking those, young squabs, chickens, frogs, quails, or any game that seems good, provided they are well-finished and of good taste
6. Cover with the same forcemeat and on top place some slices of veal, ham, and bacon, and put it to cook slowly between two fires
7. When cooked, turn it out onto a plate, remove the bacon slices and fat, and serve with essence sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Guizado de Lebre para Entrada (Hare Stew for an Entrée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- thick lard
- melted lard
- broth
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- onion studded with cloves
- bundle of parsley
- fine herbs
- liver
- blood
- juice of sour oranges

## Directions

1. Take a hare, clean it, and cut it into quarters
2. Lard these with thick lard, braise in melted lard, dust with flour, moisten with broth and white wine
3. Season with salt, pepper, an onion studded with cloves, a bundle of parsley, and fine herbs
4. When cooked and the sauce reduced and of good taste, remove the fat and serve with juice of sour oranges or also with the liver and blood as in the preceding recipe
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Guizado de Lebre para Huma Entrada (Hare Stew for an Entrée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- bacon planks
- broth
- quarter measure of wine
- salt
- pepper
- bundle of parsley
- thyme
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- onion studded with cloves
- head of garlic
- raw liver
- broth
- blood
- fine capers

## Directions

1. Prepare the hare as in the preceding recipe and set the blood aside, cut it into pieces
2. Put it to simmer in a small pan with bacon planks, a measure of broth, a quarter measure of wine, salt, pepper, a bundle of parsley, thyme, bay leaf, marjoram, an onion studded with cloves, and a head of garlic
3. After it is well cooked, remove the bacon planks, the bundle, the onion, and the garlic
4. Pound the raw liver and pass it with broth through a sieve, add it with the blood
5. Put the hare on the fire with little sauce, bind it gradually without letting it boil
6. Serve with fine capers on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Guizado de Lebres em Filetes (Hare Stew in Fillets)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- bacon planks
- butter or melted lard
- meat essence
- green onion
- pepper
- limes

## Directions

1. Stiffen the hare over coals and rub it with its own blood
2. Cover it with bacon planks, put it on the spit, and put it to roast, basting with butter or melted lard
3. After roasted, remove the meat and cut it into very thin fillets
4. Put them in meat essence, adding a little finely chopped green onion mixed with pepper, and when hot without boiling, serve with lime juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Hervas quaesquer que quizerem (Any Herbs as Desired)
body:
## Ingredients

- herbs as desired
- eggs, beaten
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Take any herbs and scald them in boiling water and braise them
2. When they are well scalded, fry them, and then pass them through beaten eggs, and in the manner of bundles layer them in a plate or frying pan until it is full with all the seasonings, braise gently and send them to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Jalea de Carne (Meat Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- one dozen sheep feet
- water
- white wine
- half arratel of sugar
- ten egg whites
- lemon juice from two lemons

## Directions

1. Cook a chicken and a dozen sheep feet in enough water so that when well cooked they release the bones
2. Add half a quart of white wine
3. When the broth reaches a quantity of one and a half quarts, remove the feet and chicken
4. Shred them in a cloth and press very well into a pan
5. Add half arratel of sugar and ten beaten egg whites with the juice of two lemons
6. Bring to a boil
7. When boiled, strain through cloth three or four times until the jelly comes out clear (if it is cold, strain between two braziers)
8. Pour into dishes and when set, send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lagostas Guizadas (Braised Lobsters)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster
- water
- salt
- butter
- sage
- chives
- truffles
- white wine
- cullis
- garlic
- olive oil
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Catch the lobsters, tie them with twine, and place in boiling salted water
2. Cook for half an hour, then remove and let cool
3. Open them, remove the intestine from the tail, and cut into equal fillets
4. Brown in butter with sage, chives, and chopped truffles
5. Add half a quartilho of white wine, half a tablespoon of cullis, a clove of garlic, and a drop of olive oil
6. Let everything simmer little by little
7. When the sauce is reduced and of good flavor, serve hot with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lagostas Guizadas (Stewed Lobsters)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lobsters
- butter
- sauce
- chives
- truffles
- half a quartilho of white wine
- half a spoon of culí
- one clove of garlic
- a drop of oil
- lime juice

## Directions

1. After the lobsters are cooked, let them cool
2. Open them and remove the intestine from the tail
3. Cut them into equal fillets
4. Brown them in butter with sauce, minced chives, and truffles
5. After browning, add half a quartilho of white wine, half a spoon of culí, one clove of garlic, and a drop of oil
6. Let everything simmer little by little
7. When the sauce is reduced and of good taste, serve the lobsters with lime juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lampreia Assada (Roasted Lamprey)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lamprey
- hot water
- wine
- orange juice
- nutmeg
- all seasonings

## Directions

1. Wash and clean a lamprey in hot water
2. Remove the teeth, bile, and gills
3. Make one cut in the middle of the gills and another at the navel so the intestines can be removed without dirtying the inside of the lamprey
4. Remove the intestines
5. Place the lamprey on a pan to drain the blood
6. Roast it on a spit
7. Collect the drippings in the same pan
8. When roasted, season the pan sauce with a splash of wine, juice of one orange, nutmeg, and all seasonings
9. Serve the sauce apart from the lamprey
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lampreia Assada no Espeto (Lamprey Roasted on a Spit)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamprey
- olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- fine herbs
- bread slices
- butter
- sweet sauce or pleasant sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the lamprey into equal pieces
2. Marinate in olive oil with salt, pepper, spices, and fine herbs
3. Cut an equal number of bread slices
4. Thread the lamprey pieces and bread slices alternately onto a small spit
5. Tie to another larger spit
6. Roast, basting with butter
7. When roasted, serve with sweet sauce or pleasant sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lampreia Cozida (Boiled Lamprey)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lamprey
- wine
- olive oil
- vinegar
- juice of two sour oranges
- garlic leeks
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- water

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare a lamprey as described above
2. Put a splash of wine and another of olive oil, vinegar and juice of two sour oranges in a pan
3. Let this mixture stay while six garlic leeks are sautéed with a splash of olive oil
4. When the leeks are half sautéed, put the whole lamprey inside
5. Cover it with water and cook
6. When almost cooked, add the sauce in which it was in with pepper, clove, and nutmeg
7. When cooked, serve it on slices
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lampreia em Conserva (Lamprey Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or many lampreys
- blood
- olive oil
- pepper
- salt
- half a quartilho of olive oil
- half a quartilho of white wine
- a little vinegar
- pepper
- nutmeg
- three or four dried bay leaves

## Directions

1. Clean lampreys as described
2. Roast them in the oven in a pan with their blood, a splash of olive oil, pepper, and salt on top
3. When roasted, remove them from the sauce
4. Boil this same sauce with half a quartilho of olive oil, half a quartilho of white wine, and a little vinegar so the sauce is not too strong
5. While boiling, season with pepper, nutmeg, and three or four dried bay leaves
6. Let it cool
7. Put the lampreys in a barrel
8. Pour the cold sauce over them, covering them well with the sauce
9. Seal the barrel
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lamprêa Assada nas Grelhas (Lamprey Roasted on the Grill)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One lamprey, cleaned and trimmed
- Butter, melted
- Parsley
- Minced chives
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Bread crumb, finely grated
- Sweet sauce or hot remoulade sauce with mustard

## Directions

1. Clean and trim the lamprey
2. Cut into four pieces or curl it
3. Pass it for an instant through melted butter with parsley, minced chives, seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
4. Cover with finely grated bread crumb
5. Place on the grill
6. Roast and baste on both sides until of good color
7. Serve with sweet sauce or hot remoulade sauce with mustard
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lamprêa Assada no Espeto (Lamprey Roasted on the Spit)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One lamprey cut into equal pieces
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Fine herbs

## Directions

1. Cut the lamprey into equal pieces
2. Marinate in oil with salt, pepper, spices, and fine herbs
3. Cut the same amount of other ingredients to match
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lamprêa Guizada com Molho Doce (Lamprey Braised with Sweet Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One lamprey, bled and cleaned
- Red wine
- A piece of browned butter
- Sugar in stone form
- Cinnamon
- Salt
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Bleed, clean, and prepare the lamprey as in the previous recipe
2. Cut it into pieces
3. Simmer it in red wine with a piece of browned butter, sugar in stone form, cinnamon, and salt
4. When cooked, add the blood
5. Stir so that it does not curdle
6. Give it a simmer
7. When bound, serve with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lamprêa Guizada para Huma Entrada (Lamprey Braised as an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One lamprey, bled
- The blood of the lamprey
- Hot water
- Butter
- Flour
- Browned flour
- A little wine
- A spoon of broth
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- A bunch of fine herbs
- Onion with three cloves
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Bleed and set aside the blood of one lamprey
2. Place the lamprey in hot water
3. After being scalded and cleaned with the back of a knife, remove the tripe
4. Cut into equal pieces
5. Braise for an instant in butter with browned flour
6. Add a little wine and a spoon of broth
7. Season with salt, pepper, nutmeg, a bunch of fine herbs, and onion with three cloves
8. Let simmer little by little
9. When cooked and the sauce reduced, add the blood
10. After binding, serve hot with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Leborada (Hare Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh hare, not washed
- hare offal
- water
- melted lard, cleaned of cracklings
- herbs, minced
- onion
- toasted bread crumbs the size of an egg
- vinegar
- clove
- pepper
- saffron
- cumin
- good wine
- bread slices
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Skin a fresh hare without washing it and remove the offal, which after cleaning of hairs should be washed of blood
2. Place the hare to braise in a pot with the same water in which the offal was washed of blood, with melted lard cleaned of cracklings, minced herbs, and onion
3. Once cooked, add a piece of toasted bread crumbs (the size of an egg) crumbled in vinegar, with spices, clove, pepper, a little saffron, cumin, and good wine
4. Place on a plate over slices of bread and send to the table with slices and juice of sour lemon
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lebre Armada (Armed Hare)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- two arratels of mutton
- quarter of lard
- herbs
- butter
- cheese soup
- lard slices

## Directions

1. Remove the meat from a hare's loin and legs, leaving the bones intact, and mince it with two arratels of mutton, a quarter of lard, and some herbs
2. Once all is minced, arrange it in a buttered frying pan, so the hare retains its original shape
3. Finally arrange lard slices on top and send to the table over cheese soup
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lebre Assada com Pelle (Hare Roasted with Skin)
body:
## Ingredients

- young hare
- lard slices
- salt
- pepper
- twine

## Directions

1. Skin the young hare in fresh water, removing only the down
2. After cleaning and washing it well, stuff it between the skin and the meat with some slices of lard seasoned with salt and pepper
3. Tie it well with twine and place on the spit to braise
4. Once roasted, remove the twine and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lebre Cozida (Cooked Hare)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- quarter of lard
- two heads of garlic
- herbs
- clove
- pepper
- ginger
- cumin
- vinegar
- hare blood

## Directions

1. Carefully drain the blood from a hare
2. After washing it well, place it to cook with a quarter of lard, two heads of garlic, and some herbs
3. Season with clove, pepper, ginger, cumin, and vinegar
4. Once cooked, add the blood
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lebre em Potagem (Hare in Pottage)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare, well washed and larded
- lard
- onions
- herbs
- seasonings
- wine
- capers
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Roast a well-washed and larded hare
2. Once roasted, mince a little lard and place it to melt in a tall pot
3. Once melted, add two onions, all the herbs, and all the seasonings
4. Once braised, remove the cracklings with a skimming spoon and add half a quartilho of wine, seasoning with everything necessary
5. Pour this pottage over the hare, which should be placed on a plate, with capers and lemon slices on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Letria de Ovos (Egg Threads)
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of sugar in high point
- one dozen egg yolks beaten
- soft eggs or almond cakes

## Directions

1. Once one arratel of sugar is at high point, gradually pour in one dozen beaten egg yolks through an eggshell with a small hole in the bottom so the egg flows in a thin thread
2. While pouring, stir the sugar pot very well so the sugar does not soften its consistency
3. Once all egg is poured, place the threads on a plate, open with a fork, and add soft eggs or almond cakes in the middle, serve at table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lingoiça (Portuguese Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat from the legs and loins (or all lean pork meat)
- some bacon
- pepper
- cloves
- white wine
- garlic
- salt
- a little vinegar

## Directions

1. Put pork meat from the legs and loins, cut very finely, into a glazed bowl with some bacon, pepper, and cloves on top
2. Keep in a preserve of white wine, garlic, salt, and a little vinegar for two or three days, in quantity equal to the meat
3. When ready to make the sausages, place a clean board across the bowl and put the meat on it to drain the brine
4. Make the sausages
5. Hang them to dry in the chimney
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lingoiça Assada (Roasted Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- sausage
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Roast one rod of sausage
2. Cut into small pieces
3. Serve with pepper and lemon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lingoiça de Outro Modo (Sausage - Second Preparation)
body:
## Ingredients

- sausage
- eggs
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the sausage into slices
2. Bind with two eggs
3. Form into a cartouche and brown it well
4. Serve with pepper and lemon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Linguado cozido (Boiled Sole)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one large sole
- broth or court-bouillon
- sauce à la Hollandaise or oil, vinegar, pepper, and salt

## Directions

1. Scale, clean, and trim a large sole
2. Make an incision along the back from head to tail
3. Put it to cook in a broth or court-bouillon like other fish, letting it simmer slowly
4. When cooked, serve with sauce à la Hollandaise, or when that is not available, eat with sauce of good oil, vinegar, pepper, and salt
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Linguados Assados com Funcho (Flounder Roasted with Fennel)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 flounders
- salt
- pepper
- melted butter
- fennel branches
- anchovies
- browned butter
- a drop of wine
- half a spoon of cullis

## Directions

1. Scale, clean, and wash 2 or 3 flounders
2. Cut off the heads and the tips of the tails
3. Open them along the back
4. Season with a little salt and pepper
5. Pass them through melted butter
6. Place them to roast slowly on grills with fennel branches underneath
7. When roasted on both sides, serve with a sauce of anchovies dissolved in browned butter, a drop of wine, and half a spoon of cullis
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Linguados em Polpetas e Fritos (Flounder Patties, Fried)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 flounders
- finely chopped forcemeat
- butter
- 4 onion slices
- a drop of white wine
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- buttered paper
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- clarified butter
- fried sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare 2 or 3 flounders and remove the fillets
2. Cover them with a little finely chopped and well-bound forcemeat
3. After rolling and tying them, arrange in a small casserole with butter underneath, 4 onion slices, a drop of white wine, seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
4. Cover with buttered paper and simmer for half an hour with fire below and above
5. Remove from the casserole, pass twice through beaten eggs covered with breadcrumbs
6. Fry in clarified butter
7. Serve after frying with fried sauce around it
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Linguados feitos em Vinho branco (Sole in White Wine)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- soles
- salt
- pepper
- one onion studded with cloves
- bay leaves
- fine herbs
- six small green onions
- lemon slice
- butter
- white wine
- broth
- bread crumbs
- orange or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the soles, cut off heads and tails, wash and trim them
2. Arrange them in a pan, whole or in portions, with salt, pepper, one onion studded with cloves, bay leaf, fine herbs, six small green onions, a slice of lemon, a piece of butter, a quarter-pot of white wine, and a little broth
3. Put on a well-heated stove to simmer
4. When cooked, thicken with a little finely grated bread crumbs and a piece of washed butter
5. Serve after thickening with a little sauce and orange or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Linguados Fritos para Prato de Alface (Fried Sole for Lettuce Dish)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- sole
- finely ground salt
- flour
- clarified butter or hot oil

## Directions

1. Scale, clean, and wash the sole thoroughly
2. Season with finely ground salt
3. Open along the sides with the tip of a knife, from head to tail
4. Dry very well in cloth
5. Coat with flour
6. Fry in clarified butter or very hot oil
7. When fried and golden in color, serve garnished with fried parsley
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Linguados Recheados para Dia de Carne (Stuffed Flounder for Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 flounders
- bacon strips
- onion slices
- ham slices
- chicken breasts
- fish loins
- calf udders, cooked
- bacon
- beef fat
- bread soaked in milk
- egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- Spanish sauce or clear essence sauce
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Clean, prepare, and remove the backbones from 2 or 3 flounders as described above
2. Line the bottom of a baking dish with bacon strips, onion slices, and some ham slices
3. Place the flounders over all this
4. Stuff them with a forcemeat made of chicken breasts, fish loins fried, cooked calf udders, bacon, and beef fat, all seasoned and bound with breadcrumbs soaked in milk and egg yolks
5. When stuffed, cover with grated bread
6. Place in the oven to cook
7. After cooked and browned, serve with Spanish sauce or clear essence sauce with lime juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Linguados Recheados para Dia de Peixe (Stuffed Flounder for Fish Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- flounders
- butter
- onion slices
- finely chopped forcemeat à crème
- clarified butter
- egg yolks
- sour orange juice or lime juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the flounders as stated, cut off the heads and tips of the tails
2. Open them through the middle
3. Remove the backbone
4. Place in a baking dish with butter and onion slices underneath
5. Stuff with a finely chopped and well-bound forcemeat à crème
6. Brush with clarified butter and egg yolks, all mixed together
7. Place in the oven or under the cover of a baking dish to cook
8. When cooked and well-colored, serve with anchovy sauce and cullis, and with sour orange juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Linguados à Borgonhona (Sole à la Burgundy)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- soles
- butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- garlic clove
- bay leaves
- thyme
- marjoram
- red wine
- sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Clean, trim, and wash the soles, cut them into equal portions
2. Put in a pan with a piece of washed butter dusted with flour
3. Season with salt, pepper, a garlic clove, a bundle of bay leaf with sprigs of thyme, a bay leaf, and sprigs of marjoram
4. Add a quarter-pot of good red wine and put to simmer slowly
5. When cooked and the sauce is reduced, remove the herb bundle and serve with sour orange sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Linguados à Provençal (Sole à la Provençal)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- soles
- salt
- oil
- flour
- butter
- flour
- small onion
- mushrooms
- parsley
- garlic clove
- wine
- substance or broth
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- onion
- cloves
- capers
- sour orange or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and trim the soles, open them along the back with the point of a knife from head to tail
2. Sprinkle with a little salt, dry them, flour them, and fry in oil
3. When fried, in a pan melt a piece of butter with a handful of flour and stir with a wooden spoon over the fire
4. When browned, sauté in it a small onion, some mushrooms, parsley, a garlic clove, all finely chopped
5. After everything is sautéed, add a glass of wine and a little substance or broth, season with salt, pepper, a bay leaf, and an onion with two cloves
6. Let it simmer slowly; when this sauce is reduced, cut the heads and tails from the fried soles and put them to simmer two or three minutes in this sauce with finely chopped capers on top, and serve afterward with sour orange or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Linguas de Carneira ao Gratémi (Sheep Tongues au Gratin)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one silver or other heat-proof plate
- two handfuls of grated breadcrumb interior
- one piece of butter or scraped bacon
- four raw egg yolks
- salt
- chopped chives
- a little broth tempered with salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Take a silver plate or other heat-proof plate and place at the bottom a little forcemeat made with two handfuls of grated breadcrumb interior, one piece of butter or scraped bacon, four raw egg yolks, salt, chopped chives, and a little broth tempered with salt and pepper
2. Mix everything well and line the bottom of the plate
3. Place over gentle heat until it sets
4. Drain off the fat and arrange the cooked sheep tongues on top with their sauce from above or any other sauce that seems better
5. They can also be served after cooking on a silver plate with a little broth, grated cheese, and browned in the oven
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Linguas de Carneiro (Lamb Tongues)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb tongues
- pork fat (toucinho)
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cook the tongues
2. Cut lengthwise from top to bottom
3. Fry in pork fat with pepper and lemon on top
4. Serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lombas de Porco Montez por Différentes Modos (Wild Boar Loins Prepared Various Ways)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Lombos de porco montez (wild boar loins)
- Toucinho fino (fine bacon)
- Molho de peverada ligada (bound pepper sauce)

## Directions

1. Cut the loins along their entire length and trim of all small skins that cover them
2. Lard with fine bacon
3. After marinating and roasting, serve with bound pepper sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lombo de Porco (Pork Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork loin
- fat and bones removed
- bacon lards
- salt
- pepper
- bacon
- water
- onion
- cloves
- wine
- vinegar
- toasted flour
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. After removing the fat and bones from the loin, cut it into pieces of three quarters each
2. Season with salt and pepper, and lard the pieces of loin lengthwise with thick bacon lards
3. Fry them in a pot with melted bacon, cleaned of scraps, so they become well browned
4. Cook them in water in a pot, and when they are half cooked, add an onion studded with cloves, a little wine, a splash of vinegar
5. After cooking, add a little toasted flour dissolved in the same broth of the loin to make the sauce thick
6. Send to the table on toasted bread slices
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lombo de Vaca de Empada de Espeto (Beef Loin Roasted on a Spit with Pastry Crust)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin from preserved stock
- thin pastry dough
- butter

## Directions

1. After the beef loin has been preserved, roast it on a spit
2. When roasted, cover it with a thin sheet of dough
3. Wrap it in buttered paper and return it to the fire until the dough is cooked
4. When cooked, remove the paper, place it on a plate, and send to the table
5. This same method can be done with any roast
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lombo de Vaca em Manguito (Beef Loin in Sleeve)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- bacon, chopped
- parsley
- small onions
- mushrooms
- garlic
- shallots
- salt
- pepper
- twine
- bacon slices
- paper

## Directions

1. Take a beef loin and after preparing it, pierce it with a stick from one end to the other
2. With a knife enlarge the hole and fill it with chopped bacon, parsley, small onions, mushrooms, garlic, shallots, salt, and pepper
3. Tie both ends together with twine
4. Place it to roast covered with bacon slices and paper on top
5. When roasted, serve with spicy shallot sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lombo de Vitela Estofada (Braised Veal Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal loin, cleaned of nerves and skin
- half arratel of lard
- quarter pound of butter
- quarter litre of white wine
- two heads of garlic
- vinegar
- water
- whole pepper
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- half dozen cloves
- two bay leaves
- some quarters of quince

## Directions

1. Once a veal loin is cleaned of nerves and skin, place it in a pan with half arratel of lard, quarter pound of butter, quarter litre of white wine
2. Add two heads of garlic, a splash of vinegar, a splash of water, whole pepper, cinnamon, nutmeg, half dozen cloves, two bay leaves, and some quarters of quince
3. Seal the pan and cook slowly over gentle heat
4. Once cooked, season as needed
5. Place the loin on slices of bread and send to the table
6. This dish can also be made larded, and in the same manner one makes venison and loin of any meat
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Lombos de Linguados Assados no Espeto, para Dias de Carne (Flounder Loins Roasted on Skewer, for Meat Days)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- flounder loins
- raw chopped forcemeat made of chicken breasts
- scraped bacon
- sauce
- shallots
- egg yolks
- bread soaked in milk
- bacon strip
- buttered paper
- breadcrumbs
- piquant sauce or clear essence
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Remove the loins from the flounders and cut them into equal fillets
2. Cover them with a little raw chopped forcemeat made of chicken breasts, scraped bacon, sauce, and shallots, seasoned and bound with egg yolks and bread soaked in milk and well squeezed
3. Roll them like patties and cover with a strip of bacon
4. Put in a small skewer, tie to another larger skewer, cover with a sheet of buttered paper
5. Place to roast
6. After roasting, remove the paper, cover with fine breadcrumbs, and place to brown
7. When browned, serve with piquant sauce or clear essence and lime juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lombos de Rodovalho de frencando para dia de carne (Turbot Loins Braised for a Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot loins, larded
- white wine
- water
- salt
- onion slices
- lemon slices
- fine herbs
- veal glaze
- ham
- cullis
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. With the loins larded as described above, serve them briefly in white wine and water, seasoned with salt, onion slices, lemon slices, and fine herbs
2. When they are barely cooked, finish cooking them larded-side down over a glaze made of veal and ham
3. When they are browned as they should be, remove them and break down with a little cullis what remains stuck to the bottom of the pan
4. Pass through a sieve onto the plate where they are to go on the table and serve with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lombos ou Filetes de Linguado à Basílica (Flounder Loins or Fillets Basilica Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 or 12 flounder fillets
- salt
- pepper
- wine
- white vinegar
- onion slices
- garlic
- bay leaf
- thyme
- marjoram
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- pork butter
- fried sauce

## Directions

1. Marinate 10 or 12 flounder fillets in salt, pepper, wine, white vinegar, onion slices, garlic, bay leaf, thyme, and marjoram
2. After these have taken on the flavor of the marinade, place on a cloth and dry them
3. Pass 2 or 3 times through beaten eggs covered with breadcrumbs
4. Fry in pork butter
5. When fried, serve garnished with fried sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lombos, Polgares, e Pás de Veado (Loins, Tenderloins, and Feet of Venison)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- loins of venison
- fine lard
- paper with butter
- meat essence sauce
- soft roe
- capers
- vinegar
- vinegar
- sugar
- little salt
- four cloves
- cinnamon
- limes

## Directions

1. Lift the loins from one end to the other and after trimming all the skins, lard them with fine lard
2. Put them on a skewer, wrap them in buttered paper, tie them to a large spit, and put them to roast
3. When roasted, remove the paper and having taken on good colour, serve them with meat essence sauce with soft roe, capers, and a touch of vinegar
4. They can also be made with a sweet sauce made with vinegar, sugar, little salt, four cloves, cinnamon, and a little limes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Lombs de Porco Montez por Diferentes Modos (Wild Boar Loins in Different Ways)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar loins
- fine lard
- salt
- pepper
- peverada sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the loins along their entire length and trim all the thin membranes that cover them
2. Lard them with fine lard
3. After marinating and roasting, serve with peverada sauce thickened
4. Alternatively, lard them with thick lard well seasoned, as with the leg meat, and make them into pies lined with chopped lard underneath and strips of fine lard on top; serve cold as an intermediate course
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Láparo em Papilhotas (Rabbit in Parchment)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- garlic clove, finely chopped
- salt
- pepper
- paper sheets
- thin slices of bacon
- rabbit quarters

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit into quarters
2. Season the finely chopped garlic with salt and pepper
3. On each piece of paper cut into quarters, place a thin slice of bacon with some of the seasoned garlic mixture and a rabbit quarter in the middle with another portion of the mixture, then another slice of bacon
4. Wrap the paper around the rabbit pieces tightly to form parchment packages
5. Do the same with the remaining pieces
6. Place them on a grill with a low flame over a sheet of paper greased with olive oil
7. Once roasted on both sides, serve them dry in their parchment packages as an appetizer
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Láparos em Papilhota (Hares in Papillote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- salt
- pepper
- hare livers
- bacon, scraped
- sage
- chives
- truffles
- shallot
- garlic clove
- paper
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Clean the hares and cut into four or five pieces
2. Break the larger bones with the flat of the knife and season with little salt and pepper
3. Make a forcemeat with the livers of the same hares, scraped bacon, sage, chives, truffles, and shallot, with one garlic clove finely chopped, seasoned with salt and pepper
4. Take sheets of paper and cut into quarters
5. On each one place a small strip of bacon with a little of the forcemeat, and the piece of hare in the middle with the same amount of forcemeat and another strip of bacon
6. Wrap the paper carefully in the shape of a papillote
7. Do the same with the remaining pieces
8. Place to roast on a grill over gentle heat and over a sheet of paper brushed with olive oil
9. After roasting both sides, serve dry in their papillotes for an appetizer
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Língua de Vaca ao Natural (Beef Tongue Natural)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- bacon, thick slices
- parsley
- bay leaf
- thyme
- marjoram
- onion studded with cloves
- celery
- carrots
- parsnips
- white wine
- one cup
- salt
- pepper
- water or broth

## Directions

1. Blanch the beef tongue in boiling water
2. Remove the skin and let it cool
3. Lard it with thick bacon and place it to cook in a light braise
4. Add a bouquet of parsley, bay leaf, thyme, marjoram, an onion studded with cloves, vegetables such as celery, carrots, and parsnips, one cup of white wine, a bacon slice, salt, and pepper
5. Moisten with water or broth
6. When cooked, split it lengthwise along the middle without separating it
7. Serve with a sauce of chopped parsley, a little broth, salt, coarse pepper, and a splash of vinegar
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Língua de Vaca Assada (Roasted Beef Tongue)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- bacon, fine slices
- shallot sauce or sweet sauce
- vinegar
- sugar
- cloves
- cinnamon stick

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue in a light braise as above
2. Remove the skin and let it cool
3. Lard it with fine bacon
4. Place it on a spit and roast until the bacon is colored
5. Serve with shallot sauce or sweet sauce made with vinegar, sugar, cloves, and whole cinnamon
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Língua de Vaca em Popietas (Beef Tongue Roulade)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- forcemeat of veal or chicken
- bacon slice
- breadcrumbs
- spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue until nearly done as above
2. Remove the skin and let it cool
3. Cut into thin, equal slices lengthwise
4. Cover each slice with raw forcemeat of veal or chicken about the size of a new crown coin in silver
5. Roll each slice over itself and cover with a bacon slice
6. Spit them on a small spit covered with paper
7. When nearly roasted, remove the paper
8. Cover with breadcrumbs and when colored, serve with spicy sauce or as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Língua de Vaca à Polonesa (Beef Tongue Polish Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- sugar
- orange peel candied
- cinnamon pieces
- sugar
- wine
- half pound
- one quarter measure
- substance

## Directions

1. Prepare the beef tongue and place it in boiling water to remove the skin
2. Once peeled, place it to cook in a braise
3. When cooked, remove it and split it in the middle without separating it
4. Lard it with candied orange peel that has been dusted with sugar and with small pieces of fine cinnamon
5. Put half a pound of sugar, one quarter measure of wine, and a little substance in a pan and bring to a boil
6. Place the tongue inside to cook gently
7. When the sauce is reduced and made, remove it to its plate and pour the sauce over it
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Língua de Vacca de Fumo (Smoked Beef Tongue)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- salt
- smoke

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as pig tongues, with the difference that they should not be blanched, but only placed gently in cold water
2. Prepare and arrange the thick end of the tongue very well
3. Once well dried, salt it and leave it for two, three, or four days in salt water
4. Thread it through the thin point and hang it in the chimney in the smoke so that the smoke penetrates it well and it dries thoroughly
5. Prepared this way it will keep as long as desired, and cooks in the same manner as the other or pig tongues
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Língua de Vacca à Parmezã (Beef Tongue with Parmesan)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- broth
- Champagne wine or other generous white wine
- reduction
- Parmesan cheese, grated
- clear broth

## Directions

1. After preparing and skinning the tongue, cook it in broth
2. Cut into slices and arrange in a casserole with broth, half a quarter-liter of Champagne wine or other generous white wine, and half a spoonful of reduction
3. Let it boil until little sauce remains
4. Pour half of this sauce into the bottom of the serving dish, sprinkling Parmesan cheese on top
5. Arrange the tongue slices on top of this, pour the remaining sauce over them, and cover everything with grated Parmesan
6. Place to brown in the oven or under the lid of a covered pan
7. When well browned, serve, first pouring a little clear broth over it
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Línguas de Vaca Lampareadas (Braised Beef Tongues)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- bacon
- wine
- vinegar
- seasonings
- toasted flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. When a tongue is cooked and cut into slices, place it to braise in a pot with half an arratel of melted bacon, cleaned of scraps
2. Add a splash of wine and another of vinegar, and all the seasonings
3. After braising, add a little toasted flour to make the sauce thick and brown, and sugar if desired
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Manjar Branco (White Almond Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- a chicken breast very well cooked, shredded and broken apart
- two canadadas of milk
- two arratels of sugar
- one and a quarter arratels of rice flour
- half a canadada of milk for cooling
- one arratel of sugar
- flower water

## Directions

1. Once a chicken breast is very well cooked, shred it and break it apart with a spoon in a pot.
2. Add two canadadas of milk, two arratels of sugar, and one and a quarter arratels of rice flour and mix while cooking.
3. While cooking, add little by little half a canadada of milk for cooling and one arratel of sugar.
4. When cooked (you can tell by inserting the point of a knife and it comes away clean), add flower water.
5. Remove from heat and cut into pieces.
6. In the same way it can be made with fish or lobster instead of chicken.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Manjar Branco Amarello (Yellow White Almond Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- a chicken breast shredded and broken apart
- one and a half canadadas of milk
- two arratels of sugar
- one arratel of rice flour
- half a canadada of milk added little by little
- a dozen egg yolks
- flower water

## Directions

1. Once a chicken breast is more than half cooked, shred it and break it apart with a spoon in a pot.
2. Cook with one and a half canadadas of milk, two arratels of sugar, and one arratel of rice flour.
3. While cooking, add little by little half a canadada of milk.
4. When almost cooked, add soft eggs, a quantity of a dozen egg yolks.
5. After they are cooked, add flower water, remove from heat, and put on the plate.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Manjar Branco Amarelo (Yellow Almond Blancmange)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken breast, partially cooked and shredded
- one and a half canada of milk
- two arratels of sugar
- one arratel of rice flour
- half canada of milk (for cooling)
- egg yolks, about a dozen
- flower water

## Directions

1. Once a chicken breast is more than halfway cooked, shred it and break it down with a spoon in a pan
2. Mix with one and a half canada of milk, two arratels of sugar, and one arratel of rice flour
3. Put on to cook; while cooking, add half canada of milk little by little
4. When almost cooked, add soft eggs, about a dozen yolks
5. After cooking, add flower water
6. Remove from heat and serve on a plate
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Manjar Branco Assado (Roasted Almond Blancmange)
body:
## Ingredients

- almond blancmange
- one or two eggs
- some flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat almond blancmange very well with one or two eggs and some flour
2. Drop spoon by spoon into a baking pan that is placed over hot coals
3. Space each bit of blancmange apart from the others, separating them with flour below
4. Cover with a lid of hot coals and roast
5. When roasted, place on a plate with scraped rock sugar on top and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Manjar Branco Banhado (Bathed White Almond Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- a chicken breast half cooked, shredded
- cinnamon
- half a canadada of milk
- one and a quarter arratels of arratel

## Directions

1. Shred a chicken breast half cooked and cook in a pot with cinnamon and half a canadada of milk and one and a quarter arratels.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Manjar Branco Banhado (Coated Almond Blancmange)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken breast, lightly cooked and shredded
- one and a half canada of milk
- one and a half arratels of rice flour
- three arratels of sugar in pieces

## Directions

1. Shred a lightly cooked chicken breast
2. Put to cook in a pan with one and a half canada of milk and one and a half arratels of rice flour
3. While cooking, add three arratels of sugar in pieces little by little, piece by piece
4. When cooked, place in a basin
5. Shape into small balls the shape of Zaragoza plums
6. Put in the oven to brown and cover with sugar
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Manjar Branco de Gele de Carne (Blanc Mange with Meat Aspic)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half arratel of sweet almonds
- Half dozen bitter almonds
- Milk
- Aspic broth
- Sugar
- Two cloves
- One stick of cinnamon
- Lemon zest
- Salt
- Orange flower water

## Directions

1. Grind in a stone mortar half arratel of sweet almonds and half dozen bitter almonds, moistening from time to time with a little milk
2. After ground and dissolved in two or three spoonfuls of aspic broth, season with sugar, two cloves, one stick of cinnamon, lemon zest, and salt
3. Place on the heat, and when hot, strain with force two or three times through a cloth
4. Add a little milk to it
5. Pour into its dish and add some drops of orange flower water
6. Pass a sheet of white paper over it, and when cold and set, serve for a side dish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Manjar Branco de Peras (Almond Blancmange with Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- half canada of milk
- half dozen large pears or one dozen small pears, cleaned, peeled, and without seeds
- two canadas of milk
- two arratels of sugar
- one arratel of rice flour
- half canada of milk (for cooling)
- flower water

## Directions

1. Put half canada of milk on to cook with half dozen large pears or one dozen small pears, cleaned, peeled, and without seeds
2. When halfway cooked, place in a pan and break down with a spoon
3. Add two canadas of milk, two arratels of sugar, and one arratel of rice flour
4. Put on to cook; while cooking, cool with half canada of milk added little by little
5. When cooked, add flower water
6. Remove from heat and serve on plates
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Manjar Branco à Portuguesa (Portuguese Blanc Mange)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One canful of milk
- Five quarters of rice flour
- Breast of a half-cooked hen
- Salt
- One and a half pounds of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve in a pot with one canful of milk five quarters of rice flour, to which add the breast of a half-cooked hen
2. Having well minced it, season with salt and one and a half pounds of powdered sugar
3. After everything is mixed and strained through a sieve, place on high heat, stirring always with great speed with a copper spatula
4. As it thickens, add gradually another canful of milk
5. When the flour is cooked and at the proper consistency, before it cools, pour into small dishes
6. Put in the oven to brown, and serve hot or cold, as desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Manjar Real (Royal Almond Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- a chicken breast half cooked and shredded
- the crumb of a vintem of bread, grated
- one quarter of well pounded almonds
- two and a half arratels of sugar at espadana stage

## Directions

1. Put a chicken breast half cooked and shredded in a pot and break it up very well with a spoon.
2. Mix with the crumb of a vintem of bread grated, one quarter of well pounded almonds, and two and a half arratels of sugar at espadana stage.
3. After beating everything together, cook over a gentle flame until it thickens.
4. Once thick, put on plates and send to the table.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Manjar Real à Portuguesa (Portuguese Royal Blanc Mange)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One hen
- Salt
- Bread crumbs
- One arratel of sweet almonds
- Four arratels of sugar
- Chicken breast

## Directions

1. Pluck a hen in hot water, clean it, and wash it, and put it to cook in water with a little salt
2. When almost cooked, strain the broth through a sieve and remove the fat
3. Soak the crumb of two vintém pieces of bread in it, and when well soaked, squeeze through cloth
4. Pour it into a mortar in which there is one arratel of well-ground sweet almonds
5. Grinding everything again, strain through a sieve, removing the chicken breast
6. Pour everything into four arratels of sugar at the point of hardening
7. Bring to gentle heat, stirring always with a spoon until it incorporates to proper consistency
8. Pour into dishes or small bowls, and serve when it seems good
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Marmelada (Quince Paste)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the quinces in water, and when almost cooked, place them in cold water
2. Once cool, clean them inside, peel them, break them up with a wooden spoon, pass them through a sieve, and take to heat what passed through, stirring always so it does not stick
3. Once dry, add sugar at the point of thin hair, mix very well with the spoon, take it to heat again, stirring always, and remove from fire once it rises to a boil
4. Pour into small earthenware pots or covilhetes, allow to cool, cover, and keep for when necessary
5. In the same manner are made pears, apples, and peaches
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Marmellada Commum (Common Quince Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the quinces into quarters, clean and trim them without seeds or core
2. Cook until soft, then pass through a fine sieve
3. Place two arratels of sugar at thread point with one arratel of the quince mass
4. Once the mass is well dissolved in the sugar, place the pan on the fire just until it comes to a boil
5. Remove from the fire and add amber or musk if desired
6. Place in small paper cones and set to dry in the sun
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Marmellada Crúa (Raw Quince Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- quince

## Directions

1. Take two arratels of clean sugar and bring to ball point
2. Add one arratel of cooked quince that has been trimmed, cut into slices, and weighed
3. As soon as the sugar reaches ball point, break apart the quince slices by hand into small pieces and add to the sugar pan away from the fire
