4. Mix the quince in the sugar very well with a spoon
5. Return to the fire, stirring so the quince does not stick to the bottom of the pan
6. Once it boils a little and makes bubbles, remove from the fire and place in small paper cones
7. If desired, add fragrances when the sugar is at the right point and before adding the quince
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Marmellada de Gálea (Quince Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean and trim the quinces without seeds or core and weigh four arratels of them
2. Place to cook in two arratels of sugar until thick
3. Once thick, place everything in a cloth and squeeze into the same pan, pressing with a press
4. Place the pan with the strained liquid back on the fire to cook until thicker, at which point the jelly is done
5. Remove from the fire and add fragrances if desired
6. Place in glasses if it is fine
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Marmellada de Sumos (Quince Juice Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place two arratels of quinces in quarters in three arratels of sugar at light point so they cook in it
2. As they cook, break them apart with a spoon
3. Once well cooked and dry, add fragrance if desired
4. Place in small paper cones and set to dry in the sun
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa de Amêndoas (Almond Paste)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- hot water
- egg white
- a little orange blossom water
- very fine sugar

## Directions

1. Steep the almonds in hot water, peel them, pound them in a stone mortar, adding from time to time a little beaten egg white and a small spoonful of orange blossom water
2. When pounded and reduced to a paste, dry at the fire with very fine sugar, stirring constantly with a spoon until it separates from the pot
3. Spread on the table with powdered sugar, and it serves for tarts, tartlets, bases for croquants, boxes, small baskets, flowers, and other different works
4. The scraps pounded with egg whites to soften them serve to fry, to bake in the oven, and for various garnishes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa de Amêndoas (Almond Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- hot water
- egg white
- a small spoonful of orange blossom water
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Pass the almonds through hot water, peel them, pound them in a stone mortar, adding from time to time a little beaten egg white and a small spoonful of orange blossom water
2. When pounded and reduced to paste, dry it on the fire with fine sugar, stirring constantly with a spoon until it comes away from the pot
3. Then pour it onto the table with powdered sugar, and serve for tarts, tartlets, bottoms of croquants, boxes, small baskets, flowers, and other different works
4. The trimmings pounded with egg whites to soften them serve for frying, for cooking in the oven, and for various garnishes
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa Folhada (Puff Pastry)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One eighth of flour
- Water
- Salt
- Butter, equal amount to the dough

## Directions

1. Spread on a table one eighth of flour, knead with water and salt, little by little, in such a way that after being bound it is neither soft nor hard
2. Work it and let it rest for half an hour
3. Then spread with a rolling pin until it is the thickness of a finger
4. Spread on top an equal amount of butter as there is dough, roll up very well so that the butter remains enclosed inside
5. Spread with the rolling pin and continue doing the same three or four times
6. Finally reduce to the thickness that seems necessary, and it will serve for meat tarts, sweet tarts, small pastries, timbales, fried pastries, and other puff pastry pieces
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa Folhada (Puff Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- one eighth of flour
- water
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Place on the table one eighth of flour, knead it with water and a little salt, in such a way that after being bound it be neither soft nor hard
2. Knead and let it rest half an hour
3. Afterwards stretch it with a roller until it reaches the thickness of a finger
4. Place on top of it an equal amount of butter as there is dough, and roll it very well so that the butter remains enclosed inside
5. Stretch it with the roller, continue doing the same three or four times
6. Finally reduce it to the thickness that seems necessary, and it will serve for meat tarts, sweet tarts, small pastries, timbales, fried dishes, and other oven pieces
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa Frole (Frole Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- fine sugar
- egg yolks
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Take equal amounts of flour and fine sugar, knead them with egg yolks and fresh butter
2. When formed, stretch it with the dough roller, cut it into pieces as seems fit, and put it to bake in a mild oven
3. When baked, glaze it with sugar and a hot roller, and serve for various side dishes
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa Frolé (Shortbread Dough)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- fine sugar
- egg yolks
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Take equal quantities of flour and fine sugar, knead with egg yolks and fresh butter
2. When formed, extend with the rolling pin, cut into pieces as desired, and bake in a gentle oven
3. When baked, glaze with sugar using a hot spatula, and serve for various inter-course dishes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Brioches e Outros Bolos (Dough for Brioche and Other Buns)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- hot water
- half an ounce of bread or beer yeast
- half an eighth of flour
- one eighth of flour
- eight eggs
- one pound of butter
- half an ounce of salt
- a little water

## Directions

1. Mix the dough with hot water, half an ounce of bread or beer yeast, and half an eighth of flour; when well combined, wrap in cloth and place in a warm location, let it ferment a quarter hour if in summer, or one hour if in winter
2. Then place on a table with one eighth of flour, eight eggs, one pound of butter, half an ounce of salt, and a little water, and knead everything together with the palms of the hands two or three times
3. Dust with flour, wrap again in cloth, and let ferment nine or ten hours
4. Remove the dough, cut into pieces, shape with the hand, flatten, brush with beaten eggs, place in the oven to bake; if small, bake for half an hour, and if large, one and a half hours
5. These buns are eaten hot, cold, or as desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Brioches e Outros Bolos (Dough for Brioches and Other Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- half an ounce of bread ferment or yeast
- half an eighth of flour
- one eighth of flour
- eight eggs
- one arratel of butter
- half an ounce of salt
- a little water

## Directions

1. Knead with hot water half an ounce of bread ferment or yeast and half an eighth of flour
2. When well bound, wrap it in a cloth, place in a warm place, and let it ferment a quarter of an hour if it is in summer, and one hour if in winter
3. Then place it on a table with one eighth of flour, eight eggs, one arratel of butter, half an ounce of salt, and a little water, and knead everything together
4. With the palm of the hand two or three times, sprinkle with flour, wrap it again in the cloth, and let it ferment nine or ten hours
5. Take out the dough, cut it into pieces, mold them with the hand, flatten them, brush them with beaten eggs, put them in the oven to bake
6. If they are small, only for half an hour, and if they are large, an hour and a half
7. These cakes are eaten hot or however you wish
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Canudos (Dough for Rolled Wafers)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- fine sugar
- a piece of butter
- two eggs
- a bit of grated lemon rind

## Directions

1. Take equal amounts of flour and fine sugar, a piece of butter, two eggs, and a bit of grated lemon rind
2. Knead with hot water so that it is not soft
3. Let it rest, and use it for whatever seems best
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Canudos (Dough for Tubes)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- fine sugar
- a piece of butter
- two eggs
- a piece of lemon peel, grated

## Directions

1. Take equal quantities of flour and fine sugar, a piece of butter, two eggs, and a piece of grated lemon peel, knead with hot water so it is not too soft
2. Let it rest, and it serves for whatever is needed
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Crocantes (Dough for Croquants)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- fine sugar
- egg whites
- a little orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Place on a table equal quantities of flour and fine sugar, moisten with egg whites and a little orange blossom water
2. When the dough is bound and formed, but not too soft, dust with flour, extend the dough with a rolling pin, and it will serve for croquants, tarts, small pastries, and other small inter-course dishes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Crocantes (Dough for Croquants)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- fine sugar
- egg whites
- a little orange flower water

## Directions

1. Place on a table an equal quantity of flour as sugar, knead it with egg whites and a little orange flower water
2. When the dough is bound and formed, but not too soft, sprinkle the table with flour, stretch the dough with the roller, and it will serve for croquants, tarts, small pastries, and other small side dishes
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Empadas de Caixa (Dough for Box Pastries)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One quarter of flour
- Butter
- Hot water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Take one quarter of flour and knead it on a table with butter and hot water, but make it stiffer than the preceding dough
2. After being very well worked, make two balls
3. From one, raise the box or boxes with skill on sheets of paper and put inside whatever seems good to you, after being seasoned and finished
4. Cover with part of the other dough
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Empadas de Fiambre (Dough for Meat Pastries)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half an alqueire or one quarter of flour
- Two or three arratels of butter
- Some pieces of salt
- Four egg yolks
- Water, as necessary

## Directions

1. Spread on a table half an alqueire or one quarter of flour with two or three arratels of butter, some pieces of salt, and four egg yolks
2. Knead everything very well, adding the necessary water little by little until it is well bound and consistent
3. Divide into two portions
4. Work each one very well
5. After being well worked, use for tarts, empadas, or whatever is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Empadas de Hambre (Dough for Hunger Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- half an alqueire or one quarter of flour
- two or three arratels of butter
- some pinches of salt
- four egg yolks
- necessary water

## Directions

1. Place on a table half an alqueire or one quarter of flour with two or three arratels of butter, some pinches of salt, and four egg yolks
2. Mix everything very well, adding the necessary water little by little until it is well bound and consistent
3. Afterwards divide it in two halves, knead each one very well
4. After being well kneaded, use for tarts, pastries, or whatever is necessary
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa para empadas de hambre (Dough for Meat Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- half an alqueire or one quarter of wheat flour
- two or three arratels of butter
- some stones of salt
- four egg yolks
- necessary water

## Directions

1. Place on a table half an alqueire or one quarter of wheat flour with two or three arratels of butter
2. Add some stones of salt and four egg yolks
3. Mix everything very well
4. Add the necessary water little by little until it is well bound and consistent
5. Divide it into two equal parts
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Escalados (Dough for Scalded Dumplings)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of flour
- half a pound of butter
- one ounce of ground salt
- twelve and a half fresh eggs
- three or four ounces of yeast

## Directions

1. Place on a table two pounds of flour, half a pound of butter, one ounce of ground salt, and twelve and a half fresh eggs, and knead everything two or three times with the palm of the hand
2. Then add three or four ounces of yeast broken into pieces, and knead everything again with the palm of the hand five or six times
3. Add the dough, dust with flour, and wrap in a towel until the next day
4. Remove, place on the table, shape into a roll, cut into small pieces, shape on both sides, place in boiling water stirred from time to time with a skimmer so it does not boil over
5. When the dumplings rise above the water, remove them, place in cold water, then place in a sieve to drain
6. When necessary, put in the oven to bake, and serve hot or cold as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Escaldados (Dough for Scalded Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of flour
- half an arratel of butter
- one ounce of ground salt
- eighteen eggs
- three or four ounces of ferment in small pieces

## Directions

1. Place on the table two arratels of flour, half an arratel of butter, one ounce of ground salt, eighteen fresh eggs, and knead everything two or three times with the palm of the hand
2. Then add to it three or four ounces of ferment made into small pieces, knead everything again with the palm of the hand five or six times
3. Then add salt to the dough, sprinkle with flour, and wrap it in a towel until the next day
4. Take it out, place it on the table, make it into a roll, cut it into small pieces, shape it on one and the other side by putting it in boiling water, stirred from time to time with the skimmer so that it does not boil over
5. When the small cakes rise above the water, remove them
6. Place them in cold water, then place them in a sieve to drain
7. When necessary, put them to bake in the oven, and eat them hot or cold, as you wish
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa para Pão (Bread Dough)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Wheat flour, the finest and purest
- Yeast, necessary quantity
- Water, sufficient amount
- Salt, to temperament

## Directions

1. Take the finest and purest wheat flour and mix it with the necessary amount of yeast
2. Add sufficient water to knead, observing the temperature of the water, which should be tempered with salt
3. Work forcefully for some time, and after it is very well worked and mixed with the yeast, let it rest in a place that is neither cold nor excessively hot so it may ferment and rise
4. When risen, remove from the kneading trough and place on a table
5. Cut into equal pieces and form loaves of the desired size
6. After shaping, put to bake in the oven, carefully observing the degree of heat
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa para pão (Bread Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- wheat flour, finest and most pure
- necessary quantity of leaven
- sufficient water
- salt

## Directions

1. Take the finest and most pure wheat flour and mix it with the necessary quantity of leaven
2. Add sufficient water for kneading, observing the temperature of the water, which should be tempered with salt
3. Work it with force for some time, and after it is very well worked and mixed with the leaven
4. Leave it in a place that is neither cold nor excessively hot, for it to ferment and rise
5. When it is leavened, remove it from the kneading trough and place it on a table
6. Cut it into equal pieces and form the loaves to the desired size
7. After shaping, put them to bake in the oven, carefully observing the degree of heat
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa Real (Royal Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a saucepan of salted warm water
- a little sugar
- two ounces of butter
- flour
- a bit of grated lemon rind
- eggs

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan half a saucepan of water seasoned with salt, a little bit of sugar, and two ounces of butter, and place on the fire
2. When it boils for an instant, add flour to it and mix with the spoon until the dough binds and becomes of firm consistency
3. Working always with the spoon and over the fire until it detaches from the bottom
4. Add to it a little bit of finely grated lemon rind, and add eggs one by one
5. When the dough is soft and without lumps, it serves for cream puffs, small pastries, and other different side dishes
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa à Duquesa (Duchess Dough)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half an eighth of flour
- two fresh eggs
- a piece of butter
- a little salt
- Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Take half an eighth of flour, two fresh eggs, a piece of butter, and a little salt, and knead with Madeira wine, but do not make it soft
2. Let it rest, and afterwards it serves for different inter-course dishes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa à Duqueza (Duchess Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- half an eighth of flour
- two fresh eggs
- a piece of butter
- a little salt
- Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Take half an eighth of flour, two fresh eggs, a piece of butter, and a little salt, and knead together with Madeira wine, but do not let it become soft
2. Let it rest, and afterwards use it for various intermediate dishes
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa à Hispanhola (Spanish-Style Dough)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one eighth of flour
- hot water
- pork butter
- pounded salt
- two or three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix one eighth of flour with hot water, pork butter, pounded salt, and two or three egg yolks, but do not make it too soft
2. When kneaded, let it rest a short time, extend with the rolling pin until thin, cut into two or three equal pieces
3. Brush the top with hot pork butter, make well-closed rolls, and after cutting lengthwise and rounding, it will serve for tarts, pastries, and other uses
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa à Ingleza (English Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pot of milk
- half an arratel of butter
- a little salt
- lemon peel
- a loaf of hair
- flour
- clarified butter
- grated sugar

## Directions

1. Put in a pot half a pot of milk and half an arratel of butter, a little salt, lemon peel, and a loaf of hair
2. When it boils, add to it the necessary flour, as for fritters, and after it has been dried on the fire and cooled, extend it with bread dough, and when placed to the necessary thickness, cut into equal squares
3. Fry in clarified butter, serve with grated sugar on top, and glaze with a hot spatula
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa à Ingleza (English-Style Dough)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a canada of milk
- half a pound of butter
- a little salt
- lemon peel
- a stick of cinnamon
- flour
- grated sugar

## Directions

1. Place in a pot half a canada of milk, half a pound of butter, a little salt, lemon peel, and a stick of cinnamon
2. When boiling, add the necessary flour as for fitters, and after dried on the fire and cooled, extend with the rolling pin and cut into equal squares
3. Fry in butter, and serve with grated sugar on top and glazed with a hot spatula
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Massa á Hespanholla (Spanish-Style Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- one eighth of flour
- hot water
- pork butter
- salt
- two or three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Knead one eighth of flour with hot water, pork butter, pounded salt, and two or three egg yolks, but do not let it be soft
2. When kneaded, let it rest a little while
3. Stretch it with the roller until it becomes thin
4. Cut it in two or three equal pieces, spread pork butter on top while hot, make some well-closed rolls, and after being cut across and rounded, it will serve for tarts, pastries, and whatever is necessary
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massa á Ingleza (English-Style Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a saucepan of milk
- half an arratel of butter
- a little salt
- lemon peels
- a loaf of lady fingers
- necessary flour

## Directions

1. Place in a saucepan half a saucepan of milk and half an arratel of butter, a little salt, lemon peels, and a loaf of lady fingers
2. When boiling, remove the necessary flour from it, as for buns, and after being dried on the fire and cold, stretch it with the dough roller, and placed at the necessary thickness, cut it
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massapães de Ovos (Almond and Egg Paste Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of very finely ground almonds
- two arratels of sifted stone sugar
- rose water
- two dozen egg yolks
- cinnamon
- flour

## Directions

1. Knead very well two arratels of very finely ground almonds with two arratels of sifted stone sugar and rose water
2. Place in a pot with two dozen egg yolks, stirring constantly so they do not stick, until the paste is firm
3. Remove the pot from the heat and cool over a basin of water
4. Form small balls, place in a basin with flour and cinnamon, and bake well in the oven with cinnamon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Massas para Empadas de Caixa (Dough for Box Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of flour
- butter
- hot water

## Directions

1. Take one quarter of flour and knead it on a table with butter and hot water, but let it be stiffer than the preceding one
2. After being very well kneaded, make two cakes, from one lift the box or boxes skillfully, and on sheets of paper, and put inside to cook everything that seems good to you, after being seasoned, finished, and covered with part of the other dough
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Maçãa do Peito e Ponta da Alcatra ao Natural (Beef Brisket and Rump Tip Prepared Plain)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef brisket or rump tip
- Salt
- One onion studded with cloves
- Various roots or vegetables such as carrots, leeks, parsnips, and celery
- Water

## Directions

1. Soak the meat in water, then wash it in two or three waters
2. Tie it with twine
3. Place it in a pot over heat with sufficient water and scum it very well, adding a little cold water from time to time
4. Once well skimmed, let it boil on one side only, seasoned with salt, one onion studded with cloves, and some roots or vegetables such as carrots, leeks, parsnips, and celery
5. Remove the piece of beef from the pot, trim it, and place it on a plate
6. Serve with sauce poured around it or with the garnishes and sauces that seem best
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Melindres (Almond Meringues)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten egg yolks
- half an arratel of powdered sugar from the Island of Madeira

## Directions

1. Put ten egg yolks in a pot with half an arratel of powdered sugar from the Island of Madeira
2. When this mixture becomes thick, form the melindres
3. Place with a spoon onto papers, making them the size of pastilles
4. Bake on low heat so they do not burn
5. When baked, serve at the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Melícias (Almond Confections)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half arratel of ground almonds
- One arratel of sugar in thread stage
- Five réis of grated bread
- A small amount of white honey
- Musk
- Cinnamon
- Cloves
- Half arratel of cow butter

## Directions

1. Cook in a pot half arratel of ground almonds with one arratel of sugar in thread stage, five réis of grated bread, a small amount of white honey, musk, cinnamon, cloves, and half arratel of cow butter.
2. When this becomes hard, shape the melícias in pig intestine.
3. Pass them through hot water and dry them in a reed basket.
4. They are eaten on fish days, roasted in a frying pan.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Meringas Recheadas (Filled Meringues)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Dozen egg whites
- Finely chopped lemon zest
- Powdered and sieved sugar

## Directions

1. Beaten with rods and well raised, take a dozen egg whites, add a little finely chopped lemon zest and powdered sugar that is well sieved
2. Mix everything
3. Place in small pieces the size of walnuts on sheets of paper sprinkled with fine sugar
4. Put to cook on the same table, under the lid of a dome-shaped oven with heat on top
5. When cooked and of good color, remove from the middle with a small spoon the white
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Meringas Recheadas (Filled Meringues)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- egg whites
- lemon zest, chopped finely
- powdered sugar, well sifted
- fruit jelly

## Directions

1. Beat egg whites with rods until well risen
2. Add a little finely chopped lemon zest and well-sifted powdered sugar, mixing everything together
3. Place in small pieces the size of walnuts on paper leaves, dusted with fine sugar
4. Bake over the same table, under the lid of a dome oven, with fire on top
5. When cooked and of good color, remove the unattached egg white from the middle with a small spoon
6. Replace the egg white with fruit jelly, and join some together
7. Serve immediately before they soften
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Metodo Excellente para Conservar os Presuntos (Excellent Method for Preserving Hams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- strong vinegar
- vine ash, sifted
- carqueja

## Directions

1. After the hams are well dried and hung, pass them through strong vinegar and powder them, and cover them with sifted vine ash; and hang them again until they dry
2. Place them then in a box with the same sifted ash on top and below, and thus arrange layers of hams and ash until the box is full, and between the layers a little carqueja
3. Having done this, nail the box lid in such a way that air cannot enter it, and when embarking, take care to place it in the coolest and driest part of the ship
4. By this method certainly the ham can be eaten with all its perfection, even if the voyage is of two or three years, and circumnavigate the globe
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Mexilhões com Achar (Mussels with Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- strong vinegar
- cinnamon stick
- pepper
- vinegar
- mustard
- nutmeg
- salt
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Boil a little strong vinegar, and when boiling, add whole cinnamon and pepper, vinegar, mustard, and nutmeg, all poorly ground
2. After seasoning the vinegar with these seasonings, pass the mussels through it
3. After passing through, place them in layers in a jar or pot, and in each layer sprinkle some of the said seasonings
4. When the jar is full, pour the boiling vinegar on it, and if it is not enough to cover the mussels, add cold vinegar, and on top salt and olive oil to form a layer so they do not spoil
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Mexilhões de Aveiro à Moda (Breaded and Fried Mussels from Aveiro)
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels from Aveiro or other
- butter
- vinegar
- garlic
- pepper
- orange
- butter

## Directions

1. Bread the mussels
2. Fry them in butter
3. Pass them through a sauce made of vinegar, garlic, pepper, orange, and butter
4. Serve at table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Mexilhões para Dia de Carne (Mussels for Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- bacon fat
- truffles
- ham
- sauce
- pepper
- substance
- culí
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Clean and open the mussels and remove all shells
2. Remove any crabs inside and reserve the water they release
3. Pass them through melted bacon fat with slices of truffles and a piece of ham
4. Add a sprig of sauce and a pinch of pepper
5. Add a spoon of substance and let simmer little by little
6. When the truffles are cooked, bind with a little culí and remove all the fat
7. Add the mussels with a drop of their own water
8. Keep on low heat for a short time
9. Before boiling, serve with lime juice as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Mexilhões para Dia de Peixe (Mussels for Fish Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- butter
- chives
- sauce
- pepper
- garlic
- bay leaf
- culí

## Directions

1. Open the mussels and remove them from the shells
2. Clean them as in the previous recipes
3. Sauté briefly in a casserole with butter, chives, and minced sauce
4. Add two small spoonfuls of their own water
5. Season with a little pepper, one clove of garlic, and a bay leaf
6. Bind with a little culí
7. Keep on low heat for an instant
8. Without boiling, serve as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Mexilhões para dia de peixe (Mussels for Fish Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels
- Butter
- Chives
- Parsley, chopped
- Salt
- Pepper
- Garlic clove
- Bay leaf
- Cullis
- Orange or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Open the mussels and remove them from the shells, cleaning them as done previously
2. Pass them through the fire in a saucepan with butter, chives, and chopped parsley
3. Add two small spoonfuls of their own water
4. Season with a little pepper, one clove of garlic, and a bay leaf
5. Bind with a little cullis
6. Leave for a moment over gentle heat without allowing to boil
7. Serve with orange or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Mexilhões à Provençal (Mussels Provençal)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- oil
- sauce
- garlic
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the mussels
2. Put them in a casserole and heat to open them
3. Remove one half of the shell, keeping the other half with the mussel inside
4. Sauté in oil with minced sauce, garlic, and pepper
5. After sautéing, add half a spoon of their own water
6. Without boiling, serve hot as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Miolos Albardados (Breaded Brains)
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- salt
- pepper
- eggs
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the brains into slices with salt and pepper on top
2. Pass through beaten egg and fry in good butter
3. When fried, make a tangy sauce of lemon and pepper
4. Place over the brains on a plate and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Miolos de Carneiro, de Porco, ou Vaca (Lamb, Pork, or Beef Brains)
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- butter
- eggs
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Clean the brains, removing the membrane
2. Fry in butter
3. Bind with two eggs
4. Serve with pepper and lemon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Miolos de Tegellada (Brains - Roasted Preparation)
body:
## Ingredients

- brains
- eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare the brains as above
2. Place in a small pot with eggs on top and brown
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Miolos de Vacca (Beef Brains)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brains
- water
- broth
- white wine
- pork fat
- salt
- pepper
- herbs

## Directions

1. Immediately place the brains in water, being well washed and cleaned of the membranes that cover them outside
2. Blanch them in boiling water
3. Then cook them in a half broth made with broth, white wine, pork fat, salt, pepper, and a bundle of dried herbs
4. When cooked, remove them from the broth and serve with a spicy sauce of good flavor, or with a ragout of whole small onions
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Miolos de Vacca Fritos (Fried Beef Brains)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brains
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- small onions
- sauce
- garlic
- thyme
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- butter
- flour
- pork fat

## Directions

1. Cut into pieces and marinate with salt, pepper, vinegar, small onions, sauce, garlic, thyme, bay leaf, marjoram, a small piece of butter passed through flour
2. Once well flavored with the marinade and dried very well, flour all the pieces, fry them, and serve garnished with fried sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Miolos de Vacca à Italiana (Calf Brains Italian Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf brains
- Water
- Butter
- Onion
- Parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Eggs
- Bread
- Essence sauce
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the brains and firm them in hot water as described above, then cut them into dice
2. Place them in a casserole with butter, onion, parsley, seasoned with salt and pepper, and half a dozen beaten eggs for each brain
3. Place on heat and mix everything with a spoon, then place in a small buttered timbale garnished with slices of very thin bread
4. Finish cooking in the oven and when well browned, serve with clear essence sauce on top and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Modo de Fazer a Geléa (Method of Making Jelly)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal feet, knuckles, calves feet, or old chickens
- Turkey feet
- All things that are gelatinous

## Directions

1. According to the quantity of jelly desired, take veal feet, knuckles, calves feet, or old chickens
2. Take turkey feet and everything that is gelatinous
3. Soak, wash, and blanch
4. Then put to cook for three or four hours
5. When cooked and the broth reduced and at the proper consistency
6. Pass through a sieve
7. Remove all the fat
8. Season with sugar, lemon juice, cinnamon, and half a bottle of white wine
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molejas de Vitella em Caixa (Veal Sweetbreads in a Box)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal sweetbreads
- Oil or melted bacon with salt
- Onions
- Truffles
- Mushrooms
- Shallot
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Bacon sheet
- Lime juice

## Directions

1. After the sweetbreads are blanched, cut them into thin slices
2. Marinate them with little oil or melted bacon with salt, finely chopped onions, truffles, mushrooms, shallot, salt, pepper, and all well chopped
3. Make seven or eight paper boxes, grease the bottoms with oil, fill them with the sliced sweetbreads arranged properly with fine herbs
4. Cover with a sheet of bacon and roast them in the oven or under a lid or on grills with a sheet of paper greased underneath, and serve with lime juice
5. They can also be made with fine herbs cooked in a little broth and white wine, covered with a sheet of bacon, and after cooked, remove the fat and serve with the same sauce, adding a little broth
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molejas de Vitella em Espichos (Veal Sweetbreads on Skewers)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Bacon
- Veal sweetbreads
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Shallot
- Garlic
- Flour
- Broth
- Egg yolks
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of lard bacon into small thin and square pieces and put them in a casserole, heat them on the fire
2. When half cooked, add the sweetbreads also cut into dice and seasoned with salt, pepper, parsley, shallot, little garlic, all well chopped
3. Turn them several times on the fire, dust with flour, moisten with a spoon of broth, and let it boil
4. When reduced and with little sauce, thicken it with four egg yolks so it becomes thick and the sauce sticks to the meat
5. Then put the pieces of sweetbreads on skewers interspersed with pieces of bacon, pass them through the sauce, cover with breadcrumbs, roast them on grills, and serve them dry as an entrance dish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Aspic Picante (Spicy Aspic Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of veal and ham
- sufficient broth
- one quarter of white wine
- a bundle of parsley and fine herbs
- a sprig of mint
- coriander seed
- two cloves
- nutmeg
- ginger
- cloves of garlic
- four shallots
- salt
- pepper
- slices of lemon without peel
- two small spoons of fine olive oil
- good white vinegar

## Directions

1. Put in a pan pieces of veal and ham, sufficient broth, one quarter of white wine, a bundle of parsley and fine herbs, a sprig of mint, coriander seed, two cloves, nutmeg, ginger, cloves of garlic, four shallots, salt, pepper, slices of lemon without peel, and two small spoons of fine olive oil, and let it simmer little by little; when reduced, add to it half a cup of good white vinegar, and let it simmer; when simmering, remove the olive oil from it, bind it with half a spoonful of cullis, pass it through a sieve, and tasting good, serve it for whatever is desired.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Chalotas (Shallot Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six shallots
- beef or veal substance
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Chop five or six shallots
2. Place in a saucepan with beef or veal substance, salt, pepper, and lemon juice
3. Put over heat to warm, but do not let it boil
4. Serves for roasted veal, beef, and mutton, and chops grilled on the grill
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Conserva (Preserve Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- solho in preserve of wine, vinegar, and garlic
- olive oil
- black seasonings
- dried bay leaves

## Directions

1. When the solho is in a preserve of wine, vinegar, and garlic (more wine than vinegar), roast it
2. After roasting, let it cool
3. Boil half the broth it was in with the same amount of olive oil, all black seasonings, and some dried bay leaves
4. When it has boiled, season it as needed and let it cool
5. Put the cold solho in this preserve to be eaten immediately; however, if it is to be kept or sent away, add less vinegar
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Flores com Azeite (Flower Sauce with Oil)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two green onions
- a little parsley
- salt
- pepper
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop two green onions and a little parsley
2. Place in a sauce boat with salt, pepper, oil, and vinegar
3. Serve cold
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Laranja (Orange Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of cullis of veal and ham
- a piece of fresh butter
- some fillets of orange peel
- juice of oranges squeezed
- pepper

## Directions

1. Put in a pan one spoon of cullis of veal and ham, add to it a piece of fresh butter, some fillets of orange peel, and the juice of squeezed oranges; season with pepper, and when bound, serve it for ducks and ducklings roasted, etc.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Marselha (Marseille Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of veal and ham
- melted bacon fat
- one cup of white wine
- two spoons of broth
- two cloves of garlic
- a bundle of parsley and fine herbs
- two cloves
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumb interior soaked in milk
- four egg yolks, pounded
- a little bit of milk, reduced

## Directions

1. Fry for an instant in melted bacon fat pieces of veal and ham; then add to it one cup of white wine, two spoons of broth, two cloves of garlic, a bundle of parsley and fine herbs, two cloves, salt, pepper, and let it simmer little by little; when cooked, pass it through a sieve with breadcrumb interior soaked in milk, four pounded egg yolks, and a little bit of reduced milk; and having heated it, and without letting it boil, it will serve for chickens, little squabs, and for everything else that is necessary.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Pobres (Poor Man's Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped onion
- salt
- pepper
- water

## Directions

1. Place in a bowl or sauce boat chopped onion, salt, pepper, and water
2. Serve cold
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Pobres Variation 1 (Poor Man's Sauce Variation 1)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- shallots
- water
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Chop some shallots
2. Place in a saucepan with water, salt, pepper, and lemon juice
3. Put to warm, but do not let it boil
4. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Pobres Variation 2 (Poor Man's Sauce Variation 2)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- two cloves of garlic
- shallots
- water
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- sour orange juice
- cloth

## Directions

1. Chop onions, two cloves of garlic, and shallots
2. Dissolve everything in a drop of water and white wine
3. Put to boil tempered with salt and pepper
4. Add sour orange juice
5. Strain through a cloth
6. Serve as seems best
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Presunto (Ham Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of ham
- white wine
- one spoonful of substance (meat essence)
- pepper
- fine herbs
- one small onion with two cloves
- half a spoonful of calf meat jelly

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of a pan with slices of ham
2. Put it on the heat to render its fat on both sides
3. When it begins to stick, add a little white wine and one spoonful of substance
4. Season with pepper, fine herbs, and one small onion stuck with two cloves
5. When reduced to half, add half a spoonful of calf meat jelly and let it boil a little longer
6. Pass through a sieve and serve for pies, tarts, stews, and whatever else is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Remolada (Remoulade Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one shallot, very finely minced
- chives, very finely minced
- parsley, very finely minced
- two cloves of garlic
- olive oil
- vinegar
- half a spoonful of cullis
- salt
- pepper
- a bundle of parsley
- bay leaf
- two cloves
- mustard

## Directions

1. Mince very finely one shallot, chives, parsley, two cloves of garlic; pass them in a little bit of olive oil in a pan on the fire; then add to it a little bit of vinegar and half a spoonful of cullis, salt, pepper, a bundle of parsley, bay leaf, two cloves, and let everything simmer for an instant; remove from the fire, remove the bundle, the olive oil, and the bay leaf, add mustard, and tasting good, serve it for roasted meats on the grill and on the spit, as needed.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Remolada Frio (Cold Remoulade Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two shallots
- capers
- chives
- parsley
- half clove of garlic
- fine olive oil
- mustard
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mince two shallots, capers, and chives, parsley, and half clove of garlic, pound it afterwards very well; when pounded, dissolve it in fine olive oil, mustard, and vinegar, seasoned with salt and pepper, and serve it apart in a sauce boat. This sauce serves for partridges, chickens, and capons roasted on the spit or on the grill, as stated.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Rosbife (Roast Beef Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- four onions
- lard or melted butter
- substance (meat essence)
- calf meat jelly
- salt
- pepper
- two small spoonfuls of mustard

## Directions

1. Peel four onions, cut them into slices or dice
2. Sauté them in lard or melted butter
3. When beginning to brown, add substance and calf meat jelly
4. Season with salt and pepper, and let them boil gently
5. When cooked, remove the fat, add two small spoonfuls of mustard, and serve for whatever is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho de Salsa (Parsley Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half spoonful of broth
- a little cullis
- one egg yolk
- tender chopped parsley
- a drop of vinegar
- a piece of butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place in a saucepan half spoonful of broth, a little cullis, one egg yolk, tender chopped parsley, a drop of vinegar, a piece of butter, and pepper
2. Put over heat, stirring always until it is bound
3. Serves for all kinds of game or fish, as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho do Almirante (Admiral's Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a little bit of restored sauce or clear essence
- pickled shallot
- capers, minced
- six rocamboles
- minced pimiento
- nutmeg, grated
- a piece of butter
- powdered flour
- lemon juice or verjuice

## Directions

1. Put on the fire a pan with a little bit of restored sauce or clear essence, pickled shallot, minced capers, six rocamboles, minced pimiento, grated nutmeg, a piece of butter, and powdered flour, and stir until it is bound; and tasting good, serve it for whatever is desired with lemon juice or verjuice.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho do Duque de Nevers (Duke of Nevers Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two slices of ham
- two roots
- one onion
- two cloves of garlic
- melted bacon fat
- one spoonful of broth
- one spoonful of cullis
- one drop of tarragon vinegar
- cooked and reduced broth

## Directions

1. Cut into dice two slices of ham, two roots, and one onion, and two cloves of garlic, and fry everything in melted bacon fat; after frying, add to it one spoonful of broth, another of cullis, one drop of tarragon vinegar, and let it simmer little by little in cooked and reduced broth, remove the fat from it, pass it through a sieve, and serve it for whatever is desired.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho para Galinholas (Sauce for Woodcocks)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three woodcocks
- red wine
- a little bit of restored sauce
- salt
- pepper
- one bay leaf
- quarters of woodcocks with fillets
- juice of sour oranges
- breadcrumb interior, fried

## Directions

1. Having roasted two or three woodcocks, remove their breasts and thighs; set aside the livers, and pound the bones, entrails, and livers, after pounding very well, dissolve in red wine and a little bit of restored sauce, and let it simmer seasoned with salt, pepper, and one bay leaf; when reduced, pass it through a sieve, put inside the quarters of woodcocks with fillets and juice of sour oranges; when hot, but not boiling, serve it for ducks, ducklings, snipes, curlews, teals, and other birds, garnished with pieces of fried breadcrumb interior.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho Verde Frio (Cold Green Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- chervil
- pimpinella
- tarragon
- mint
- purslane
- green chives
- fine olive oil
- half clove of garlic, minced
- salt
- pepper
- one drop of water
- vinegar
- two small spoons of mustard

## Directions

1. Pound in a stone mortar chervil, pimpinella, tarragon, mint, purslane, green chives, each according to its strength; when pounded, dissolve everything in fine olive oil, half clove of garlic minced, salt, pepper, one drop of water, vinegar, two small spoons of mustard; mix everything, and tasting good, serve it apart in a sauce boat.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho Verde Picante (Spicy Green Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- melted bacon fat
- pieces of veal and ham
- powdered flour
- one cup of white wine
- two spoons of broth
- parsley
- chervil
- tarragon
- mint
- chive stems
- lemon juice
- four egg yolks
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Fry in melted bacon fat pieces of veal and ham, powdered flour; after frying, add to it one cup of white wine, two spoons of broth, and let it simmer little by little; then pound parsley, chervil, tarragon, mint, chive stems, and squeeze everything with lemon juice, extract the green juice, dissolve it with four egg yolks, bind the sauce with this, pass it through a sieve; and tasting good, serve it for chickens, veal, lamb, kid, and for everything that is tender.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Alemã (German Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked ham
- three cucumbers
- small onions
- parsley
- one garlic clove
- broth
- pepper
- one bay leaf
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Sauté in butter cooked ham, three cucumbers, small onions, parsley, and one garlic clove, all chopped
2. After this is sautéed, add one spoonful of broth, season with pepper and one bay leaf
3. Let it boil gently
4. When the broth is reduced, bind it with two egg yolks
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Allemã (German Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Shallot
- Turnip greens
- Sorrel
- Toasted bread soaked in vinegar
- Rhine wine, half a bottle
- Sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Pound shallot tails and turnip greens in a stone mortar, but one thing against the already made
2. Add a handful of sorrel, a slice of toasted bread soaked in vinegar; when everything is well pounded, dissolve in a casserole with half a bottle of Rhine wine
3. Season with sugar and salt, put on the fire, and stirring always with a spoon, let it boil for an instant
4. Strain through a sieve, and it will serve for game as the preceding one
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Almêa para Veado (Almond Sauce for Venison)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One bottle of wine
- Same amount of vinegar
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Two lemon slices
- Cherries or plums cooked in sugar

## Directions

1. Put in a casserole one bottle of wine and the same amount of vinegar, add sugar, cinnamon, two lemon slices, and bring to a boil until reduced to half
2. Having cooked some cherries or plums in a little sugar, mix in the sauce, strain through a sieve, and it will serve for venison, roebuck, hare, and all the wild game
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Bexaméla (Béchamel Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- large pieces of veal
- large pieces of ham
- two roots
- one onion
- melted lard
- a piece of butter dusted with flour
- beef broth
- beef milk reduced
- salt
- pepper
- one clove of garlic
- one onion studded with cloves
- fresh herbs

## Directions

1. Cut into large pieces some pieces of veal and ham, two roots, and one onion
2. Pass everything through the fire in a saucepan with melted lard and a piece of butter dusted with flour
3. Then add broth and reduced beef milk, salt, pepper, one clove of garlic, one onion studded with cloves, fresh herbs
4. Let it boil gently and slowly
5. When cooked and reduced, pass it through a sieve
6. This sauce is used for roasted game fillets, vegetables, and other garnishes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Bexaméla (Béchamel Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of veal and ham, in large dice
- two roots
- one onion
- melted bacon fat
- a piece of butter
- powdered flour
- restored sauce
- reduced cow's milk
- salt
- pepper
- one clove of garlic
- one onion studded with cloves
- fine herbs

## Directions

1. Cut in large dice pieces of veal and ham, two roots, and one onion; pass everything through the fire in a pan with melted bacon fat and a piece of butter powdered with flour; then add restored sauce and reduced cow's milk, salt, pepper, one clove of garlic, one onion studded with cloves, fine herbs, and let it simmer little by little; when cooked and reduced, pass it through a sieve, and serve this sauce for slices of roasted game, vegetables, and other garnishes.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Colet (Coulet Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two lettuces
- green parsley
- water
- two cloves of garlic
- one spoonful of broth
- broth
- butter
- three or four egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pound two lettuces and green parsley in a stone mortar
2. Boil in water and pass into a saucepan with two cloves of garlic, one spoonful of broth, and broth
3. Let it boil slowly and gently
4. When reduced, pass through a sieve
5. Add a piece of butter and three or four chopped egg yolks
6. When bound and good tasting, serve with lemon juice in the quantity that seems best
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Gasconia (Gascon Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One onion in thin slices
- Olive oil
- Pork essence
- Parsley
- Chives
- Two cloves
- Fine herbs
- Two lemon slices without skin
- Four cloves of garlic

## Directions

1. Cut one onion into thin slices and sauté in olive oil; after sautéing, add pork essence, a bundle of parsley, chives, two cloves, fine herbs, two lemon slices without skin, and four cloves of garlic
2. Let it cook little by little; when reduced and of good taste, remove the olive oil, strain through a sieve, and serve for what is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Hispanhola (Spanish Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of veal
- slices of ham
- one partridge
- two roots
- broth
- half a carafe of white wine, already boiled
- one spoonful of meat jelly
- pepper
- coriander seed
- garlic cloves
- parsley
- fine herbs
- onion studded with cloves
- good olive oil
- two lemon slices

## Directions

1. Line a pan with slices of veal and slices of ham, add one partridge and two roots
2. Put on the heat to render its fat gently
3. When it sticks, add a drop of broth and half a carafe of white wine already boiled
4. Add one spoonful of meat jelly, season with pepper, coriander seed, garlic cloves, parsley, fine herbs, onion studded with cloves, and good olive oil
5. Let it boil gently for the space of two hours
6. Then add two lemon slices and when reduced, remove the fat, pass through a sieve, and it will serve for whatever is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Holandeza (Dutch Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- good butter
- flour
- broth
- one chopped anchovy
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pulverize flour with a good piece of fresh butter
2. Put it in a pan with a spoonful of broth
3. Add one chopped anchovy that has been rinsed with salt, season with salt and pepper
4. Put on heat, stirring constantly until it is bound
5. Then add lemon juice
6. This sauce serves for any game or fish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Inglesa (English Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- clear essence
- one chopped anchovy
- fine capers
- two whole small onions
- a piece of fresh butter
- two hard egg yolks, chopped
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put clear essence in a pan with one chopped anchovy, fine capers, two whole small onions, and a piece of fresh butter
2. Place on heat and stir constantly until it is bound
3. Add two hard egg yolks chopped over a cloth and lemon juice
4. This sauce serves for cooked game, roasted, or as it seems fit
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Provençal (Provençal Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Two sweetbreads
- Truffles
- Cucumber
- Chives
- Parsley
- Olive oil
- White wine, one cup
- Two cloves of garlic
- Pepper
- Half a bay leaf
- Two lemon slices
- Veal culls
- Pork culls
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Finely chop two sweetbreads, truffles, cucumber, chives, and parsley, and sauté everything in olive oil
2. When sautéed, add one cup of white wine, two cloves of garlic, pepper, half a bay leaf, and two lemon slices
3. While cooking, add one spoonful of veal culls and pork culls; when it has good taste, remove the fat, strain through a sieve, and serve with lemon juice in everything that is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Romanã (Roman Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal substance and pork essence
- Roots
- Onions in rings
- White wine, one cup
- Two spoonfuls of culls
- Capers
- Four shallots
- Two cloves of garlic
- Coriander seed
- Two lemon rings without skin
- Salt
- Pepper
- Two cloves

## Directions

1. Guard the substance of a casserole with veal and pork, roots, and onions in rings, and put on the fire to brown little by little
2. When the substance is browning, add one cup of white wine and two spoonfuls of culls, add capers, four shallots, two cloves of garlic, coriander seed, two lemon rings without skin, season with salt, pepper, and two cloves, and let it cook little by little
3. When reduced, bind with a little culls, and of good taste, remove the fat, strain through a sieve, and serve for what is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Toscana (Tuscan Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One spoonful of Roman sauce
- Good amount of fresh butter
- Flour
- Parsley
- Tarragon
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Put in a casserole in place of culls one spoonful of Roman sauce, add a good amount of fresh butter sprinkled with flour, parsley, and chopped tarragon, put on the fire, and stirring always with a spoon until it is bound and of good taste
2. Serve for chickens, capons, and for everything else that is necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molho à Tudésca (German Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Shallot tails
- Turnip greens
- Handful of sorrel
- Toasted bread slice soaked in vinegar
- Rhine wine, half a bottle
- Sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Pound in a stone mortar shallot tails and turnip greens, but one thing against the already made
2. Add a handful of sorrel, a slice of toasted bread soaked in vinegar; when everything is well pounded, dissolve in a casserole with half a bottle of Rhine wine
3. Season with sugar and salt, put on the fire, and stirring always with a spoon, let it boil for an instant; strain through a sieve, and it will serve for game as the preceding one
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Molhos Picantes (Spicy Sauces)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half cup of vinegar
- Two cloves of garlic
- Onion rings
- Dried marjoram
- One bay leaf
- Whole parsley
- Two lemon slices without skin
- Salt
- Pepper
- Substance
- Half culls

## Directions

1. Put in a casserole half a cup of vinegar, two cloves of garlic, onion rings, dried marjoram, one bay leaf, whole parsley, two lemon slices without skin, salt, and pepper, and bring to a boil
2. When boiling for an instant, add one spoonful of substance and half culls, and let it cook little by little
3. When reduced and of good taste, strain through a sieve, and serve for hares, venison, and all the wild game you wish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Morcellas (Spiced Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a half arratel of rye bread or grated bread
- One and a half arratel of fried cow butter
- One arratel of ground almonds
- Two and a half arratel of clarified sugar
- One and a half dozen eggs, beaten with half egg whites
- Seven eighths of pinched clove
- A little pepper
- Two fingers of cooked salt
- One eighth of pinched cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put one and a half arratel of rye bread or grated bread in a mortar and pass through a sieve.
2. Mix with one and a half arratel of fried cow butter and mix very well with a spoon.
3. Cover it for a bit.
4. Add one arratel of ground almonds and mix everything together.
5. Cook with two and a half arratel of clarified sugar, one and a half dozen eggs beaten with half egg whites, seven eighths of pinched clove, a little pepper, and two fingers of cooked salt.
6. Remove from heat and sprinkle one eighth of pinched cinnamon on top.
7. Shape the morcellas in straight pig intestine, not too full, and dry them in a reed basket in a place where they do not get smoke.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Mutton with Bacon and Bread (Carneiro com Toucinho)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton, split in half
- half arratel of bacon
- herbs and seasonings
- all spices
- cheese, half arratel, grated
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Split the mutton in half with half arratel of bacon and its herbs
2. Season with all spices
3. When almost cooked, strain the broth into a hollow dish or frying pan over slices of bread covered with half arratel of grated cheese
4. Place over hot coals
5. Put the mutton on top with lemon juice and serve
6. It can also be made with roasted mutton
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Mãos de Porco (Pork Hands)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork hands
- cinnamon
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook the pork hands
2. Cut in half lengthwise
3. Cover with cinnamon and breadcrumbs
4. Brown on a grill and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Mãos de Porco Albardadas (Breaded Pork Hands)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork hands
- eggs
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cook the pork hands
2. Split lengthwise, season with salt and pepper
3. Coat with egg
4. Serve with pepper and lemon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Mãos de Porco de Caravonada (Pork Hands Caravonada)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork hands
- breadcrumbs
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put the pork hands to cook
2. When cooked, cut them in half and coat them with breadcrumbs and cinnamon
3. Brown them on grills, and after browning, send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Mãos de Vitela (Veal Hands)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal hands
- eggs
- six raw egg yolks
- one arratel of butter
- two spoonfuls of broth
- quarter pound of sugar
- vinegar
- pepper
- nutmeg
- salt
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Cook and fry the veal hands
2. Bread them with eggs and place them on a soup called Joldresi
3. To make this soup, beat six raw egg yolks in a pan with one arratel of butter, two spoonfuls of broth, quarter pound of sugar, a bit of vinegar, pepper, nutmeg, and salt
4. Put all this over the fire and when it begins to boil, which is the sign it is done, put the breaded veal hands inside
5. Place them in a small pot on coals so it does not break and on bread slices send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Mãos de Vitela de Outro Modo (Veal Hands Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal hands
- eggs
- butter
- egg yolk
- hen or lamb broth
- sugar
- white wine
- vinegar
- ginger
- cinnamon
- pepper
- nutmeg
- clove

## Directions

1. Peel the veal hands as one would peel a suckling pig
2. Cook them well in water and salt until the bones loosen
3. Pass them through beaten eggs and fry them in butter
4. Make a sweet and sour broth with egg yolk, hen or lamb broth, sugar, white wine, and vinegar, seasoned with ginger, cinnamon, pepper, nutmeg, and clove
5. Send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Noz de Vitella com Ervilhas Verdes (Veal Sweetbread with Green Peas)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two veal sweetbreads
- green peas
- bacon or melted butter
- parsley
- chives
- cloves
- stock
- cullis
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take one or two veal sweetbreads very well larded as the first ones, and place them to cook, finishing them well as the fricandeau ones
2. Take some green peas and pass them through the fire with bacon or melted butter, a sprig of parsley, chives, and cloves
3. After giving it four turns, moisten them with stock, and once cooked, finish binding with a little cullis
4. Then pour a little stock into the casserole of the glaze to scrape off what remained stuck to the bottom, pass it through a sieve, and pour it into the pea ragout
5. Once cooked and of good taste, serve by placing the veal sweetbread on top, and you may also give it a ragout of spinach à la crème, noting that all these fricandeau entries are very rich and should be made with all possible care
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Noz de Vitella de Fricandó com Ragú Branco de Chicórias (Veal Sweetbread Fricandeau with White Chicory Ragout)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal sweetbread
- two chicories
- two onions
- melted butter
- flour
- stock
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- cream
- milk
- cullis
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take one veal sweetbread, prepare it, trim it, lard it, and finish it with its glaze as described in the previous recipes
2. Take two chicories, blanch them in hot water
3. Cut two onions into fillets and pass them through a casserole with melted butter
4. After passing them, dust them with flour and add a spoonful of stock
5. Cut the chicories into pieces and add them to the onion fillets
6. Season with a little salt and pepper, and once cooked and of good taste, remove the fat and bind with egg yolks dissolved in cream and milk
7. Alternatively, after the chicories are blanched, squeeze them and tie them with string and cook them, then cut into equal pieces
8. Place them in a casserole with a little cullis, add what remained stuck to the bottom of the casserole in which the veal cooked, give it four boils, and when of good taste, serve hot with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Noz de Vitella em Caixa com Hervas Finas (Veal Sweetbread in Paper with Fine Herbs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal sweetbread or other piece of leg
- melted bacon or olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- truffles
- shallots
- cucumbers
- butter
- lemon juice
- white vinegar

## Directions

1. Trim the veal sweetbread or any other piece of leg, beat it between two cloths with the flat of a knife
2. Marinate it afterwards in melted bacon or a little olive oil with salt, pepper, parsley, truffles, shallots, and cucumbers all finely minced
3. Remove it from the marinade, place it in a paper box lined with butter, and place it to roast on top of a grill over another buttered paper with gentle fire, taking care not to burn the paper
4. Once roasted in this way on both sides, it will be served in its same box with lemon juice or with a splash of white vinegar
5. If the veal sweetbreads are small and tender, being very well trimmed and beaten between two cloths, marinate them as above, adding some bay leaf, and roasted also in a box, remove them and serve on a plate with good sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Noz de Vitella em Popietas (Veal Loin in Roulades)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one loin of veal, well aged and pale
- minced meat the size of a five-reis coin
- eggs, beaten
- bacon slices
- fresh butter
- fine bread crumbs
- Spanish sauce or essence with lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take one loin of veal well mortified and very pale, and clean it well of its skins as if for fricassee.
2. Cut lengthwise into very thin slices, which you will flatten well with a knife.
3. Once thus prepared and well equal, place on top of each a little minced meat the size of a five-reis coin, which you will unite well with the flat of the knife wetted in beaten eggs.
4. Roll these slices very tightly and well closed, and wrap them in bacon slices of the same size, and fasten them with skewers so they cook on the spit covered with paper.
5. Once cooked, remove the paper and bacon, brush them with fresh butter corrected and prepared with fine bread crumbs, give them good color.
6. Serve them on a plate with Spanish sauce underneath or an essence with lemon juice, and serve hot as an appetizer.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Noz de Vitella à Sultana (Veal Sweetbread à la Sultana)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one well mortified veal sweetbread
- fine bacon lard
- finely minced chicken or ham breasts
- string
- spit

## Directions

1. Take one well mortified veal sweetbread, trim it of its skins, and beat it between two cloths as for fricandeau
2. Lard it well with fine bacon and make a hole from one side only with the spit
3. Stuff it very well with a fine mince of chicken or ham breasts
4. Close the opening with a cork and tie it very well with string
5. Tie it then to a spit and place it to roast, covered with buttered paper
6. Remove the paper afterwards to give it good color, and once roasted, remove the string and serve with good veal substance if desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Nozes de Vitella em Popietas à Italiana (Veal Loin Roulades Italian Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two loins of veal
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- one clove of garlic
- fine herbs
- cucumbers
- truffles
- half a quarter-liter of good oil
- lemon juice
- raw minced meat, well bound
- bacon slices
- veal slices
- ham
- onion rounds
- substance broth
- cullis

## Directions

1. Take one or two loins of veal, prepare them and cut them as the above, marinate them in a saucepan with salt, pepper, parsley, chives, one clove of garlic, fine herbs, cucumbers and truffles, all chopped fine, half a quarter-liter of good oil and lemon juice.
2. After marinating for some time, place on top a little raw minced meat, well bound, and in such form that it remains well closed.
3. After rolling the slices, tie them with twine.
4. Line a saucepan with bacon slices, veal, ham, and onion rounds; arrange the roulades on top and cover them in the same manner as you prepared the base below.
5. Put them to cook over low heat: when cooked, transfer them to another saucepan, and pour into the saucepan in which they cooked a little substance and a little cullis, and put it to simmer.
6. Once the sauce is reduced and of good taste, strain it through a sieve and pour it over the roulades, and keep them well heated until serving.
7. They can also be served with truffle sauce, capers, and sorrel, all very finely chopped, or with Italian sauce.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Nóz de Vitella de Fricandó (Veal Sweetbread Fricandeau)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two veal sweetbreads, well mortified and of the whitest available
- lard made of fine bacon
- a piece of ham
- onion studded with cloves
- sprig of parsley
- veal stock without salt
- bacon lard
- cullis
- juice of lemon

## Directions

1. Take one or two veal sweetbreads that are well mortified and of the whitest available, trim them of all their skins
2. Place them between two cloths and beat them with the flat of a knife so that they become flat
3. Lard them afterwards with fine bacon
4. Soak them for a short time in cold water, then blanch them in another water
5. Once blanched, place them in a casserole with a piece of ham, an onion studded with cloves, and a sprig of parsley
6. Cover with strips of bacon and moisten with veal stock without salt, and cook
7. When cooked, remove it from the pot, strain the stock through a sieve, and remove the fat
8. Place the stock on the fire, and once reduced to caramel and of good color, place the veal sweetbread with the larded side down
9. Place on gentle heat until the glaze sticks to the bacon
10. Once finished, remove from the fire, scrape off what remains stuck to the bottom of the casserole with a little stock, add a little cullis, strain through a sieve, and serve hot with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Olha Franceza (French Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- two pigeons
- two partridges
- rabbit
- two arratels of cured ham and sausage
- bundle of aromatics
- three onions
- four heads of garlic
- four heads of lettuce
- grains
- whole spices
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Cook in a pot a chicken, two pigeons, two partridges, a rabbit, two arratels of cured ham and sausage, with a bundle of aromatics underneath, three onions, four heads of garlic, four heads of lettuce, grains, whole spices, vinegar, and water to cover everything
2. When cooked, season with all the seasonings and top with a bundle of aromatics
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Olha Podrida (Stewed Pot)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of very fat beef
- chicken
- game bird
- partridge
- pigeons
- rabbit
- hare
- pork ear or shoulder if in season
- piece of bacon
- sausages
- sausage links
- pork loin
- turnips or radishes
- three heads of large garlic
- grains
- two or three dozen chestnuts
- salt
- aromatics

## Directions

1. Place in a pot to cook a piece of very fat beef, a chicken, a game bird, a partridge or pigeons, a rabbit, a hare, a pork ear or shoulder if in season, a piece of bacon, sausages, sausage links, and pork loin, all mixed with turnips if available or radishes, three heads of large garlic, grains, two or three dozen chestnuts, salt, and aromatics
2. When cooked, serve on a plate over bread slices
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Olha Podrida em Massa (Layered Meat Pie in Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four arratels of beef brisket
- Four arratels of pork loin
- Two chickens
- Two partridges
- Four pigeons
- Half a dozen thrushes
- Two rabbits
- Half a dozen pork tongues
- Turnips
- Cabbage
- Garlic
- Grains
- All whole spices
- Chopped bacon
- One alqueire of flour
- Butter
- Eggs
- Two loaves of ten réis bread
- Beef breast slices

## Directions

1. Brown all the meats and vegetables in chopped bacon, each item separately
2. When all are browned and half-cooked in bacon, knead one alqueire of flour with butter and eggs, removing two eggs' worth of dough
3. Place one portion of dough with paper underneath on a baking sheet
4. Arrange two loaves of ten réis bread as a base, topped with sliced beef breast, then arrange the aforementioned meats and vegetables in good order on top
5. Cover with another layer of dough, extending it to cover everything, and make the rim very thick so it does not burst
6. Make an opening on top to pour in the liquid left from browning the meats
7. Make two dough handles as on a panela and a worked top crust
8. Place in an oven to roast slowly
9. When serving, drain it and remove the sauce
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Olha Podrida em Massa (Stewed Pot in Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- shredded pork fat (toucinho)
- four arratels of beef chuck
- four arratels of pork loin
- two chickens
- two partridges
- four pigeons
- half a dozen thrushes
- two rabbits
- half a dozen pork tongues
- turnips
- cabbages
- garlic
- grains
- whole spices
- flour

## Directions

1. Braise in shredded pork fat the following things, each separately: four arratels of beef chuck, four arratels of pork loin, two chickens, two partridges, four pigeons, half a dozen thrushes, two rabbits, half a dozen pork tongues, turnips, cabbages, garlic, grains, and all the whole spices
2. When everything is braised, which is halfway cooked in pork fat, knead a bushel of flour
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Olhos de Vitela (Veal Eyes)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal eyes
- Broth
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Shallot
- Verjuice or ham essence

## Directions

1. The veal eyes after being cut all around, and well trimmed, are cooked in a braise, and served in the same manner as the ears mentioned in the preceding Article
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Omeletas ou Trouxas de Ovos (Omelets or Egg Bundles)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- herbs
- chopped chives
- bacon or butter

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs in a casserole, season with salt, pepper, herbs, and chopped chives
2. Pour into a certain heat of bacon or very hot butter
3. When the eggs are set and browned on one side, turn the bundle over
4. When cooked and browned, turn onto its plate with care and serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Orelhas de Vitela de Diferentes Modos (Veal Ears Various Ways)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a dozen veal ears
- Broth
- White wine
- Onion slices without skin
- Parsley
- Laurel
- Marjoram
- Onion studded with cloves
- Whole pepper
- Lard slices

## Directions

1. Take half a dozen well-scalded veal ears, and very white, place them to cook in a pot with broth a quarter of white wine, slices of an onion without skin, a pinch of salt, laurel, and marjoram, an onion studded with cloves, whole pepper, and slices of lard; after cooking slowly little by little, serve them with spicy sauce
2. They can also be stuffed with chopped à la crème, well bound, and passed through eggs, and breadcrumbs two or three times, and served fried garnished with gravy
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Orelhas de Vitela à Artara (Veal Ears à Artara)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal ears
- Broth
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Shallot
- Verjuice or lemon juice
- Bone marrow
- Bread cooked in milk
- Grated cheese
- Egg yolks
- Ham essence

## Directions

1. After well scalding and cleaning the ears, open them somewhat on the underside, and pass a skewer through them so that they remain open; place them to cook in a white braise as the preceding ones; and when cooked, and dry, pass them through melted butter, cover them with breadcrumbs, place them to brown on the grill, and having good color, serve them with sauce made with broth, salt, pepper, chopped shallot, and verjuice
2. Also after the ears are cooked and cold, they can be stuffed with a chopped of bone marrow of bread cooked in milk, grated cheese, butter, and egg yolks; and passing them through melted butter, and covering them with breadcrumbs and cheese, place them to brown in the oven, or under a lid, and serve them thus dried; or when not, with sauce of clear ham essence
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ostras ao Natural (Oysters Served Natural)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- pepper
- lime

## Directions

1. Open and trim the oysters
2. Serve them raw in their shells with pepper and lime
3. They may also be eaten open on grills with fire underneath and a hot pan
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ostras com Queijo Parmezão (Oysters with Parmesan Cheese)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- oysters
- pounded pepper
- minced sauce
- half a cup of white wine
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Garnish the bottom of a plate with washed butter
2. Arrange oysters on top sprinkled with pounded pepper and minced sauce
3. Add half a cup of white wine and cover with another piece of butter and grated Parmesan cheese
4. Bake in the oven and when golden, serve as an entrée like the preceding ones
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ostras de Caçarola (Oysters in Casserole)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- their own water
- washed butter
- half a clove of garlic
- minced sauce
- pepper
- one or two small spoonfuls of white wine
- fine breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Open oysters and put them with their own water in a casserole
2. Place on heat and remove before boiling
3. Trim them and arrange on a plate with washed butter on the bottom
4. Add half a clove of garlic and well minced sauce, pepper, and one or two small spoonfuls of white wine
5. Cover with another piece of butter and fine breadcrumbs
6. Cook in the oven or under a cover
7. When cooked and golden, serve as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ostras de Comadres (Godmothers' Oysters)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- crumb of bread boiled in milk
- sauce
- brains
- chives
- washed butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- egg yolks

## Directions
