1. Blanch the oysters without letting them boil
2. Drain them in a sieve
3. Chop them finely and place in a large stone bowl
4. Pound with crumb of bread boiled in milk, sauce, brains, and chives, all previously chopped
5. Add a piece of washed butter, little salt, pepper, and nutmeg
6. Bind with some egg yolks and fill the same shells with this mixture
7. Cover with fine breadcrumbs and bake in the oven
8. When golden, serve as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ostras de Espichos (Oysters on Skewers)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- butter
- sauce
- chives
- half a clove of garlic
- flour
- egg yolks
- broth
- lime juice
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Open and blanch the oysters in their own water
2. Remove before boiling, clean, and drain in a sieve
3. Brown briefly in butter with sauce, chives, and minced half clove of garlic
4. Add a little flour and bind with egg yolks diluted in broth and lime juice
5. Let cool and thread on silver skewers
6. Sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs, place on grills
7. When golden, serve without sauce as a hors d'oeuvre
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ostras em Concha Assada nas Grelhas (Oysters in Shell Roasted on Grills)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- pepper
- sauce
- salt
- oil or melted butter
- breadcrumbs
- lime juice

## Directions

1. Open the oysters and trim them around the edges
2. Season each one with pepper, minced sauce, oil or melted butter
3. Sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs
4. Roast on grills, turning with a hot pan
5. Serve with lime juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ostras em Conchas de Romeiro (Oysters in Pilgrim Shells)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- fish
- pilgrim shells
- fine herbs
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Make a minced mixture of oysters combined with a piece of fish, prepared the same way as the previous recipe
2. After pounding and seasoning the same way, garnish the bottom and edges of pilgrim shells with this mixture
3. Place two or three oysters seasoned with fine herbs and butter in the middle
4. Cover as before with the same minced mixture and fine breadcrumbs on top
5. Bake in the oven and when well golden, serve as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ostras em Forma de Sonhos (Oysters in the Form of Dreams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- white wine
- garlic
- onions
- fine herbs
- salt
- pepper
- lime
- flour
- eggs
- white wine
- clarified butter
- fried sauce

## Directions

1. Open and blanch the oysters as in the preceding recipe
2. Clean them and marinate in white wine with garlic, onions, fine herbs, salt, pepper, and lime slices
3. After marinating the necessary time, drain and dry in a cloth
4. Pass through a batter made with flour, eggs, and white wine
5. Fry in clarified butter until golden
6. Serve as an entrée garnished with fried sauce in a circle around them
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outra compota de Maçans (Another Apple Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- apples without sufficient consistency
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Take all apples that do not have sufficient consistency to cook whole, cut them in half, clean them on the inside, prick them on top with a needle, and without peeling them, place them in cold water
2. Then put them to cook with the necessary sugar, and let them boil gently and slowly
3. When cooked and before they dissolve into marmalade, arrange them on the plate, then reduce the sugar to the necessary point, and pour it over the apples
4. Serve as the preceding compote
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outra Empada Quente de Lombos de Lebres (Another Hot Hare Loin Pie)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- All the skins and meat from hare loins
- Half an arratel of scraped bacon, seasoned with little salt, pepper, some spices, sausage, shallots, truffles, all finely chopped
- Half an arratel of bacon, cut into small cubes
- Another half arratel of ham, cut into small cubes
- A quartilho of milk
- Six egg yolks
- A spoonful of breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Remove all skins and meat from the hare loins and make a chopped mixture with half an arratel of scraped bacon seasoned with little salt, pepper, some spices, sausage, shallots, truffles, all finely chopped
2. Put in a bowl with half an arratel of bacon and another half arratel of ham, both cut into small cubes, a quartilho of milk, and six egg yolks, and mix all with a spoonful of breadcrumbs
3. After mixing well, fill the pie box, cover with bacon slices, and finish the pie
4. Put in the oven to cook for four or five hours
5. When cooked, open it, drain the fat
6. Serve with good sauce and lime juice
7. If making for a larger dish, you can add veal loin, mutton leg, or veal, etc.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outra Massa Folhada (Another Puff Pastry)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef fat, weighing two or three arratels
- Dough

## Directions

1. Pound very well all the membranes in a piece of beef fat weighing two or three arratels, and after being very finely minced, pound until it becomes very soft and creamy like butter
2. Spread it on the dough, roll it up, and spread it as in the preceding recipe four or five times
3. It will serve for tarts, small pastries, and for everything that is made of meat and eaten hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outra Massa Folhada (Another Puff Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three arratels of beef fat
- rolled dough

## Directions

1. Remove very well all the skins from a piece of beef fat weighing two or three arratels
2. After being very well chopped, pound it until it becomes very soft and smooth like butter
3. Spread it over the rolled dough and stretch it like the preceding one four or five times
4. It will serve for tarts, small pastries, and for everything that is meat and eaten hot
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Outra Peru Defosfado para uma Entrada (Another Deboned Turkey for an Entree)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey (singed and cleaned)
- Minced veal or chicken (picado de vitella ou de galinha)
- Cream (creme)
- Stuffing (salpicaõ)
- Twine (barbante)
- Fine bacon (toucinho fino)

## Directions

1. After the turkey is singed and cleaned, debone it through the neck, and fill it with a minced veal or chicken with cream, or if not, with a stuffing like the one above.
2. Leave it lengthwise in its natural form, and after closing and sewing with twine, put it to cook in a braze.
3. When cooked and dry, remove the twine and serve it with good sauce. It can also be larded with fine bacon and finished like the one above, and served in the same way.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outra Sopa de Leite (Another Milk Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half an arratel of sweet almonds
- milk
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- bread slices
- or fine biscuit
- beaten egg whites or meringue

## Directions

1. Scald and peel half an arratel of sweet almonds, pound them, adding a few drops of milk from time to time
2. When pounded, dissolve them in some boiled milk, seasoned as above
3. Pass through a cloth two or three times
4. Heat without boiling and serve over very thin and dry bread slices from the oven, or over fine biscuit
5. Garnish with beaten egg whites or meringue
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outra Sopa de Nata (Another Cream Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large bowl of cream
- four eggs with whites
- half a quartilho of olive oil
- one arratel of cheese
- one arratel of bread slices
- one arratel of sugar
- some washed butter

## Directions

1. Grease a plate or frying pan
2. Make a layer of bread slices
3. Beat some of the cream and egg mixture and sprinkle powdered sugar on top
4. Add a layer of cheese slices with more cream mixture and sugar
5. Add small pieces of washed butter
6. Continue layering until all ingredients are used, ending with cheese
7. The soup should be well soaked
8. Place in the oven
9. When half cooked, pour two bowls of cream on top without eggs or sugar
10. Finish cooking and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Outras Aljumélas (Stuffed Anchovies)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- anchovies
- fine forcemeat filling
- white wine

## Directions

1. Prepare anchovies opened and without spines as stated
2. Soak
3. Replace the spines with a fine, bound forcemeat
4. Arrange as if they were whole
5. Pass through a batter made with white wine
6. Fry and when of good color, serve with fried sauce in a ring around them
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outras Creadilhas (Another Preparation of Lamb Sweetbreads)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb sweetbreads
- breadcrumbs
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cover with fine grated bread and fry as the preceding ones
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outras Creadilhas (Marinated Lamb Sweetbreads)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb sweetbreads
- vinegar
- water
- salt
- pepper
- onion slices
- sauce
- fresh herbs
- beaten eggs
- fine grated bread

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and cut into quarters, marinate in vinegar, water, salt, pepper, onion slices, sauce, and fresh herbs
2. After marinating, dry, pass through beaten eggs and fine grated bread
3. Like the preceding ones, when browned, serve garnished with sauce as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outras Fatias de Peixe (Other Fish Slices)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fish fillets
- salt
- pepper
- shallot
- nutmeg, grated
- butter, washed
- egg yolks
- egg whites
- bread crumb cooked in milk
- fine breadcrumb

## Directions

1. Take fish fillets, pound in a stone mortar and season with salt, pepper, shallot, grated nutmeg, a piece of washed butter bound with egg yolks and beaten egg whites, and bread crumb cooked in milk
2. After everything is pounded and mixed, place the slices in a buttered baking dish
3. Garnish with the chopped mixture, cover with fine breadcrumb
4. Bake in the oven or under a lid
5. When cooked and browned, serve with clear sauce on top and lemon juice as an entrée dish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outras Fatias à Portuguesa (Other Portuguese-style Slices)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumb
- sugar, boiling
- fresh egg yolks
- powdered sugar
- pounded cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut the bread crumb into thin slices
2. Toast on both sides on grills
3. When browned, arrange on a plate, pass briefly through boiling sugar, add fresh whole egg yolks
4. When cooked, place on the slices with powdered sugar and pounded cinnamon
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outras Fatias à Portugueza (Portuguese-Style Sliced Bread)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bread with crumb
- clarified butter
- egg yolks
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut the crumb of bread into thin slices
2. Toast on both sides on grills
3. When browned, arrange on a plate
4. Pass briefly through boiling sugar
5. Add fresh whole egg yolks
6. When cooked, place on the slices with sugar in point and powdered cinnamon on top
7. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outras Filetes de Linguados em Caixa, para Dia de Carne (Other Flounder Fillets in Box, for Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 flounder fillets
- salt
- pepper
- whole fine herbs
- lime juice
- chopped forcemeat à crème made of fried capon breasts
- paper boxes
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove the fillets from 2 or 3 flounders
2. Remove the skin
3. Season with salt, pepper, whole fine herbs, and lime juice
4. After marinating for a while, dry them on a cloth
5. Roll them like patties with a finely bound chopped forcemeat à crème made of fried capon breasts
6. Put in paper boxes with a little forcemeat underneath
7. Cover with breadcrumbs
8. Brush with butter
9. Place in a baking dish and put in the oven to cook
10. After cooked and well-colored, serve with lime juice or sour orange juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outras Pelotas de Vitella (Other Veal Balls)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two loins of veal
- raw minced meat made of veal or chicken or chicken livers with bacon
- five or six small boned birds

## Directions

1. Take one or two loins of veal, and after cutting and trimming them, marinate them in the same manner as the above, placing after marinating a little raw minced meat made of veal, or chicken, or also chicken livers with bacon.
2. On top of the minced meat five or six small boned birds that have also been in the marinade for some time.
3. Once the balls are finished being filled with the same minced meat, close them in such form that they remain very round, and put them to cook in the same manner as the above, and serve them with their own sauce or with another whatever seems better.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outras Postas de Solho Assado (Other Roasted Turbot Portions)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot cut in portions the thickness of two fingers
- fish broth
- white wine
- bay leaves
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- fine bread crumb
- ravigote sauce or hot sauce with butter

## Directions

1. Cut turbot in portions the thickness of two fingers
2. Cook in a marinade made of fish broth, white wine, two bay leaves, salt, pepper, and a piece of butter
3. Let simmer little by little
4. When cooked, cover with fine bread crumb, roast on the grill, dripping with melted butter and moderate heat
5. When roasted, serve with ravigote sauce or hot sauce with butter
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outras Sopas de Bocões por Diferentes Modos (Other Chicken Dumplings Soup by Different Methods)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- minced veal
- bacon scraped
- bread crumb moistened in milk
- egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg grated
- shallot
- sage minced
- Parmesan cheese grated
- cream filling
- brains filling
- herb fillings
- nata
- grated bread

## Directions

1. In place of chicken breasts, make a minced veal that will be pounded with scraped bacon, a bread crumb moistened in milk but squeezed, some egg yolks, seasoned with salt, pepper, nutmeg, shallot, and minced sage
2. After everything is well pounded, form dumplings of the same size as the preceding ones
3. Cook them and serve them over bread crusts fried in broth with the necessary broth and substances
4. They can also be served over bread crusts boiled in broth, covered with grated Parmesan cheese, and browned in the oven
5. The dumplings made with pasta can be made with various fillings such as creamed minced meat, brain filling Italian style, fillings with various herbs made with cream, cheese, grated bread and egg yolks as for ravioli
6. After being served in broth, they are served in soup bowls with succulent and good-tasting broths
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outro Culí de Perdizes ou de outra quaisquer caça (Another Partridge or Game Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four arratels of veal
- Half an arratel of ham
- Two onions in slices
- Two roots
- A little melted bacon fat
- A little flour
- Three or four spoons of stock
- Three or four spoons of substance
- Salt, pepper, two cloves
- One bundle of parsley and fine herbs
- Some slices of truffles
- Some mushrooms
- Pieces of bread
- Two dressed and pounded partridges

## Directions

1. Put three or four arratels of veal, half an arratel of ham, two onions in slices, two roots in a casserole
2. When it sticks, add a little melted bacon fat and a little flour, and stirring always until it takes color, add three or four spoons of stock and three or four spoons of substance
3. Season with salt, pepper, two cloves, one bundle of parsley and fine herbs, some slices of truffles, and some mushrooms
4. Let it simmer little by little
5. When everything is cooked, add some pieces of bread and two dressed and pounded partridges
6. Pass through a strainer and when warm and without boiling, use for everything that is necessary
7. In place of partridges you may use chickens, capons, woodcocks, ducks, wild pigeons, quails, and rabbits, whatever you prefer
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outro Guizado de Veado (Another Venison Stew)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- venison meat
- thick lard
- melted lard
- broth or hot water
- quarter measure of red or white wine
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- lemon
- onion studded with cloves
- bundle of fine herbs
- cullis
- vinegar
- limes

## Directions

1. Take a piece of venison meat from any part, cut it into equal pieces
2. Lard it with thick lard well tempered, then pass it through melted lard on the fire
3. When browned, moisten it with broth or hot water and a quarter measure of red or white wine
4. Season with salt, pepper, bay leaf, a piece of whole lemon, an onion studded with cloves, and a bundle of fine herbs
5. Let it simmer slowly; when cooked and the sauce reduced, bind it with a little cullis and little vinegar, and serve with lime juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outro Modo de Manjar (Another Blanc Mange)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound of sweet almonds
- Milk
- Breast of capon
- Rice flour
- Sugar
- Salt
- Cinnamon
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Soak half pound of sweet almonds in hot water, shell them, and grind them, moistening from time to time with a little milk
2. Add to this a capon breast
3. Grind everything and make into a mass, dissolve with rice flour and one canful of milk
4. Strain through a sieve
5. Season with sugar and a little salt, whole cinnamon, and lemon juice
6. Place on the heat and stir always with a spoon, let it thicken little by little
7. When cooked and with good taste, serve hot or cold, as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outro Presunto para Empada Quente (Another Ham for Hot Pie)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- presunto (ham)
- pranchas de vacca (beef slices)
- vitela (veal)
- hervas finas (fine herbs)
- raizes (roots)
- cebola cravejada (clove-studded onion)
- um quartilho de vinho branco (a quarter of white wine)
- toucinho pizado (pounded bacon)
- manteiga lavada (washed butter)
- pranchas de toucinho (bacon slices)

## Directions

1. After the ham is roasted, trimmed, and without salt, put it to cook in a braise with slices of beef, veal, fine herbs, roots, clove-studded onion, and a quarter of white wine
2. When almost cooked, remove it and let it cool
3. Meanwhile, line the bottom of the pie with pounded bacon or washed butter
4. Place the ham inside with the same braise sauce and covered with bacon slices, close the pie with the dough, and put it in the oven to cook for three or four hours
5. After it is cooked, drain the fat and serve it with sauce like the preceding one
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outro Presunto para uma Entrada (Another Ham for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- um presunto bom (a good ham)
- pranchas de vitela (veal slices)
- toucinho (bacon)
- salsa (parsley)
- hervas finas (fine herbs)
- cebola cravejada (clove-studded onion)
- raizes (roots)
- uma cabeça de alhos (one head of garlic)
- ragú de espinafres à crema (spinach ragout with cream)
- caldo (broth)

## Directions

1. Take a good ham, trim it, remove the bones from the inside through the bottom
2. Soak it for two or three days, and after removing the salt, put it to boil for half an hour in water
3. After it is set, line the bottom of a pan with slices of veal and bacon, a layer of parsley, fine herbs, a clove-studded onion, some roots, and one head of garlic
4. Cover it with other slices of veal and bacon, and put it to cook gently between two fires for ten or twelve hours
5. When cooked, serve it with spinach ragout with cream well made
6. Alternatively, after removing the ham, the veal slices can be moistened with a spoonful of broth, and after boiling a little, this broth can be strained through a sieve, the fat removed, and reduced to a glaze consistency; and when the ham is browned in it, serve it with the same sauce or with vegetable ragout, whichever seems better
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outro Pudim à Ingleza (Another English Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of chopped beef fat
- strong cream made of rice flour or wheat flour
- eight egg yolks
- lemon peel
- preserved orange blossom
- salt
- cinnamon
- nutmeg flower
- sugar
- a drop of brandy
- butter
- butter sauce
- wine of the Kingdom
- sugar
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of chopped beef fat with a strong cream made of rice flour or wheat flour, eight egg yolks, lemon peel, preserved orange blossom, seasoned with salt, cinnamon, nutmeg flower, sugar, and a drop of brandy
2. After everything is incorporated, put it in a pot with the bottom lined inside with butter, and bake in an oven for the space of two hours
3. Remove it, turn it over onto its plate, coat with sugar and a hot spatula, and serve with butter sauce, wine of the Kingdom, sugar, and lemon peel
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Outro Pudin a Ingleza (Another English Pudding)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of beef fat
- strong cream made from rice flour or wheat flour
- eight egg yolks
- lemon peel
- preserved orange flower
- salt
- cinnamon
- nutmeg flower
- sugar
- one drop of brandy
- butter
- Rhine wine
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Take one pound of beef fat and mix with a strong cream made from rice flour or wheat flour, eight egg yolks, lemon peel, and preserved orange flower
2. Season with salt, cinnamon, nutmeg flower, sugar, and one drop of brandy
3. After incorporating everything, place in a casserole with the bottom greased inside with butter
4. Put in an oven and bake for two hours
5. Remove, turn onto its plate, glaze with sugar using a hot spatula, and serve with butter sauce, Rhine wine, sugar, and lemon peel
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outro Rodovalho assado (Another Roasted Turbot)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot
- broth
- white wine
- butter
- onion slices
- salt
- whole pepper
- cloves
- fine herbs
- four egg yolks
- clarified butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the turbot, either in portions or whole
2. Cook it in an adobo made with broth, white wine, a piece of butter, onion slices, salt, whole pepper, cloves, fine herbs, letting it simmer slowly
3. When cooked, place it in a baking dish with butter on the bottom
4. Dissolve four egg yolks in clarified butter and a little heat, pour this over the fish
5. Cover with finely grated bread crumbs and put in the oven to brown, or brown it under a cover, and serve afterward with one of the preceding sauces
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outro Rodovalho Cozido para Dia de Jejum (Another Boiled Turbot for a Fast Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- roots
- onions
- garlic
- salt
- whole pepper
- cloves
- fine herbs
- butter
- turbot
- milk
- béchamel sauce
- shrimp coulis
- curry

## Directions

1. Let half a portion of water simmer little by little for an hour, seasoned with roots, onions, garlic, salt, whole pepper, cloves, and fine herbs, and a piece of butter
2. After it has simmered, strain it through a sieve, put the fish in a large pot with a napkin underneath, pour it the aforementioned broth with as much hot milk, and put it to cook little by little over a gentle fire without raising a boil
3. Once cooked, serve with various sauces, such as Hollandaise sauce, béchamel, shrimp coulis, or any other that seems fitting
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outro Rodovalho para huma Entrada em Dia de Carne (Another Turbot as a Starter on a Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot
- bacon

## Directions

1. Choose a turbot of the size and proportion of the plate it will serve, and after it is cleaned and trimmed, lard it with fine bacon, and if it is excessively large, remove the loins and lard them
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outro Rodovalho para huma entrada em dia de carne (Another Turbot for a Meat Day Entree)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one turbot of appropriate size
- pork lard
- bacon strips
- salt
- onion slices
- two or three cloves
- herbs
- ham slices
- Rhine wine or white wine
- Spanish sauce
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Choose a turbot of appropriate size for the plate it is to serve on
2. Clean and trim it, then lard it with bacon; if it is excessively large, remove the loins and lard them
3. Line the bottom of a baking dish with bacon strips
4. Place the fish on top
5. Season with a little salt, onion slices, two or three cloves, herbs, and ham slices
6. Cover with more bacon strips and place in the oven
7. After some time in the oven, pour in a quarter-pot of Rhine or white wine and let it finish cooking slowly
8. When cooked and well-colored, serve with Spanish sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outros Ovos Recheados (Other Stuffed Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce leaves
- sorrel leaves
- borage leaves
- chervil
- parsley
- butter
- hard egg yolks, one dozen
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- salt
- pepper
- garlic, half clove
- raw egg yolks
- fine breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Boil in water lettuce leaves, sorrel leaves, borage leaves, a little chervil, and a little parsley
2. After chopping all and squeezing out, sauté in a pot with butter
3. After sautéing, pound with one dozen hard egg yolks, bread soaked in milk, seasoned with salt, pepper, and half a clove of garlic, bind with raw egg yolks
4. Stuff the eggs as said above, cover with fine breadcrumbs, brown them, and serve hot
5. Also after being stuffed, they can be served à Basilica, that is, fried and garnished with fried parsley
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Outros Pudins à Ingleza (Other English Puddings)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- ordinary dough
- butter
- a dozen apples, sliced
- grated sugar
- eggs, beaten
- string or cloth
- butter sauce

## Directions

1. Spread a piece of ordinary dough on a buttered cloth
2. Arrange a dozen apples sliced in layers with grated sugar
3. Fold the edges of the dough and unite with beaten eggs
4. Tie with string or in a cloth and place in boiling water
5. Continue boiling for two hours
6. Drain and serve sprinkled with sugar and butter sauce on top
7. Note: puddings of quinces, pears, peaches, apricots, cherries, and all kinds of fruits are made the same way
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos com Ervas Finas em Caixa (Eggs with Fine Herbs in Box)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- truffles
- ham
- hard egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- chives
- fresh egg
- water

## Directions

1. Grease molds inside with butter, like for pastries
2. Cover the bottoms and sides with truffles, ham, cooked hard egg yolks, all chopped fine with herbs, chives, seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
3. Break a fresh egg into each mold
4. Dust on top with the same chopped garnishes that were placed below
5. Put to cook inside a casserole with a little water at the bottom, covered with its lid and coals on top
6. When cooked, remove from the casserole, turn the molds onto their plate, and serve with glace or clear essence sauce, or as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos com Çumo de Azedas (Eggs with Sorrel Juice)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two bundles of sorrel leaves
- washed butter, powdered with flour
- two egg yolks
- salt
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pound two bundles of sorrel leaves in a mortar
2. Press out the juice through a cloth into a saucepan
3. Add a bit of washed butter powdered with flour
4. Add two egg yolks, little salt, and grated nutmeg
5. Place over heat and stir with a spoon until thickened and well-flavored
6. Serve with fresh poached eggs on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Cozidos com a Casca (Boiled Eggs in Shell)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh eggs
- water

## Directions

1. The eggs should be fresh from the same day; place them in boiling water and leave them two minutes on the fire
2. Then remove the pot and cover it with its cold lid, and the eggs inside for another minute
3. Remove them and serve hot on a folded napkin
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Cozidos com a Casca (Soft-Boiled Eggs in the Shell)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh eggs from the same day
- water

## Directions

1. Bring water to a boil
2. Place fresh eggs in boiling water
3. Leave on the heat for two minutes
4. Remove the pot from heat, cover with its lid, and leave the eggs inside for another minute
5. Remove the eggs and serve hot on a folded napkin
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos de Capote (Capote Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eggs
- half dozen egg whites
- six egg yolks
- chopped sorrel
- capers
- salt
- chives, finely chopped
- pepper

## Directions

1. Break eggs into a plate with butter at the bottom
2. Beat half dozen egg whites separately until stiff
3. Fold in six yolks with chopped sorrel, capers, salt, and finely chopped chives
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Beat and mix everything together
6. Cover the eggs already in the plate with this mixture
7. Bake in the oven or under a cover
8. When cooked and without hardening them, serve hot for an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos de Comadres (Godmothers' Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- six onions cut into pieces
- butter
- flour
- half spoonful of beef reduction
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg, grated
- dozen beaten eggs
- six egg whites, stiffly beaten
- essence sauce
- butter

## Directions

1. Braise six onions cut into pieces in butter
2. When braised and cooked, dust with flour
3. Add half spoonful of beef reduction
4. Season with salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
5. Let simmer slowly
6. When thickened and well-flavored, add a dozen beaten eggs and six stiffly beaten whites
7. Mix everything with the onions
8. Transfer to another buttered saucepan
9. Bake in the oven
10. When cooked, turn it over onto its plate
11. Serve with essence sauce bound with a bit of butter
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos de Espelho (Mirror Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eggs, fresh and whole
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Butter the bottom of a plate
2. Place it over heat
3. Break eggs one by one into it, keeping them whole
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Cook over gentle heat
6. Pass a hot spatula over the top
7. When cooked and the yolk is not hard, serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos de Fricassé Branco (White Fricassee of Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- cow butter
- one onion, chopped and powdered with flour
- hot water or milk
- pepper
- eggs, cooked and hard, sliced in rounds
- egg yolks
- milk
- nutmeg, grated
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Sauté in cow butter a chopped onion, dust with flour after sautéing
2. Add a little hot water or milk if available, season with pepper
3. After it has boiled and the sauce is reduced, cut the hard cooked eggs into rounds and place them to heat inside the sauce
4. Give it a boil and thicken with egg yolks mixed in milk, with grated nutmeg and lemon juice
5. Serve hot as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos de Fricassé Pardo (Brown Fricassee of Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- clarified butter
- flour
- four onions cut in thin fillets
- white wine
- meat stock
- salt
- pepper
- egg whites cut in fillets
- egg yolks
- vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Sauté in clarified butter with flour four onions cut in thin fillets
2. After sautéing add a glass of white wine and half a spoon of meat stock, season with salt and pepper and leave to simmer little by little
3. Add the egg whites cut in fillets, keeping the yolks whole, and when the sauce is reduced and of good taste, serve it over the yolks with a splash of vinegar or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos de Meringas (Meringue Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen egg yolks
- six egg whites
- half a quarter of milk
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon
- a little grated lemon peel
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Beat a dozen egg yolks and six egg whites in a casserole with half a quarter of milk
2. Season with salt, sugar, cinnamon, and a little grated lemon peel
3. Whisk and stir with butter over heat
4. Pour into the plate in which it is to be served, with lemon juice and six beaten egg whites mixed with fine sugar
5. Put in the oven or under a lid to brown
6. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos de Senhoras (Ladies' Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful of sweet almonds
- two bitter almonds
- milk
- two roasted and chopped partridge breasts
- salt
- coriander seed
- sugar
- lemon peel
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Grind in a stone mortar a handful of sweet almonds and two bitter almonds, adding a few drops of milk at the same time
2. After well ground, add two roasted and chopped partridge breasts
3. When everything is well ground, dissolve with reduced milk
4. Season with salt, coriander seed, sugar, and lemon peel
5. Heat over the flame
6. Add the necessary egg yolks
7. Pass through a strainer
8. Cook over water in its own plate
9. Serve hot as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos em Filetes à Provençal (Egg Fillets Provençal)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five onions cut in thin slices
- olive oil
- flour
- broth or hot water
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- one dozen hard eggs cut in fillets
- egg yolks
- vinegar
- parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Cut four or five onions in thin slices, sauté in olive oil dusted with flour
2. After sautéing add half a spoon of broth or hot water, season with salt, pepper, and a bay leaf
3. Add one dozen hard eggs cut in fillets, and when the sauce is reduced and of good taste, thicken with egg yolks mixed in a little good vinegar and chopped parsley
4. Serve as an entrée like the above
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos em Restoraõ (Eggs in Restorative)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal, ham, game, and roots (for the restorative)
- herbs such as spinach, sorrel, parsley, tarragon

## Directions

1. Make a restorative with veal, ham, game, and roots
2. Clarify it
3. Pound the desired quality of herbs such as spinach, sorrel, parsley, tarragon
4. After pounding, squeeze through a cloth
5. Pour into the restorative and serve as the previous preparation
6. With poached eggs on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos em Timbales e Outros Moldes (Eggs in Timbales and Other Molds)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- ten or twelve egg yolks
- half of their egg whites
- spoonful of veal substance
- good quality coulis
- clarified butter

## Directions

1. Beat ten or twelve yolks with half of their egg whites
2. Add a spoonful of veal substance and good coulis
3. Pass through a strainer or sieve
4. Pour into molds, first buttered with clarified butter
5. Place in a vessel filled with boiling water
6. Cook with gentle heat below and above
7. When cooked, turn over onto the plate
8. Serve with substance underneath for an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Estrelados (Fried Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Eggs
- Pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take a small amount of butter and melt it in a frying pan.
2. Pour the eggs into it, being careful not to break the yolks.
3. Using a spoon, pour the egg whites over the yolks until they finish setting.
4. Sprinkle pepper on top and add lemon juice.
5. Send to the table.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Fritos com Diversos Molhos (Fried Eggs with Various Sauces)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter for meat days
- cow butter for fish days

## Directions

1. For meat days, fry in butter; for fish days fry in cow butter, both melted and well hot
2. Fry the eggs so they remain round and cooked in such a way that the yolk is not hard
3. Serve with the same sauces and ragús mentioned for the previously mentioned eggs
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Fritos à Italiana (Fried Eggs Italian Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh eggs
- beaten eggs
- bread
- grated Parmesan cheese
- butter or pork fat, very hot
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. Poach eggs in water and keep them fresh
2. Trim them and let them cool
3. Pass twice through beaten eggs, bread, and grated Parmesan
4. Fry in very hot butter or pork fat
5. Remove before they harden
6. Serve garnished with fried parsley
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Mexidos (Scrambled Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eggs, beaten
- cream of milk
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Melt a little butter in a pot, add a few beaten eggs with a little cream of milk, seasoned with salt and pepper
2. Stirring always with a spoon until cooked, serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Mexidos com Legumes (Scrambled Eggs with Vegetables)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- dozen egg yolks
- half dozen egg whites
- melted butter
- two or three spoonfuls of ragout of asparagus tips, well finished

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks and whites very well
2. Put them in a saucepan with melted butter
3. Add two or three spoonfuls of well-finished asparagus tip ragout
4. Stir everything together until cooked and well-flavored
5. Serve as above with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Mexidos com Legumes (Scrambled Eggs with Vegetables)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen egg yolks
- half dozen egg whites
- butter
- two or three spoonfuls of asparagus tips ragú, well finished

## Directions

1. Take one dozen egg yolks and half a dozen egg whites, beat very well and place in a pot with melted butter, to which add two or three spoonfuls of a well-finished ragú of asparagus tips
2. Stirring everything together until cooked and of good taste, serve as the above
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Mexidos à Provençal (Scrambled Eggs Provençal)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen egg yolks
- half dozen egg whites
- butter
- anchovies, chopped
- garlic, a little bit
- nutmeg, grated
- Parmesan cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Beat one dozen egg yolks with half a dozen egg whites, place in melted butter with chopped anchovies and a bit of garlic, grated nutmeg, to which you can also add grated Parmesan cheese
2. Stirring always until cooked, serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Molles (Soft Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of sugar at high point
- fifteen egg yolks
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Pour fifteen egg yolks into one arratel of sugar at high point
2. Stir very well while cooking
3. Once cooked, pour onto a plate and serve with cinnamon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Molles de Vários Modos (Poached Eggs Several Ways)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh eggs
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- various sauces: meat sauce, ham essence, Robert sauce, roe and capers sauce, white sauce, green sauce
- vegetable ragús: asparagus, lettuce, chicory, celery, spinach, sorrel, truffle ragú, sweetbreads, cucumbers

## Directions

1. Heat a half pot of water with salt and a little vinegar on the heat
2. When boiling, cook fresh eggs in it, breaking them so they remain separate from each other
3. Remove before they harden and pass them to warm water
4. Trim them and serve with various sauces such as meat sauce, ham essence, Robert sauce, roe and capers sauce, white sauce, green sauce, vegetable ragús like asparagus, lettuce, chicory, celery, spinach, sorrel, truffle ragú, sweetbreads, cucumbers, or any other that seems good
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Molles ou Escalfados por Outro Modo (Soft or Poached Eggs Another Way)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh eggs, two to three days old
- water

## Directions

1. Place fresh eggs with their shell in boiling water and leave them to boil five or six minutes
2. Immediately pass them to cold water and peel gently so they don't break and remain whole
3. After shelling, place them to heat in warm water and when hot, serve them whole with any of the sauces or ragús mentioned
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Recheados com Queijo Parmezão (Eggs Stuffed with Parmesan Cheese)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs, cooked and hard
- cold water
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- fresh butter
- grated cheese
- parsley, chopped
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg, ground
- egg yolks
- butter for the pan
- fine breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook eggs hard and place in cold water, shell them, cut lengthwise in two halves, remove the yolks and pound them in a mortar with bread soaked in milk, a piece of fresh butter, grated cheese, chopped parsley, salt, pepper, and ground nutmeg
2. Bind with five or six egg yolks, butter the bottom of a plate and put a little of the mixture on it, arrange the hard egg whites filled with the same mixture in a circle and covered as if they were whole, brush with butter on top, cover with fine breadcrumbs, and place to cook in the oven
3. When browned, serve hot as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos Verdes (Green Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- large handful of spinach leaves
- water
- fresh eggs

## Directions

1. Wash and grind a large handful of spinach leaves
2. Dissolve in half spoonful of water
3. Squeeze and pass through a strainer
4. When the water is green, bring it to a boil
5. Poach fresh eggs in it
6. When cooked and trimmed, serve with aspic sauce or clear essence sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos à Bexaméla (Eggs Béchamel Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve or fifteen egg yolks
- half of their egg whites
- a spoonful of béchamel sauce well seasoned and finished
- butter
- lemon juice
- pieces of bread crumb fried

## Directions

1. Beat twelve or fifteen egg yolks with half of their egg whites
2. Add a spoonful of well-seasoned and finished béchamel sauce
3. Mix everything
4. Stir in a little melted butter over heat
5. When cooked and slightly soft, serve with lemon juice, garnished with pieces of fried bread crumb
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos à Calvinista (Calvinist Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half spoonful of beef or veal substance, braised
- eggs, fresh and whole
- pepper
- butter (in the cooking plate)

## Directions

1. Put half spoonful of braised beef or veal substance in a plate
2. Break eggs one by one into it, keeping them whole
3. Season with a pinch of pepper
4. Place over gentle heat to cook slowly
5. Pass a hot spatula over the top
6. Do not let the yolks harden
7. Serve immediately for an entrée course
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos à Crema (Eggs in Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a little butter
- the crumb of two or three vintens of bread
- half a canada of very hot milk
- salt
- fine sugar
- cinnamon
- covered orange flower
- a little grated lemon peel
- twelve or fifteen egg yolks
- the same number of egg whites beaten separately

## Directions

1. Put in a casserole a little butter, the crumb of two or three vintens of bread, and half a canada of very hot milk over the bread
2. When the bread is soaked, season with salt, fine sugar, cinnamon, covered orange flower, and a little grated lemon peel, all in powder and well chopped
3. Add twelve or fifteen egg yolks with the same number of egg whites beaten separately
4. Mix everything well
5. Transfer to a casserole lined inside with buttered paper
6. Put in an oven with moderate heat
7. When cooked, turn onto its plate, remove the paper, brown with sugar and a hot spatula, and serve
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos à Delfina (Delphine Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound clarified and hot butter
- dozen to dozen and a half fresh eggs
- salt
- ground cinnamon
- fine sugar
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Beat fresh eggs in a saucepan with half pound of hot clarified butter
2. Season with salt, ground cinnamon, fine sugar, and pistachios
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos à Delfina (Eggs Delfina Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- clarified butter
- a dozen or dozen and a half fresh eggs
- salt
- ground cinnamon
- fine sugar
- pistachios
- almonds in slivers
- orange flower
- six bitter almond biscuits
- all chopped and in powder

## Directions

1. Beat in a casserole with melted and hot clarified butter a dozen or dozen and a half fresh eggs, seasoned with salt, ground cinnamon, fine sugar, pistachios, almonds in slivers, orange flower, six bitter almond biscuits, all chopped and in powder
2. Mix everything well with a spoon
3. Place in the plate in which it is to be served
4. Cook in the same plate with heat from below and a lid with heat on top
5. When cooked, dust with fine sugar
6. Brown with a hot spatula
7. Serve as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos à Franciscana (Franciscan Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of bread fried in butter
- fricassee of hard eggs, well-bound
- other cooked eggs
- clarified butter
- egg yolks
- bread and grated cheese, mixed

## Directions

1. Put in the serving plate slices of bread fried in butter
2. On top place a well-bound and well-flavored fricassee of hard eggs
3. Cover with another well-made bundle of cooked eggs
4. Sprinkle with clarified butter and some egg yolks
5. Cover with bread and grated cheese mixed together
6. Bake in the oven or under a cover until it takes color
7. Serve hot for an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ovos à Prussiana (Prussian Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound grated Parmesan cheese
- quarter pound fresh butter
- parsley
- chopped chives
- pepper
- nutmeg
- quarter liter white wine
- egg whites and yolks from ten or twelve eggs
- bread marrow, fried

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan the grated Parmesan, fresh butter, chopped parsley and chives, pepper, nutmeg, and white wine
2. Let it boil for an instant
3. When the cheese is melted, beat the whites of ten or twelve eggs to stiff peaks
4. Mix in the yolks
5. Pour everything into the saucepan and stir together over heat
6. When cooked and well-flavored, serve garnished with pieces of fried bread marrow
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Paezinhos à Delfina (Delphina Rolls)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen small round breads the size of an apple
- milk
- sugar
- cream
- egg yolks
- sugar
- lime peel
- candied orange peel
- clarified butter

## Directions

1. Remove the outer crust from a dozen small round breads the size of an apple, open them from the bottom, take out all the crumb, dip for a few moments in milk and sugar
2. Make a filling of cream, egg yolks, sugar, lime peel, and well-chopped candied orange peel
3. Fill the rolls with this, place in each one the piece of crust that was removed, tie them in a cross with a string
4. Fry them in clarified butter, and when fried, remove the string, dust with fine sugar, pass a hot spatula over them
5. After glazed and well coloured, serve hot as an intermezzo dish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Paladares de Vacca com Mostarda (Beef Palate with Mustard)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palates
- water
- butter
- onions
- broth
- broth reduction
- mustard

## Directions

1. They must first be well washed and cooked in water, then cleaned of all the skins
2. Cut into fillets
3. Put a piece of butter in a casserole and place it on the heat with two or three onions cut in slices
4. When the onions are half cooked, add the palates
5. When everything is well cooked through, moisten with a little broth and half a spoonful of broth reduction
6. When the sauce is reduced and of good flavor, remove the fat, add a little mustard, and serve well hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Paladares de Vacca Fritos (Fried Beef Palate)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palates
- broth
- white vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- bay leaf
- cloves
- butter
- flour
- pork fat
- beer or white wine

## Directions

1. After preparing and cooking as above, cut equal pieces about the thickness of a finger
2. Make them take flavor in a hot marinade made of broth, white vinegar, salt, pepper, a clove of garlic, a bay leaf, two cloves, and a piece of butter and little flour
3. After being in this for two or three hours, dry them well on white cloth, flour them, and fry them in pork fat
4. Serve with cold sauce
5. Alternatively, instead of flouring, pass them through a batter made of flour mixed with beer or white wine, a little olive oil, and seasoned with salt
6. Serve well hot with fried sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Paladares à Parmezã (Beef Palate with Parmesan)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palates
- béchamel sauce
- egg yolks
- egg whites
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- bread

## Directions

1. After the palates are cooked, marinated, and cold, cut them into small dice
2. Add them to a well-made béchamel sauce
3. When cold, add some egg yolks with the whites well beaten, and mixed afterward carefully with a small spoonful of bread
4. Put them in a paper box greased with washed butter or in a plate dusted with breadcrumbs, and brown in the oven
5. Serve well hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Panela de Vaca sem Covê (Beef Stew without Cover)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- bacon
- six large onions
- sage
- marjoram
- oregano
- cilantro

## Directions

1. Wash the beef and place it to cook in a pot with a piece of bacon
2. When cooked, add six large onions with some cuts in the middle of each so they do not break, and whole spices: a bundle of herbs, sage, marjoram, oregano, and cilantro
3. When very well seasoned, remove it from the fire, and after half an hour send it to the table
4. This dish is suitable for the months of May, June, July, August
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Panela de Vaca sem Covê (Beef Stew without Covê)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- lard

## Directions

1. Once the beef is washed, place it to cook in a pan with a piece of lard
2. Once cooked
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pargos and Other Fish à Hollandeza (Bream and Other Fish Dutch Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish, prepared
- Water
- Salt
- Butter sauce
- pickled onion
- capers
- salt
- parsley, chopped
- nutmeg, grated
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the fish and make a salt brine of water and salt
2. Bring to a boil; when boiling, put the fish inside and let it continue to boil slowly
3. When cooked, remove it and serve with butter sauce, a pickled onion, capers, salt, chopped parsley, grated nutmeg, and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pargos or Robalos Enrolados (Rolled Sea Bream or Bass)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Sea bream or bass, scaled and cleaned
- Salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- butter, melted
- egg yolks
- bread, finely sliced
- shrimp tail sauce or other sauce of choice

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the fish, remove the bones as in preceding recipes, but leave the heads attached at both ends; if you do not wish to leave the heads, leave a piece of the tail
2. Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice, straighten it on a plate if it is silver, cover the fillets with bacon
3. Roll the fish around the head or tail, which should remain in the middle
4. Pour melted butter mixed with egg yolks over top
5. Cover with finely sliced bread
6. Brush with oil as is customary and put to cook in the oven or under a lid
7. When cooked, drain the fat and serve with shrimp tail sauce or any other sauce desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Partos de Cabrito Assados (Roasted Kid Goat Quarters)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- kid goat or lamb, whole or in quarters
- paper sheets buttered with butter
- bacon, finely cut
- shallot sauce
- enxova
- green onion, chopped
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take kid goat or lamb, either whole or in quarters
2. Almost always roast them in a spit covered with buttered paper sheets
3. Alternatively, after soaking in water, lard them with finely cut bacon and covered with buttered paper, place them to roast in the spit
4. When roasted and of good color, serve with a little shallot sauce as a roast dish
5. They can also be served with a sauce made with an enxova and chopped green onion mixed in substance, with salt, pepper, and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pasteis Communs (Common Meat Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two arratels of mutton, finely chopped
- One quarter of chopped bacon
- Aromatics
- One spoon of broth
- Vinegar
- All ground seasonings
- Three egg yolks
- One eighth of flour
- One quarter of sugar
- Half an arratel of butter
- Two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Brown the finely chopped mutton with one quarter chopped bacon and aromatics, adding one spoon of broth
2. When half-cooked, season with vinegar and cook with all ground seasonings
3. Thicken with three beaten egg yolks and let cool
4. Toast one eighth of flour with one quarter of sugar, half an arratel of butter, and two egg yolks, kneading the dough very well
5. Shape pastries in boxes of desired size and fill with the meat
6. Cook them
7. When serving, dissolve one egg yolk in one spoon of broth with lemon juice and put a small amount of this sauce through a hole in each pastry so the meat becomes more tender
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pasteis de Cidrão (Citron Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- two slices of citron
- a dozen egg yolks
- one arratel of sugar in thread stage
- flower water
- musk
- tender pastry dough the size of a cruzado

## Directions

1. Pound very well two slices of citron in a mortar.
2. Cook with a dozen egg yolks in one arratel of sugar at thread stage, flower water, and musk.
3. Once well cooked, make small boxes of tender dough the size of a cruzado and bake them empty.
4. Fill them with the citron paste and bake with pastille sugar on top in place of a cover.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pasteis de Lombo de Vaca (Beef Loin Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- One preserved beef loin, half-roasted
- Half an arratel of chopped bacon
- Pepper
- Clove
- Nutmeg
- Lemon

## Directions

1. When the beef loin is more than half roasted, brown it in half an arratel of bacon in slices
2. When browned, season with pepper, clove, nutmeg, and lemon
3. Make pastry boxes as for common pastries, cook with the meat inside, and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pasteis de Passarinhos (Bird Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two dozen small birds
- Half an arratel of chopped bacon
- Small slices of pork loin
- All ground seasonings

## Directions

1. Brown two dozen small birds in half an arratel of bacon with small slices of pork loin, seasoning with all ground seasonings
2. Cook and let cool
3. Shape pastry boxes as described for common pastries, filling with the birds
4. Cook and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pasteis de Tutano (Marrow Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two dozen marrow bones
- One arratel of sugar in thread stage
- One dozen egg yolks
- Bread crumbs
- One eighth of flour
- One quarter of sugar
- Four eggs
- Pork butter
- One splash of white wine
- Pork butter for frying
- One arratel of sugar for coating
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Pass two dozen marrow bones through hot water and cut into small pieces
2. Cook in one arratel of sugar in thread stage
3. When cooked, add one dozen egg yolks beaten with a little bread crumb to thicken, and cook
4. Let cool
5. Meanwhile, knead one eighth of flour with one quarter of sugar, four eggs, pork butter, and one splash of white wine, kneading very well
6. Make small pastries, cut with a pastry wheel, fry in pork butter, pass through one arratel of sugar and cover with cinnamon
7. Serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pasteis de Ubre de Vaca (Beef Udder Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef udder, cooked and sliced
- Half an arratel of chopped bacon
- One drop of water
- All seasonings
- Beaten eggs
- Lemon juice
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the beef udder and cut into slices, brown in half an arratel of bacon with one drop of water
2. Season with all seasonings and let cool
3. Take the dough as described above and make boxes without lids the size of an orange
4. After cooking, fill with udder and beaten eggs on top to serve as a lid, lemon juice, and sugar
5. Brown and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pastel de uma Perna de Carneiro (Pie of One Leg of Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- One leg of lamb
- Half an arratel of bacon
- Two ounces of butter
- Two onions
- A splash of vinegar
- Whole seasonings
- A bundle of all aromatics
- Water
- About half a dozen beaten eggs
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put into a pot one leg of lamb, half an arratel of bacon, two ounces of butter, two onions, a splash of vinegar, whole seasonings, and a bundle of all aromatics.
2. Cook in little water until the lamb is more than half cooked.
3. Remove it and mince all the lamb separately, and likewise mince the aromatics separately.
4. In a shallow bowl buttered, layer lamb, layer of bacon, and layer of aromatics until all is finished.
5. Pour about half a dozen beaten eggs over the top and put it to brown on a low flame.
6. When done, make some sops of the said substance outside and after they are well soaked, turn the bowl onto the plate, and break the bowl so that the sop remains whole.
7. Place the pie on top, pour lemon juice over it, and send it to the table.
8. This can also be made with loins and veal, or whatever meat you wish.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pastelilhos Brancos (White Pastries)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken breast
- Beef fat
- Lard
- Veal udders
- Parsley
- Chives
- Salt, pepper
- Bread soaked in milk
- Four egg yolks
- Puff pastry dough

## Directions

1. Make a chopped mixture of chicken breast, beef fat, lard, and cooked veal udders
2. After chopping, pound in a mortar
3. Season with salt, pepper, parsley, chopped chives
4. Bind with a piece of bread soaked in milk and four egg yolks
5. Form small boxes of dough the size of a new silver crusado and the height of two fingers
6. Fill with the chopped mixture
7. Cover with another small piece of dough
8. Cook in the oven
9. Once cooked, remove all the chopped mixture from inside
10. Dissolve in a little broth, egg yolks, milk, and lemon juice
11. Once bound, place back in the forms
12. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pastelilhos de Carne (Meat Pastries)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of veal
- Beef fat
- Parsley
- Chives
- Salt, pepper, nutmeg grated
- Three eggs
- A little milk
- Puff pastry dough

## Directions

1. Make a chopped mixture with a piece of veal, beef fat, parsley, chives
2. Season with salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
3. Bind with three eggs and a little milk
4. Take as much as a walnut and place in the middle of forms lined with puff pastry underneath and on top sealed with beaten eggs
5. After gilding, cook in the oven
6. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pastelilhos de Natas (Cream Pastries)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Six handfuls of cream
- Fifteen egg yolks
- Fine sugar
- Puff pastry dough

## Directions

1. Whip fine sugar with six handfuls of cream and fifteen egg yolks
2. Thicken over the fire
3. Line the forms with puff pastry afterwards
4. Place the cream inside
5. Put them to cook in the oven
6. When the dough is cooked and has good color
7. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pastelilhos de Ostras (Oyster Pastries)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry dough
- Oysters
- Parsley
- Garlic
- Pepper
- Butter

## Directions

1. Line the forms with puff pastry
2. Put a little chopped mixture in them
3. On top of this place an oyster that has been first passed through a little parsley and chopped garlic
4. Season with pepper
5. Cover with another piece of chopped mixture and this with washed butter
6. Put its pastry cover
7. Gild, cook in the oven
8. Once cooked, serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pastelilhos de Peixe (Fish Pastries)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry dough
- Fillets of eels, bream, and hake
- Butter
- Salt, pepper, chives
- Parsley
- Bread cooked in milk
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Line the forms with puff pastry as is customary
2. Put inside a small amount of chopped mixture made with fillets of eels, bream, hake
3. Nourish with sufficient butter
4. Season with salt, pepper, chives, and chopped parsley
5. Bind with bread cooked in milk, some egg yolks, and lemon juice
6. Fill the forms
7. Cover with a small piece of dough
8. Gild, cook in the oven
9. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pastelilhos por Outro Modo (Pastries Another Way)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Breasts of capons or roasted chickens
- Bound essence of good taste

## Directions

1. Cut into dice or thin fillets the breasts of capons or roasted chickens
2. Place them in a bound essence of good taste
3. Let it cool
4. Once cold, place a small spoonful in each form lined with puff pastry
5. Finish and cook like the previous ones
6. Serve in the same manner
7. They can also be made from chopped chicken breast with cream
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pastelinhos de Boca de Dama (Lady's Mouth Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- one eighth of flour
- half an arratel of sugar
- three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Knead one eighth of flour with half an arratel of sugar and three egg yolks.
2. Once well kneaded and firm, shape the pastries the size of two tostões and two fingers high.
3. Bake them empty; when half cooked, fill them with royal almond paste and brown them with a cover of coals or send them to the oven.
4. Brown them and send to the table.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pastelinhos de Manjar Branco (White Almond Paste Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen portions of white almond paste
- six egg yolks
- flower water
- pastille sugar

## Directions

1. Beat a dozen portions of white almond paste with six egg yolks and flower water.
2. Once well beaten, make small boxes (as mentioned above) covered with pastille sugar on top and brown them.
3. Send to the table.
4. They can also be made from cold dough in the shape of a half moon.
5. In the same way they can be made with royal almond paste or cooked cream.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pastelinhos de Ovos Malles (Malle's Egg Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- fifteen egg yolks
- one arratel of sugar at high thread stage
- tender pastry dough the size of a cruzado
- sugar at thread stage

## Directions

1. Pour fifteen egg yolks into one arratel of sugar at high thread stage and mix very well until they become thick.
2. Make small boxes of tender dough the size of a cruzado and fill them with the eggs.
3. Bake them and pass through sugar at thread stage.
4. Send to the table.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pastéis de Bribigões (Clam Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- clams
- parsley
- onion
- egg yolks
- puff pastry dough

## Directions

1. After shelling the clams, sauté them with parsley and onion
2. Season them with four egg yolks (as one does with minced fish)
3. Make pastries of puff pastry dough the size you wish
4. Serve at table hot
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pato com Molho (Duck with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- one roasted duck
- a small amount of butter
- ground pepper
- some saffron
- ripe grapes (if available)

## Directions

1. Place a little diluted vinegar in a cooking vessel under the duck to be roasted so that it collects the drippings
2. Add a small amount of butter, ground pepper, and some saffron to the drippings
3. If you have ripe grapes, squeeze a bunch of them into the sauce
4. When the duck is roasted, brown it and give it a boil in the sauce vessel
5. When ready to serve, place the duck with the sauce on a plate over bread slices
6. Send to table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Patos de Piverada (Ducks with Pepper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck
- white wine
- garlic clove
- nutmeg
- pepper
- bay leaf
- duck liver, half-roasted
- sauce

## Directions

1. While roasting the duck, collect the drippings
2. Add a splash of white wine to these drippings with a clove of garlic, nutmeg, pepper, and a bay leaf
3. Allow to boil
4. Pound a half-roasted duck liver and mix with a little sauce until soft
5. Add to the boiling liquid
6. Place the duck on toasted bread slices and pour the sauce on top
7. Send to table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Patos Dourados (Golden Ducks)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck, quartered
- melted bacon fat
- beaten eggs
- cream soup without sugar

## Directions

1. After the duck is well cooked, immerse the quarters in melted bacon fat
2. When well immersed so the quarters remain whole, pass them through beaten eggs
3. Place them in cream soup without sugar
4. Send to brown
5. Serve at table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Paõ de Vitella (Veal Loaf)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal leg
- veal from the leg
- bacon
- ham
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- one clove of garlic
- four egg yolks
- bacon slices
- pistachios
- sweet almonds

## Directions

1. Cook a veal leg and reserve the best part
2. Remove the nerves and skin well from the remainder and make a forcemeat with bacon and ham, seasoned with salt, pepper, fine herbs, and one clove of garlic
3. Take the best part of the veal, a piece of ham, and bacon
4. Cut everything in dice and combine with the forcemeat
5. Add four egg yolks
6. Line a casserole with bacon slices, arrange everything inside, and cook in a moderate oven
7. Once cooked, let it cool to serve sliced
8. Other times you may add pistachios and sweet almonds cut in small dice and serve with a napkin underneath
9. It can also be served hot with Spanish sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peito de Vacca no Espeto (Beef Brisket on the Spit)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef brisket
- bacon for larding
- white wine
- vinegar
- onions
- garlic
- sausage
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- lemon slices
- butter
- spicy sauce or anchovy and caper sauce or other sauce as preferred
- paper for wrapping

## Directions

1. It is necessary that it be first half-cooked in a good braise
2. After it is cold and larded with bacon as is done for fricandeau, marinate for three or four hours in white wine, vinegar, onions, garlic, sausage, salt, pepper, fine herbs, and lemon slices
3. After, place on the spit and wrap very well in paper greased with butter
4. When well browned and colored, serve with spicy sauce or anchovy and caper sauce or as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peito de Vacca à Alemoa (Beef Brisket German Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Six or eight arratels of beef brisket
- broth
- a bundle of sausage
- chives
- two cloves of garlic
- thyme
- one bay leaf
- six or eight quarters of cabbage
- seven or eight whole onions
- salt
- whole pepper
- a dozen whole sausages

## Directions

1. Take six or eight arratels of beef brisket and after cutting into seven or eight equal parts, well trimmed, and soaked, blanch in boiling water
2. Place to cook in broth with a bundle of sausage, chives, two cloves of garlic, thyme, and one bay leaf
3. When half-cooked, add six or eight quarters of cabbage, which are also first blanched and squeezed, well tied, with seven or eight whole onions, everything seasoned with little salt and whole pepper
4. When everything is almost cooked, add a dozen whole sausages
5. When everything is finished cooking, remove the meat and vegetables and place to drain
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peito de Vacca à Parmezã (Beef Brisket Parmesan Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A whole beef brisket
- thick bacon for larding
- twine
- beef slices
- veal slices
- bacon slices
- onions cut in rounds
- carrots
- parsnips
- white wine
- broth
- salt
- grated cheese
- ham essence or spicy sauce or other sauce as preferred

## Directions

1. Take a whole beef brisket, remove the larger bones, and lard it with thick bacon through the middle as possible
2. Tie it very well with twine
3. Place to cook in a proportioned pot so it fits snugly, on the bottom of which have first been placed some slices of beef, veal, and bacon
4. On top of the beef brisket place onions cut in rounds, carrots, parsnips, with other slices of beef and bacon
5. Cover the pot very well and place between moderate fire below and above to braise for about an hour
6. Dampen with white wine and broth; let simmer gently to cook slowly
7. When cooked, place on the plate in which it is to be served with a little salt-free broth and grated cheese on top
8. Place in the oven in this form and when well colored, serve with ham essence or spicy sauce or any other sauce as preferred
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peito de Vitela Recheado (Stuffed Veal Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast, preserved for three days
- beef breast, yellow
- lard
- clove
- pepper
- nutmeg
- lemon
- dozen marrow bones
- toasted bread slices
- preservation broth

## Directions

1. After a veal breast has been in preservation for three days, cook a yellow beef breast in the pot until half cooked
2. Mince it with a piece of lard and season with clove, pepper, nutmeg, and lemon
3. Use this meat to stuff the veal breast
4. Place the stuffed veal breast in a pan with a dozen marrow bones on top
5. Roast it in the oven with the preservation broth
6. Once roasted, place it on toasted bread slices and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Peito de Vitela à Ingleza (Veal Breast English Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- large veal breast
- thick bacon for larding
- ham
- salt
- pepper
- fine spices
- nutmeg
- parsley
- shallots
- thyme
- marjoram
- cloth
- twine
- broth
- white wine

## Directions

1. Take a large veal breast and remove the bones
2. Open it in the middle and lard with thick bacon and ham
3. Season with salt, pepper, fine spices, nutmeg, minced parsley, shallots, thyme, and marjoram
4. Let it rest seasoned for one day to the next
5. Roll it tightly and wrap in cloth, tie with twine
6. Braise in a wet broth with broth and white wine
7. Once cooked, let it cool in its own braise
8. After cooling and trimming, serve whole on a napkin
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peitos de Capões ou de Frangas para Entrada (Breasts of Capons or Chickens for a Starter)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Five or six breasts of capons or young hens
- Fine bacon for larding
- Water
- Veal pieces
- Ham slice
- Bundle of parsley
- Onion studded with cloves
- Bacon slices
- Broth
- Glaze
- Cucumber ragú or chicory fillets in béchamel sauce or other sauce

## Directions

1. Take five or six breasts of capons or young hens, remove the skins and breast bones, leaving only the wing tips, lard them with fine bacon, and cook them in water after larding.
2. When larded, place them to cook in a casserole with some pieces of veal, a slice of ham, one bundle of parsley, one onion studded with cloves.
3. Cover with bacon slices, add a little broth, and place to cook simmering gently.
4. When cooked, pass the broth through a sieve, reduce it and thicken it to the consistency of glaze.
5. Place the breasts on top with the larding underneath, and when the glaze is set on them and of good color, serve hot with cucumber ragú or chicory fillets in béchamel sauce or any other sauce that seems better.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peitos de Perús para uma Entrada (Turkey Breasts for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey breasts with thighs removed
- bacon lardons
- bacon strips
- veal
- ham slices
- bouquet of parsley, bay leaf, marjoram, and clove-studded onion
- water

## Directions

1. Trim the turkey breasts from which the thighs have already been removed, and beat them between two cloths with the back of a knife
2. Remove the skin and lard them very well with minced bacon
3. Remove them from soaking, then blanch them in water
4. Once this is done, put them to cook covered with bacon strips, veal, ham slices, a bouquet of parsley, bay leaf, marjoram, and a clove-studded onion
5. Once cooked, remove the fat from the broth and let it boil until it reaches the consistency of a glaze, pour it over the breasts, and once it has set and has a good color, remove them and serve with chicory sauce à béchemel or with any other vegetable sauce that seems fitting
6. Note however that one must detach what remains stuck to the bottom of the casserole with a single drop of broth
7. They can also be served with clear essence, strained through what remained of the glaze, and with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peitos de Vitela Recheados (Stuffed Veal Breasts)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breasts
- chicken or partridge breasts
- bacon, half-cooked
- veal livers
- bread cooked in milk
- egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- shallots
- twine
- hot water
- fine bacon for larding
- buttered paper

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal breasts and open them with a knife between the skin and ribs
2. Soak them in water for some time and clean them well
3. Stuff with a mixture made from minced chicken or partridge breasts, half-cooked bacon, veal livers, bread cooked in milk, and egg yolks, all well seasoned with salt, pepper, parsley, and shallots
4. Tie the opening with twine
5. Braise in hot water until cooked through and cooled
6. Lard with fine bacon and cook as for fricandeau, finishing in the same manner
7. Serve hot covered with buttered paper, or if not larded, covered with bacon slices with spicy sauce underneath
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peitos de Vitella por diferentes modos (Veal Breast in Different Ways)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- butter
- ham
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- broth
- hot water
- egg yolks
- milk
- verjuice or apple juice
- green peas
- asparagus tips
- sorrel
- other vegetables

## Directions

1. Parboil the veal breasts and soak them in water for some time, blanch them, and cut them into equal pieces
2. Pass them through the fire with a piece of butter, a slice of ham, a bouquet garnish with little salt and pepper, and dust with flour
3. Moisten with broth and hot water
4. When cooked, remove the bouquet and fat, thicken with four egg yolks mixed in milk, and serve with verjuice or apple juice
5. They can also be made with green peas and thickened with egg yolks as above
6. If not, after being sautéed, moisten them with stock and a little broth
7. They can also be larded as one does with fricandeau and finished the same way, and served with a ragout of asparagus tips, peas, sorrel, or any other vegetable garnish
8. These pieces of veal breast after being cooked in a braise can be served in terrines garnished with cabbage and pieces of intermingled lardoon, or with any other ragout that seems best
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peitos de Vitella recheados (Stuffed Veal Breast)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- ground veal breast
- ham
- veal sweetbreads
- bread cooked in milk
- egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Parboil the veal breasts, open them with a knife between the skin and ribs, soak them for some time, and clean them very well
2. Stuff with a ground mixture in cream, made of breasts of turkey or chicken, half-cooked bacon, veal udders, bread cooked in milk, and egg yolks, all well seasoned with salt, pepper, and parsley
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peixadinhas Fritas (Fried Small Fish)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- small fish
- salt
- flour
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Clean the small fish well and wash them thoroughly
2. Season with finely ground salt, dry them, flour them, and fry them in very hot olive oil over a brisk fire
3. Once fried, serve with fried parsley in a circle
4. In the same way all small fish are served, both from the sea and from the rivers
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peixe Frito em Manteiga (Fish Fried in Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small piece of butter
- Fish
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice
- Parsley
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Take a small piece of butter in a bowl and melt it in gentle heat
2. Once melted, remove the scum, and the salt will remain at the bottom
3. Pour the butter into a frying pan where the fish will be fried
4. After the fish is fried, make a sauce by taking a new bowl and drawing off the butter with a spoon so that it does not break
5. Add two or three egg yolks according to the quantity of fish, lemon juice, and pour everything over the fish
6. Serve with sprigs of parsley and lemon slices on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha
