title: Peixe Inteiro Assado, Cozido, e Frito (Whole Fish Roasted, Boiled, and Fried)
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole fish
- water

## Directions

1. Divide the fish into three parts with two cuts, leaving the head as one part, the middle as another, and the tail as another
2. Hold it by the head and tail, fry the middle part, and turning it in the same manner, fry it on the other side
3. After the middle is fried, roast the tail on a grill on both sides
4. When roasted, wrap the cold and roasted part in paper, and holding it with your hands, boil the head in a pot of boiling water
5. Place the fish on a plate on top of a pastry without a lid, and make three small boxes inside, putting hot sauce for roasted in one, cold sauce for boiled in another, and escabeche for the cold part in the third
6. When done, send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Peixe Morisco (Moorish Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- two fish steaks
- half an arratel of butter
- one quartilho of white wine
- two heads of garlic
- all seasonings whole
- lemon

## Directions

1. Put two fish steaks in a pan with half an arratel of butter, one quartilho of white wine, two heads of garlic, and all the seasonings whole
2. Cover the pan and seal it on the outside with dough so the steam does not escape
3. Cook over low heat
4. When cooked, place the fish on slices
5. Put lemon on top and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pelotas de Nozes de Vitella (Veal Loin Balls)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two loins of veal
- fine bacon for larding
- oil or melted bacon
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- lemon slices
- bay leaves
- chicken or turkey breasts
- veal meatballs
- thin slices of ham
- cullis

## Directions

1. Take one or two loins of veal well trimmed so they remain round, cut them across their entire width into three or four pieces, which you will flatten very well between two cloths with the flat of a knife.
2. Lard them afterward with fine bacon, and once larded, marinate them in a little oil, or in place of this, melted bacon, with salt, pepper, onions, lemon slices, and bay leaves.
3. At the same time marinate the pieces that are trimmed from around the loins with some chicken or turkey breasts, veal meatballs, and thin slices of ham.
4. After marinating everything for some time, place the veal on a washed cloth with the larding facing down, and arrange on top the slices, meatballs, breasts, etc., and cook everything around as a ball, and thus closed put them to cook in a saucepan with bacon slices, veal, ham, onion rounds, and thus covered above and below, cook them over low heat.
5. Once cooked and of good color, remove the balls, and adding a spoonful of cullis to what remained at the bottom of the saucepan, put it to simmer, and reduced and of good taste, remove the fat, and serve them hot with lemon juice.
6. If they do not have good color, they can be glazed afterward, as we have already said.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pepinos com Queijo Parmezão (Cucumbers with Parmesan Cheese)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers
- Broth
- Butter
- Ham essence
- Parmesan cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Stuff and cook the cucumbers in broth
2. Arrange on a plate with a little butter underneath
3. Pour over them a little ham essence mixed with butter
4. Cover with grated cheese and place in the oven
5. After browning, clean the plate edges and serve as the preceding dish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pepinos Recheados (Stuffed Cucumbers)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Five or six cucumbers
- Minced meat with cream
- Broth
- Salt pork slices
- Onion
- Fine herbs
- Ham essence
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Halve and remove the seeds from five or six cucumbers
2. Blanch them and stuff with minced meat with cream, well bound
3. Place to cook in broth with salt pork slices, onion in rounds, and fine herbs
4. When cooked, place to drain in a sieve
5. Serve with ham essence and lemon juice for a course between meals
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pepinos à Bexaméla (Cucumbers in Béchamel Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four cucumbers
- Béchamel sauce
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut into equal pieces three or four cucumbers
2. Blanch and strain them
3. Place in a good-tasting béchamel sauce
4. Let simmer a little, and when cooked, bind with egg yolks
5. Serve for a course between meals
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pepitoria (Pepitória)
body:
## Ingredients

- wings, necks, and other poultry innards (except beaks and feet)
- hot water
- salt
- bacon
- whole onions
- herbs
- pig's lard or butter
- flour
- vinegar
- saffron
- hard egg yolks
- roasted or fried poultry livers

## Directions

1. Pluck the wings, necks, and other poultry innards in hot water, making a cut through the middle of each neck and each head so the brains can cook
2. Cook everything together in water and salt with bacon, whole onions, and herbs
3. When cooked, remove the bacon, onions, and herbs
4. Chop while hot and return to the pan, seasoning with spices, and let cook further
5. In a separate pan, heat some pig's lard or butter until very hot
6. Add flour so that it forms a dough in the pan, making white bubbles that remain very white
7. Pour this with the same pan into the main pan with vinegar and saffron
8. When the pepitoria is ready, pour into a dish to be covered with hard egg yolks and garnished with roasted or fried poultry livers
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perada (Pear Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean and trim the pears and place three arratels of them to cook in water
2. Once cooked, grate or pound them and add to four arratels of sugar at esparto point
3. Stir until dry
4. Once dry, remove from the fire and add fragrance if desired
5. Place in small paper cones
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Peras de Conserva (Preserved Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Select pears that are not very ripe
2. Pierce them with a borer in a cross pattern
3. Place them to cook in a pan of boiling water
4. Once cooked, remove with a skimming spoon and clean the bottoms by placing in cold water
5. Wash them in four waters
6. Place them in sugar at hair point in a quantity that covers the pears
7. Give them nine boilings over nine days for this preserve to thicken
8. If you wish to coat the pears, remove them from the preserve and place to drain on a sieve
9. Once drained, place them in boiling sugar until it reaches pearl point
10. Remove the pan from the fire and place on the ground
11. Remove the pears with small tongs and spoon sugar over them with a spoon
12. Place on a sieve or grill, wrap in paper, and do not place in a humid location because they will spoil
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perdigotos em Agua Ardente (Young Partridge in Spirits)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Young partridges, cleaned and singed
- Young partridge livers
- Scraped bacon
- Bacon slices
- Veal slices
- Ham slices
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Chopped chives
- Spirits
- Substance broth
- Cullis
- Good butter
- Orange juice
- Whole pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and singe the young partridges with legs tucked inside the body or when they have the feet inside the breast, open them from the back and stuff them with a small mixture made from their own livers and scraped bacon
2. Place to cook with bacon slices, veal slices and ham slices and season as is customary
3. When almost cooked, add a small glass of spirits
4. After finished cooking, remove them and moisten what remains at the bottom of the pot with a spoon of substance and half a spoon of cullis, reduce it, and once reduced, pass it through a sieve
5. Remove all the fat, add a piece of good butter, and once finished binding over the fire, serve hot with orange juice and whole pepper
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perdigotos em Caixa Cozidos Debaixo da Cinza (Young Partridges in a Box Cooked Under Ashes)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Four young partridges
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Lemon juice
- Paper sheets
- Bacon slices
- Veal slices
- Ham slices
- Onion slices
- Bay leaf
- Marjoram
- Twine
- Water

## Directions

1. Take four young partridges and after cleaning, prepare and beat them between two cloths
2. Marinate them with salt, pepper, fine herbs, and lemon juice
3. After marinating, wrap them in paper sheets with slices of bacon, veal, and ham, some onion slices, bay leaf, and marjoram, both above and below
4. After wrapping well, tie with twine
5. Sprinkle the paper box with water and place to cook between ashes covered with coals
6. When cooked, carefully open the box
7. Remove the young partridges and serve with the sauce that seems most appropriate
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perdigotos em Ilhota (Young Partridge en Papillote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four young partridges, eviscerated and singed
- Olive oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Chopped chives
- Truffles
- Garlic
- Parchment paper

## Directions

1. After eviscerated and singed three or four young partridges with legs tucked inside the body, flatten them between two cloths with the flat of a knife
2. Marinate them in good olive oil, salt, pepper, parsley, chopped chives, truffles and half a garlic clove, all finely chopped
3. After marinating for two or three hours to take flavor, wrap each one in half a sheet of paper with the same seasonings and place to roast on grills on a gentle fire in such a way that the paper does not burn
4. Once roasted on both sides, serve in the same paper
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perdigões assados com potagem de salsa real (Roasted Partridges with Royal Parsley Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two partridges
- olive oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- parsley, finely chopped
- onion, thinly sliced

## Directions

1. Take two partridges, and after roasting them, carve them and place them in a new bowl with olive oil and vinegar boiling with pepper
2. Let them cool, then make a parsley sauce that is very finely chopped, and onion sliced very thin all around, and pour it over the partridges already placed on the plate, and thus send them to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perdiz Cozida (Boiled Partridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- one partridge
- one quarter pound of bacon
- two ounces of butter
- six grains (peppercorns)
- all the seasonings and herbs
- vinegar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook one partridge with one quarter pound of bacon, two ounces of butter, six grains, all the seasonings and herbs, and vinegar
2. Cook until done with enough broth to fill a vessel
3. Send to table with lemon juice
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perdiz Doce (Sweet Partridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- one roasted partridge
- four cooked and chopped egg yolks
- one quarter pound of ground almonds
- one pound of refined sugar
- butter or bacon fat
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. After roasting one partridge, insert four cooked and chopped egg yolks and one quarter pound of ground almonds mixed with one pound of refined sugar
2. Fry the partridge in butter or bacon fat
3. After frying, pass it through boiling sugar
4. Place on a spit and dry it with cinnamon and powdered sugar
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perdizes Assadas à Franceza (Partridges Roasted French-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- two whole partridges with heads and feet
- melted bacon fat
- two slices of bread
- bread (from pounding)
- sauce from the partridges
- olive oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- toasted bread slices
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. First roast the two whole partridges with their heads and feet in a frying pan with melted bacon fat
2. Toast two slices of bread and pound them in a mortar
3. Mix with the sauce from the partridges (as one makes mustard)
4. Place this mixture in a cooking vessel with a splash of olive oil and two of vinegar, pepper, clove, and nutmeg
5. Place over hot coals until it comes to a boil
6. Pour over the partridges which should be on a plate over toasted bread slices
7. Add lemon juice on top
8. Send to table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perdizes de Miguel Dias (Miguel Dias's Partridges)
body:
## Ingredients

- two roasted partridges, quartered
- two cloves of garlic
- olive oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- two bay leaves

## Directions

1. Bring to a boil in a vessel two roasted partridges cut in quarters with two cloves of garlic, olive oil, vinegar, salt, pepper, and two bay leaves
2. When they finish boiling, send to table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perdizes de Peito Picado (Partridges with Chopped Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- two partridges
- one chicken breast
- one quarter pound of bacon
- four eggs
- all the seasonings
- vinegar
- lemon
- bacon lard
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Remove the breasts from two plucked partridges
2. Chop the breasts with one chicken breast and one quarter pound of bacon
3. Season in the mortar with four eggs, all the seasonings, vinegar, and lemon
4. Fill the partridges with this mixture, larding them with bacon and egg yolk as done with lamb neck
5. Cook for three hours on gentle heat in a frying pan with one spoonful of broth
6. Place the partridges on a plate and thicken the substance with two egg yolks
7. Pour over the top with prosciutto bits
8. Send to table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perdizes em Bolsa (Partridges in a Pouch)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridges or young partridges
- Medium bacon for larding, seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
- Ordinary paste
- Butter
- Bacon slices
- Veal slices
- Chopped meat
- Whole truffles
- Scraped and seasoned bacon
- Eggs
- Napkin
- Broth
- Water
- Salt
- Whole pepper
- White wine
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the partridges or young partridges, tender and stuffed as above
2. Lard them with medium bacon seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
3. Spread a piece of ordinary paste on a buttered and floured napkin
4. Arrange slices of bacon, veal, and some chopped meat on the paste
5. Arrange the partridges on top with their breasts facing down, and place whole truffles in the spaces between
6. Cover with scraped and seasoned bacon
7. Coat the paste around the edges with eggs so it can stick together
8. Fold carefully in the shape of a pouch
9. Fold the napkin in the same way
10. Tie with twine and place to cook in a pot with broth, water, salt, whole pepper, and white wine, simmering
11. When cooked, remove from heat and let drain
12. Open the napkin, turn onto the serving plate, open the pouch carefully
13. Remove the bacon and veal slices and place a Spanish sauce inside
14. Serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perdizes em Granadino (Partridges in Granadine Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Young partridges or young partridge, plucked and singed
- Ham
- Calf sweetbreads
- Chicken livers
- Truffles
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Chopped chives
- Scraped bacon
- Lard
- Fine bacon for larding
- Bacon slices
- Veal slices
- Ham slices
- Parsley bouquet
- Bay leaf
- Orange slices
- Onion studded with cloves
- Broth
- Aspic
- Essence sauce
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pluck and singe the young partridges or young partridge and completely bone them
2. Stuff them with a mixture made of ham, calf sweetbreads, chicken livers and truffles, all cut into small dice and seasoned with little salt, pepper, parsley and chopped chives, mixed with some finely chopped or scraped bacon
3. After stuffing, sew them all around with twine so they remain round and braise them in lard
4. After braising, lard them with fine bacon with the lardoons and place in a pot to cook between bacon slices, veal slices, ham slices, a parsley bouquet, bay leaf, orange slices and an onion studded with cloves with some broth
5. When cooked, remove them, strain the broth through a sieve, remove the fat, mix it with a little aspic, reduce it, and once reduced, place the young partridge on top with the larding facing down
6. Once the aspic is set, remove the twine and serve with essence sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perdizes Novas e Perdigotos (Young Partridges and Young Partridge)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Young partridges or young partridge
- Bacon for larding or covering
- Parchment paper
- Lard
- Lemon slices without skin

## Directions

1. To distinguish young partridges from old ones, they should have the first feather of the wings sharp and with a white mesh at the end
2. After mortifying for two or three days, clean them and lard them or cover them with bacon slices placed in the breast wrapped in parchment paper
3. Place to roast
4. After roasted and of good color, they serve as a roasted dish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perdizes Velhas Feitas em Breza com Diferentes Guarnições (Old Partridges Braised with Different Garnishes)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridges, plucked and eviscerated with legs tucked inside the body
- Thick bacon for larding, tempered with salt, pepper, fine herbs and fine spices
- Bacon slices
- Veal slices
- Some root vegetables
- Onion rings
- Whole salt
- Whole pepper
- Garlic cloves
- Bay leaves
- Whole cloves
- Vegetable ragú and root vegetables
- Or lentil coulis
- Or cabbage
- Or chestnut ragú
- Or olive ragú
- Or truffle ragú
- Or thistle shoot ragú

## Directions

1. After the partridges are plucked and eviscerated with legs tucked inside the body and trussed, lard them with thick bacon tempered with salt, pepper, fine herbs and fine spices
2. Line the bottom of a pot with bacon slices and veal slices, some root vegetables and onion rings
3. Place the partridges inside, well arranged with breasts facing down, and season with whole salt, whole pepper, some garlic cloves, bay leaves and whole cloves
4. Cover with veal slices and bacon slices
5. Cover the pot with its lid and cook slowly, placing coals above and below
6. When cooked, remove them and serve with the vegetable and root ragú that suits best, or with lentil coulis, or with cabbage, or with chestnut ragú, olive ragú, truffle ragú or thistle shoot ragú
7. They can also be served in hot and cold pies and cooked in the pot they can be used to garnish soups
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perdizes à Portugueza (Portuguese-Style Partridges)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- lard strips of prosciutto
- cord
- olive oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- French capers

## Directions

1. Once the partridges are plucked, make six lengthwise cuts in each
2. Lard them inside the cuts with strips of prosciutto
3. Tie them tightly all around with cord
4. Place to roast with fire below and above in a shallow pan with olive oil, vinegar, and pepper
5. When roasted, place on a plate
6. Allow the sauce to boil with a few French capers
7. When boiled, pour over the partridges
8. Send to table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perna de Carneiro Armada (Armed Leg of Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- One leg of lamb
- Half an arratel of bacon
- Aromatics (cheiros)
- Three eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Vinegar
- Lemon juice
- Butter
- Bacon lards
- Egg yolk, cooked
- Blanched almonds
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mince all the meat from a leg of lamb with half an arratel of bacon and aromatics, being careful to leave the bone of the leg whole.
2. Temper the meat on the chopping block with all the seasonings, three eggs, bread crumbs, vinegar, and lemon juice.
3. In a buttered skillet large enough to hold it, begin assembling the leg so that it appears whole, composing it with its bacon lards, cooked egg yolk, and blanched almonds.
4. Put it to cook on a low flame.
5. When cooked, strain the substance with two egg yolks and place it on the plate with slices of bread underneath, lemon juice and cinnamon on top.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perna de Carneiro Armada (Mutton Leg Armed)
body:
## Ingredients

- one mutton leg
- half arratel of bacon, chopped
- herbs
- all spices
- three eggs
- grated bread
- vinegar
- lemon juice
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop all the meat of a mutton leg with half arratel of bacon and herbs, keeping the leg bone whole
2. Season the meat on the chopping block with all spices, three eggs, grated bread, vinegar, and lemon juice
3. In a buttered frying pan that fits the leg, begin to arm the leg
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perna de Carneiro com couve flor e queijo Parmezaõ (Lamb Leg with Cauliflower and Parmesan Cheese)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb leg
- Pork belly lard
- Broth
- Parsley
- Marjoram
- Bay leaf
- Garlic
- Onion
- Cloves
- Salt
- Sauce
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese
- Cauliflower

## Directions

1. Lard the lamb leg with thick pork belly, place it to cook with broth, a bunch of parsley and marjoram, a bay leaf, a head of garlic, an onion with two cloves, and little salt
2. When cooked, strain the broth through a sieve and remove the fat from it
3. Add a spoonful of sauce and a piece of washed butter, tie everything together, and pour a little sauce in the bottom of the serving plate
4. Arrange the lamb leg on top garnished with pieces of cauliflower cooked separately, pour the remaining sauce over it, cover everything with grated cheese, and place it in the oven
5. When browned, clean the edge of the plate and serve as an appetizer
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perna de Carneiro com legumes (Lamb Leg with Vegetables)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb leg
- Broth
- Carrots
- Turnips
- Onions
- Celery
- Pork belly
- Parsley
- Salt
- Cloves
- Water

## Directions

1. Take an aged lamb leg, trim it of fat and bone ends, firm it in hot water, tie it with twine, and place it to cook in a pot with broth
2. Add a dozen pieces of tournéed carrots, a dozen pieces of turnips, six whole onions, three or four celery stalks, all firmed in water and well squeezed, tied with their twine, seasoned with little salt and some cloves, a piece of lard, and a bunch of parsley
3. When everything is cooked, strain the broth through a sieve and reduce it in a casserole
4. Once reduced, place the leg in to brown, and when the glaze adheres to it, serve it garnished with all its vegetables and a sauce passed through the casserole to deglaze what sticks
5. It can also be larded with thick pork belly and cooked in plain water with a bunch of parsley, an onion studded with cloves, and a piece of ham; and when cooked and well browned with its glaze, serve it with its chicory ragout, sorrel, or any other sauce, observing to deglaze with the same sauce what remains stuck to the bottom of the casserole
6. Also after trimming, lard it with fine pork belly and prepare and serve it as a fricandó
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perna de Carneiro Recheado (Stuffed Leg of Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- One leg of lamb
- Minced lamb
- Seasonings

## Directions

1. Prepare the leg of lamb as for armed leg, as described above, with the only difference being that to stuff it, the meat must be carefully removed from one part of the leg, and through the same opening it must be stuffed with the same minced and seasoned meat.
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Peros de Conserva para o Inverno (Preserved Pears for Winter)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a hundred pears
- three canadas of water
- two arratels of sugar

## Directions

1. Clean the pears very well, cut off the tip of the stem, and scrape the rest
2. Put them in water and cook in boiling water until they are soft to the touch when pressed between the fingers
3. Remove with a skimmer and place in cold water, then drain
4. Soak for two hours in water with dissolved sugar
5. Arrange on perforated trays with the stem end facing up
6. Place in a mild oven and leave overnight
7. Remove in the morning and soak again in sugar water
8. Return to the oven in the same way and continue this process for four or five days
9. The last time they should be completely dry, then store in a dry and airy place for as long as necessary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru Assado com Presunto (Roasted Turkey with Ham)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one young turkey
- ham cut finely like bacon
- bacon slices
- lard

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare a young turkey, then lard it
2. Cut ham finely like bacon, taking care to cut it lengthwise
3. After well larded, place it in the roasting spit well covered with bacon slices and paper leaves
4. When roasted, serve with clear ham essence, shallot, and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru Assado com Presunto (Turkey Roasted with Ham)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One tender turkey
- ham lardons

## Directions

1. Prepare one tender turkey, blanch it, and lard it
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru Assado à Italiana (Turkey Roasted Italian Style for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One turkey
- chicken livers
- butter
- lemon slices
- parsley
- marjoram
- shallots
- tarragon
- sorrel
- hot water
- egg yolks
- white wine
- good olive oil
- anchovies
- capers
- salt
- pepper
- cullis

## Directions

1. Clean one turkey and fill it inside with a mince made of chicken livers as previously described
2. Straighten it, tie it with twine, and put it to blanch over heat in a casserole with pork butter, lemon slices, parsley, and marjoram whole, and leave it so it remains very white
3. Once blanched, let it cool, and when cold, place it on the spit covered with bacon strips and paper, and put it to roast
4. Afterwards take a little parsley, small onions, tarragon, sorrel, and blanch everything in hot water, squeeze it very well afterwards, chop everything very fine, put what is necessary in a casserole with four egg yolks, half a cup of white wine, a little good olive oil, a pair of anchovies, a pair of capers, all well chopped, salt, pepper, and a little cullis, and put it over the heat with two lemon slices without skin, and stir always around with a wooden spoon, taking care that it does not curdle
5. Once bound and of good taste, and the turkey roasted, remove the paper and bacon, place it and arrange it on its platter with the aforementioned sauce on top, and serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru com Molho de Estragão para Huma Entrada (Turkey with Tarragon Sauce for an Entrée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Turkey liver
- Fine herbs
- Bacon slabs
- Paper leaves
- Butter
- Egg yolks
- Grated bread
- Tarragon
- Water
- Ham essence
- Beef butter
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. After mortifying the turkey and preparing it for roasting, stuff it with a hash made from its liver and fine herbs
2. Cover with bacon slabs and paper leaves
3. Roast, and when almost done, remove the paper and bacon slabs
4. Coat the legs with a little butter thickened with two egg yolks
5. Dust very well with grated bread
6. Place near the fire to brown well
7. When well browned, serve with tarragon sauce
8. For the sauce: boil the tarragon in water, then squeeze and finely chop it, put in a casserole with ham essence and a piece of good well-washed beef butter
9. Place on heat, stirring and mixing with a wooden spoon so it does not break
10. When bound, serve under the turkey with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru em Adobo (Turkey in Adobo Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey (killed, mortified three or four days)
- Bacon (toucinho grosso)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Saffron (salsa)
- Garlic (alho)
- Shallot (echalota)
- Bacon planks (pranchas de toucinho)
- Broth (caldo)
- White wine (vinho branco)
- Roots (raizes)
- Onions studded with cloves (cebolas cravejadas)
- Bundle of fine herbs (capella de hervas finas)
- Pork lard (manteiga de porco)

## Directions

1. After the turkey is dead and mortified three or four days, empty it skillfully, tuck the legs inside, and singe it over coals.
2. After being well cleaned, lard it with thick bacon seasoned with salt, pepper, saffron, garlic, and shallot, all finely chopped.
3. Cover it with bacon planks, tie it with twine, and put it to cook in a pot or braising pan of its size, with broth, white wine, roots, onions studded with cloves, a bundle of fine herbs, seasoned with salt and whole pepper.
4. Let it simmer for five or six hours. Once cooked, let it cool in the same adobo, and after cooling, remove it and put inside the turkey's body the broth in which it was cooked, which should be jellied, and cover the turkey with pork lard in various decorative patterns, place it on its plate with a folded napkin underneath, and serve it.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru em Forma de Granada para uma Entrada (Turkey in Pomegranate Form for an Entree)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey (prepared and deboned)
- Bacon plank (prancha de toucinho)
- Paper leaves (folhas de papel)
- Twine (barbante)
- Pork fat or melted bacon (gordura de porco ou toucinho derretido)
- Fine bacon (toucinho fino)
- Turkey breast (peito de peru)
- Partridge breasts (peitos perdizes)
- Chicken breasts (peitos de galinhas)
- Ham (presunto)
- Truffles (trufas)
- Veal gizzards (molejas de vitella)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs (hervas finas)
- Scraped bacon (toucinho raspado)
- Bacon planks (pranchas de toucinho)
- Veal pieces (bocados de vitella)
- Ham (presunto)
- Bundle of fine herbs (capella de hervas finas)
- Onion studded with cloves (cebola cravejada)

## Directions

1. After the turkey is prepared and deboned, spread it on the table on top of a cloth, insert inside a plank of bacon, and finish filling with paper leaves.
2. After filling, sew it all around with twine, prepare it in a casserole in pork fat or melted bacon, and after cooling, lard it like a fricandeau.
3. After finishing larding, remove the paper and bacon from inside, and fill it with a raw stuffing made of turkey breast, partridge breasts, chicken breasts, ham, truffles, veal gizzards, all cut into cubes, and seasoned with salt, pepper and fine herbs, mixed with scraped bacon.
4. Once stuffed, sew it all around again, and put it to cook in broth with bacon planks, pieces of veal, ham, a bundle of fine herbs, and onion studded with cloves, letting it simmer slowly.
5. Once cooked, pass the broth through a sieve, reduce it in caramel and give it good color, make the glaze stick like that of fricandeau, and serve it as a ragout or sauce that seems best.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru em Fricandeau (Turkey in Fricandeau)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Young turkey (peru novo)
- Bacon plank (prancha de toucinho)
- Fine bacon (toucinho fino)
- Broth without salt (caldo sem sal)
- Veal planks (pranchas de vitella)
- Ham (presunto)
- Onion studded with cloves (cebola cravejada)
- Bundle of saffron (capella de salsa)

## Directions

1. After a young turkey is cleaned and cleaned with the legs tucked inside according to custom, singe it over a lit furnace and rub it with a plank of bacon.
2. After being well dried, lard it with fine bacon and put it to soak, and put it to cook in broth without salt, with planks of veal, ham, onion studded with cloves, a bundle of saffron, and planks of fine herbs.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru em Galatina para Huma Entrada, ou para Fiambre (Turkey in Galatine for an Entrée, or for Cold Meat)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three turkeys
- Chicken breast
- Best of the thighs
- Cured ham
- Bacon
- Pistachios
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Veal meat
- Bacon
- Cured ham
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Fine herbs
- Sauce
- Shallot
- Egg yolks
- String
- Bacon slabs
- Linen cloth
- Broth
- White wine
- Condiments
- Fine herbs
- Ham essence
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Take two or three turkeys according to the number of galatines needed, singe and clean them, open them along the backbone without tearing the skin
2. Remove the breasts and best parts of the thighs, cutting them into fillets
3. Also cut cured ham, bacon, pistachios, and hard-boiled eggs into fillets
4. From the remaining meat mixed with veal, a piece of bacon and another of cured ham, make a raw hash seasoned with salt, pepper, spices, fine herbs, sauce, and shallot
5. After finely chopping, pound in a stone mortar and bind with egg yolks
6. Take the turkey skins, spread them on cloth, and cover with the hash about a finger's thickness
7. Arrange on top the fillets of turkey meat, cured ham, and the others each in its order, lengthwise, as if the turkeys were whole
8. Sew the skins closed with thread, cover on top with bacon slabs and a piece of linen cloth on top of these
9. Place to cook in a broth of broth, white wine, condiments, and fine herbs
10. When cooked, let cool in the same broth
11. When cold, drain very well, trim neatly, and serve as a cold meat dish for entrees
12. They may be cut into rounds or served whole with ham essence sauce, or with Spanish sauce of good flavor
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru em Globo para uma Entrada (Turkey in Globe Form for an Entree)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey (singed and cleaned)
- Gizzards (molejas)
- Fat livers (figados gordos)
- Truffles (trufas)
- Ham planks (pranchas de presunto)
- Scraped bacon (toucinho raspado)
- Saffron (salsa)
- Shallot (chalota)
- Fine herbs (hervas finas)
- Garlic clove (dente de alho)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Pork lard (manteiga de porco)
- Bacon planks (pranchas de toucinho)
- Veal pieces (bocados de vitella)
- Onions
- Fine herbs
- Broth (caldo)
- Half a canada of white wine (meia canada de vinho branco)

## Directions

1. After the turkey is singed and cleaned, open it along the ribs, remove all the bones.
2. Take some gizzards, fat livers, truffles, and ham planks, cut everything into small cubes, and mix them with scraped bacon, saffron, shallot, fine herbs, a clove of garlic, little salt, and pepper.
3. Fill the turkey with this stuffing, sew it all around with a needle and twine, and close it in such a way that it becomes entirely round, like a ball.
4. After preparing it in pork lard, put it to cook in a braze with bacon planks, pieces of veal, onions, seasonings, fine herbs, a little broth, and half a canada of white wine.
5. When cooked, remove the twine and serve it with a good sauce or ragout, or with the sauce that seems best.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru Moderno (Modern Turkey)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- four egg yolks
- half cup of white wine
- good oil
- garlic
- pair of anchovies
- pair of mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- two lemon slices peeled
- paper
- bacon

## Directions

1. In a casserole with four egg yolks, half a cup of white wine, a little good oil
2. Add finely minced anchovy, mushroom (the note indicates this is garlic), salt, and pepper
3. Put on the heat with two lemon slices without skin and stir constantly around with a wooden spoon, being careful that it does not break
4. When bound and of good taste, and the turkey is roasted, remove the paper and bacon, place and arrange it on its plate with the aforementioned sauce on top, and serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru para Guizados e Assados (Turkey for Stews and Roasts)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Bacon (toucinho)
- Salt
- Paper leaves (folhas de papel manteigado)

## Directions

1. First it must be bled while still warm. In summer remove the entrails immediately without opening it, and after hanging for two days in a cool place and mortified, open it, empty it, and straighten it with all cleanliness.
2. Then singe it over a well-lit furnace, and rub it while hot with a piece of bacon, and after being dried and singed, tie it skillfully by the thighs, lard it with fine bacon, put it on the spit, cover it with leaves of buttered paper, and put it to roast with care.
3. When nearly roasted, remove the paper and finish roasting; when the larded part is well browned, remove it from the spit, and serve it as hot as possible.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru Recheado com Salchichas e Castanhas para Huma Entrada (Turkey Stuffed with Sausages and Chestnuts for an Entrée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Chestnuts
- Turkey livers
- Chicken livers
- Scraped bacon
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sauce
- Onion
- Fine herbs
- Sausages
- Pork lard
- Broth
- Ham essence
- Almond kernel from chestnuts
- Cured ham essence
- Cullis

## Directions

1. Toast the chestnuts by the fire so their skin can be removed, and rub them very well
2. Mix the whole chestnuts with a hash made from the livers of the same turkey and chickens, scraped bacon, butter, salt, pepper, sauce, onion, and fine herbs
3. Stuff the turkey with this mixture after it has been mortified
4. Add some sausages that have already been browned
5. Close and tie with string
6. Brown in a casserole with pork lard
7. After browning, place in a covered roasting pan with bacon slabs and paper leaves
8. Roast on low heat for two or three hours
9. Remove from the roasting pan and untie the string
10. Serve with a ragú made from the almond kernels of the chestnuts and pieces of sausages cooked in broth, bound with ham essence and cullis
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru à Crema (Turkey Cream Filling for Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One or two turkeys, depending on the size of the platter
- bacon lardons
- paper sheets
- veal, bacon, and beef fat
- salt
- pepper
- shallot
- fine herbs
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- six egg yolks
- egg whites
- turkey gizzards, truffles, livers, or other choice ingredients

## Directions

1. Prepare and pluck the turkeys, place them on a spit covered with bacon strips and paper sheets on top
2. Roast them
3. Meanwhile, make a fine mince of veal pieces, bacon, and beef fat, all chopped and cut into small pieces, and pass them over moderate heat seasoned with salt, pepper, shallot, and fine herbs
4. Once the turkeys are roasted and cooled, remove the breast meat, mix it with the mince, chop it on the table, and pound it very well in a mortar
5. Add to it a bread crumb soaked in milk but cooled, and bind it with six egg yolks and egg whites beaten until stiff
6. Place the turkeys in a baking dish with bacon strips underneath, fill the place where the breasts were removed with the prepared mixture, placing in the middle a ragout of turkey gizzards, truffles, livers, or any other choice ingredient, as long as it is cold and well bound
7. Cover it with the rest of the mince, seal it with beaten eggs and the back of a knife blade so it appears whole, cover it afterwards with finely grated bread crumb, and put it to cook in the oven
8. Once cooked and browned, serve with good essence or Spanish sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru à Crema Assado (Roasted Turkey with Cream Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One turkey
- twine
- bacon lardons
- ham of medium thickness
- half a Canada of hot milk
- butter
- fine herbs
- dried coriander
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- eggs
- flour

## Directions

1. After the turkey is prepared, singed, and straightened as is customary, blanch it in a furnace, tie it with twine, and lard it with bacon and ham of medium thickness
2. After larding, soak it in a casserole with half a Canada of hot milk, a piece of butter, fine herbs, dried coriander, salt, pepper, and onion slices
3. After marinating for three or four hours, place it on the spit covered with bacon strips and paper sheets
4. When half roasted, remove the paper and bacon, baste it with its marinade
5. Then thicken this marinade with a little milk, two egg yolks, a piece of butter, flour, and salt
6. Once thickened, gradually pour it over the turkey little by little so that a crust forms over it and takes on a good golden color
7. When roasting is finished, remove it and serve with a peppery sauce underneath or with any other piquant sauce that seems fitting
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru à Montmorency (Turkey à Montmorency)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one young turkey
- bacon slices
- paper leaves
- bacon finely minced
- forcemeat
- ragú of small birds or calf sweetbreads
- truffles
- mushrooms
- twine
- paper leaves

## Directions

1. Prepare and clean a young turkey, open it along the back
2. Remove the carcass up to the croup and the large breast bones
3. Tuck the legs inside and fill with a slice of bacon and some paper leaves
4. Sew it back along the back as if it were whole, tie it, and put it to blanch in lard
5. After blanching, lard it with finely minced bacon
6. Remove the paper and the bacon inside, and stuff it with good forcemeat and a ragú of small birds or calf sweetbreads, truffles, and mushrooms in the middle
7. Sew it closed with twine, pass it with skewers, and put it to roast on the spit covered with paper leaves
8. When roasted and of good color, serve with clear essence of good taste and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru à Perigord para Huma Entrada (Turkey Périgord Style for an Entrée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Two or three arratels of truffles
- Scraped or pounded bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- String
- Bacon slabs
- Paper leaves
- Clear ham essence
- Truffles
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take two or three arratels of truffles and after washing and peeling them very well, mix them with scraped or pounded bacon, seasoned with salt and pepper
2. Stuff the turkey with this mixture right after it has been killed and straightened
3. Tie with string
4. Allow to mortify and take on the taste of the truffles for three or four days
5. Wrap in bacon slabs and paper leaves
6. After roasting, serve with a sauce of clear ham essence, chopped truffles and egg yolks, and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Peru à Princeza para huma Entrada (Turkey à la Princesse for an Entrée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one turkey
- breadcrumbs
- lard
- egg yolks
- capers
- parsley
- other condiments

## Directions

1. Prepare and clean the turkey, cut it in half, beat it between two cloths
2. Remove the larger bones and put it to cook in a good broth
3. Once cooked and cold, trim it, cover it with fine grated bread as is customary, and fry it in lard
4. When fried and colored, serve hot with remoulade sauce made with egg yolks, capers, parsley, and other condiments as described in the section on sauces
5. Alternatively, it can be grilled and served with the same sauce or with another spicy sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perum com Salsa Real (Turkey with Royal Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one roasted turkey
- the cooking liquid
- six quarters of quince
- four quarters of medlar
- vinegar
- all seasonings

## Directions

1. Roast the turkey as in the previous dish
2. Strain the sauce into a bowl set on the fire
3. Cook six quarters of quince and four quarters of medlar in this liquid
4. Season with vinegar and all seasonings so it becomes piquant
5. Cut the turkey into pieces and place on slices
6. Pour the sauce over with the fruit quarters and lemon
7. Serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perum de Sopa Branca (Turkey with White Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- one turkey
- one small piece of good butter
- seasonings
- one garlic clove
- a measure of vinegar
- a measure of water
- one quarter of ground almonds
- black spices
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Roast the turkey in an oven in a pan with a small piece of good butter, seasonings, one garlic clove, a measure of vinegar, and a measure of water
2. When roasted, strain the sauce into a bowl
3. Add one quarter of ground almonds and bring to a boil
4. Season with black spices
5. Carve the turkey and place on slices
6. Pour the sauce over with lemon juice
7. Serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perum Estilado (Distilled Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cleaned and plucked turkey
- half an arratel of ham
- one Portuguese gold coin
- one stone of sugar weighing one quarter
- two egg yolks
- two medlars or one quince, or if not available, two sour apples
- two ounces of butter
- all seasonings whole
- vinegar
- salt
- little water

## Directions

1. Stuff the cleaned and plucked turkey with half an arratel of ham containing one Portuguese gold coin, one stone of sugar weighing one quarter, two egg yolks, two medlars or one quince (or two sour apples if these fruits are not available)
2. Place the turkey in a pot with two ounces of butter, all whole seasonings, vinegar, salt, and little water
3. After arranging everything in the pot, seal it so steam does not escape
4. Cook slowly over gentle heat
5. Serve to the table on slices with lemon juice on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perum Salsichado (Sausage-Styled Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cleaned and plucked turkey
- bread from the mass
- lard, thick and seasoned with salt and pepper
- diced bacon
- six sour apples
- all seasonings whole and ground (except saffron)
- a measure of wine
- little vinegar
- one quartilho of water

## Directions

1. Grind the turkey with the bread
2. Blanch it in a pot with water and salt
3. Lard it first with thick, seasoned lard
4. Place in a pot with diced bacon, six sour apples, all seasonings whole and ground (except saffron), a measure of wine, little vinegar, and one quartilho of water
5. Seal the pot so steam does not escape
6. Place in the oven for three hours, or surround with embers around the fire for four hours, turning the pot frequently
7. When done, place on a plate over slices with lemon juice on top, or verjuice if available
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perum Salsichado de Outro Modo (Sausage-Styled Turkey Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- one young turkey or turkey hen, well larded
- one piece of pork loin
- one piece of bacon or ham
- all black seasonings
- a measure of vinegar
- half quartilho of water

## Directions

1. Lard the young turkey or hen well
2. Place in a pot with a piece of pork loin, a piece of bacon or ham, all black seasonings, a measure of vinegar, and half quartilho of water
3. Seal the pot so steam does not escape
4. Cook slowly over gentle heat
5. When cooked, open and adjust seasoning as needed
6. Place on slices with lemon juice on top
7. Serve the turkey to the table, garnished with slices of the same larded loin
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perum, ou paio, ou gallinha Morisca (Moorish Turkey, Bacon, or Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one turkey
- one arratel of bacon
- mutton broth
- half a dozen walnuts, crushed
- a small amount of mustard
- a slice of grated bread
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Take one turkey and braise it in quarters in one arratel of bacon
2. Once braised, place it in a frying pan over slices with the same bacon
3. Make a sauce outside of mutton broth with half a dozen crushed walnuts and a small amount of mustard made with a slice of grated bread
4. When this sauce boils, season it with all the seasonings and pour it over the turkey quarters placed in the frying pan, thus brown it and send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perú em Fricandão (Turkey Fricandeau)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- bacon strips
- veal slices
- ham
- onion studded with cloves
- bundle of herbs
- salt
- clear essence
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare a young turkey with legs tucked inside according to custom
2. Sear it in a hot oven and rub with a bacon strip
3. Once well dried, lard it with fine bacon
4. Place in a pot and add broth without salt, veal slices, ham, studded onion, herb bundle, and bacon strips
5. Once well skimmed and cooked, strain the broth through a sieve and reduce it
6. Finish the glaze as is done for veal fricandeau
7. Place the turkey with the larding facing down and attached to the bacon with good color
8. Remove the string and serve with clear essence passed through the liquid remaining in the casserole and lemon juice
9. May also be served with ragout of cucumbers, endive, béchamel, creamed spinach sauce, sorrel sauce, or any other preferred sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perú sem Osso (Boneless Turkey in Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Fatback lard, salted and peppered
- Quarter measure of flour
- Turkey feet
- Turkey head and beak

## Directions

1. Prepare the boneless turkey in the same manner as boneless chicken was prepared, larding it very well with salted and peppered fatback lard
2. Knead together a quarter measure of flour, making a tender dough
3. Place the turkey in the dough, seasoning it on the outside, and seal the pastry very well
4. Place the turkey's feet in one bowl and the head with beak in another, so that it appears alive
5. Cook the pastry pie in gentle heat and serve at the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Perú à Breza para huma Entrada (Turkey Braised for an Entree)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- thick bacon
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- veal slices
- onions
- roots
- cloves
- white wine
- broth

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the turkey, straightening it with legs inside the body
2. Sear it and lard it with thick bacon seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
3. Tie it with string
4. Place it in a well-supplied braise with bacon, veal slices, onions, roots, salt, pepper, cloves, and fine herbs
5. Position between two fires to sweat
6. Moisten with broth and some white wine
7. When cooked, place to drain and remove the string
8. Serve with vegetable ragout or any other preferred ragout
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perú à Cardeal para huma Entrada (Turkey Cardinal Style for an Entree)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- turkey livers
- parsley
- chives
- truffles
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- scraped bacon
- egg yolks
- butter colored with shrimp shells
- shrimp cullis

## Directions

1. Take a mortified young turkey or hen turkey for two or three days
2. Stuff it with a forcemeat made from its own livers, parsley, chives, truffles, mushrooms, seasoned with salt, pepper, and scraped bacon, bound with two egg yolks
3. Tie the turkey leaving the legs extended
4. Gently separate the skin on the breast without breaking it
5. Fill it with butter colored with crushed shrimp shells to give it color
6. Secure the skin at the wing tips
7. Roast on a spit covered with bacon strips and paper leaves
8. When roasted, remove the paper and string, and serve with shrimp cullis sauce and other sauces
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perú à Chalota Assado para huma Entrada (Roasted Turkey with Shallots for an Entree)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- young tender turkey
- turkey liver
- bacon
- parsley
- chives
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- shallots
- fresh butter
- white bacon
- paper leaves

## Directions

1. Begin with a young, tender turkey
2. After cleaning very well, make a forcemeat from its own liver and other livers with scraped bacon, parsley, chives, salt, pepper, fine herbs, shallots, and fresh butter, all finely chopped
3. Stuff this mixture inside the turkey
4. Sear it in a casserole with melted white bacon that is very clean
5. When cooled, tie it to a spit covered with bacon strips and paper leaves
6. When roasted, place on a plate with essence sauce, finely chopped shallots, and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perú à Provençala para huma Entrada (Turkey Provençale Style for an Entree)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey hen
- fat livers
- veal sweetbreads
- melted bacon
- flour
- white wine
- broth
- herb bundle
- salt
- pepper
- roe
- capers
- olives without pits
- cullis
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare and mortify a tender turkey hen
2. Place it on a spit covered with bacon strips and paper leaves
3. While it roasts, make a stew of fat livers and veal sweetbreads that are passed through the fire in melted bacon with a little flour
4. Moisten with a quart of white wine, a little broth, and a herb bundle, seasoned with little salt and pepper
5. When reduced and of good taste, add chopped roe, capers, and pitted olives
6. Finish binding with a little cullis and remove the fat
7. Remove the turkey hen from the spit, place on its plate, and serve with its ragout on top and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perúa com Hervas Finas para huma Entrada (Turkey Hen with Fine Herbs for an Entree)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- tender turkey hen
- raw bacon
- parsley
- chives
- fine herbs
- salt
- pepper
- pork butter
- bacon strips
- paper leaves

## Directions

1. Take a tender mortified turkey hen
2. Separate the skin from the breast meat
3. Stuff with a forcemeat made of raw chopped bacon pounded in a mortar with parsley, chopped chives, and all manner of fine herbs, seasoned with salt and pepper
4. After stuffing the breast and placing the same forcemeat inside the body of the turkey hen, sear it in a casserole with pork butter and let it cool
5. Once cooled and cleaned, place it on a small spit and tie it to a large spit covered with bacon strips and paper leaves
6. Roast with care
7. When roasted, remove the paper and bacon, serve with sauce or ragout of all kinds of garnishes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Perúa Nova com Enxovas para huma Entrada (Young Turkey Hen with Roe for an Entree)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey hen
- turkey livers
- scraped bacon
- roe
- parsley
- chives
- truffles
- egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- pork butter
- bacon strips
- paper leaves
- ham essence
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. After preparing the turkey hen, stuff it with a forcemeat made from its own livers, scraped bacon, three roe, parsley, chives, truffles, all finely chopped and pounded in a mortar, bound with two egg yolks, and seasoned with little salt and pepper
2. Straighten the leg after stuffing and close it so nothing escapes
3. Sear it in pork butter and after cooling and cleaning well, cover it with bacon strips and paper leaves
4. Place to roast as is customary
5. After roasting, remove the bacon strips and paper leaves, place on its plate, and serve with ham essence sauce, one roe dissolved inside, juice and lemon fillets
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pescada Cozida para Entrada (Boiled Hake as a Starter)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- hake
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- butter
- flour
- egg yolks
- parsley
- onion
- fish roe
- capers
- broth
- pepper
- nutmeg
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Large hake are ordinarily boiled or fried: the large ones are served whole or in steaks, and are served in water, salt, and a little vinegar, served plain garnished with parsley; or with a sauce made of washed butter, some flour powder, two egg yolks, parsley, chopped onion, fish roe, capers, and half a spoon of the same broth in which the hake was cooked, and after seasoning with pepper and nutmeg, bind on the fire and serve with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pescada Glazada para Entrada (Glazed Hake as a Starter)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- hake
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- fine herbs
- flour
- butter or olive oil
- butter
- flour
- fish substance
- white wine
- fish roe
- capers
- bay leaf
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Braise the hake along the sides, from the head to the tail; remove the loins and marinate for the space of an hour in vinegar, salt, pepper, onion slices, and some fine herbs
2. Dry them in a cloth, flour them, and fry them in clarified butter or in very hot olive oil over a brisk fire
3. Once fried, put them in a butter, flour, and meat sauce with some fish substance, white wine, fish roe, and capers, seasoned with salt, pepper, and a bay leaf
4. Once it has simmered and the sauce is reduced, serve with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pessegada (Peach Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Trim the peaches and cut into slices
2. Place to cook in water
3. Once cooked, place on a sieve and put a pressing board on top with a heavy weight so they drain
4. Once drained, add three arratels of peaches to three arratels of sugar at wire point
5. Cook until it makes small bubbles
6. Remove from the fire and add fragrance if desired
7. Place in small paper cones
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pessegos em Calda (Peaches in Sugar Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- three arrátels of peaches
- three arrátels of sugar in thread point

## Directions

1. After drained, place three arrátels of peaches in three arrátels of sugar at thread point
2. Cook until it forms small bubbles
3. Remove from heat
4. Add scent if desired
5. Pour into small baskets
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pessegos Seccos (Dried Peaches)
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches (not small, not soft, very dry)
- sugar in ribbon point

## Directions

1. Clean the peaches in a sieve
2. Select those that are not small nor soft
3. Dry them very well
4. Remove the pits with an iron tool that runs around like expelling the pit
5. Replace it in the peach after doing this to all
6. Keep them four days in sugar at ribbon point, giving it one boiling each day
7. On the last boiling remove them from the sugar to take out the pits
8. Cover them with the same sugar which should be at the proper point
9. Drain them on a grate or sieve
10. Place them on a tray to dry in the sun so cold does not strike them
11. When keeping them after being well dried, place them in linen cloth so they do not dampen
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pessegos Seccos (Dried Peaches)
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean the peaches in a strainer, making sure they are not small or soft, and very dry
2. Remove the pits with an iron tool that runs in a circle to eject the pit
3. Put it back in the peach once the pit is removed
4. After doing this to all, keep them four days in sugar at esparto point, giving them one boiling each day
5. On the last boiling remove them from the sugar to remove the pits and cover with the same
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pitoro (Braised Meat Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- one loin of veal, pork, or venison
- bacon
- pepper
- a small amount of toasted flour
- four egg yolks
- broth, two spoonfuls

## Directions

1. Take a loin and cut it into very thin slices, and fry them in bacon half-fried
2. Then add pepper and a small amount of toasted flour, four egg yolks, so that it thickens the broth, which you are to add, which should be two spoonfuls, and then serve it over slices to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pollegares de Vitela (Veal Tenderloins)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal tenderloins
- breadcrumbs
- parsley, chopped and ground
- three hard egg yolks
- butter
- French soup

## Directions

1. After the veal tenderloins have been roasted on the spit
2. Make a mixture of breadcrumbs, chopped and ground parsley, and three hard egg yolks
3. Coat the tenderloins with butter first
4. Spread the mixture on them
5. Brown them and place them on French soup
6. Send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pombinhos de Compota com molho branco (Young Pigeons in White Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen young pigeons
- butter
- onion
- cloves
- parsley
- ham
- flour
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- milk
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take half a dozen young pigeons, blanch them, empty them, straighten them, and tuck the feet inside the body
2. Stiffen them in hot water, then place them in a casserole with butter, clove-studded onion, a sprig of parsley, and a piece of ham
3. Pass everything over the fire for a brief moment, dust them with flour, add a little broth, season with salt and pepper
4. When cooked, remove the fat, parsley, and onion, thicken the broth with egg yolks dissolved in milk
5. When thickened, serve hot with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombinhos de Compota com molho pardo (Young Pigeons in Brown Sauce)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- young pigeons
- bacon
- butter
- ham
- parsley
- herbs
- clove-studded onion
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- broth
- beef substance
- white wine
- cullis
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. After cleaning the pigeons and tucking the feet inside, brown them over the fire with rendered bacon or butter, a slice of ham, a sprig of parsley, and fine herbs
2. Dust with a little flour after browning, add a little broth, a spoonful of beef substance, and a little white wine
3. Let simmer slowly and gently until cooked, reduce the sauce, remove all fat, finish thickening with a little cullis
4. Serve hot with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombinhos de Compota à Provençale (Young Pigeons Provençal Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen young pigeons
- olive oil
- parsley
- green onions
- marjoram
- cloves
- garlic
- flour
- broth
- white wine
- truffle slices
- mushrooms
- egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take half a dozen young pigeons, prepare them the same way as the preceding recipes, but using olive oil instead of butter
2. Add a bouquet of parsley, green onions, marjoram, a few cloves, and a clove of garlic
3. After cooking and seasoning, dust with a little flour, add a splash of broth and a glass of white wine, along with slices of truffle and mushrooms
4. When cooked and of good flavor, remove the bouquet and oil, thicken with egg yolks dissolved in broth, add minced parsley, a bit of garlic, and lemon juice
5. Serve hot as an entrée
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombinhos em Compota de Ervilhas (Young Pigeons in Pea Compote)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six young pigeons
- green peas
- broth
- eggs
- milk

## Directions

1. After cleaning and straightening the pigeons, cook them in a casserole as described for the white sauce preparation
2. Add two arratels of green peas and a little broth
3. When cooked and of good flavor, thicken with eggs and milk, and serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombinhos em Conchas com Queijo Parmesão (Young Pigeons in Shells with Parmesan Cheese)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- six young pigeons
- fresh butter
- sweetbreads
- truffles
- ham
- stock
- veal coulis
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Take six young pigeons and after preparing and straightening them, place in a casserole with fresh butter, sweetbreads, truffles, and ham, all cut into small dice
2. Pass through the fire, then moisten with stock and thicken with a little veal coulis
3. When cooked and the sauce is reduced, put a little grated Parmesan cheese in the bottom of the shell
4. Place the pigeon on top with its garnish and a little sauce, with grated cheese on top
5. When finished this way, put in the oven to glaze and serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos a Bazilica (Pigeons Basil Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen pigeons
- egg
- fine breadcrumbs
- pork lard
- fried sauce

## Directions

1. Take half a dozen pigeons and after scalding and cleaning them, straighten them with legs inside
2. Stuff them with fine forcemeat and cook in a white braise
3. When cooked and cold, pass through beaten egg, cover twice with fine breadcrumbs
4. Fry in pork lard and serve garnished with fried sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos a Santa Menhout (Pigeons Saint Menhout)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- milk
- fine herbs
- fine breadcrumbs
- remoulade sauce
- egg
- fried sauce

## Directions

1. Cut pigeons in half, flatten them like chickens in a marinade with milk and fine herbs
2. After cooking, cover with fine breadcrumbs, place on grills and serve with hot remoulade sauce
3. If you want to serve them fried, pass them through beaten egg, cover with breadcrumbs, and after frying, serve on a folded napkin, garnished with fried sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos assados nas grelhas (Grilled Young Pigeons)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- four large fat young pigeons
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion
- good olive oil
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- fine breadcrumbs
- marinade

## Directions

1. Take four large fat young pigeons, clean and singe them, tuck the legs inside, split them down the back or breast
2. Pound them between cloths with the knife blade
3. Season with salt, pepper, parsley, minced onion, good olive oil, bay leaf, and marjoram
4. Let sit for two or three hours to absorb the flavors
5. Dust with fine breadcrumbs and grill over coals, basting with the marinade
6. When grilled and of good color, serve with shallot sauce or spicy sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos de D. Fernando (D. Fernando's Pigeons)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted pigeons
- a small piece of bacon
- a small piece of butter
- all spices
- aromatics
- white wine
- a measure of vinegar
- verjuice if available, or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Roast the pigeons
2. Sauté a small piece of bacon with a small piece of butter, all spices, and aromatics
3. Add white wine, a measure of vinegar, and verjuice if available (or lemon juice) so it becomes appetizing
4. When seasoned, place the pigeons on slices of bread
5. Pour the sauce over
6. Serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pombos de Fricandó (Pigeons Fricandeau Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- prepared pigeons
- bacon
- ham
- parsley
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- clove-studded onion
- broth
- veal glaze

## Directions

1. Prepare the pigeons by singing, tucking legs inside, flattening between cloths with the knife blade, then turning over and cleaning
2. Lard with fine bacon and soak briefly in cold water, then retighten in hot water
3. Place between bacon sheets, a slice of ham, a sprig of parsley, bay leaf, marjoram, clove-studded onion, and a little broth
4. Simmer slowly and gently until cooked, strain the broth through a sieve, remove the fat, add to a veal glaze
5. After reducing and achieving good color, glaze the pigeons and serve with the sauce or vegetable garnish desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos de Marinada (Marinated Pigeons)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- marinade
- egg whites
- flour
- pork lard
- fried sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the pigeons in half, flatten them, and marinate them as mentioned in marinated chickens and serve the same way
2. You can also cook them in the same marinade, and after cooking, pass through egg whites, flour, fry them, and serve garnished with fried sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos de Salsa Negra (Pigeons with Black Herb Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- young pigeons
- bacon, melted
- a small piece of onion
- parsley, chopped
- made parsley sauce, a small piece
- all black seasonings
- a measure of white wine
- a measure of vinegar
- a small piece of well-browned flour

## Directions

1. Half-fry young pigeons in melted bacon
2. Remove the pigeons from the bacon
3. In the same bacon, fry a small piece of onion and chopped parsley
4. When fried, add it to a bowl with a small piece of made parsley sauce, all black seasonings, a measure of white wine, and a measure of vinegar
5. Place the pigeons on top and finish cooking them in this sauce
6. Make the sauce thicker with a small piece of well-browned flour
7. Place on slices and serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pombos Dourados (Golden Pigeons)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons, cleaned
- ham
- choice meats, cut into dice-sized pieces
- broth from the pan
- one egg yolk
- golden toast

## Directions

1. Clean the pigeons and open them along the back
2. Stuff with ham and choice meats cut into dice-sized pieces
3. Close the pigeons again and place in a pan
4. Add one spoon of broth from the pan
5. Cook slowly over heat with a cover of coals on top
6. When cooked, brush the top with one egg yolk
7. Place on golden toast and serve to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pombos em Colher para uma Entrada (Pigeons on a Spoon for an Entrance Course)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six small young pigeons
- reed tube
- melted bacon
- whole sauce
- onion slices
- fine herbs
- salt
- pepper
- lemon slices
- bacon slices
- paper leaves
- aspic sauce or remoulade sauce
- béchamel or white coulis or shrimp coulis or green parsley or Dutch green sauce

## Directions

1. Take five or six small young pigeons and after bleeding, scalding, and straightening them with legs inside the body, pass a reed tube through them to keep them in the given shape
2. Marinate them in melted bacon, whole sauce, onion slices, fine herbs, salt, pepper, and lemon slices
3. After they take on the taste of the marinade, wrap in bacon slices and paper leaves and roast them so they stay white
4. When roasted with the paper and bacon removed, serve with aspic sauce, remoulade sauce, or Italian sauce, or they can be cooked in a white braise between bacon slices and served with béchamel, white coulis, shrimp coulis, green parsley, or Dutch green sauce, or whatever sauce seems best
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos em Fol para Entrada (Pigeons in Foil for an Entrance Course)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six young pigeons
- bacon slices
- fine herbs
- milk
- salt
- whole pepper
- cloves
- dry coriander
- flour
- white wine
- clarified butter
- egg whites
- pork lard

## Directions

1. Take five or six young pigeons and after emptying, singeing, and cleaning them well, leaving their wings, feet, and heads, stuff them with a fine forcemeat made à la crème
2. Cook them between bacon slices and fine herbs, moistened with a half can of boiled cow's milk, seasoned with salt, whole pepper, cloves, a little dry coriander, cooking them slowly and taking care not to cook them too much
3. When cooked, make a sauce with flour, white wine, a bit of clarified butter, and two beaten egg whites, moisten the pigeons in it and fry them in pork lard
4. When fried and well-colored, serve garnished with fried sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos em Papetão (Pigeons in Pastry Case)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon slices
- forcemeat
- pigeon ragout with garnishes
- ham essence
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of the pastry case with bacon slices, cover the bottom with a layer of forcemeat, put in the middle a ragout of pigeons with their garnishes
2. Cover with the same forcemeat and bacon slices and cook in the oven
3. When cooked, turn over onto a plate, remove the bacon slices, drain all the fat, arrange on the plate and serve hot with ham essence and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos Enredados (Bacon-wrapped Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 whole squabs
- bacon
- seasonings
- Portuguese puff pastry
- paper
- string

## Directions

1. Wrap two whole squabs in bacon and season with seasonings, then roast them on a spit
2. When they are browned, cover each one with a piece of Portuguese puff pastry
3. Wrap them in a sheet of paper and tie with string, then roast again on the spit until the pastry is cooked
4. Remove the paper and send to the table hot
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pombos Enredados (Tangled Pigeons)
body:
## Ingredients

- two whole pigeons
- bacon
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Wrap two whole pigeons in bacon, seasoned with seasonings
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pombos Novos (Young Pigeons)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- young pigeons
- bacon slices
- paper leaves
- chicken livers
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- shallot

## Directions

1. Clean and singe young pigeons and cover them with bacon slices
2. They serve as a roasted dish and for various appetizers on the spit
3. After singing and drawing them, mince the livers with a little bacon seasoned with little salt, pepper, parsley, and shallot
4. Stuff them with this mixture and cover them with a slice of bacon and paper leaves
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos Recheados (Stuffed Pigeons)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons, opened along the back, breast, and legs
- finely minced and seasoned mutton

## Directions

1. Open the pigeons along the back, breast, and legs
2. Stuff them with finely minced and seasoned mutton (prepared as in the mutton stuffing recipe)
3. Cook with gentle heat in a shallow bowl with simmering broth
4. Season with all spices and thicken so the broth becomes thick in the plates with egg yolks
5. Place on slices with lemon juice and cinnamon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pombos Recheados (Stuffed Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 squabs
- mutton, finely chopped
- spices

## Directions

1. After opening the squabs along the back, breasts, and legs, stuff them with finely chopped mutton, seasoned according to the dish's style
2. Cook them over gentle heat in a low pot with boiling broth
3. Season with all the spices and thicken the broth with egg yolks so it becomes thick in the plates
4. Place on slices with lemon juice and cinnamon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pombos Soprados para uma Entrada (Soufflé Pigeons for an Entrance Course)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- young, large, and fat pigeons
- bacon
- ham
- veal sweetbreads
- truffles
- cucumbers
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Take young, large, and fat pigeons and remove all their bones
2. Fill them with a forcemeat made of bacon, ham, veal sweetbreads, truffles, and cucumbers, seasoned with little salt, pepper, and fine herbs, and mixed with three beaten egg whites
3. After stuffing and sewing closed, wrap in bacon slices and paper leaves
4. Roast them until done, then remove the bacon slices and paper
5. Serve with Italian sauce of good taste or any other sauce that seems appropriate
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos Turcos (Turkish Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 squabs
- half an arratel of bacon
- one quartilho of broth or hot water
- spices
- bread of ló
- half a canada of milk
- 6 egg yolks
- flour
- 2 cardoons
- spices

## Directions

1. After cleaning, cut the squabs in half and braise them in bacon and broth or hot water
2. When half-cooked, season with all the seasonings
3. Remove from the broth and place on slices of bread of ló moistened in half a canada of milk that has been cooked with six egg yolks and a little flour until thick
4. Meanwhile, cook two cardoons cut into small pieces; when half-cooked, remove from the first water and finish cooking in the squabs' broth, seasoning with all the seasonings
5. Arrange the squabs on the plate, surrounding them with cardoon, and pour the cooked milk on top
6. Brown in the oven with heat from below and above, and send to the table browned
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pombos Velhos em Breza (Old Pigeons in Braise)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- old pigeons
- thick bacon
- veal or beef slices
- roots
- onion studded with cloves
- bouquet of fine herbs
- salt
- pepper
- beef broth
- white wine

## Directions

1. Clean and singe the pigeons and lard them with thick bacon
2. Place them to cook in a braise with slices of veal or beef, roots, onion studded with cloves, and a bouquet of fine herbs, all seasoned with salt and pepper
3. Cover them all with other slices of bacon, veal, or beef, and place them to braise between two fires
4. After a while, add a spoonful of broth and a quarter bottle of white wine
5. Let them simmer gently
6. When cooked, serve them with sauces and vegetable garnishes that seem best, such as Lombard cabbages, small whole onions, turnips, and peas
7. They can also be used for terrines and hot pies
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pombos à Delfina (Pigeons Delphine Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- young pigeons with down still on
- bacon sheets
- lemon slices without peel
- parsley
- veal sweetbreads
- essence sauce
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. The pigeons should still have down on them; after bleeding, plucking, and cleaning, turn them over with feet inside the body
2. Stiffen in hot water, then cook between bacon sheets, two lemon slices without peel, and a sprig of parsley until done
3. Serve garnished with veal sweetbreads, flanked by fine bacon, glazed like fricandeau, with essence sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ponta da Alcatra no Forno (Beef Brisket Point in the Oven)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A point of beef brisket of desired weight
- thick well-seasoned bacon for larding
- a quartilho of white wine
- bacon slices for cover

## Directions

1. Take a point of beef brisket of desired weight and lard it with thick well-seasoned bacon
2. Place in a vessel appropriate to its size with a quartilho of white wine
3. After seasoning, cover with bacon slices and cover the vessel with its lid
4. Seal the lid with dough, place in the oven for five or six hours
5. Remove and serve with its own sauce, removing the fat first
6. The round piece of the leg can also be served in the same manner
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ponta da Alcatra à Ingleza (Beef Brisket Point English Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef brisket weighing fifteen or twenty pounds
- salt
- whole sausage for larding
- water
- cabbage
- English potatoes
- turnips
- carrots
- other garnishes as preferred
- good melted butter

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef brisket weighing fifteen or twenty pounds and rub it well all over with salt
2. After the salt is absorbed, lard it with whole sausage
3. Place to cook in a large pot with water only
4. After boiling two hours, prick with a larding needle: if blood comes out, it is not cooked; but when it begins to exude substance, remove from the fire
5. Serve garnished with cabbage, English potatoes, turnips, carrots, or any other garnish as preferred, with good melted butter
6. It can also be eaten afterwards cold
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Popetão de Pombos (Pigeon Forcemeat Loaf)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
