## Ingredients

- a little veal meat
- beef fat
- pork belly
- salt
- false pepper
- minced shallot
- herbs
- fine herbs
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- four egg yolks
- a little grated nutmeg
- sheets of pork belly
- ragù of pigeons
- essence sauce
- cullis

## Directions

1. Take a little veal meat, beef fat, and pork belly, seasoned all with salt, false pepper, minced shallot, herbs, and fine herbs, and mince it all very well
2. Once minced, pound it in a mortar with a bread crumb soaked in milk, four egg yolks, and a little grated nutmeg
3. Line a forcemeat mold with sheets of pork belly
4. Place part of the forcemeat inside
5. Put in the middle a ragù of pigeons
6. Cover with the rest of the forcemeat and sheets of pork belly as below
7. Put to cook in the oven
8. When cooked, turn it out onto its plate
9. Remove the sheets of pork belly and all the fat
10. Serve with essence sauce and a little cullis as an entrance course
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Popetão de Pombos com o seu Sangue (Pigeon Forcemeat Loaf with its Blood)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- minced meat of one hare
- meat of two rabbits
- ham
- piece of beef loin
- salt
- pepper
- false pepper
- minced chive
- spices
- fine herbs
- pork belly cut into small pieces the size of lentils
- three or four egg yolks
- sheets of pork belly
- ragù of small pigeons
- pigeon blood
- essence sauce
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Make a forcemeat of hare meat, rabbit meat, ham, and a piece of beef loin, seasoned with salt, pepper, false pepper, minced chive, spices, and fine herbs
2. Once everything is minced, mix it with pork belly cut into small pieces the size of lentils
3. Bind it with three or four egg yolks
4. Line a casserole with sheets of pork belly
5. Place part of the forcemeat inside
6. Put in the middle the ragù of small pigeons bound with their blood and egg yolks
7. Cover with the rest of the forcemeat
8. Put sheets of pork belly on top
9. Put to cook in the oven
10. When cooked, remove the pork belly, turn it out onto the plate
11. Drain the fat
12. Serve with sauce of good taste and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Popietas de Vitella à Chantilly (Veal Roulades Chantilly Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- tender pieces from the leg of veal
- good oil
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- truffles
- cucumbers
- lemon juice
- shallot sauce

## Directions

1. Obtain the tenderest pieces from the leg of veal as if for roulades, and after making them very equal, beat them between two cloths with the back of a knife.
2. Marinate them in a little good oil, salt, pepper, parsley, chives, truffles, cucumbers, all finely chopped, and a little lemon juice.
3. After they take the taste of the marinade, roll them as roulades, thread them on a small spit, tie them on a spit, and put them to roast.
4. Once roasted, serve them with shallot sauce of good taste.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Popitees para dias de Peixe (Forcemeat Loaves for Fish Days)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- all the meat of a fish
- other good fish
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- a little dry coriander pounded
- false pepper
- onion
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- egg yolks
- sheets of buttered paper
- ragù of sole loins and shrimp tails
- cullis of shrimp or other sauce

## Directions

1. Take all the meat of a fish, of other good fish
2. Season with salt, pepper, spices, and a little dry coriander pounded, false pepper, and onion
3. Mince it very well on a board
4. Then pound it with bread crumb soaked in milk and bind it with egg yolks
5. Line a forcemeat mold with sheets of buttered paper
6. Place the forcemeat at the bottom and around
7. Put in the middle a ragù of sole loins and shrimp tails
8. Cover with the rest of the same forcemeat and on top a sheet of buttered paper
9. Put to cook in the oven
10. When cooked, remove the paper, turn it out onto the plate, remove the other paper
11. Serve with cullis of shrimp or with any other sauce that seems good
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Postas de Salmão em Caixa (Salmon Steaks in Paper Cases)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three salmon steaks, two fingers thick
- good olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- small onions
- truffles
- cucumbers
- garlic clove
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Marinate two or three salmon steaks, two fingers thick, in good olive oil, salt, pepper, parsley, small onions, truffles, cucumbers, and a garlic clove, all finely chopped
2. Make paper boxes lined inside with butter and place in each one its salmon steak with all the marinade
3. Grill on a slow fire on both sides
4. Once grilled, squeeze lemon juice on top and serve in the same boxes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Postas de Salmão à Mestre d'Hôtel (Salmon Steaks Maître d'Hôtel)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two salmon steaks
- olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- butter
- onion
- parsley
- broth
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Season one or two salmon steaks with olive oil, salt, pepper, and fine herbs
2. Grill them on both sides on a grill
3. Once grilled, place on a plate over low heat with a piece of washed butter, onion, chopped parsley, and a drop of broth
4. Let it simmer, and once the butter is incorporated, serve with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pratinho de Alcachofras (Small Plate of Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- butter
- herbs
- vinegar
- seasonings
- egg yolks
- cinnamon
- lemon

## Directions

1. Scald the artichokes and cut the tips and feet of them
2. Sauté them in butter with herbs, vinegar, and all the seasonings
3. Bind them with egg yolks
4. Serve at table with cinnamon and lemon on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pratinho de Mexilhões (Mussel Small Dishes)
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels from Aveiro
- flour
- eggs
- butter
- vinegar
- garlic
- pepper
- orange
- butter

## Directions

1. Coat the mussels from Aveiro with flour and eggs, and fry them in butter
2. Pass them through a sauce of vinegar, garlic, pepper, orange, and butter, and send them to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pratinhos de Azevias (Shad Small Dishes)
body:
## Ingredients

- shad
- flour
- eggs
- escabeche sauce

## Directions

1. Fry the shad dressed in flour and eggs
2. Place them on the small dish with a sauce of escabeche and send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pratinhos de Lamprea (Lamprey Small Dishes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lamprey
- pepper
- orange

## Directions

1. Roast a lamprey, taking its drippings
2. After roasted, cut it into very thin slices, and pour the drippings on top with pepper and orange
3. Send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pratinhos de Salmonetes (Red Mullet Small Dishes)
body:
## Ingredients

- red mullets
- butter
- small amount of chopped onion
- parsley
- capers
- small amount of prepared mustard

## Directions

1. After the red mullets are roasted, make a sauce by frying in butter a small amount of chopped onion, parsley, and capers
2. When fried, add on top a small amount of prepared mustard, and giving it two more turns in the pan, cover the red mullets with this sauce on top and send it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Prato de Tutanos (Marrow Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- bone marrow from three or four bones
- two spoons of broth
- half arratel of sugar
- white wine
- salt
- chopped herbs
- lemon juice
- cinnamon
- saffron
- spices
- two dozen beaten egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- cinnamon and sugar (mixed)

## Directions

1. Boil the bone marrow cut into pieces the size of a thumb in a pan with two spoons of broth
2. Add half arratel of sugar, white wine, a pinch of salt, chopped herbs, lemon juice, cinnamon, saffron, and all spices
3. When it has boiled two or three times, pour over two dozen beaten egg yolks mixed with breadcrumbs, cinnamon, and sugar
4. Brown in a frying pan with low heat underneath and on top so it cooks slowly and takes on good color
5. Detach from the pan with a spatula and arrange upright on a plate over pastry dough or toasted slices, with three or four egg pasta tubes around the plate, or without them, send hot to the table (for they are not good fried)
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Presunto Assado para Huma Entrada (Roasted Ham for an Entry)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- warm water
- Madeira wine
- peverada sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the ham and remove its skin, soak it in warm water until the salt is removed, dry it in cloth, and soak it again in Madeira wine for ten or twelve hours
2. After this time, place it on the spit, and being half roasted, baste it from time to time with the same wine in which it was soaked, in such a way that it falls in the dripping pan
3. Having finished roasting thus, remove it and serve with thickened peverada sauce or with other suitable sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Presunto Assado para uma Entrada (Roasted Ham for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- presunto (ham)
- água morna (warm water)
- vinho da Madeira (Madeira wine)

## Directions

1. After the ham is cleaned and the skin is removed, soak it in warm water until the salt is removed
2. Dry it in a cloth
3. Then soak it again in Madeira wine for ten or twelve hours
4. Place it on the spit and roast it, basting from time to time with the same Madeira wine in which it was soaked, so that it drips into the drip pan
5. When finished roasting, remove it and serve it with pepper sauce or any other sauce that seems appropriate
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Presunto Cozido para Prato de Finamente (Boiled Ham for an Elegant Course)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- water
- roots
- cloves
- whole pepper
- fine herbs
- wine

## Directions

1. Prepare the ham and soak it for two or three days, wrap it in a cloth and tie it with string, and place it to cook in a pot or a braising pan of the length of the ham, with four canadas of water, some roots, cloves, whole pepper, and a bundle of fine herbs
2. When half cooked, add two canadas of wine, and cooking slowly for five or six hours, when well cooked, let it cool in its broth; then place it to drain, remove the skin carefully without damaging the fat, cover it all with fine grated bread mixed with a little pepper, brown it in the oven or with a hot pan, and serve it on a napkin for an intermediate course
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Presunto de Porco Montez (Wild Boar Ham)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar hams
- salt
- saltpeter
- aromatic herbs (thyme, bay leaf, marjoram, oregano)
- juniper berries, crushed
- water
- wine dregs
- vinegar

## Directions

1. After cutting the legs for hams, singe and clean them well
2. Make a sufficiently strong brine with salt, saltpeter, all manner of aromatic herbs such as thyme, bay leaf, marjoram, oregano, juniper berries crushed, moistened with as much water as wine dregs
3. After infusing for twenty-four hours, clarify it, then place the hams in the brine and leave them there for fifteen or twenty days
4. After this time, press them in a press for some days, remove them, place in a well-sealed room, and perfume them with smoke from aromatic herbs and some crushed juniper berries
5. When dry, keep them in a cool and fresh place to be used when desired: this is for sliced ham, pies, entries, and for the spit, prepared in the same manner as domesticated pork hams are served
6. Alternatively, after rubbing with crushed salt and well dried in the oven, arrange them in a salting box, salt mixed with saltpeter on the meat side, and cover with fine salt, seasonings, fine herbs, and crushed juniper berries, and thus they will cure: the salting box filled and covered with cloth or its lid, placed in a cool location, leave them for three or four weeks; remove them then, place between two boards with sufficient weight on top or pressed in a press for three or four days, perfume them, and keep them as stated
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Presunto na Breza para Huma Entrada (Braised Ham for an Entry)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- veal or calf meat slices
- cloves
- whole pepper
- salt
- fine herbs
- garlic head
- broth
- white wine
- vegetable ragout or spinach ragout with cream
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Soak the ham for three days until desalted, remove its skin, tie it with string and place it to cook in a braise with slices of veal or calf meat, seasoned with cloves, whole pepper, a type of sauce, fine herbs, and a head of garlic, place it thus to sweat two hours on low heat; then pour broth and white wine to it, and let it simmer slowly for five or six hours
2. When cooked, remove it and serve with a well-finished vegetable ragout or spinach ragout with cream for an entry
3. If you wish to serve this same ham for cold cuts, let it cool in its own braise, and covered with grated bread browned, serve it on a napkin garnished with sauce, slices of bread, or whatever else seems appropriate
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Presunto à Ingleza (Ham English Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- presunto (ham)
- agua simples (simple water)
- nabos (turnips)
- cenouras (carrots)
- molho de manteiga (butter sauce)
- batatas (potatoes)
- repolhos (cabbages)
- fabas (beans)
- ervilhas (peas)
- espinafres (spinach)
- caldo (broth)
- farinha (flour)
- azeite (oil)

## Directions

1. After the ham has been soaked for two days and trimmed, cook it in simple water and serve it garnished with turnips, carrots, and butter sauce, or with potatoes and the same sauce; with cabbages, or with any other vegetables such as beans, peas, spinach cooked in the same broth, with butter sauce thickened with a little flour and a drop of broth or water, so that it is not too thick and is made with oil
2. One should note that the ham should not be allowed to cook too much, and that the vegetables in the garnish should be naturally green and each one of its natural color
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Prezunto (Cured Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- half an arratel of lean cured ham
- pork fat (toucinho)
- wine
- bay leaves
- capers
- pepper

## Directions

1. Fry half an arratel of lean cured ham cut into small pieces in pork fat
2. In the same pork fat make a small sauce with a splash of wine and two bay leaves
3. Add capers and serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pudim com Maçã (Apple Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of ordinary dough
- butter
- a dozen apples
- grated sugar
- eggs
- butter sauce

## Directions

1. Extend a piece of ordinary dough on a buttered cloth napkin, arrange on it a dozen apples cut into slices and grated sugar
2. Fold the ends of the dough and unite them with beaten eggs, tie with string or in a cloth napkin, and put into boiling water, in which it will continue for two hours
3. Then let it drain, and serve dusted with sugar with butter sauce on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pudim de Arroz (Rice Pudding)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- rice, cooked in milk and dried
- butter
- ten or twelve egg yolks
- grated sugar
- salt
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- grated lemon peel
- one drop of brandy
- egg whites, beaten
- dough border

## Directions

1. Cook rice in milk and dry it
2. Pound in a stone mortar with butter, ten or twelve egg yolks, grated sugar, seasoned with salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, grated lemon peel, and one drop of brandy
3. Add the beaten egg whites
4. Place everything in a dish with a dough border around it and bake in the oven for about one hour
5. When golden, serve with powdered sugar on top
6. Note: puddings of vermicelli or any other pasta are seasoned and served the same way
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pudim de Arroz (Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice cooked in milk
- butter
- ten or twelve egg yolks
- grated sugar
- salt
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- grated lemon peel
- a drop of Malmsey wine
- beaten egg whites
- dough border

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in milk and make it dry, pound it in a stone mortar with butter, ten or twelve egg yolks, grated sugar, seasoned with salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, grated lemon peel, and a drop of Malmsey wine
2. Add to it afterwards the beaten egg whites
3. And putting everything on a plate with a border of dough around the edge, bake in the oven for the space of one hour
4. When browned, serve with powdered sugar on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pudim de Pão (Bread Pudding)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- the crumb of three or four small portions of bread
- half a pint of boiling milk
- fifteen egg yolks
- a quarter pound of clarified butter or beef marrow
- lemon or orange peel, chopped
- grated sugar
- some Corinth raisins
- salt
- fine spices
- half a pint of Madeira wine
- egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. Place the bread crumb in a casserole and pour boiling milk over it
2. When soaked, add fifteen egg yolks, a quarter pound of clarified butter or beef marrow, chopped lemon or orange peel, grated sugar, some Corinth raisins
3. Season with salt and fine spices, add half a pint of Madeira wine
4. Mix everything with beaten egg whites
5. Tie in a cloth greased inside with butter and floured
6. Place in a pan with boiling water and continue until cooked
7. Drain, place on its plate, and serve with sauce like the previous puddings
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pudim de Pão (Bread Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- crumb of three or four small loaves of bread
- half a pot of milk
- fifteen egg yolks
- a quarter of clarified butter or beef marrow
- lemon or orange peel, chopped
- grated sugar
- some currant raisins
- salt
- fine spices
- half a quartilho of Madeira wine
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Put in a pot the crumb of three or four small loaves of bread, pour over it half a pot of boiling milk
2. When soaked, add to it fifteen egg yolks, a quarter of clarified butter or beef marrow, chopped lemon or orange peel, grated sugar, some currant raisins, and season with salt and fine spices, and half a quartilho of Madeira wine
3. Mix everything with the beaten egg whites, tie in a cloth napkin lined with butter, and put in a pot of boiling water, and continue until it is cooked
4. Then let it foam, put it on its plate, and serve with sauce like the preceding ones
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pudim à Ingleza (English Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two pounds of flour
- water or milk
- chopped beef fat
- eight or ten eggs
- Alicante raisins
- some sugar
- a brandy coin's worth of brandy
- salt
- nutmeg flower
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Dissolve one or two pounds of flour in water or milk, then add to it chopped beef fat, eight or ten eggs, Alicante raisins, some sugar, a brandy coin's worth of brandy
2. Season with salt, nutmeg flower, and ground cinnamon
3. Mix everything together very well, put it in a cloth napkin, and put it into boiling water, and continue to boil for two to three hours
4. When cooked, let it drain, then remove from the cloth napkin, dust with sugar, and finish with butter sauce
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pudin à Ingleza (English Pudding)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- water or milk
- beef fat
- eggs
- raisins
- sugar
- brandy
- salt
- nutmeg
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Soak one or two pounds of flour in water or milk
2. Add chopped beef fat, eight or ten eggs, raisins from Alicante, some sugar, a dram of brandy
3. Season with salt, nutmeg flower, and ground cinnamon
4. Mix everything very well
5. Put in a napkin
6. Tie it and drop into boiling water
7. Continue boiling for two to three hours
8. Once cooked, drain it
9. Remove from napkin
10. Dust with sugar
11. Serve with butter sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pudin à Ingleza (English Pudding)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of flour
- beef fat, chopped
- eight or ten eggs
- Alicante pears
- sugar
- a small amount of brandy
- salt
- nutmeg flower, grated
- cinnamon, ground

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with chopped beef fat, eight or ten eggs, Alicante pears, sugar, and a small amount of brandy
2. Season with salt, nutmeg flower, and ground cinnamon
3. Mix everything very well
4. Wrap in a cloth, tie it, and place in boiling water
5. Continue boiling for two to three hours
6. When cooked, drain and remove from the cloth
7. Sprinkle with sugar and serve with butter sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pupelino (Pupelino)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- eggs
- clarified butter
- fine sugar
- lemon or orange rind

## Directions

1. Make a royal dough with a pound of flour mixed with eggs as for dreams
2. Line a casserole inside with clarified butter, add the dough, and bake in the oven for two hours
3. When cooked, cut in half with a knife point
4. Remove all the raw dough from inside, line with clarified butter, dust with fine sugar and finely chopped lemon or orange rind
5. Place one piece on top of the other, spread butter on the outside, cover with sugar, brown with a hot bread paddle, and serve as a between-course dish
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pá de Vitella recheada (Stuffed Veal Shoulder)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shoulder
- ground veal meat
- veal sweetbreads
- truffles
- chicken livers
- ham
- herbs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Once the shoulder is boned, remove all the meat from inside and make a ground preparation of it as is customary
2. After washing the shoulder very well, dry it in a clean cloth
3. Put a little of the ground mixture with a salpicon on top, made of veal sweetbreads, truffles, chicken livers, ham, all cut into dice and seasoned with herbs
4. Cover with the rest of the ground meat and tie it all around the shoulder, seal it closed with a needle as if it were a pouch
5. Place it to cook in a braise
6. When cooked, remove the twine and serve with Spanish sauce or any other sauce that seems good
7. You can also put a ragout of squabs or small birds in the middle of the ground mixture instead of the salpicon, or stuff the shoulder with a ground mixture Italian style made with brains, herbs, grated bread, cheese, cream, and egg yolks; finally it can be stuffed with whatever seems best according to the taste of those you serve
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pá de Vitella à Italiana (Veal Shoulder Italian Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shoulder
- veal sweetbreads
- veal tongue
- butter
- small onions
- sorrel
- lettuce
- peas
- chervil
- ham stock or broth
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Take a veal shoulder and carefully lift the skin, leaving it attached only at the tip of the bone, and remove all other bones
2. Lard the meat with lardoons of thick bacon tempered with salt, pepper, herbs, and a hint of garlic, all finely chopped and dusted with flour, and cover it with its skin
3. Tie it with twine and place it to cook in a braise
4. In a separate pan, sauté small onions, sorrel, lettuce, peas, and chervil in good butter and reduce by heat with stock or broth, or broth and braise until cooked and well-flavored
5. Remove the veal shoulder from the braise, remove the twine, lift the skin
6. Pour the herb ragout over the meat and cover it with the same skin
7. Place it on its serving platter with some of the herb ragout underneath, and brush it with clarified butter mixed with two or three egg yolks
8. Cover everything with grated Parmesan cheese, place it in the oven to brown, and serve hot, cleaning the edges of the platter very well
9. If you do not have broth or stock, after the herbs have been sautéed in butter and lightly floured, moisten them with milk and thicken with egg yolks also mixed in a little milk
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pás ou Palgares de Vitella à Campaneza, feitas no forno (Veal Shoulder or Palm Cuts Campania Style, Made in the Oven)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shoulder or palm cut
- butter
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- parsley
- garlic
- bay leaves
- cloves

## Directions

1. Take a veal shoulder or palm cut, or any other piece of it, cover it with butter
2. Place it in an earthenware pan with half a quartilho of water, a little vinegar, salt, pepper, two onions in slices, some sprigs of parsley, some cloves of garlic, two bay leaves, and two cloves
3. Cover it with a sheet of paper greased with butter
4. Place it in the oven for two or three hours
5. When browned, remove it, strain the sauce through a sieve, and serve hot over the veal
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pão com Morilhas e Outras Guarnições (Bread with Morels and Other Garnishes)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread, prepared as described above
- chicken breasts, roasted
- morels
- water
- good olive oil
- two slices of ham
- flour
- half a cup of white wine
- one spoon of substance
- pepper
- salt
- bunch of fine herbs
- one chive studded with cloves
- a little roux
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the bread as described above and stuff it with roasted chicken breasts and fry it as the preceding recipe
2. Cut the morels in two halves
3. Wash them in two or three waters
4. Sauté them in a little good olive oil and two slices of ham
5. After sautéing, dust them with flour
6. Add half a cup of white wine and one spoon of substance
7. Season with pepper, salt, a bunch of fine herbs, and one chive studded with cloves
8. Let them simmer little by little
9. Once cooked and the fat removed, thicken with a little roux
10. Place them in their dish with fried bread in the middle and lemon juice
11. Serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pão com Presunto para Huma Entrada (Bread with Ham for a Course)
body:
## Ingredients

- one round loaf of bread
- chopped partridge breasts
- good essence sauce
- lemon zest
- milk
- flour
- pork butter
- larded and glazed gizzards
- sliced ham
- essence sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the top crust of one round prepared loaf of bread
2. Stuff it with chopped partridge breasts placed in a good well-bound essence sauce
3. Place under it the piece of crust that was removed
4. Tie it in a cross with string
5. Dip it in milk for a few moments
6. Dust it with flour
7. Fry it in pork butter
8. After fried and well colored, remove the string
9. Place it on its dish
10. Garnish it with larded and glazed gizzards and sliced ham
11. Serve it with essence sauce of good taste
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pão de Amendoas e de Pistaches (Almond and Pistachio Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of almonds
- half a pound of pistachios
- egg whites
- a bit of citron
- lemon zest
- one pound of grated sugar
- fifteen fresh egg yolks
- beaten and raised egg whites
- butter
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch in hot water one pound of almonds and half a pound of pistachios
2. Place them in cold water, drain them in a cloth, rinse them
3. Grind them in a stone mortar with a few egg whites
4. When ground and reduced to a paste, add to it a bit of chopped citron and lemon zest, one pound of grated sugar, fifteen fresh egg yolks
5. Mix everything very well
6. Add to it the beaten and raised egg whites
7. Grease a dish with butter
8. Put everything on it
9. Join it around
10. Dust it with fine sugar
11. Put it to cook in the oven with moderate heat
12. After baked, serve it glazed and well colored
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pão de Amêndoas e de Pistaches (Almond and Pistachio Bread)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of almonds
- half a pound of pistachios
- egg whites
- a bit of citron
- lime peel
- one pound of grated sugar
- fifteen fresh egg yolks
- egg whites, beaten and raised

## Directions

1. Soak one pound of almonds and half a pound of pistachios in hot water, then place them in cold water, drain them in a cloth, and dry them
2. Pound them in a stone mortar with a few egg whites
3. When pounded and reduced to a paste, add a bit of chopped citron and lime peel, one pound of grated sugar, and fifteen fresh egg yolks
4. Mix everything very well, add the beaten and raised egg whites
5. Butter a dish, put everything in it, butter it around the edges, dust with fine sugar
6. Put in the oven to bake with moderate heat
7. After baking, serve glazed
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pão de Creme para Entre-Meio (Cream Bread for a Side Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- one round loaf of bread of thirty reis
- milk
- sugar
- franchipane cream
- lemon zest
- fine grated sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the top crust of one round loaf of bread of thirty reis
2. Open it from the bottom part, remove all the crumb from it, and pour it into milk and sugar for a few minutes
3. Then remove it to a sieve and let it drain
4. From this make a franchipane cream well bound
5. Stuff the bread with it
6. Place under it the piece of crust that was removed
7. Arrange it on the dish in which it is to be served with a little of the same cream underneath and on top
8. Its fillets of lemon zest in symmetry
9. Dust it with fine grated sugar
10. Put it to cook in the oven
11. When baked, glazed, and well colored, serve it hot
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pão de Creme para Entremeio (Cream Bread for Intermezzo)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one round loaf of thirty reis bread
- milk
- sugar
- franchipane cream
- lime peel

## Directions

1. Remove the outer crust of a round loaf, open it from the bottom, take out all the crumb, and dip it in milk and sugar for a few minutes
2. Then place it on a sieve and let it drain
3. Make a well-bound franchipane cream, fill the bread with it, put underneath the piece of crust that was removed
4. Arrange it on the plate where it is to be served with a little of the cream underneath and on top, its slices of lime peel in symmetry
5. Dust with fine grated sugar, put in the oven to bake
6. When baked, glazed, and well coloured, serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pão de Ló de Amendoas (Almond Sponge Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of almonds cut in half
- one arratel of finely ground almonds
- two and a half arratels of stone sugar

## Directions

1. Add one arratel of almonds cut in half and one arratel of finely ground almonds to two and a half arratels of stone sugar
2. After two turns over the fire, remove and beat very well until it rises
3. Place on stone or in a buttered basin, stirring with it so it loses its gloss
4. Cut into slices and serve at table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pão de Ló Fofo (Light Sponge Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of clean sugar
- fifteen eggs
- three quarters of sifted flour

## Directions

1. Take one arratel of clean sugar and place in a pot, add fifteen eggs on top and beat with a beater or spoon very well until thick
2. Once thick, check if the oven is prepared, then add three quarters of sifted flour and beat very well so the flour does not settle at the bottom
3. Once well beaten, pour into a basin or baking vessel, beating constantly at the bottom until it goes into the oven so the flour does not settle
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pão de Ló ou Bolo de Saboia (Sponge Cake or Savoiardi)
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty egg yolks
- sugar equal to the weight of the eggs
- grated lemon rind
- conserved orange flower
- egg whites
- flour equal to half the weight of the eggs
- clarified butter
- fine sugar for glaze
- egg white for glaze
- lemon juice for glaze

## Directions

1. Put twenty egg yolks in a bowl with sugar equal to their weight, grated lemon rind, and conserved orange flower
2. Beat with two spoons for half an hour
3. Add the beaten egg whites
4. After well mixing, gradually add flour equal to half the weight of the eggs with care
5. Pour into a copper mold of your choice, lined first with clarified butter
6. Bake in a moderate oven for one and a half to two hours
7. When cooked and golden, serve plain, or cover with a glaze made of fine sugar, egg white, and lemon juice
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pão de Ló ou Bolo de Saboya (Sponge Cake)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty egg yolks
- Fine sugar, weight equal to the eggs
- Grated lemon peel
- Conserved orange flower
- Egg whites
- Flour, weight equal to half the eggs
- Clarified butter

## Directions

1. Place twenty egg yolks and fine sugar equal to the weight of the eggs in a bowl, along with grated lemon peel and conserved orange flower
2. Beat this for half an hour with two spoons
3. Then add the well-beaten egg whites, and after mixing well with the yolks, gradually add, carefully, flour equal to half the weight of the eggs
4. Pour everything into a mold of any desired shape, first greased inside with clarified butter, and bake in a moderately heated oven for an hour and a half to two hours
5. When baked and with good color, serve as is, or if desired, cover with a glaze made of fine sugar, egg white, and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pão de Ló Torrado (Toasted Sponge Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one arratel of clean sugar
- ten eggs
- one arratel of sifted flour

## Directions

1. Place one arratel of clean sugar in a pot, add ten eggs on top and beat very well until thick
2. Check with the oven if the flour can be added, which should be one arratel sifted
3. Once approved, add it while beating very well for some time so the flour does not settle, pour into a basin, and beat constantly at the bottom until it enters the oven
4. When it comes out, slice it and return the slices to a basin in the oven to toast lightly, ensuring it does not burn
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pão de Ló á Bernouille (Sponge Cake à Bernouille)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half an eighth of flour
- Four eggs with their whites
- A piece of chopped lemon peel
- A drop of flower water
- Water

## Directions

1. Take half an eighth of flour, four eggs with their whites, a piece of chopped lemon peel, and a drop of flower water
2. Knead all with plain water
3. After kneading well, let it rest for an hour
4. Roll it out
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Pão em Talhadas de Melão (Bread Sliced Like Melon)
body:
## Ingredients

- round bread
- sweetbreads
- artichoke hearts
- ham
- roux
- grated cheese
- five or six lamb tails
- braising liquid
- minced meat à crème with chives, cooked
- roux with melted butter
- grated Parmesan cheese
- essence sauce, clear

## Directions

1. Remove the crust from a round bread
2. Open it from the bottom and remove the crumb
3. Stuff it with a forcemeat of sweetbreads, artichoke hearts, and ham, all cooked and of good taste
4. Place it on its dish with a little roux underneath
5. Arrange around it five or six lamb tails cooked in braising liquid with their points upward
6. Fill the intervals with minced meat à crème with cooked chives
7. Pour over all a little roux with melted butter
8. Dust with grated Parmesan cheese
9. Cook covered under a lid
10. Drain off the fat afterward
11. Serve with clear essence sauce
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pão em Talhadas de Melão para Dias de Peixe (Bread Sliced Like Melon for Fish Days)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- butter, browned
- minced fish
- roux
- four small red mullets, cooked
- cooked chives or fillets of sole
- butter
- egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- roux
- marisco of the best taste

## Directions

1. Remove the crust and crumb from bread and pass it for an instant through browned butter
2. Stuff it with minced fish and place it on its dish with a little roux underneath
3. Cover it with a little of the same minced fish
4. Arrange on top four small red mullets already cooked
5. Garnish the intervals with cooked chives or fillets of sole
6. Sprinkle with butter and egg yolks
7. Cover with breadcrumbs and put it to brown in the oven
8. Once browned, drain off the butter, clean the dish around the edges
9. Serve with roux sauce and garnished with marisco of the best taste, or as seems best
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pãozinhos á Delfina (Delfina Bread Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen rolls the size of an apple
- milk
- sugar
- cream
- egg yolks
- sugar
- lemon zest
- conserved orange flower, finely chopped
- clarified butter
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the top crust from a dozen rolls the size of an apple
2. Open them from the bottom, remove all the crumb from them
3. Dip them for a few moments in milk and sugar
4. Make a filling of cream, egg yolks, sugar, lemon zest, and finely chopped conserved orange flower
5. Stuff the rolls with this
6. Place in each one the piece of crust that was removed
7. Tie them in a cross with string
8. Fry them in clarified butter
9. When fried, remove the string
10. Dust them with fine sugar
11. Pass a hot spatula over them
12. After being glazed and well colored, serve them hot as a side dish
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Pés de Vitela à la Dama (Veal Feet à la Dama)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal feet
- eggs
- flour
- sugar
- bay leaf
- cinnamon
- fine herbs, parsley, savory
- two cloves
- pan broth
- vinegar
- egg yolks
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cook the veal feet and cut them into small pieces
2. Remove the bones
3. Pass them through eggs and bread them by frying, passing them through eggs one or two more times
4. Place them in a pan with small pieces of flour and sugar, bay leaf, cinnamon, fine herbs, parsley, savory, and two cloves
5. Add the broth from the pan with a bit of good vinegar and bring to a gentle simmer
6. Have half a dozen or one dozen egg yolks with lemon juice, depending on the portion
7. When the pan simmers, taste it, as it should be sour and sweet according to the taste of those eating
8. Thicken this broth with the egg yolks so it becomes thick and place it on the plate and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Quadrados de Carneiro com Salsa (Lamb Squares with Parsley)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chop squares
- parsley in thin strips
- pork butter
- shallot sauce
- lemon juice
- bacon lard

## Directions

1. Take two squares of lamb chops, clean them, and prepare them as stated
2. Lard them with bocal-sized pieces of green parsley
3. Tie them to a spit and place them to roast
4. When the parsley is dry, baste them with hot pork butter and continue until roasted
5. Serve with shallot sauce and lemon juice, or they can be larded with fine bacon and after being marinated, roasted, and served with the same sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quadrados de Carneiro à Conty (Lamb Squares à Conty)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chop squares
- bacon strips
- mushrooms
- pepper
- shallots
- parsley
- chives
- tarragon leaves
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- stock
- white wine
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Take two squares of lamb chops that match well and prepare them as described above
2. Cut into slices some bacon and well-washed mushrooms
3. Season with pepper, shallots, parsley, chives, four tarragon leaves, half a bay leaf, and a little pounded marjoram, mixing everything well
4. Lard the chops and place them to cook in a casserole over gentle heat with little stock and white wine
5. When cooked and the stock is reduced, remove the fat
6. Bind with equal amounts of butter, dust with flour, and serve hot over the chops
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quarto de Cabrito de Fricassé Branco (Quarter of Kid Goat, White Fricassée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of kid goat quarter
- water
- butter
- herbs bundle
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- hot water or broth
- white truffles
- artichoke stems
- egg yolks
- milk
- parsley, chopped
- garlic
- nutmeg
- lemon juice
- peas (optional)
- cullis (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the shoulder blade from a quarter of kid goat and cut the meat into pieces the width of three fingers, as well as the breast and ribs
2. Place everything in cold water and then blanch in hot water
3. When blanched and trimmed, place the pieces in a casserole with butter, a bundle of herbs, salt, and pepper
4. Place on the fire and dust with flour
5. Moisten with hot water or broth and add white truffles and half a dozen artichoke stems
6. When everything is cooked and the sauce is reduced, remove the fat and bind with five or six egg yolks mixed in milk, chopped parsley, a bit of garlic, and nutmeg
7. When bound and of good taste, squeeze the juice of a lemon and serve hot
8. It may also be made brown by moistening with substance and binding with a little cullis
9. If desired, green peas may be added, bound with egg yolks or a little cullis
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quarto de Cabrito para huma Entrada (Kid Goat Quarter for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- fat kid goat quarter
- buttered paper
- breadcrumbs
- clear essence sauce
- enxova
- green onion, chopped
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Place a fat kid goat quarter to roast, covered with buttered paper
2. When roasted, remove the paper, baste the quarter, and cover it with breadcrumbs and give it good color
3. When well browned, serve with a sauce of clear essence, two enxovas, chopped green onion, salt, pepper, and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quarto de Cabrito à Ingleza (Quarter of Kid Goat, English Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter of kid goat
- water
- salt
- bacon slab
- cauliflower
- butter
- spinach or other greens (optional)

## Directions

1. Take a quarter of kid goat, either hind or front
2. Place it to soak and then dry very well in a cloth
3. Dust with flour and place to cook in a white broth made with water, salt, and a slab of bacon
4. When cooked, garnish with cauliflower cooked in water alone and serve with melted butter sauce
5. Spinach or other greens may also be added, observing that the kid goat quarter should not be overcooked
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quarto de Cordeiro Recheado (Stuffed Quarter of Lamb)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb quarter
- veal mince
- butter
- salt
- onion
- pepper
- beef fat
- bacon
- water
- bread crumb
- milk
- egg yolks
- spinach ragú, cucumber ragú, peas ragú, sorrel ragú, or other ragú of choice

## Directions

1. Place the lamb quarter on a clean cloth and remove the meat in pieces from the inside
2. Pass the meat pieces through the fire with a piece of butter, salt, onion, and pepper
3. Add some beef fat and some pieces of bacon soaked in water
4. After everything is cooked, make a mince and grind it in a stone mortar
5. Add a piece of bone marrow mixed with milk and bind everything with five or six egg yolks
6. Stuff the lamb quarter with this mixture, shaping it as desired
7. Tie it with twine or string and place it to cook in a braise
8. When cooked and well drained of its fat, serve with a ragú of spinach, cucumbers, peas, sorrels, or another ragú of choice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quarto de Cordeiro à Ingleza (Lamb Quarter English Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- front quarter of lamb or kid goat, roasted
- butter
- flour
- parsley
- green onion
- shallot
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg, grated
- broth
- orange juice or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Once a front quarter of lamb or kid goat is roasted, spread a good amount of butter on it
2. Then dust it with a little flour and mix in parsley, green onion, and shallot, all chopped and seasoned with little salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
3. Place the kid goat quarter, taken from the spit, on the serving plate with a little broth
4. Lift the blade with the point of a knife from one side
5. Spread the butter on top and put the blade back in its place
6. Once the butter is melted and mixed with the broth so it does not break, serve hot with sour orange or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quarto de Cordeiro à Trena para huma Entrada (Lamb Quarter Filled for an Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb quarter, roasted and cold
- bacon, chilled
- veal udders, half-cooked
- beef fat
- parsley
- green onions
- shallots
- truffles
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg, grated
- coriander seeds, dried
- bread crumb soaked in milk with butter
- four egg yolks
- some beaten egg whites
- bacon slices for lining
- clear essence sauce
- lemon juice
- veal sweetbreads
- fat livers
- small pigeon ragout

## Directions

1. Once the lamb quarter is roasted and cold, remove all the meat from the thigh and fillet and make a forcemeat with it
2. Add chilled bacon, half-cooked veal udders, a little beef fat, parsley, green onions, shallots, and truffles, all finely chopped and seasoned with salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, and a dozen dried coriander seeds
3. Pound everything in a mortar into which you will add a piece of bread crumb cooked in milk with butter, four egg yolks, and some beaten egg whites
4. Line a pie dish with bacon slices and place the lamb quarter on top, cover it with the forcemeat giving it the same shape
5. Cover with breadcrumbs and put it to cook in the oven
6. When cooked and of good color, serve with a sauce of good essence and lemon juice
7. It can also be served with a garnish in the middle made of veal sweetbreads, fat livers, and truffles, or with a small pigeon ragout, observing that it be well bound, of good taste, and cold
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quarto de Vitella com Farpa de Crema (Quarter of Veal with Cream Forcemeat)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one thumb-sized piece, loin, or quarter of veal
- veal meat from the top part
- bacon
- veal udders
- beef fat
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- shallots
- bread crumb soaked in cow's milk
- eight or ten egg yolks
- nutmeg
- egg whites
- chicken breasts
- sweetbreads
- truffles
- cooked ham
- clear cullis sauce
- lemon juice
- béchamel sauce

## Directions

1. Roast a thumb-sized piece, loin, or quarter of veal and let it cool
2. Remove all the meat from the top part
3. Make a minced forcemeat with this meat, bacon, veal udders, and all half-cooked ingredients, and beef fat
4. Season with salt, pepper, parsley, and shallots, and pound everything in a stone mortar
5. Add a bread crumb soaked in cow's milk and well-squeezed in cloth, eight or ten egg yolks, grated nutmeg, and some beaten egg whites
6. Arrange the piece of veal on a platter with bacon slices underneath
7. Place part of the forcemeat on top with a ragout in the middle made of chicken breasts, sweetbreads, truffles, and cooked ham, all cut in dice and well-bound
8. Cover with the rest of the forcemeat
9. Smooth it with egg whites and the flat of a knife so it appears whole
10. Cover with grated bread and place in the oven to cook and brown
11. Serve with a clear cullis sauce of good flavor and lemon juice, or with the same ragout made with béchamel, served hot, or with any other sauce as desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quarto de Vitella à Crema (Quarter of Veal in Cream)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of veal
- thick bacon
- three or four canadian measures of milk
- one arratel of butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- dried coriander
- one clove of garlic
- onion slices
- parsley

## Directions

1. Lard a quarter of veal with thick bacon
2. Make a marinade with three or four canadian measures of milk, one arratel of pulverized butter mixed with flour, salt, pepper, cloves from India, dried coriander, one clove of garlic, onion slices, and parsley
3. Heat the marinade and let it cool
4. Once cold, immerse the quarter of veal in the marinade for ten or twelve hours
5. Remove the veal when it has absorbed the flavor of the marinade
6. Dry it in a clean cloth
7. Place it in a roasting pan covered with bacon slices and paper leaves
8. When ready, serve with spicy sauce or covered with grated bread with pepper sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quartos de Cordeiro com Hervas Finas (Quarters of Lamb with Fine Herbs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two hind quarters of lamb
- bacon, scraped
- salt
- onion, chopped
- pepper
- fine herbs
- fresh butter
- bacon slabs
- paper
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- meat sauce
- cullis
- shallot, chopped
- lemon or bitter orange juice

## Directions

1. Take two hind quarters of lamb and skin them carefully from the kidney area to the tail, taking care not to tear the skin
2. Prepare a mixture of scraped bacon, salt, chopped onion, pepper, fine herbs, and fresh butter, all finely chopped and mixed
3. Introduce this mixture between the meat and the skin
4. Tie with string so it does not come out
5. Place on the spit covered with bacon slabs and paper leaves
6. When roasted, remove the paper and bacon, brush with butter, cover with breadcrumbs, and brown
7. When browned, serve with meat sauce made of substance with a little cullis, chopped shallot, salt, pepper, and lemon or sour orange juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quartos de Cordeiro de Vários Modos (Quarters of Lamb in Various Ways)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted lamb or kid goat quarters
- butter
- ham pieces
- onions
- carrots
- flour
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- cloves
- egg yolks
- verjuice or white vinegar or lemon juice
- milk
- velouté sauce

## Directions

1. After the lamb or kid goat quarters are roasted and cooled, remove all the meat and cut it into fillets
2. Serve with white fricassée sauce or béchamel
3. For white fricassée: place butter, ham pieces, onions, carrots (all dusted with flour) in a casserole and pass through the fire
4. Moisten with broth and season with salt, pepper, a bundle of fine herbs, and two cloves
5. When cooked and reduced, pass everything through a sieve and then place the fillets inside to warm without boiling
6. When warm, bind with three egg yolks, adding a little verjuice, or if not available, white vinegar or lemon juice
7. They may also be served à béchamel: cut the ham, veal, and carrot pieces into dice and instead of broth moisten with milk and a little velouté, finishing as the white fricassée described above
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quartos de Cordeiro ou de Cabrito à Italiana (Quarters of Lamb or Kid Goat, Italian Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two front quarters of lamb or kid goat
- veal mince
- chicken breast pieces
- ham
- string
- Italian mince made of different herbs, egg yolks, cream, cheese, breadcrumbs, and fine herbs

## Directions

1. Split two front quarters of lamb or kid goat and remove all the bones from the inside
2. Stuff with a mince of veal, in which you may mix some pieces of chicken breast and ham
3. Tie with string after stuffing, giving it the shape you wish, either round or long, but leave the bone of the tip of the shoulder
4. Place to cook in a white braise
5. When cooked, remove the string and serve with chopped parsley of good taste
6. You may also stuff with an Italian mince made of different herbs, egg yolks, cream, cheese, breadcrumbs, and fine herbs
7. The same can be done to front quarters of veal and serve with Italian sauce of good taste
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Quartos Traseiros de Porco Montez (Wild Boar Hind Quarters)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Porco montez (wild boar)
- Toucinho grosso (thick bacon)
- Sal (salt)
- Pimenta (pepper)
- Especias finas (fine spices)
- Vinagre (vinegar)
- Alhos (garlic)
- Hervas finas (fine herbs)
- Molho picante (spicy sauce)

## Directions

1. Skin the boar and quarter it
2. Lard the hind quarters with thick bacon seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine spices
3. Marinate in salt, pepper, vinegar, garlic, and all types of fine herbs
4. After marinating, roast on a spit and serve with spicy sauce
5. Alternatively, they may be served cooked in an adobo or as stewed meat
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Queijo da Cabeça de Porco (Pork Head Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork head cheese
- vinegar
- capers

## Directions

1. Prepare the cheese as described in folio 73
2. Cut into small slices sufficient for a plate
3. Serve with vinegar and capers on top
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Queijo de Cabeça de Porco (Pig's Head Cheese)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's head
- Salt
- Pepper
- Thyme
- Bay leaves
- Marjoram
- Cloves of India
- Coriander seeds
- Nutmeg
- Garlic
- Ham slices
- Parsley
- Water
- Broth
- White wine
- Onion
- Roots
- Tomato

## Directions

1. Take a pig's head, cut it, singe it, and clean it as described above
2. Open it from the bottom and remove all the bones
3. Soak it for a while to remove the blood and when dry, lift all the meat and bacon without damaging the skin
4. Cut the lean meat into thin sheets, do the same with the fat, cut the ears into fillets, season everything with salt, pepper, thyme, bay leaves, marjoram, cloves of India, coriander seeds, nutmeg, all well crushed, and add two cloves of garlic
5. When seasoned, place the skin of the head in a casserole, arrange the sheets of meat on top interspersed with the fat, some slices of ham, and pieces of parsley, with the meat colored and almost filling the skin
6. Prick all around with a needle, and after closing, wrap it in a cloth and tie it very well with twine
7. Cook it for six hours in water, broth, white wine with whole salt, pepper, cloves, rounds of onion, roots, garlic, thyme, bay leaves, marjoram
8. After cooked and almost cold, remove it, drain it a little, press it in a press or place it under a heavy weight, and when well pressed, remove it, trim it, and serve it on a napkin garnished with parsley and rounds of ham or ham slices
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Queijo de Cabeça de Porco (Pork Head Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork head
- ground spices
- saffron

## Directions

1. Cook the pork head as described above
2. After cooking, mince it very well at the chopping block and season with ground spices without saffron
3. Place in a bread mold or form and press in a press
4. This cheese is not placed in vinegar but is kept until it pastes
5. This can also be made from wild boar head
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Queijo de Paio e Prezunto (Cured Sausage and Ham Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- paio (Portuguese cured sausage)
- four arratels of ham
- wine
- bacon
- pepper
- clove
- thyme
- marjoram

## Directions

1. After soaking a paio and four arratels of ham for twelve hours in good wine, mince everything very well
2. Braise it in melted bacon with good pepper, clove, thyme, marjoram, and a little wine in which it was soaked
3. When everything has simmered, pour it into thick cloth and make cheese in the form described above
4. This cheese is cold food
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Queijo Derretido com Ovos (Melted Cheese with Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- cheese, fatty and grated
- white wine
- butter
- anchovies
- sauce
- pepper
- nutmeg
- egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Place in a casserole over fire half a pound of grated fatty cheese, half a quart of white wine, a piece of washed butter, two anchovies and one of chopped sauce, pepper, and nutmeg
2. Mix everything with a spoon until the cheese melts, add four egg yolks and their beaten whites
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Queijo Derretido com Ovos (Melted Cheese with Eggs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half an arratel of fat cheese, grated
- half a quartilho of white wine
- a piece of washed butter
- two scallions
- one of parsley, minced
- pepper
- nutmeg
- four egg yolks
- egg whites, beaten

## Directions

1. Place on heat in a saucepan half an arratel of fat cheese, grated, and half a quartilho of white wine, a piece of washed butter, two scallions, and one of parsley minced, pepper, and nutmeg, and stirring everything with a spoon until the cheese melts.
2. Add to it four egg yolks and their beaten whites, and mix everything together and cook.
3. Place on the plate garnished with pieces of fried bread crumb.
4. Serve hot as a side dish.
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Queizadinhas de Amendoa (Little Almond Cheeses)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a quarter arratels of sugar at low thread point
- one arratel of finely chopped almonds
- eight eggs (six with yolks, two with whites)
- cinnamon
- pastry dough made with butter and a little sugar

## Directions

1. Put one and a quarter arratels of sugar at low thread point in a pan
2. When at this point, remove from heat and add one arratel of finely chopped almonds, breaking apart with a spoon as it is added
3. Return to heat while it rises in boiling and add eight eggs (six with yolks, two with whites)
4. When it boils a bit, it is done
5. Sprinkle cinnamon on top and remove from fire
6. Take pastry dough made with butter and a little sugar and shape the little cheeses
7. After the mixture cools, shape and send to the oven
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Rabos de Carneiro (Sheep Tails)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep tails
- water
- salt
- pepper
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- melted butter or fine oil
- shallot sauce or lime juice

## Directions

1. Take the quantity of tails judged necessary and soften them in hot water
2. Trim them and tie them two by two
3. Cook them in a half broth made with broth, two onions, two roots, a bundle of parsley, and fine herbs, seasoned with little salt and pepper
4. When cooked, they can be served in different ways
5. To roast them on the grill, pass them first through beaten eggs and cover with fine grated breadcrumbs
6. Drizzle with melted butter or fine oil and cover a second time with breadcrumbs
7. Return to roasting, basting from time to time with a little butter
8. When well colored, serve with shallot sauce, lime juice, or with a hot parsley sauce
9. They can also be fried in pork butter and garnished with fried parsley
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú Branco de Cardos (White Ragout of Cardoons)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Cardos (cardoons)
- Caldo branco (white stock)
- Bexamela (béchamel sauce)
- Gemas de ovos (egg yolks)
- Noz-moscada (nutmeg)
- Vinagre branco (white vinegar)

## Directions

1. Cook the cardoons in white stock like the previous recipes
2. Drain them
3. Place them in béchamel sauce of good taste
4. Give them four simmers in it
5. Thicken with egg yolks
6. Add nutmeg and a splash of white vinegar
7. Serve for side dishes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Alcachofras (Ragout of Artichokes)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes, cut into quarters
- Ham essence or ham broth
- Cullis
- Flour
- Broth
- Egg yolks
- Milk

## Directions

1. Cut artichokes into quarters, trim them, blanch them, and cook them in a light braise
2. When cooked, place them in a ham essence
3. Give them four boilings
4. Bind them with a little cullis and serve for garnishes of first courses
5. They can also be made white, dusted with flour, cooked in broth, and bound with egg yolks and milk, serving in this way for side dishes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Alfaces e de Aipos (Ragout of Lettuce and Celery)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Alfaces (lettuce)
- Aipos (celery)
- Bréza (braize)
- Óleo de basilica (basil oil)

## Directions

1. Make ragoûts of lettuce or celery the same way as chicory ragoûts
2. Serve for garnishing entrees and side dishes
3. They can also be served stuffed, cooked in braize, and fried with basil
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Azedas (Ragout of Sorrel)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Azedas (sorrel)
- Manteiga (butter)
- Cebola picada (minced onion)
- Essência (essence)
- Gemas de ovos (egg yolks)
- Leite (milk)

## Directions

1. Wash and blanch the sorrel in water
2. Drain through a sieve
3. Pound them
4. Sauté minced onion in butter
5. Add the sorrel to this sauté with a little essence
6. Let them simmer for an instant
7. When of good taste, thicken with egg yolks dissolved in milk
8. Serve under any entree or for side dishes with eggs on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Azetonas (Ragout of Olives)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Um cento de azetonas (one hundred olives)
- Água (water)
- Culis de vitela (veal extract)
- Enxova picada (minced anchovies)
- Capella (bouquet garni)
- Vinho branco (white wine)

## Directions

1. Take one hundred olives
2. Remove the pits so they appear whole
3. Blanch them in water
4. Drain them
5. Simmer for an instant in veal extract with minced anchovies, bouquet garni, and white wine
6. After simmering sufficiently, remove the bouquet garni
7. Season to taste
8. Serve for entrees of thrushes, woodcock, or any game
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Camarões (Ragout of Shrimp)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Shrimp, boiled and tails removed
- Melted salt pork
- Truffles, sliced
- Mushrooms
- Ham
- Broth or stock
- Salt
- Pepper
- Shrimp cullis

## Directions

1. Boil the shrimp and remove the tails
2. Sauté in melted salt pork with slices of truffles, mushrooms, and a piece of ham
3. When everything is sautéed, add a spoon of broth and season
4. Allow to cook
5. When cooked, bind with a little shrimp cullis
6. Finish and ensure good flavor
7. Serve for whatever purpose desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Cardos (Ragout of Cardoons)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Pés de cardos (cardoon stalks)
- Vinagre (vinegar)
- Água fria (cold water)
- Branco com manteiga (white stock with butter)
- Gordura de vacca (beef fat)
- Rodas de limão (lemon slices)
- Essência (essence)
- Manteiga (butter)
- Vinagre (vinegar)

## Directions

1. Cut cardoon stalks into equal pieces
2. Boil them in water with a drop of vinegar
3. Place them in cold water
4. Remove the strings
5. Cook them in white stock with butter or beef fat and lemon slices
6. When cooked, drain them
7. Simmer for an instant in good essence
8. Add a small piece of butter and a splash of vinegar
9. Serve for entrees
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Castanhas (Ragout of Chestnuts)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Meio cento de castanhas verdes (fifty green chestnuts)
- Vinho branco (white wine)
- Caldo ou substância (stock or essence)
- Culis de presunto (ham extract)
- Culis de vitela (veal extract)
- Salhichas (sausages)

## Directions

1. Take fifty green chestnuts
2. Shell them
3. Peel them in boiling water
4. Finish cooking them in white wine and stock or essence
5. When cooked and whole, thicken with ham or veal extract
6. Season to taste
7. Serve for terrines and other entrees
8. Sausages can also be added
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Chicórias (Ragout of Chicory)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicórias (chicory)
- Barbante (twine)
- Essência (essence)
- Chaloita picada (minced shallot)
- Sumo de limão (lemon juice)

## Directions

1. Wash the chicory very well
2. Blanch them
3. Drain and tie them with twine
4. Cook them in a light braize
5. When cooked, drain them
6. Cut them into equal pieces
7. Place them in a good essence
8. Let them simmer for an instant
9. Add minced shallot
10. Then add lemon juice
11. Serve to garnish entrees
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Couve-flor (Ragout of Cauliflower)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower, divided into florets
- Water
- Salt
- Flour
- Butter or salt pork
- Meat essence
- Butter

## Directions

1. Divide the cauliflower into pieces and remove the skin
2. Cook in water with salt, flour, and a piece of salt pork or butter
3. When cooked, arrange around any piece of meat with essence and a piece of butter bound on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Cristas (Comb Ragout)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two dozen combs
- white broth
- ham essence
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Blanch and skin two dozen combs
2. Place to cook in white broth
3. When cooked, place in ham essence
4. Give it four boilings in it
5. Remove it
6. Add the juice of one lemon
7. Serve as the preceding
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Ervilhas (Ragout of Peas)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas
- Melted salt pork or butter
- Broth or stock
- Parsley bouquet
- Small onion
- Cloves
- Veal cullis
- Ham cullis
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Sauté the peas in melted salt pork or butter
2. After sautéing, add a little broth, a parsley bouquet, and a small onion with two cloves
3. Allow to cook
4. When cooked, remove the fat, bind with veal cullis and ham cullis
5. When it has good flavor, serve as garnishes for first courses with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Espargos (Ragout of Asparagus)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus, cut into equal pieces
- Butter
- Ham slice
- Flour
- Broth or stock
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley bouquet
- Cullis
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the asparagus into equal pieces and blanch them
2. Pass them through butter with a slice of ham and dust with flour
3. Add a little broth, season with salt, pepper, and a parsley bouquet
4. Allow to boil for an instant
5. When cooked, remove the butter, parsley bouquet, and ham
6. Bind with cullis and lemon juice
7. When it has good flavor, serve for garnishes of any first course
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Espinafres (Ragout of Spinach)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach leaves, boiled and chopped
- Butter or melted salt pork
- Ham essence or good broth
- Béchamel sauce
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Blanch and cook the spinach leaves in plain water, chop them, and press them well
2. Sauté them in butter or melted salt pork
3. After sautéing, add a good ham essence or broth, or if not available, moisten with béchamel sauce
4. Bind with egg yolks and serve underneath first courses, larded with ham and other suitable garnishes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Falpicadé (Ragout of Chopped Meat)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweetbreads, cut into dice
- Ham
- Strips of meat
- Artichoke hearts
- Cucumbers
- Flour
- Butter
- Broth or stock
- White wine
- Parsley bouquet
- Fine herbs
- Garlic clove
- Salt
- Pepper
- Liver, cut into dice
- Egg yolks
- Milk
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut sweetbreads, ham, strips of meat, and artichoke hearts into dice
2. Dust with flour and sauté in butter until everything is sautéed
3. After sautéing, add a little broth and white wine
4. Add a parsley bouquet and fine herbs, a garlic clove, salt, and pepper
5. Allow to cook until done
6. When cooked, add liver cut into dice and blanched
7. Reduce the sauce, remove the fat, and it is ready to serve for various purposes
8. It can also be bound with egg yolks, milk, and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Feijões (Ragout of Beans)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender green beans
- Water
- Butter
- Small onion
- Parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Broth or stock
- Veal cullis
- Ham cullis

## Directions

1. Select the most tender and greenest beans, break off the tips, and cook them in plain water
2. After cooking, sauté them in butter with small onion and chopped parsley, seasoned with salt and pepper
3. When sautéed, moisten them with broth
4. Bind them with veal cullis and ham cullis
5. Serve for garnishes of first courses
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Mexilhões (Ragout of Mussels)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels, opened and shelled
- Mussel cooking liquid
- Truffles
- Cucumbers
- Butter or melted salt pork
- Pepper
- Parsley bouquet
- Fine herbs
- White wine
- Broth or stock
- Veal cullis
- Ham cullis

## Directions

1. Open the mussels over heat and shell them, reserving a little of their own liquid
2. Separately, sauté truffles and cucumbers in melted butter or salt pork, seasoned with pepper, a parsley bouquet, and fine herbs
3. After sautéing, add a drop of white wine and broth
4. Allow to simmer gently and slowly
5. When reduced, bind the sauce with veal cullis and ham cullis
6. Add the mussels with their liquid
7. Ensure good flavor and that it does not boil
8. Serve this ragout for all first courses with mussels
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Molejas (Sweetbreads Ragout)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- eight sweetbreads of lamb or veal
- melted lard
- a slice of ham
- flour
- broth
- substance
- pepper
- a bunch of parsley
- fresh herbs
- one onion with two cloves
- mushroom caps
- artichoke hearts
- cullis
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Soak eight sweetbreads of lamb or veal in water
2. Blanch and brown in melted lard and a slice of ham
3. Dust with flour
4. Add a little broth and substance, pepper, a bunch of parsley and fresh herbs, one onion with two cloves
5. Let it cook
6. When cooked, add mushroom caps and artichoke hearts
7. Bind everything with a spoonful of cullis
8. Remove the fat
9. Serve with lemon juice to garnish pies, tarts, or any first course desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Nabos (Ragout of Turnips)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Nabos (turnips)
- Água (water)
- Manteiga (butter)
- Toucinho derretido (melted bacon)
- Bocado de presunto (piece of ham)
- Farinha (flour)
- Substância (stock)
- Sal (salt)
- Pimenta (pepper)
- Capella (bouquet garni)
- Cebola com dois cravos (onion with two cloves)

## Directions

1. Cut turnips into equal pieces
2. Boil them in water for an instant
3. Drain them
4. Brown them over heat in a pan with butter or melted bacon and a piece of ham
5. Dust with flour
6. Add a little stock
7. Season with salt and pepper, bouquet garni, and onion with cloves
8. Let simmer slowly
9. When cooked, remove the bouquet garni and fat
10. Thicken with extract
11. When of good taste, serve for terrines and garnishing entrees
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Ostras (Ragout of Oysters)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Oyster beards
- Butter or melted salt pork
- White wine
- Broth or stock
- Pepper
- Parsley bouquet
- Small onion
- Cloves
- Cullis
- Oysters, blanched and trimmed

## Directions

1. Sauté the oyster beards in butter or melted salt pork
2. After sautéing, add half a cup of white wine and half a spoon of broth
3. Season with pepper, a parsley bouquet, and a small onion with two cloves
4. Allow to reduce
5. Bind with a little cullis
6. Add two or three dozen oysters, blanched and trimmed
7. Place over heat and allow to cook without boiling until it has good flavor
8. Serve for various garnishes
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Ovinhos e Grãos de Gallo (Chicken Eggs and Testicles Ragout)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- hot water
- chicken testicles and eggs
- cold water
- ham essence

## Directions

1. Blanch in hot water a small plateful of chicken testicles and eggs
2. Then place the blanched ones in cold water
3. Remove the skins
4. Place in ham essence
5. Let boil in it for a moment
6. When good tasting, remove from heat
7. Add the juice of one lemon
8. Serve as the preceding for garnish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Pepinos (Ragout of Cucumbers)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers, cut into dice
- Onions
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Browned butter
- Ham slice
- Broth or stock
- Pepper
- Veal cullis

## Directions

1. Peel and cut cucumbers and onions into dice
2. Marinate them in vinegar and salt
3. Squeeze them in a cloth
4. Sauté them in browned butter with a slice of ham
5. After sautéing, moisten with broth, season, and allow to cook
6. When cooked, remove the fat and bind with veal cullis
7. Serve for all first courses of braised meat, roasted meat, or larded meat
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Perrexil e outros Legumes (Ragout of Parsley and Other Vegetables)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Folhas de perrexil (parsley leaves)
- Água fervendo (boiling water)
- Substância (stock)
- Culis de vitela (veal extract)
- Culis de presunto (ham extract)

## Directions

1. Soak the parsley leaves
2. Pass them through boiling water to remove salt and vinegar
3. Drain and squeeze them
4. Simmer for an instant in a little stock
5. When cooked, thicken with veal and ham extract
6. Serve for garnishing entrees
7. Cucumbers, green beans, and other preserved vegetables are served the same way
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Presunto (Ham Ragout)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- six slices of ham of the same size and well equal
- water
- lard melted
- white wine
- cullis
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut six slices of ham of the same size and well equal
2. Place two hours to soak in water
3. Dry very well
4. Brown in melted lard on both sides
5. Remove from heat
6. Add to what remained stuck to the bottom of the saucepan a little white wine and a little cullis
7. Give it four boilings
8. Add pepper and a drop of vinegar
9. Remove the fat, pass through a sieve
10. Serves as garnish for first course
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Repolhos e Coaves Lombardas (Ragout of Cabbage and Lombard Cabbage)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Repolhos (cabbage)
- Coaves lombardas (Lombard cabbage)
- Barbante (twine)
- Qualquer caça ou peça de carne (any game or meat)
- Essência (essence)
- Picados (minced ingredients)
- Várias guarnições (various garnishes)
- Peças de carne de porco (pork meat)
- Chouriços (chorizo)
- Salhichas (sausages)
- Presunto (ham)
- Molho claro (clear sauce)

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbages into quarters
2. Blanch them in water
3. Drain and tie them with twine
4. Cook them in a light braize
5. When cooked, drain them
6. Arrange them around any game or meat
7. Serve with essence on top
8. They can also be served stuffed with minced ingredients and various garnishes
9. They can also be cooked with pieces of pork, chorizo, sausages, and ham with clear sauce on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Repolhos e Couves Lombardas (Ragout of Cabbage and Savoy Cabbage)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage, cut into quarters
- Water
- Binding material
- Meat or game

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage into quarters, blanch in water, squeeze, and tie with binding material
2. Cook in a light braise
3. When cooked, drain and arrange around any game or piece of meat
4. Serve with essence on top
5. It can also be served stuffed with minced meat and various garnishes in the middle
6. It can also be cooked with pieces of pork, sausages, and charcuterie
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Salpicadé (Mixed Ragout)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads cut in pieces
- ham cut in pieces
- mushroom caps cut in pieces
- artichoke hearts cut in pieces
- cucumber pieces
- flour
- butter
- substance
- white wine
- a bunch of parsley
- fresh herbs
- one clove of garlic
- salt
- pepper
- liver cut in pieces

## Directions

1. Cut into pieces sweetbreads, ham, mushroom caps, artichoke hearts, and cucumbers
2. Dust with flour and brown in butter
3. After browning everything, add a little substance and white wine, a bunch of parsley and fresh herbs, one clove of garlic, salt, and pepper
4. Let it cook
5. When cooked, add some livers cut into pieces and blanched
6. When the sauce is reduced, remove the fat
7. Serves for everything
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú de Trufas (Truffle Ragout)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles
- a slice of ham
- melted lard
- broth
- half cup of white wine
- one head of garlic
- one onion with two cloves
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel and cut truffles into thin slices
2. Brown with a slice of ham and melted lard
3. Then add broth, half cup of white wine, one head of garlic, and one onion with two cloves and pepper
4. Let it boil slowly and gently
5. When cooked and without fat, add one spoonful of cullis
6. Serves as garnish for any first course
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Ragú Franco de Chicórias (French Ragout of Chicory)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicórias (chicory)
- Bréza (braize)
- Molho à bexamela (béchamel sauce)
- Gemas de ovos (egg yolks)

## Directions

1. Blanch the chicory
2. Cook them in a braize
3. Drain them
4. Cut them into equal pieces
5. Let them simmer for an instant in béchamel sauce
6. Thicken with egg yolks
7. Season to taste
8. Serve for various entrees and also for side dishes with poached eggs on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rahos de Carneiro com Arroz (Sheep Tails with Rice)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- hot water
- ordinary broth
- broth from the half-broth preparation, somewhat fatty
- sheep tails cooked in broth
- eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Take the quantity of rice needed and wash it very well two or three times with hot water
2. Cook it in a small pan with ordinary broth, adding a spoonful of the fatty broth from the half-broth preparation
3. When almost cooked and half cooled, cover the bottom of the plate with a little rice
4. Arrange the cooked sheep tails on top
5. Cover with the rest of the rice, preserving each tail's shape
6. Brush with beaten eggs and when the rice is colored, drain off the fat
7. Clean the edge of the plate and serve hot as an entree
8. They can also be served à la Parmesan with a little broth and grated cheese at the bottom of the plate, and when arranged, pour the rest of the sauce thickened with a piece of washed butter over them, cover well with grated cheese, place in the oven to brown, and serve, adding a little broth
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Raios de Carneiro à Bruziana (Sheep Tails Bruiziana)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen sheep tails
- one cabbage in quarters
- one arratel of entremeared bacon cut into pieces without separating from the rind
- water
