- salt
- whole pepper
- a bundle of parsley
- chives
- two cloves
- one head of garlic
- one sprig of fennel
- broth without salt
- breadcrumb slices
- butter
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Put all the tails, cabbage and bacon to boil in plain water for the space of half an hour
2. Remove to cold water and squeeze the cabbage very well
3. Tie it with twine and arrange the tails and cabbage in a small pan with the bacon, six whole onions on top
4. Season with little salt, whole pepper, a bundle of parsley, chives, two cloves, one head of garlic, and one sprig of fennel
5. Moisten all with broth without salt and cook slowly until tender
6. Cut slices of breadcrumb interior in round form the size of new crusados in silver
7. Pass these through melted butter until they turn golden color
8. Then put half a handful of flour in the butter that remains in the casserole, return to gentle heat, stirring always until the flour is colored
9. Moisten it with the same broth in which the cabbages were cooked, with a little vinegar
10. Cook for half an hour to cook the flour
11. Remove the tails and cabbages from the pan, drain them of fat, and arrange them on the plate
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Receita para preparar azeitonas à moda Franceza (Recipe for Preparing Olives in the French Manner)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- ash
- water (two, or three to five almudes)
- green olives (two or three alqueires)
- cold water
- salt
- bay leaves
- fennel branches
- oregano branches
- rosewood

## Directions

1. Take the quantity of ash that is judged necessary, and boil it in two, or three to five almudes of water, so that after boiling a strong lye results
2. Let it rest and cool; strain through a cloth
3. Take two or three alqueires of green olives, place them in the lye, and let them sit until the lye penetrates to the pit, which will happen in the space of twenty-four hours
4. Pour off the lye, add cold water, and changing it three or four times until it comes out clear, let them remain in it for eight days, changing the water in the morning and in the afternoon
5. When the olives are a clear green color and have lost their bitterness, place them in a clay pot or in glazed jars
6. Cover them with brine, seal them, place them in a cool place, and they can be used after fifteen days, or when needed
7. Note that the brine shall be made only with water and the necessary salt, and it should be poured hot with some oregano branches, some fennel branches, and some bay leaves, rosewood, and after boiling with all this, let it rest and pour it over the olives hot, as stated
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Recheio de Peixe (Fish Stuffing)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two portions of hake without skin or bones
- Two onions
- Parsley
- Two eggs
- Seasonings
- Three more raw eggs
- Five réis of grated bread
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Chop two portions of hake without skin or bones
2. Fry two chopped onions with parsley, and add two beaten eggs with seasonings in the same frying pan, stirring until the eggs curdle
3. Mix everything with the hake that is chopped on the cutting board, and chop it again with three more raw eggs and five réis of grated bread
4. Season with all the seasonings, salt, vinegar, and lemon
5. This mixture can be used to stuff any fish desired, or to fill eggs, eggplants, onions, chicories, lettuces, cabbages, turnips, and artichokes
6. It can also be made into patties, meatballs, rolls, and everything else desired, adding or diminishing according to the quantity
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Rejlorão (Fish Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a dozen onions, sliced in rings
- Four parsnips
- Carrots
- Butter
- Fish stock or vegetable stock
- Roux
- Fish fragments or pieces of other fish

## Directions

1. Slice half a dozen onions in rings, cut four parsnips and carrots into pieces
2. Stew them in butter until the onions are browned as appropriate
3. Add fish stock or vegetable stock and some roux
4. Once everything is cooked, seasoned, and of good taste, pass through a sieve
5. Let it rest and use for soups, ragus, and sauces
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rejlorão (Meat Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four arratels of veal
- Fatias of ham
- One chicken quartered
- Two roots
- Two onions
- Some cloves of garlic
- One bundle of parsley and fine herbs
- Two cloves
- A little melted bacon fat
- One drop of good oil

## Directions

1. Place at the bottom of a pot three or four arratels of veal, slices of ham, one chicken in quarters, two roots, two onions, some cloves of garlic, one bundle of parsley and fine herbs, two cloves, a little melted bacon fat, and one drop of good oil
2. Cover the pot and place on high heat
3. When the meat is done and tender, add sufficient stock and thicken
4. Let it simmer little by little for four hours
5. Once reduced and of good taste, pass through a sieve and remove the fat
6. Use for various ragus and sauces
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rejlorão para dias de peixe (Fish Broth for Fish Days)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of butter
- Sliced onions
- Pieces of good fish
- Four crushed juniper berries
- Roots
- One bundle of parsley and fine herbs
- One head of garlic
- Two or three cloves
- One glass of white wine
- One glass of thin stock

## Directions

1. Melt a piece of butter in a pot
2. Add sliced onions, pieces of good fish, four crushed juniper berries, roots, one bundle of parsley and fine herbs, one head of garlic, and two or three cloves
3. Let it cook on gentle heat for half an hour
4. Move to a stronger heat, and when it begins to stick, add one glass of white wine and one glass of thin stock
5. Let it simmer little by little, and when reduced and of good taste, pass through a sieve
6. Use for all sauces and ragus
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Risóis (Fried Pastry Rolls)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bread dough or puff pastry
- forcemeat or herb filling
- beaten eggs
- clarified butter or pork butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Roll out with the rolling pin a piece of soft dough or folded puff pastry
2. Cut into strips the width of a hand breadth
3. Place on top at intervals small pieces of forcemeat à crème or other herb filling, well bound
4. Wet the dough all around with beaten eggs
5. Fold the dough one over the other lengthwise
6. When sealed, cut with a pastry wheel
7. Fry in clarified or pork butter
8. After frying, serve with grated sugar on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Robalos and Tainhas à Maruja (Bass and Mullet Sailor Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Bass or mullet, cleaned and prepared
- Equal portions
- One or two dozen small onions, half-cooked in water and drained
- Salt
- pepper
- salted capers
- fine herbs
- One clove-studded onion
- Butter
- flour, browned
- Wine
- Water or broth

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned and prepared fish into equal pieces
2. Arrange in a proportioned casserole with one or two dozen small onions, half-cooked in water and drained
3. Season with salt, pepper, a spoonful of salted capers, fine herbs, and a clove-studded onion
4. Add a broth made of butter and browned flour, wine, and a drop of water or broth on top
5. Let simmer slowly
6. When the fish is cooked and the sauce reduced, remove the capers and clove-studded onion
7. Serve with orange or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rodovalho ao Caldo Branco (Turbot in White Broth)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot
- water
- salt
- napkin
- milk

## Directions

1. After cleaning the turbot at the gills and without opening it, wash it very well and serve it in a round pot with a napkin underneath and a half portion of water and salt
2. Let it simmer for half an hour little by little, and when it is almost cooked, remove the pot from the fire, place it over a few brazas, pour it a pint of hot milk, let it sit for half an hour without boiling
3. Once the fish is cooked, suspend the napkin so it does not break, and place it on the plate in which it will be served, send it to the table garnished with parsley
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rodovalho assado com hervas finas (Roasted Turbot with Fine Herbs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- onion slices
- lemon slices
- garlic cloves
- fine herbs
- washed butter
- bread crumbs
- beet sauce or other piquant sauce

## Directions

1. If the turbot is large, after cleaning, cut it in two or four portions
2. Marinate in oil with salt, pepper, onion slices, lemon slices, garlic cloves, and all manner of fine herbs for two or three hours until it has taken on the flavor of the marinade
3. Line the bottom of a baking dish with washed butter
4. Place the fish on top and add its marinade
5. Cover with finely grated bread crumbs and put in a moderately hot oven to cook
6. When roasted and well-colored, serve with beet sauce or any other piquant sauce
7. If you do not have the convenience of an oven, roast the fish on grills and serve in the same manner
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rodovalho para Dia de Carne (Turbot for a Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon slices
- veal slices
- turbot
- salt
- whole pepper
- onion with cloves
- fine herbs
- ham slices
- white wine
- coulis

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of a large pot with bacon slices and thin, beaten veal slices, place the turbot on top, season with little salt, whole pepper, onion studded with cloves, a handful of fine herbs, and two slices of ham
2. Cover everything with bacon slices and let it sweat between two fires
3. After that, pour it a quarter of white wine and finish cooking it little by little
4. Once cooked, place it carefully on the plate and serve with the sauce from the bottom of the same braising pot, strained with a little coulis
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rolas e Codornizes com Freiunta (Turtledoves and Quails with Ham)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Turtledoves or quails
- Ham slices
- Good quality butter
- Garlic cloves
- Bay leaf
- Vinegar
- White wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cooking stock

## Directions

1. Take the necessary fat turtledoves or quails, clean, prepare, and lard them
2. Roast them covered with sheets of bacon and paper leaves
3. When roasted, sweat half a dozen slices of ham with good quality butter
4. Brown the ham on both sides and remove it
5. In what remains in the pan, add two crushed garlic cloves, one bay leaf, one drop of vinegar, one drop of white wine, and a little cooking stock
6. Season with pepper and salt if needed
7. Let it simmer briefly
8. Strain through a sieve
9. Pour over the turtledoves or quails and serve
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rolas e Codornizes com Repolho para Entrada (Turtledoves and Quails with Cabbage for Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One cabbage
- Lean bacon
- One dozen quails
- Cooking stock
- Braising liquid
- Capelet
- One onion studded with cloves
- Ham essence

## Directions

1. Take one cabbage and blanch it in hot water, squeeze it well
2. Cook it in a well-tinned pan with a piece of lean bacon also rolled, one dozen well-cleaned quails larded in melted bacon
3. Add some cooking stock and a little of the braising liquid, one capelet, and one onion studded with cloves
4. When everything is cooked, skim the fat
5. Arrange in its dish and serve with good quality ham essence on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rolas e Codornizes em Gratém para Entrada (Turtledoves and Quails in Gratin for Appetizer)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Turtledoves or quails
- Fat livers
- Fennel (fiolho or funcho)
- Bread crumbs
- Fat from the pan
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Pluck, clean, and lard the quails or turtledoves
2. Open them somewhat along the sides and fill with a mixture of chopped fat livers in which fennel should dominate
3. Cook them in a good dry braise
4. When cooked, spread some of the same mixture on the bottom of the serving plate and place the birds on top
5. When the liver is set, skim the fat from it
6. Place the turtledoves or quails on top with Spanish sauce over them
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rolas e Codornízes com Louro (Roasted Quails and Rails with Laurel)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted quails or rails
- Their own livers
- Scraped bacon
- Sausage
- Chopped chives
- Two egg yolks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bacon slices
- Paper leaves
- Ham stock
- Six laurel leaves
- Orange juice

## Directions

1. After singeing and emptying, stuff with a mixture made from their own livers, scraped bacon, sausage, chopped chives, two egg yolks, seasoned with salt and pepper
2. Put them on a spit covered with bacon slices and paper leaves
3. When roasted, serve with ham stock and six laurel leaves previously boiled in water with orange juice
4. Alternatively, open them along the sides and pass them through the fire in a pan with melted bacon, salt, pepper, sausage, and chopped chives
5. After thus sautéed, remove them, cover with fine breadcrumbs, roast on the grill on both sides, and serve with shallot sauce and orange or lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rolas e Codornízes em Caçarola para uma Entrada (Quails and Rails in Casserole for an Entrée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Quails or rails
- Fat hen livers
- Ham slices
- Salt
- Pepper
- Seasonings
- Broth
- Essence
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the quails or rails with legs folded inside the body, open slightly along the sides, and stuff with a mixture of fat hen livers
2. Cover the bottom of a casserole with ham slices, seasoned as usual, place the birds inside and cover with other slices like those at the bottom
3. Let them sweat over gentle heat for two hours
4. When cooked, remove the birds, add a drop of broth to the casserole, bring to boil for a moment, pass through a sieve, skim the grease, and reduce
5. When reduced, put the birds back in with breasts down, arrange them on their plate when of good color
6. Add a little essence to what remains in the bottom of the casserole, pour it over the birds, and serve with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rolas e Codornízes em Gratin para Entrada (Quails and Rails Gratinéed for an Entrée)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Quails or rails
- Fat livers
- Fennel (funcho)
- Braise

## Directions

1. After singeing, cleaning, and folding the legs of the codornices or quails, open them somewhat along the sides and fill them with a mixture of fat livers in which fennel (also called funcho) should dominate
2. Put them to cook in a good dry braise
3. When cooked, spread on the bottom
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rolas e Codornízes à Italiana (Quails and Rails Italian Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Quails or rails
- Good olive oil
- Laurel leaves
- Whole marjoram
- Ham slices, thin and square-cut
- Lemon juice
- Salt
- Pepper
- Garlic clove
- White wine
- Substance (meat extract)
- Stock (culí)

## Directions

1. After cleaning, open the quails or rails along the sides and marinate them in good olive oil, whole laurel leaves, whole marjoram, thin square-cut ham slices, lemon juice, salt, and pepper
2. A quarter hour before serving, pass them through the fire in a casserole with the same marinade
3. After thus passed, remove them and arrange on their plate
4. To the casserole add a garlic clove, a drop of white wine, substance, and some stock, reduce
5. When reduced and of good flavor, pass through a sieve, pour over the codornices or quails, and serve with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rolas e Codornízes à Perigord (Roasted Quails and Rails Périgord Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted quails or rails
- Hen livers
- Scraped bacon
- Fine herbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Melted bacon
- Veal slices
- Ham slices
- Seven or eight truffles
- Sausage
- Laurel leaf
- Marjoram
- White wine
- Substance (meat extract)
- Stock (culí)
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. After singeing and emptying, stuff with a mixture of hen livers, scraped bacon, fine herbs, salt, and pepper
2. Stiffen them in melted bacon
3. Place in a pan lined at the bottom with veal and ham slices
4. Arrange the stuffed birds on top, seasoned with salt, pepper, and fine herbs, with a whole peeled truffle, a sausage made with laurel leaf and marjoram
5. Cover with slices like those at the bottom
6. When half cooked, add a glass of white wine
7. When done, remove the birds and truffles, add to the remaining broth half a spoon of substance and some stock, reduce
8. When reduced and of good flavor, remove the grease, pass through a sieve, pour over the birds, and serve hot with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rolas ou Codornízes Assadas nas Grelhas com Hervas Finas (Roasted Quails or Rails on the Grill with Fine Herbs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Quails or rails
- Olive oil
- Melted bacon
- Fine herbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine breadcrumbs
- Shallot sauce
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the quails or rails very well with legs folded inside the body, open along the sides, beat with the flat of a knife
2. Pass them in a casserole for an instant over the fire with olive oil, melted bacon, and all kinds of fine herbs, salt, and pepper
3. Let them cool, and after cooling cover with fine breadcrumbs, baste with what remains in the casserole
4. Put them to roast on the grill, and after roasting serve with shallot sauce and lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Rossaquinos (Cheese and Anchovy Appetizers)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- butter
- salt
- flour
- soft cheese
- eggs
- anchovies
- pepper
- puff pastry

## Directions

1. Put in a casserole a quart of water, a quarter pound of butter, and some salt stones, and place on fire
2. When boiling, add two handfuls of flour
3. When in dough and well dried, place in a stone mortar
4. Add half a pound of soft and fatty cheese, five or six eggs, two chopped anchovies, and some pepper
5. Mix all thoroughly and garnish some thin copper molds with puff pastry
6. Place on each one a piece of this forcemeat the size of a nut
7. Bake in a moderate oven
8. After baking, serve as an entrée
9. Can also be baked in buttered paper and served without dough
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salchichas de Carne de Porco (Pork Sausages)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- tender pork meat
- pork fat
- pork lard
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- fine herbs
- parsley, chives, and shallots, chopped (optional)

## Directions

1. Chop very finely some tender pork meat with an equal amount of its fat and a piece of lard
2. Season with salt, pepper, spices, and fine herbs
3. If you wish, add chopped parsley, chives, and shallots
4. Fill the intestines using a wide funnel as is customary
5. Tie them
6. Make them the length you wish
7. Fry them on grills
8. Serve them
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salchichas de Champanhes (Champagne Sausages)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat from a pork leg or loin
- An equal amount of fat, cut into dice
- Salt, pepper, spices, and fine herbs
- Half a bottle of white wine

## Directions

1. Pound the meat from a pork leg or loin
2. Cut an equal amount of fat into dice
3. Season with salt, pepper, spices, and fine herbs
4. Add half a bottle of white wine
5. Mix everything well and let it marinate
6. Stuff the intestines as is customary
7. Let them smoke for a couple of days
8. Once dried, serve them as desired
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salchichas de Porco (Pork Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of lean pork from the leg
- one arratel of fat pork
- salt
- fennel seed, finely ground
- one splash of white wine
- clean pork intestines

## Directions

1. Chop the lean pork from the leg and fat pork very finely together on a chopping block
2. Season with salt, finely ground fennel seed, and a splash of white wine
3. Fill clean pork intestines with the mixture
4. Pass the sausages through hot water first
5. Hang them in the chimney in a house to dry
6. Fry them in a pan and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Salchichas de Vitela (Veal Sausages)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- tender veal meat
- veal lard
- pork lard
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- fine herbs

## Directions

1. Make them in the same way as the preceding sausages
2. With the difference that instead of pork meat, use tender veal meat with an equal amount of pork fat and a piece of pork lard
3. Season and finish as pork sausages
4. Serve them fried in the same way
5. They can also be made with breasts of capons, partridges, turkeys, and any kind of meat or game you wish
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salchichas de Vitella (Veal Sausages)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal, the most tender
- Lard, equal amount to veal
- A piece of fat

## Directions

1. Make them in the same form as the preceding ones, with the difference that instead of pork meat, use tender veal with an equal amount of lard and a piece of fat
2. Season and finish as pork sausages
3. Serve roasted in the same manner
4. They may also be made from breasts of capons, partridges, turkeys, and any quality of meat and game desired
5. If you do not want to eat them roasted, they can also be served cooked with white wine sauce, with Parmesan cheese, with cabbage, and with any vegetable ragout
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salchichões (Large Sausages)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Ten to twelve arrobas of pork meat
- Half a bottle of white wine
- An equal amount of fat and lard, cut into small dice
- A little saltpetre, salt, pepper, and fine spices

## Directions

1. Take ten to twelve arrobas of pork meat and pour half a bottle of white wine over it
2. Wrap in a white cloth
3. Press it in a press or between two boards loaded with stones
4. Leave to sit for two days
5. After that, chop coarsely on a table
6. Add an equal amount of fat and lard cut into small dice
7. Season with a little saltpetre, salt, pepper, and fine spices
8. Add half a bottle of white wine
9. Mix everything in a bowl and cover for ten or twelve hours
10. Then fill the larger intestines, tie them equally, and let them drain
11. After that, smoke them for several days
12. Once dried, serve them as desired
13. In the same manner, salchichões of wild boar are made
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salcichas (Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- sausages
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Fry the sausages cut into small pieces with pepper and lemon on top
2. Serve
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Salmi de Galinhollas (Woodcock Salmi)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted woodcock
- Red wine
- Broth substance
- Cullis
- Shallot
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sour orange peel
- Sour orange juice
- Bread marrow
- Butter for frying

## Directions

1. After roasting the woodcocks, remove the breasts and thighs, and from the rest, except the gizzard, pound in a mortar
2. Dissolve it with a spoon of bread in a saucepan with red wine and a little broth substance
3. Bring to a boil and add a little cullis, a chopped shallot, salt, pepper, a small piece of sour orange peel
4. When it has good flavor and the sauce is reduced, strain through a sieve
5. Place the quarters of woodcock inside to heat
6. After becoming well heated without boiling, serve with sour orange juice, garnished with pieces of fried bread marrow
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salmi de Perdizes (Partridge Salmi)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted partridges
- Red wine
- Broth substance
- Cullis
- Shallot
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sour orange peel
- Sour orange juice
- Bread marrow
- Butter for frying

## Directions

1. After roasting the partridges, remove the breasts and thighs, and from the rest, except the gizzard, pound in a mortar
2. Dissolve it with a spoon of bread in a saucepan with red wine and a little broth substance
3. Bring to a boil and add a little cullis, a chopped shallot, salt, pepper, a small piece of sour orange peel
4. When it has good flavor and the sauce is reduced, strain through a sieve
5. Place the quarters of partridge inside to heat
6. After becoming well heated without boiling, serve with sour orange juice, garnished with pieces of fried bread marrow
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salmon for an Entree on a Meat Day
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon, in steaks two fingers thick
- Bacon, sliced
- Veal, sliced
- Ham, sliced
- Roots
- Onions
- Garlic, a few cloves
- Fine herbs
- Salt
- pepper
- Cloves
- White wine
- Butter
- Cullis

## Directions

1. Cut the steaks two fingers thick and arrange in a proportioned pot or casserole with slices of bacon, veal, ham, roots, onions, a few cloves of garlic, fine herbs, salt, pepper, and cloves
2. Add a little white wine and braise very well between two fires of coals
3. When cooked, serve with broth from the bottom of the coals, bound with a small piece of washed butter and a little cullis
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salmonetes Aljados (Grilled Red Mullet)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- red mullet
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- red mullet livers
- good oil
- lemon or orange juice

## Directions

1. Remove gills and entrails from red mullet and wash very well
2. Cut off the tail tip
3. Dry in clean cloth
4. Season with salt
5. Dip in melted butter
6. Place on grill to roast with moderate fire until golden on both sides
7. Serve with a sauce made from the fish's own livers, good oil, salt, pepper, and lemon or bitter orange juice
8. Alternatively, after grilling remove the scales and serve with butter sauce containing anchovies, to which half a spoon of shrimp paste or any other preferred sauce may be added
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salmonetes com Hervas Finas (Red Mullet with Fine Herbs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- red mullet
- melted butter
- parsley
- minced scallion
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- breadcrumbs
- remoulade sauce
- mustard

## Directions

1. Scale, clean, and wash the red mullet
2. Cut off the heads and tail tips
3. Marinate in melted butter with parsley, minced scallion, salt, pepper, and fine herbs
4. After one hour of marinating, place the pan on fire
5. When the butter is melted, cover with fine breadcrumbs
6. Place on grill to roast
7. When roasted on both sides, serve with hot remoulade sauce and mustard
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salmonetes Cozidos (Boiled Red Mullet)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- red mullet
- white wine
- butter
- salt
- roots
- onion slices
- cloves
- fine herbs
- parsley sauce

## Directions

1. Remove gills and entrails from red mullet without opening or scaling them
2. Cut off the tail tips
3. Cook in a broth made with white wine, a piece of butter, salt, roots, onion slices, cloves, and fine herbs
4. When cooked, serve garnished with parsley sauce around it
5. Alternatively, after cooking remove the scales and serve with Dutch sauce made with butter, anchovies, and capers, as is customary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Salmão Fresco (Fresh Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- vinegar
- wine
- water
- a large bunch of parsley
- pepper, pounded

## Directions

1. Clean the salmon
2. Make a broth of two parts vinegar, one part wine, and one part water
3. Add a large bunch of parsley and well-pounded pepper
4. Cook
5. When boiling, add the salmon
6. When cooked, remove it and eat it cold
7. In this same form it sits at the table the whole time it lasts
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Salpicões de Porco (Pork Forcemeat Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of lean ham
- half arratel of fat
- wine

## Directions

1. Mince very well two arratels of lean ham and half arratel of fat, and keep for twelve hours in wine broth
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Salpicões de Porco (Pork Salpicões)
body:
## Ingredients

- two arratels of lean ham
- half arratel of fat
- wine
- vinegar
- one clove of garlic, ground
- cow intestines
- pepper
- cardamom
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop the lean ham and fat very finely
2. Keep in a brine of wine, vinegar, and ground garlic for twelve hours
3. Make salpicões in cow intestines the size of sausages
4. Season well with pepper and cardamom
5. Pass through hot water and hang to dry
6. Fry in butter and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Salsichas de Porco (Pork Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- two and a half arratels of pork from the leg
- one arratel of fat pork
- salt
- fennel seed
- white wine
- clean pork casing
- hot water

## Directions

1. Mince very well two and a half arratels of pork from the leg and one arratel of fat
2. Season with salt and very finely ground fennel seed and a splash of white wine on the same chopping block
3. Make them in clean pork casing and pass them first through hot water
4. Hang them in the chimney in a house so they dry
5. Fry them in a pot and send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sarda Assada (Grilled Mackerel)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- capers

## Directions

1. Remove entrails and wash the mackerel very well
2. Cut off the head and tail tip
3. Open along the sides
4. Marinate in oil, salt, and pepper
5. Place on grill to roast
6. When well roasted, place on serving plate, open, remove the backbone
7. Serve with butter and capers sauce
8. Alternatively, serve with burned butter sauce and fried parsley
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sardinhas com Hervas Finas (Sardines with Fine Herbs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- sardines
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- minced garlic
- fine herbs
- butter or chopped meat
- breadcrumbs
- spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Scale the sardines, remove entrails, cut off heads and tail tips, wash very well
2. Marinate in oil, salt, pepper, minced garlic, and fine herbs
3. After marinating, arrange in a silver box over a little butter or chopped meat
4. Cover with fine breadcrumbs
5. Place to cook in the oven
6. When cooked, serve with a little spicy sauce on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sepa Dourada à Portugueza (Golden Bread Soup Portuguese-Style)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumb
- clarified butter
- sugar
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the crumb into dice
2. Fry in clarified butter
3. Let drain after frying
4. Dip briefly in clarified sugar, nearly at point
5. When beginning to soften, let boil in the same sugar
6. When thickened, add eighteen egg yolks
7. Mix always with the spoon and in a gentle fire
8. After everything is mixed and set, serve with powdered cinnamon on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Small Corn Cobs and Cucumbers of Preserved Corn (Canned Corn and Pickled Cucumbers)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- corn cobs, still green
- water
- vinegar
- cloves
- salt

## Directions

1. Take the quantity of corn cobs that seems right, which should still be green and tender like marrow, and boil them in water for half a quarter of an hour
2. Pass them afterwards to cold water and drain them in a bowl
3. Pour over them a hot brine made with water and a third part vinegar, some cloves and salt, and place them on hot ashes until the next day
4. On that day pour over them another equal quantity of brine, which should be done until they become well preserved
5. Afterwards put them in bottles, seal them and keep them in a cool place to use them when needed
6. The same shall be done to cucumbers, which the French call cornichons
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Solho (Turbot)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one large turbot fish
- water
- one can of white wine
- salt
- whole pepper
- cloves of India
- onion wheels
- a piece of butter
- a small sprig of sage
- thyme
- bay leaf
- some parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the turbot into pieces according to the serving dish
2. Tie the pieces with twine
3. Place them in a copper pan with its leaves, cover with water and one can of white wine
4. Season with salt, whole pepper, cloves of India, onion wheels, a piece of butter, a small sprig of sage, thyme, bay leaf, and some parsley
5. Let it simmer little by little for the space of two hours
6. When cooked, serve garnished with beef
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Solho Assado com Hervas Finas (Roasted Turbot with Fine Herbs)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot cut in portions the thickness of a finger
- butter
- pepper
- fine herbs
- salt
- chopped chives
- bread crumb
- remoulade or hot garlic sauce

## Directions

1. Cut turbot into portions the thickness of a finger
2. Marinate for two or three hours in melted butter, pepper, fine herbs, salt, and chopped chives
3. Cover with bread crumb and roast on the grill on both sides, dripping with melted butter from time to time
4. When roasted and with good color, serve with hot remoulade or hot garlic sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Solho Assado no Espeto (Turbot Roasted on the Spit)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one turbot portion the size of the serving plate
- lard
- anchovy fillets
- fish broth
- vinegar
- onion wheels
- lemon wheels
- bay leaves
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- cullis sauce
- washed butter

## Directions

1. Cut one turbot portion the size of the serving plate
2. Lard it with anchovies and anchovy fillets in pieces and seasoned
3. Tie it to a spit and place to roast
4. Drip with a marinade made of fish broth, vinegar, onion wheels, lemon wheels, bay leaves, salt, pepper, and a piece of butter
5. When roasted, serve as an entree with the sauce from the remaining marinade, bound with a spoon of cullis and a piece of washed butter
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Solho Assado para Dia de Carne (Roasted Sole for Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Lard of thick bacon, seasoned
- A large portion of sole
- Water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Some cloves of India
- Onion
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Fine herbs
- A piece of powdered butter with flour

## Directions

1. Cut lard of thick bacon, seasoned as is customary, in a large portion of sole
2. Marinate for two or three hours in warm water, vinegar, salt, pepper, some cloves of India, onion, garlic, parsley, fine herbs, and a piece of butter powdered with flour
3. Put it on a spit
4. Roast it, basting it with the most fatty part of its marinade
5. When roasted, serve it with any ragout or sauce of good taste
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Solho Assado para Dia de Carne (Turbot Roasted for a Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- thick bacon
- one large turbot portion
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- cloves of India
- chives
- garlic
- sage
- fine herbs
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Lard a large turbot portion with thick bacon, seasoned as is customary
2. Marinate for two or three hours in warm water, vinegar, salt, pepper, some cloves of India, chives, garlic, sage, fine herbs, and a piece of powdered butter with flour
3. Tie it to a spit, place to roast, dripping with the fattiest part of its marinade
4. When roasted, serve with any ragout or sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Solho de Fricandò para Dia de Carne (Fricandeau of Sole for Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine lard on one side only
- A portion of sole the width of two fingers
- Broth
- White wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Chives
- A little thyme
- Bay leaf
- Marjoram
- Three cloves of India
- Two garlic cloves
- Two slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Lard with fine lard on one side only a portion of sole the width of two fingers
2. Place it to cook in a broth made of broth, white wine, salt, pepper, a bunch of parsley, chives, a little thyme, bay leaf, marjoram, all tied in a bundle, three cloves of India, two garlic cloves, and two slices of lemon
3. When cooked, remove it
4. Place it, larded side up, on a veal aspic
5. When of good color, serve with the sauce or with a garnish of herbs or vegetables as you prefer
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Solho em Brêza para Dia de Carne (Sole Braised for Meat Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A portion of sole
- Lard of bacon
- Slices of bacon
- Veal
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs

## Directions

1. Take a portion of sole, larded with bacon
2. Line the bottom of a pan or casserole with slices of bacon and veal
3. Place the sole on top
4. Season with salt, pepper, and fine herbs
5. Cover with slices like those below
6. Cover with its lid
7. Place between two fires to braise
8. After it has braised, add a quarter litre of white wine and a little broth
9. Let it simmer little by little
10. When cooked, serve with Spanish sauce or sauce made with truffles, crayfish, capers, or as you prefer
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Solho à Maruja (Sole à la Maruja)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- A portion of sole
- Butter
- Shallot
- Chives
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Red wine
- Broth or stock
- Bay leaf
- Bread crumb
- Oil for frying

## Directions

1. Cut a portion of sole into thin strips
2. Arrange them at the bottom of a casserole first buttered
3. Add on top shallot, chives, minced shallot, little salt, and pepper
4. When the strips are browned on one side, turn them on the other
5. When cooked, remove them
6. In the butter add a little flour, a drop of red wine, a drop of broth or stock, a bay leaf, and let it simmer little by little for a quarter hour
7. When reduced and of good taste, warm the strips of sole in it
8. Serve without boiling, garnished with pieces of fried bread crumb and passed through the same sauce
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Damasco (Dreams of Apricots)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled apricots
- brandy
- sugar
- lemon rind
- flower water
- batter made with water, white wine, salt, and clarified butter

## Directions

1. Peel the apricots and open them in half
2. Marinate as described above
3. Pass them through batter made with water, white wine, salt, and clarified butter
4. Fry in very hot oil
5. When fried and golden, serve dusted with fine sugar
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Damascos (Fried Apricot Dreams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots
- Brandy
- Sugar
- Water
- White wine
- Salt
- Clarified butter

## Directions

1. Pit the apricots and open them in half
2. Marinate them as described above
3. Pass them through a batter made with water, white wine, salt and clarified butter
4. Fry them in very hot oil
5. When fried and with good color, serve dusted with fine sugar
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Forma (Molded Pastry Dreams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- milk
- two eggs
- a little Madeira wine
- clarified butter
- fine sugar
- grated lemon peel
- salt
- cinnamon, ground

## Directions

1. Mix flour in milk
2. Add two eggs, a little Madeira wine, clarified butter, fine sugar, grated lemon peel, salt, and ground cinnamon
3. After mixing everything, shape like an apple and heat an iron mold in hot oil or butter where it will be fried
4. Dip the mold in the dough and immediately place in the fryer
5. When it releases, dip back in the dough and continue this way until finished
6. After frying and glazing with sugar and a hot spatula, serve as the previous ones
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Forma (Shaped Dreams)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little flour
- milk
- two eggs
- a little Madeira wine
- clarified butter
- fine sugar
- grated lemon peel
- salt
- ground cinnamon
- oil or melted butter

## Directions

1. Dissolve a little flour in milk, and add to it two eggs, a little Madeira wine, clarified butter, fine sugar, grated lemon peel, salt, and ground cinnamon
2. After everything is mixed, and in the form of a thumb heat the mold which should be of iron in hot oil or butter, in which they are to be fried, dip it in the dough, and put it immediately in the frying pan, and when it slips off return to the dough, and continue thus to the end
3. After they are fried and glazed with sugar and a hot spatula, serve as the preceding ones
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Fruta de Seringa (Dreams of Syringe-Fruit)
body:
## Ingredients

- royal dough
- milk or water
- eggs
- pork butter or clarified beef fat
- sugar in thread

## Directions

1. Make the royal dough with milk or water and break it up with eggs as the preceding one, pass it through a syringe or any other mold that may be, fry in pork butter or clarified beef fat
2. After it is fried and passed through sugar in thread, serve between meals
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Fruta de Seringa (Piped Pastry Dreams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- royal dough made with milk or water
- eggs
- pork or clarified beef lard
- sugar cooked to hardball stage

## Directions

1. Prepare royal dough with milk or water and break it up with eggs, as in the previous recipe
2. Pass through a syringe or other mold
3. Fry in pork lard or clarified beef lard
4. When fried and coated with sugar cooked to hardball stage, serve between meals
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Manjar Branco (Dreams of White Delicacy)
body:
## Ingredients

- breasts of chicken or roasted partridges, chopped and pounded
- strong cream made of rice flour, eggs, and milk
- salt
- fine sugar
- ground cinnamon
- almond biscuits
- lemon peel
- covered orange blossom
- flour
- oil or hot butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Chop and pound the breasts of chicken or roasted partridges, and mix them with a strong cream made of rice flour, eggs, and milk
2. Season with salt, fine sugar, ground cinnamon, almond biscuits, lemon peel, and covered orange blossom
3. After everything is together and kneaded, cut into little mouthfuls the size of nuts, dust with flour, and made in hot oil or butter, serve dusted with sugar
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Manjar Branco (White Almond Paste Dreams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken or partridge breasts, cleaned and pounded
- strong cream made from rice flour, eggs, and milk
- salt
- fine sugar
- ground cinnamon
- almond biscuits
- lemon peel
- covered orange flower
- flour
- oil or hot butter

## Directions

1. Clean, pound and mince chicken or partridge breasts
2. Mix with a strong cream made from rice flour, eggs, and milk
3. Season with salt, fine sugar, ground cinnamon, almond biscuits, lemon peel, and covered orange flower
4. After mixing everything, cut into small pieces the size of walnuts
5. Sprinkle with flour and fry in hot oil or butter
6. Serve sprinkled with fine sugar
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Massa Fritos (Fried Dough Dreams)
body:
## Ingredients

- royal dough
- eggs
- flour
- pork butter
- orange blossom water
- fine grated sugar
- sugar in thread

## Directions

1. Make the royal dough and break it up with eggs as I said in the chapter on doughs, put it on a plate dusted with flour, and fry in pork butter, cutting with the tip of the handle of the skimmer
2. When fried and of the same size, serve with orange blossom water and fine grated sugar, or sugar in thread
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Massa Fritos (Fried Pastry Dreams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- royal dough made with eggs
- flour
- pork lard
- orange flower water
- grated sugar or sugar cooked to hardball stage

## Directions

1. Prepare royal dough and break it up with eggs, as described in the Chapter on Doughs
2. Place in a dish and sprinkle with flour
3. Fry in pork lard, cutting with the tip of a skimmer handle
4. When fried and of the same size, serve with orange flower water and finely grated sugar or sugar cooked to hardball stage
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Morangos (Fried Strawberry Dreams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour
- Beer or white wine
- A drop of olive oil or a piece of clarified butter
- Salt
- Egg whites
- Strawberries
- Pork lard
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Make a batter of flour mixed with beer or white wine, a drop of olive oil or a piece of clarified butter, and salt
2. When about to fry, add two or three well-beaten egg whites
3. Pass some strawberries through it and fry in pork lard
4. When fried, serve hot dusted with sugar
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Morangos e Outros (Dreams of Strawberries and Others)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- beer or white wine
- a drop of oil or a piece of clarified butter
- salt
- egg whites
- strawberries
- pork lard
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a batter of flour mixed with beer or white wine, a drop of oil or a piece of clarified butter, and salt
2. At serving time, add two or three well-beaten egg whites
3. Pass some strawberries through it and fry in pork lard
4. After frying, serve hot, dusted with sugar
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Pêssegos ou de Maçãs (Dreams of Peaches or Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- a few ripe apples
- brandy
- sugar
- a piece of lemon rind
- flower water
- flour
- pork lard

## Directions

1. Take a few ripe apples, peel them, cut into quarters, remove the cores, and marinate for two or three hours in brandy with sugar, a piece of lemon rind, and flower water
2. Drain them in a cloth, dust with flour, and fry in pork lard
3. When fried and golden, serve glazed with fine sugar like the previous ones
4. Alternatively, cut the apples into rounds, pass through batter, fry, and serve the same way
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Pêssegos ou de Maçãs (Fried Apple or Peach Dreams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Some sour apples
- Brandy
- Sugar
- A piece of lemon peel
- Flower water
- Flour
- Pork lard

## Directions

1. Take some sour apples, peel them, cut them into quarters and remove the seeds
2. Marinate for two or three hours in brandy with sugar, a piece of lemon peel, and flower water
3. Drain them on cloth, dust with flour, and fry in pork lard
4. When fried and with good color, serve glazed with fine sugar, as with the preceding recipe
5. Alternatively, cut the apples into rounds, pass them through a batter, fry them, and serve in the same manner
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos de Pêssegos ou de Maçãs (Peach or Apple Pastry Dreams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- tart apples
- brandy
- sugar
- lemon peel
- orange flower water
- flour
- pork lard
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Take some tart apples, peel them, cut into quarters, and remove the pits
2. Marinate for two or three hours in brandy with sugar, a piece of lemon peel, and orange flower water
3. Drain in a cloth, sprinkle with flour, and fry in pork lard
4. When fried and well-browned, serve glazed with fine sugar
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos em Caixa (Boxed Pastry Dreams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- cream made from rice flour
- flour from rice flour
- eggs
- milk
- salt
- fine sugar
- ground cinnamon
- almond biscuits
- lemon peel
- covered orange flower
- butter, oil, or lard, very hot
- sugar
- paper boxes

## Directions

1. Make a cream with rice flour, prepared the same way and seasoned like the white almond paste dreams
2. Fill small paper boxes the length of a finger and width of two fingers with this cream
3. Fry in very hot butter, oil, or lard
4. When fried and the paper removed, serve glazed on the bottom with sugar and a hot spatula
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos em Caixo (Dreams in Box)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice flour
- sugar
- eggs
- salt
- cinnamon
- orange blossom

## Directions

1. Make a cream with rice flour, seasoned the same way and tempered as
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos em Caixo (Dreams in Paper Boxes)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice flour
- ingredients for blancmange dreams
- paper boxes
- clarified butter
- oil
- or lard
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a cream with rice flour, prepared and seasoned like blancmange dreams
2. Fill small paper boxes with it, the length of a finger and width of two fingers
3. Deep fry them in very hot clarified butter, oil, or lard
4. When fried and the paper removed, glaze the bottom part with sugar using a hot bread paddle
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos à Delfina (Delfina Pastry Dreams)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- dough
- sugar, half the amount
- flour, half the amount
- orange flower
- lemon peel
- egg whites
- clarified butter
- grated sugar or sugar cooked to hardball stage

## Directions

1. Make a dough with half sugar and half flour, orange flower, a little lemon peel, and egg whites
2. After mixing and kneading everything, stretch with a rolling pin
3. Cut into equal patterns as you prefer
4. Fry in clarified butter and serve glazed with grated sugar or sugar cooked to hardball stage with a hot spatula
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos à Delfina (Delphine's Dreams)
body:
## Ingredients

- half sugar
- half flour
- orange flower
- a piece of lemon rind
- egg whites
- clarified butter
- sugar for glazing

## Directions

1. Make a dough with half sugar and half flour, orange flower, a piece of lemon rind, and egg whites
2. After everything is mixed and kneaded, stretch it with bread, cut it, and make it into equal designs as you prefer
3. Fry in clarified butter
4. After frying, glaze with grated sugar and serve hot with a bread paddle
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sonhos à Delfina (Dreams à Delfina)
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- flour
- orange blossom
- a piece of lemon peel
- egg whites
- clarified butter
- grated sugar
- hot spatula

## Directions

1. Make a dough, half of sugar, half flour, orange blossom, a piece of lemon peel, and egg whites
2. After everything is kneaded and worked, extend it with bread, cut it, and make into equal sketches, and as you please, fry in clarified butter
3. And after they are fried serve glazed with grated sugar and a hot spatula
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa branca de Cebola (White Onion Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three dozen small onions of equal size
- Water
- Vegetable broth with herbs
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cook the small onions in water until half cooked
2. Pass them through cold water and drain them
3. Finish cooking them in well-seasoned and good-tasting vegetable broth with herbs
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa Branca de Cebola (White Onion Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three dozen small onions of equal size
- onion broth or vegetable broth with herbs, well seasoned and of good flavor
- bread slices or crusts
- white sauce made from some almonds
- yolks of hard boiled eggs
- bread crumb soaked in broth, passed through a sieve, and heated
- or egg yolks mixed and bound with broth

## Directions

1. Boil the onions in water until half cooked, then pass them through cold water and drain them
2. Finish cooking them in vegetable broth and herb broth, well seasoned and of good flavor
3. Soak some bread crusts or slices in the same broth in which the onions were cooked
4. Serve the soup with white sauce made from some almonds, yolks of hard boiled eggs, and bread crumb soaked in broth, passed through a sieve, and heated
5. In place of the white sauce, one may serve with egg yolks mixed and bound with broth
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa Brasica de arroz (Brasica Rice Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of rice
- A little vegetable broth
- A piece of butter
- Seven or eight egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook half a pound of rice in a little vegetable broth as described in the preceding recipe, without the substance
2. Let it simmer little by little
3. Season with a piece of butter
4. When cooked, dissolve seven or eight egg yolks in broth and place on fire, stirring constantly with a spoon until they are set and without boiling
5. Season to good taste and serve mixed with the rice, everything well hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Almôndegas à Parrhezã (Parmesan Meatballs Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of partridge
- breast of hen
- four loins of rabbit
- crumb of bread moistened in milk
- beef suet
- butter
- Parmesan cheese grated
- four egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg grated
- shallots
- veal stock base

## Directions

1. Take the breast of partridge, breast of hen, and four loins of rabbit, all raw, and make a minced mixture
2. Pound it in a mortar and add a small piece of bread crumb moistened in milk but well squeezed
3. Add melted and strained beef suet, a small piece of well-washed butter, and a handful of grated Parmesan cheese
4. Add four egg yolks and season with little salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, and some shallots
5. Pound everything very well
6. After pounding, form meatballs the size of partridge eggs
7. Cook them in broth
8. When cooked, remove them with a skimmer and place in a soup bowl
9. Serve with another broth and veal substance
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Amendoa (Almond Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half canada of milk
- eight egg yolks
- half arratel of sugar
- four ounces of almonds, very well ground
- a quarter of butter

## Directions

1. Pour half canada of milk into a pan with eight egg yolks, half arratel of sugar, four ounces of very well ground almonds, and a quarter of butter
2. Beat everything together very well
3. Place in a pan to cook over low heat
4. As it thickens, pour it into a plate or frying pan
5. Brown it in the oven or on the griddle
6. Once browned, take it to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Amendoa (Almond Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half canada of milk
- eight egg yolks
- half arratel of sugar
- four ounces of finely ground almonds
- quarter of butter

## Directions

1. Pour into half canada of milk eight egg yolks, half arratel of sugar, four ounces of finely ground almonds, and a quarter of butter
2. Beat everything together very well
3. Place in a pot to cook over low heat
4. As it thickens, pour into a plate or skillet
5. Brown it in the pastry oven or in the oven
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Arroz para dias de peixe (Rice Soup for Fish Days)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Vegetable broth
- Vegetables
- Celery
- Salsify root
- A piece of butter
- Two spoonfuls of onion substance

## Directions

1. Wash the rice in three or four waters
2. Cook it in a vegetable broth with legumes, arranging so that one vegetable does not cover another, especially the celery and salsify root
3. When half cooked, add a piece of butter and two spoonfuls of onion substance
4. Let it simmer little by little until the rice takes on enough color
5. When cooked and tasting good, serve with broth that is neither very thick nor very thin
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Bocões (Chicken Dumpling Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- breasts of capons, hens, and turkey thighs
- bacon scraped
- sage
- two shallots minced
- bread crumb moistened in milk
- four or five egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg grated
- pasta dough

## Directions

1. Take raw breasts of capons, hens, and turkey thighs, scrape them with a knife and remove all skin and mince them
2. Pass the minced meat to a stone mortar and pound with scraped bacon, some sage, and two finely minced shallots
3. When well pounded, add a piece of bread crumb moistened in milk but well squeezed and four or five egg yolks
4. Season with salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg
5. Pound again and place on a plate
6. Make a piece of thin pasta, stretch it so it is very thin
7. Cut it into strips and place small portions of the minced mixture at intervals, brushed around with beaten eggs
8. Cover with another piece of pasta
9. Cut with a mold and blanch in boiling water
10. Then cook in another broth for six minutes
11. Transfer to a sieve, place in a soup tureen, and serve with broth and veal substance of good taste and color
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Chú Pardo (Brown Broth Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of beef from the point of the roast
- bread crusts fried
- carcasses of partridges, capons, or other game birds
- veal stock base
- salt
- marjoram
- piece of lime peel
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Roast a piece of beef from the point of the roast in the spit
2. When well roasted and browned, while still hot, pound it in a mortar with some fried bread crusts and carcasses of partridges, capons, or any other available game birds
3. Moisten from time to time with a little broth
4. After everything is well pounded, dissolve it in veal stock base and good broth
5. Season with salt if necessary
6. Add a little marjoram and a piece of lime peel
7. Give it four boils
8. Strain through a sieve
9. Heat again and serve as stated, over bread crusts with lemon juice
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Culi de Camarões (Shrimp Broth Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- half a hundred shrimp
- one ounce of sweet almonds
- shrimp heads and shells
- breast of a roasted hen
- four hard egg yolks
- veal stock base
- ham
- onion in quarters
- half a spoon of broth
- salt
- two cloves
- dried marjoram
- sage
- bread crusts

## Directions

1. Wash and cook half a hundred shrimp, remove their heads
2. Take one ounce of sweet almonds and pound them with the shrimp heads and shells, the breast of a roasted hen, and four hard egg yolks
3. Line the bottom of a casserole with veal stock base, a piece of ham, and an onion in quarters
4. Cover the casserole with its lid and let it sweat gently over low heat
5. When it begins to stick to the bottom of the casserole, add one spoon of broth
6. Season with salt, two cloves, dried marjoram, and sage
7. Let it simmer gently with some bread crusts
8. When the veal is cooked, strain the broth through a sieve
9. Pound the bread crusts with what remains in the mortar and dissolve everything in two spoons of the same broth
10. Heat, strain through a sieve, put everything together over heat but do not boil
11. Serve over moistened bread crusts as is customary
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Culí Pardo para dias de peixe (Brown Cullis Soup for Fish Days)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Two onions, sliced
- One carrot
- One parsnip
- Butter
- Three or four pieces of bread slices
- A little flour
- Fish broth
- One bunch of parsley
- One bunch of marjoram
- Two small onions
- Two cloves
- Some pieces of fried or roasted fish

## Directions

1. Cut the onions, carrot, and parsnip into slices and sauté everything in butter
2. Add three or four pieces of bread slices and a little flour
3. When well browned, add a quantity of fish broth and substance
4. Season with one bunch of parsley, one bunch of marjoram, two small onions, and two cloves
5. Let it simmer slowly
6. While it simmers, pound some pieces of fried or roasted fish
7. Pass it with the softened bread slices and broth through a sieve
8. Serve hot and well-flavored with other fried and placed bread slices at the bottom of the plate with stuffed bread in the middle
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Lampreia à Portuguesa (Portuguese Lamprey Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one lamprey, cleaned and bled
- half a quartilho of red wine
- butter
- leeks cut in wheels
- half a spoon of substance
- some wine
- salt
- one onion with two cloves
- a small sprig of sage and fresh herbs
- bread crusts fried and boiled in broth and fish substance or vegetable substance of good flavor

## Directions

1. After the lamprey is cleaned, limped, and bled, set the blood aside mixed with half a quartilho of red wine
2. Cut the lamprey into pieces and brown them in butter with the leeks cut in wheels
3. Add half a spoon of substance and some wine
4. Season with salt, one onion with two cloves, a small sprig of sage and fresh herbs
5. Let it simmer little by little
6. When cooked, bind the broth with the blood and serve over fried bread crusts boiled in broth and fish substance or vegetable substance of good flavor
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Leite (Milk Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two cans of cow's milk
- salt
- a piece of sugar
- two cloves of India
- whole cinnamon
- a small piece of lemon peel
- egg yolks
- bread crumb

## Directions

1. Bring two cans of cow's milk to boil, seasoned with salt, with a piece of sugar, two cloves of India, whole cinnamon, and a small piece of lemon peel
2. After boiling for four minutes, pour in two spoons of this mixture a dozen egg yolks
3. Heat over the flame, stirring always with a spoon, and when thickened, but without boiling, pour over bread crumb cut into thin slices previously moistened in the boiled milk
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Leite de Amêndoas (Almond Milk Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three cans of water
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- half a handful of dried coriander
- two quarts of well pounded almonds, passed through a cloth two or three times
- bread crumb
- bread slices dried in the oven
- biscuits

## Directions

1. Bring two or three cans of water to boil, seasoned with salt, sugar, cinnamon, lemon peel, and half a handful of dried coriander
2. When this water has boiled for three or four minutes, mix in two quarts of well pounded almonds and passed through a cloth two or three times
3. Mix well with a piece of bread
4. Squeeze very well
5. Pour this water into a small pan, place it near the heat, and when hot and of good flavor, moisten some bread slices dried in the oven with it
6. Serve garnished all around with fine biscuits or almond biscuits
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Leite de Camarões (Shrimp Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- two dozen sweet almonds
- shells from fifty shrimp
- four hard egg yolks
- bread crusts soaked in broth
- fish substance

## Directions

1. Pound in a mortar two dozen sweet almonds, moistening occasionally with water
2. When pounded, add the shells from fifty shrimp, four hard egg yolks, and pound everything well together with bread crusts soaked in broth
3. Dissolve two spoonfuls of fish substance and place over heat in a pot, but before boiling, pass through a strainer
4. When cooked, hot, and good flavor, serve over bread crusts fried in butter and cooked in the same broth
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Mexilhões para dias de peixe (Mussel Soup for Fish Days)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels
- Water
- A piece of butter
- A few sprigs of parsley
- One onion with two cloves
- Some white truffle fillets
- Cucumber pieces
- Fish broth
- One dozen almonds
- Two dozens of mussels
- Four yolks of hard eggs
- One piece of bread crumb

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the mussels in three or four waters
2. Place them in a pot with a little water and put on fire to open
3. If there is no fish broth to moisten the soup, add more water and season with a piece of butter, a few sprigs of parsley, and one onion with two cloves
4. When they open and come to a boil, remove from heat
5. Strain the broth through a sieve and remove the mussel shells
6. Place the mussels in a pot with a piece of butter, some white truffle fillets, cucumber pieces, and fish broth
7. When it comes to a boil, remove the butter and place in a white cullis made from one dozen almonds, two dozens of mussels, four yolks of hard eggs, and one piece of bread crumb, all pounded and dissolved in two spoonfuls of fish broth
8. Season first and to good taste
9. Pass through a sieve and place on low heat without boiling
10. Pour over soups cooked in the same mussel broth with stuffed bread in the middle and the white cullis on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Nata (Cream Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- slices of toasted bread
- powdered sugar
- four bowls of cream
- eight egg yolks
- a small amount of milk

## Directions

1. Grease a plate with butter
2. Line it with slices of toasted bread and sprinkle powdered sugar on top
3. Beat four bowls of cream with eight egg yolks and a small amount of milk
4. Pour this mixture over the bread slices with more powdered sugar on top
5. Bake in the oven over low heat
6. If the cream soaks into the bread, add more cream with eggs and sugar
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Peixe com Culí Branco (Fish Soup with White Cullis)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One bowl full of sweet almonds
- Some pieces of prepared fish or some fillets of fried sole, without skin
- Six yolks of hard eggs
- Two spoonfuls of fish broth
- Some mushrooms
- One bunch of parsley
- One bunch of marjoram
- Some small onions
- Two cloves
- A piece of bread crumb

## Directions

1. Pound the sweet almonds in a stone mortar until crushed
2. Add some pieces of prepared fish or some fillets of fried sole without skin and six yolks of hard eggs
3. Pound everything very well
4. Pass two spoonfuls of fish broth through a sieve and add it
5. Add some mushrooms, one bunch of parsley, one bunch of marjoram, some small onions, two cloves, and a piece of bread crumb
6. Let it simmer slowly for a quarter of an hour
7. The almonds and ground fish dissolve in this broth
8. Pass it through a sieve and reheat, stirring constantly with a spoon and without letting it boil
9. Serve over slices of bread cooked in broth but dried
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Perdiz com Lentilhas (Partridge Soup with Lentils)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One eighth measure of lentils, picked and cleaned
- Broth
- A piece of lean bacon
- Two carrots
- One bunch of celery
- A roasted partridge
- Substance made of a partridge and slices of veal

## Directions

1. Pick and clean one eighth measure of lentils and place to cook in a small pan with broth, adding a piece of lean bacon, two carrots, one bunch of celery, all first passed through boiling water and tied with twine to a roasted partridge
2. Let it simmer little by little
3. When the lentils are cooked, pass them through a sieve with substance made of a partridge and slices of veal
4. Then place to boil, skim, and when of good taste, pour over bread crusts boiled and dried as mentioned, and serve with some partridges in the middle
5. From this lentil broth one can make soups of ducks, teals, wild pigeons, and tame pigeons
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Peros Camoezes (Pear Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- pear sauce
- three or four pears
- roasted meat
- toasted bread slices
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. In a new pot, pour the sauce from any kind of roast
2. Add three or four pears in separate quarters
3. Let it boil
4. When the pears are cooked, place them with the sauce on a plate over toasted bread slices
5. Place the roast on top with lemon juice
6. Serve garnished with marrow from the same sauce
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Primavera com Ervilhas (Spring Soup with Peas)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas, one eighth measure, stewed with butter or melted bacon
- Lettuces
- Chervil
- Sorrel
- Three or four small onions
- Broth
- Veal substance
- Bread crusts boiled in broth

## Directions

1. Stew the peas with butter or melted bacon, one eighth measure of peas, stewed with lettuces, chervil, sorrel, three or four small onions, all cut finely
2. Add a little broth and veal substance
3. Let it simmer little by little
4. When everything is cooked and of good taste, remove the fat
5. Serve over bread crusts boiled in broth and dried, as mentioned before
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Primavera com Ervilhas (Spring Soup with Peas)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of peas
- butter or melted lard
- lettuce
- chervil
- sorrel
- three or four small onions
- a little broth
- substance of veal

## Directions

1. Cook one ounce of peas with butter or melted lard
2. Combine with lettuce, chervil, sorrel, and three or four small onions, all finely chopped
3. Add a little broth and veal substance
4. Let it boil little by little
5. When everything is cooked and of good taste, remove the fat
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Purga de Ervilhas para Dia de Jejum (Pea Soup for Fasting Day)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- one eighth of peas
- green onion tails
- marjoram leaves
- legurella herb
- butter
- root substance

## Directions

1. Boil in water one eighth of peas, green onion tails, some marjoram leaves, and a sprig of legurella
2. When everything is softened, strain through a sieve
3. Pour into a pot and cook in butter
4. When almost cooked, pound in a stone mortar with already-pounded green onion tails
5. After pounding, transfer to a pot, dissolve with root substance, and bring to boil
6. When boiled enough, pass through a fine strainer, and when hot and good flavor, serve over dry bread crusts cooked in root broth
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Qualquer Genero de Assado (Soup of Any Kind of Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- one vintém of bread
- bread slices
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- washed butter
- saffron
- chicken or mutton broth
- twelve eggs
- roasted meat (chicken, young roosters, pigeons, or turkey)

## Directions

1. Cut one vintém of bread into slices
2. Place one slice in a large buttered skillet
3. Cover with sugar and cinnamon
4. On this layer, place another slice the same way
5. On top place a small amount of washed butter with saffron
6. Add a little chicken or mutton broth
7. Let it stew slowly over low heat
8. Remove from heat and pour a dozen eggs on top (or fewer, depending on the skillet size)
9. Season with sugar and cinnamon
10. Take a lid with flame and place it raised slightly over the soup until it takes on good color
11. Remove from skillet and place on plate
12. Carve the roast, which can be chicken, young roosters, pigeons, or turkey
13. This is an ordinary plate
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Queijo de Caldo de Vaca (Beef Broth Cheese Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- duck
- bread slices
- butter
- beef marrow
- eggs
- herbs

## Directions

1. Cook a duck with beef
2. When cooked, let it cool
3. Slice the bread thickly
4. Place bread slices on a buttered plate
5. Place the duck cut into slices on top of the bread
6. Place other bread slices on top of these slices
7. On top place beef marrow, and continue filling the plate this way
8. When full, pour the broth over it, first poaching six eggs in it
9. Place the eggs to cook
10. Finally, place a layer of all the herbs on top
11. Pour the fat over them so that it takes on the flavor of the herbs
12. This becomes a good plate of this sort
13. Send to table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Queijo e Lombo de Porco ou Vaca (Cheese and Pork or Beef Loin Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork or beef loin
- wine
- garlic
- butter
- bread slices
- thin cheese slices
- bread crumbs
- sugar
- eggs
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take a pork or beef loin that has been marinating four days in wine and garlic
2. Roast it
3. When roasted, cut it into thin slices
4. Take a buttered skillet
5. Layer bread slices on the bottom
6. Place thin cheese slices over the bread
7. Place the loin slices on top of the cheese
8. Sprinkle with grated bread crumbs and sugar
9. Continue filling the skillet this way to the top
10. When full, pour six beaten eggs over the top
11. Bake in the oven until golden
12. When golden, make holes with a fork
13. Sprinkle sugar on top
14. Send to table with pounded cinnamon
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa de Repolho à Francesa (French Cabbage Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbages
- Half a dozen onions
- Two carrots
- One parsnip
- Salsify roots
- Vegetable broth and substance
- Salt
- Two cloves
- A piece of melted and browned butter
- Slices of bread

## Directions

1. Trim and tie the cabbages as in the Italian soup
2. Place them in a pot with half a dozen onions, two carrots, one parsnip, and salsify roots
3. Add enough vegetable broth and substance
4. Season with salt, two cloves, and a piece of melted and browned butter
5. When cooked, soak some slices of bread in the same broth from the cabbages
6. Simmer and serve surrounded with the same cabbages on top
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa Dourada (Golden Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve egg yolks
- one arratel of sugar
- one vintém of bread
- quarter of washed butter
- cinnamon
- flower water

## Directions

1. Make in the same manner as the Soup of Any Kind of Roast
2. Uses twelve egg yolks, one arratel of sugar, one vintém of bread, quarter of washed butter, cinnamon, and flower water
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa Dourada de Nata (Golden Cream Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pears
- a dozen eggs, well beaten
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- two bowls of cream

## Directions

1. Cut two pears into slices
2. Pass them through a dozen very well beaten eggs
3. Layer them in a plate greased with butter, alternating with sugar and cinnamon
4. Brown the pear slices
5. Beat two bowls of cream with the remaining eggs, sugar, and cinnamon very well
6. Pour this mixture over the pear slices
7. Brown again
8. Sprinkle more sugar and cinnamon on top
9. Send to the table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa Ordinária (Ordinary Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- bread cut into thin slices
- broth from the pot
- one spoon of veal or beef substance, with the fat removed

## Directions

1. Cut bread into thin slices and soak with broth from the pot
2. Add one spoon of veal or beef substance, with the fat removed
3. Let it boil for a moment and serve hot
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa ou Potagem à Francesa (French Soup or Potage)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pigeons
- two partridges
- one duck
- one rabbit
- one chicken
- one sausage
- one piece of pork
- one arratel of diced bacon
- herbs
- six lettuce or chicory or Murcia cabbage hearts
- all spices whole except pepper
- a large bundle of herbs
- bread
- pan bread or pelangana
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. In a large pot, braise two pigeons, two partridges, one duck, one rabbit cut in halves, and one chicken in quarters
2. Add one sausage, one piece of pork, and one arratel of diced bacon
3. Add herbs, six lettuce or chicory or Murcia cabbage hearts
4. Add all spices whole, only the pepper ground
5. Add a large bundle of herbs
6. When everything is cooked and seasoned with salt and other seasonings it needs, place two breads in a plate or pelangana made into soups and moistened with broth
7. Let them soak over the same pot
8. When ready to serve, place all the meat piece by piece on top of the soups
9. Garnish with lettuce or chicory hearts (everything very well arranged)
10. Top with lemon juice
11. Serve at table
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa parda de Cebolas (esteveras) (Brown Onion Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four dozen small round onions
- Butter
- Flour
- Fish broth and substance
- A bunch of parsley
- One bunch of marjoram
- Two small onions
- Two cloves
- One spoonful of vinegar
- Bread slices, fried and cooked in the same broth

## Directions

1. Clean three or four dozen small round onions
2. Pass them through fire in a pot with melted butter and dust with flour
3. When well browned, place in a small pot with fish broth and substance
4. Let it simmer little by little
5. When cooked, add one spoonful of vinegar and serve over fried bread slices cooked in the same broth
6. If the onions are large, cut them into slices and after frying and browning in butter, moisten with vegetable broth or water and serve the soup with a splash of vinegar
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa Saudável (Healthful Soup)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce cut into thin fillets
- celery
- sorrel
- chervil
- roots such as turnips, carrots, and parsnips
- two or three spoons of broth
- one spoon of substance, without fat

## Directions

1. Cut lettuce, celery, sorrel, chervil and some roots like turnips, carrots, and parsnips into thin fillets
2. Wash very well
3. Put in boiling water
4. Remove and place on a sieve to drain
5. Put to cook in a pot with two or three spoons of broth and one of substance
6. When everything is cooked, of good taste, and without fat
7. Serve over a layer of bread soaked in broth
source_title: Cozinheiro moderno, ou nova arte de cozinha

title: Sopa Tostada (Toasted Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ten-reis bread
- bread slices
- half arratel of cow butter
- half arratel of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut one ten-reis bread into slices
2. Butter the slices and coat them with powdered sugar on both sides
3. Place them in a plate or skillet until all are used
4. Pour the remaining butter on top
5. Brown over low heat
6. Serve at table
7. This plate also serves for any kind of roast if one wishes to use it
source_title: Arte de cozinha

title: Sopa Turca com Queijo Parmezão (Turkish Soup with Parmesan Cheese)
author: Lucas Rigaud
body:
## Ingredients

- One twenty-reis loaf of bread
- Clarified cow butter
- Broth and substance
- Grated cheese
- A cooked hen

## Directions

1. Cut a twenty-reis loaf of bread into two halves and make it into very thin slices
2. Fry them in clarified cow butter
3. When fried and well-colored, place to drain over a sieve
4. After draining, moisten with broth and a little substance
5. Place to boil for an instant
